The '''Abyss Truffle''' {{ETYK|<ruby>奈落<rp>（</rp><rt>アビス</rt><rp>）</rp></ruby>のトリュフ|Abisu no Toryufu}} is an hallucinogenic mushroom that appears in the [[Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|''Digimon Xros Wars'' manga]], [[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 02|''"Xros Heart! Our "King"!!"'']] which acts as a vessel for [[Green Zone]]'s [[Code Crown]].

Aero V-dramon Zero.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Zeromaru |image=Vdramon zero.jpg |imagesize=x320 |kan=ゼロマル |dub=Zeromaru''[[Digimon Journal]]'' (English edition): [https://digimon.net/journal/topic004/ "Digimon Highlights: UlforceVeedramon, Galacticmon and Angoramon!​"] |hp=[[Yagami Taichi (V-Tamer)|Yagami Taichi]] }} '''Zeromaru''', or '''Zero''' {{ETYK|ゼロ}} for short, is the [[partner Digimon]] of [[Yagami Taichi (V-Tamer)|Yagami Taichi]] in ''[[Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01]]'', and a member of the [[Royal Knight]]s in [[Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|the ''Digimon Xros Wars'' manga]]. =Evolution= {{Box Evo |Baby1=[[Botamon]] |Baby2=[[Koromon]]{{Note|In a flashback in ''[[V-53|"The Super Ultimate Appears"]]'', Zeromaru is depicted as a [[Botamon]] on Taichi's [[Digital Monster]] virtual pet, which would likely make it a [[Digital Monster Ver. 1]].

Zeromaru is never depicted in his [[Baby II]] or [[Child]] forms, but in ''[[V-2|"Reversal Combination"]]'', Taichi comments on the many [[Agumon]]s that they encounter that Zeromaru was once ''"like that"'' {{ETYK|おまえもあんな頃あったんだぜ}}, suggesting that his Child form was Agumon (also from the Digital Monster Ver. 1). The logical conclusion from these details is that his Baby II form would have been [[Koromon]], the Baby II Digimon from the Ver. 1.|n=VER1}} |Child=[[Agumon]]{{Note|n=VER1}} |Adult='''[[V-dramon]]''' |Perfect=[[Aero V-dramon]] |Ultimate=[[Ulforce V-dramon]] |Super Ultimate=[[Ulforce V-dramon Future Mode]] (with or without the [[Digimental (V-Tamer)|Digimental]]) }} =Profile= ==Personality== Zeromaru, like Taichi, has a lighthearted and often goofy demeanor. The two often celebrate their wins with a silly victory dance, but are just as prone to bickering over the pettiest things, as Zeromaru is highly emotional and it takes very little to offend or anger him. Despite this, his bond with Taichi is very strong and there is no genuine bad blood between them, to the point that Zeromaru is able to resist [[Vamdemon]]'s attempts to brainwash him and drive a wedge between the two.[[V-13|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Wake Up!! Combination"]] Zeromaru and Taichi have absolute trust in each other and total understanding of each others' feelings.[[V-34|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Anger Overload!!"]] Both will gladly risk their lives for the other, and neither will hesitate to enact or follow plans that put themselves in jeopardy out of faith that the other will not let them down. Zeromaru is also strongly driven by the need to protect his friends and allies, and will push himself well past his own limits if it means protecting them. He is especially protective of Taichi and his honor, and is angered by any insults toward him. He secretly hopes that his relationship with Taichi will never change, to the point that, for a time, he even stops himself from evolving.[[V-18|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Future Potential"]] ==Properties== As a [[V-dramon]], Zeromaru is an [[Ancient Species]] Digimon, which makes his [[Overwrite]]s characteristically violent and causes him to express various unusual powers in response to his emotions, such as powering up when angered.[[V-34|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "VS Omegamon ①, DEAD OR ALIVE"]] Zeromaru is the first Ancient Species Digimon known to have been raised by a [[Tamer]], and after evolving, he becomes a rare living specimen of the supposedly extinct [[Aero V-dramon]] species[[V-34|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "VS Omegamon ①, DEAD OR ALIVE"]] and, ultimately, the first-ever Ancient Species Digimon known to have evolved to the [[Ultimate]] level.[[V-43|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "HOPE ON"]] In the original run of ''[[Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01]]'', Zeromaru's evolutions are one-way and permanent, aside from [[Ulforce V-dramon Future Mode]]. This is no longer the case in ''[[V-Tamer New Courage|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01 ~New Courage~]]'', in which Zeromaru stays in his V-dramon form by default and, at first, is only able to evolve into Aero V-dramon temporarily in order to fight. Zeromaru himself speculates that this could be due to damage he took in his original adventure with Taichi, or an effect of the polyhedron.[[V-Tamer New Courage|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01 ~New Courage~'']] ==Production== ''V-Tamer 01'' creators [[Izawa Hiroshi]] and [[Yabuno Tenya]] wanted the name of Taichi's partner Digimon to include the word "zero," to both make it sound fighter jet-esque, and to pair with the {{ETYK|一|ichi}} ("one") sound in Taichi's name for {{w|binary data|a digital theme}}. However, they moved away from just calling him "Zero" for the purposes of trademarking. Since the name "Zeromaru" is a compound of two Japanese words/symbols which can be taken to mean 0— * {{ETYK|ゼロ|zero}}, a loanword from English, and * {{ETYK|丸|maru}}, "circle," {{w|Japanese numerals#Basic numbering in Japanese|which is used as both a notation (as the symbol 〇) and reading of zeroes in certain contexts}} —the potential interpretation as "00," taken together with the {{ETYK|一|ichi}} in "Taichi," resulted in the "100%" motif with which the duo are associated (e.g. being called the "100% Combination").[[Habu Kazumasa|Habu, Kazumasa]]. [https://digimon-game.bn-ent.net/free/detail.php?id=7 "関係者に聞く　スペシャルインタビュー！　≪　やぶのてんや先生　＆　渡辺けんじ先生　≫"] [''"Listen to the Staff : Special Interview! <<[[Yabuno Tenya]]-san & [[Watanabe Kenji]]-san>>"'']. ''デジモンゲームコミュ'' [''Digimon GameCommu'']. November 12 2017.
([https://garmtranslations.wordpress.com/2018/12/23/digimon-production-secrets-4-tenya-yabuno-interview-part-1-of-2/ English translation by garmmy]) In ''[[V-3|"Folder Continent's Military History"]]'', the two characters even directly call attention to the "100%" interpretation, as does [[Lord Holy Angemon]] to the reference to binary data.[[V-3|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Folder Continent's Military History"]] (pp. 12-13) =Appearances= ==Anime== ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== {{UC}} Zeromaru originated from Taichi's [[Digital Monster]] virtual pet.{{note|n=VER1}} Taichi developed a very strong attachment to Zeromaru, and often spent time with him and taking care of him.[[V-53|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "The Super Ultimate Appears"]] One day, Taichi attempted to enter the [[D-1 Grand Prix]] with Zeromaru, but was disqualified from entering as his species, [[V-dramon]], was not one that had been programmed into the devices and was suspected to be the result of cheating. Shortly after, Taichi was brought into the [[Digital World]] and met Zeromaru in the flesh. Zeromaru joined Taichi on a journey across the Digital World to find his way home, and the two quickly develop a reputation as an unbeatable team, or "100% Combination," with a flawless win rate against other Digimon.[[V-1|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "100% Tamer!!"]] When they meet [[Gabou]], he brings them to [[Holy Angel Castle]] for an audience with [[Lord Holy Angemon]], who has called on them to help stop [[Demon (V-Tamer)|Demon]]'s scheme to create a [[Ultimate#Super Ultimate|Super Ultimate]] Digimon. They accept this mission.[[V-3|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Folder Continent's Military History"]] ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|Digimon Xros Wars}}=== Zeromaru (although he is not referred to by this name) is, in his [[Ulforce V-dramon]] form, a member of the [[Royal Knights]], having been chosen by [[Homeostasis]] to join their ranks some time after his adventures with Taichi. Several hundreds of years in the Digital World's past,[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 16|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Wars!! The Prologue to the End!!"]] he, along with the other Royal Knights, tried and failed to resist the [[Bagra Army]] when it launched its assault during their search for the "red-black two-headed dragon," during which he was blinded by the miasma from [[Tactimon (Xros Wars)|Tactimon]]'s sword, and he and his squires [[Spadamon]], [[Coronamon]] and [[Lunamon]] were caught up in the shattering of the [[Digital World (Xros Wars)|Digital World]] and were sent to the Real World with no way back and no way of contacting their comrades. They took shelter in a secret base in Tokyo.[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 13|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Memories!! The Boys' Old Days"]] On April 3, 20XX, Spadamon brings [[Kudou Taiki]] to Ulforce V-dramon for an audience. Ulforce V-dramon explains the events of the battle and [[Homeostasis]]'s prophecy about the "red-black two-headed dragon," and asks Taiki to bring the group back to the Digital World with him so that they can stop the Bagra Army in time to prevent the prophecy from being fulfilled. When a battle breaks out between [[Blastmon (Xros Wars)|Blastmon]], Taiki and [[Aonuma Kiriha]], Ulforce V-dramon and his squires are present, and Ulforce V-dramon senses that [[Shoutmon (Xros Wars)|Shoutmon]] has the presence/code of his missing-in-action comrade [[Omegamon]].[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 15|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Alliance! The Final Battle in Odaiba!!"]] When Taiki returns to the Digital World, he brings Ulforce V-dramon with him in his [[Xros Loader]], which heals his eyes enough to enable him to join his fellow remaining Royal Knights in battle when they arrive to reinforce [[Xros Heart]] and [[Blue Flare]] at the [[Bagra Pandemonium]]. He and the Royal Knights volunteer to take on [[Arkadimon Ultimate|Arkadimon]] while [[Shoutmon X7]] focuses on [[Lilithmon (Xros Wars)|Lilithmon]]. With the support of a vaccine program developed by [[Duftmon]], he destroys Arkadimon with his {{AT|Dragon Impulse X}}. After the armies force Lilithmon to retreat, he reflects on his own youth with Taichi, how once again the Digital World has turned to the power of human children to be saved, and how the [[General]]s likely will not be able to stay once the war is over. The group comes under attack from [[Amano Nene]]/[[Shademon]] and [[Dark Knightmon (Xros Wars)|Dark Knightmon]], and while the Generals enter the castle to confront Bagramon, Ulforce V-dramon and the Royal Knights hold off Amano and Dark Knightmon's army of [[:Category:Composite Type|Composite Type]] Digimon.[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 17|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Warrior!! The Released Demon!!"]] They witness the formation of the "red-black two-headed dragon," [[Zeed Millenniumon]], by Nene, and he laments that they had failed to notice the warning signs that it, as a likely candidate for the "dragon's" identity, would be revived. Ulforce V-dramon and the other Royal Knights stay outside while the Generals enact their plan to undo Zeed Millenniumon's [[DigiXros]] from within,[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 19|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Apocalypse!! The Beast of Revelation!!"]] and witness both the success of their plan and the appearance of the horde of evil Digimon released from Zeed Millenniumon's separation.[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 21|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Songs!! To All The Teens Who Are Chasing After Their Dreams!!"]] After the battle, before the Generals return home, Ulforce V-dramon approaches Nene with a request for in case Digimon-related incidents occur in the Real World again. ==={{hdr|V-Tamer New Courage|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01 ~New Courage~}}=== When the polyhedron appears in the Digital World, Zeromaru and Taichi join forces once again to stop it, but before long, they are the only ones left to fight it as the endless wave of Virus Digimon it spawns takes all of their allies, and eventually [[Lord Holy Angemon]] himself, out of commission. Zeromaru takes Lord Holy Angemon to a healing capsule. The best that they could manage was to seal it away. After a battle with several of the polyhedron's Digimon, Taichi and Zeromaru are excited to encounter [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure:)|a different Yagami Taichi]] from [[Digimon Adventure:|another reality]] and his [[Agumon (Adventure:)|Agumon]]. While the excitement quickly gives way to the two Taichis bickering, Zeromaru politely introduces himself to Agumon. After explaining the situation, the other Taichi and Agumon volunteer to join them, and together they face another wave of Digimon from the polyhedron, led by a [[Belphemon: Rage Mode]] who quickly overwhelms the group. Despite this, Zeromaru is determined to keep fighting, and when Agumon insists on continuing the fight as well, the two Taichis' determination and courage synchronize, causing a massive [[Crest of Courage]] to manifest in the sky, fulfilling Lord Holy Angemon's foretelling of "new courage," and evolve the two Digimon into [[Ulforce V-dramon Future Mode]] and a specter of [[War Greymon]], who easily destroy the polyhedron. After the battle, Zeromaru (reverted into V-dramon) celebrates with Taichi and the inhabitants of Holy Angel Castle. ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure: Anode Tamer}} & {{hdr|Digimon Adventure: Cathode Tamer|Cathode Tamer}}=== Some event distribution [[V-dramon]]s, such as the one from {{w|Jump Festa|Jump Festa 2000}},ぎょっちゃん (@Gyotchan). [https://twitter.com/Gyotchan/status/1351737797405601792 "ブイドラモン（ゼロマル）のいるアノードテイマーかな🤔 理由は ・通常のプレイで出現しない、リアルイベントで配信されたこと ・20年前のデータが消えずにいること （ワンダースワンのゲームはデータが消えやすい） #フォロワーさんが絶対持ってないであろう物を晒せ #デジモン #digimon"] January 20, 2021, 3:46 AM UTC ±00:00. Tweet. bore the nickname "Zeromaru" {{ETYK|ゼロマル}} in reference to the ''V-Tamer 01'' character. It is currently unknown how many of the other events' distributed V-dramons had this nickname. ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Zeromaru |s1=HC |s2=DCG }} =Additional Information= {{R}}

Akiyama Ryo.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Akiyama Ryo |image=Ryou.gif |imagesize=x320 |kan=秋山 遼 |dub=Ryo Akiyama |age=14 (Tamers) |pd=[[Cyberdramon (Tamers)|Cyberdramon]] |dv=[[Digivice]], White/Blue [[D-3]], Dark Blue [[D-Ark]] |family=Unnamed father |vjp=[[Kanemaru Jun-ichi]] (金丸 淳一) |ven=[[Steve Staley]] }} '''Akiyama Ryo''' is a [[Tamer]] who starred as the protagonist of a series of ''Digimon'' video games for the {{w|Bandai WonderSwan}} and made several cameos in the ''[[Digimon Adventure]]'' anime series before becoming a protagonist of ''[[Digimon Tamers]]''. =Profile= =Evolution= * '''[[Justimon: Blitz Arm]] (with [[Cyberdramon]]{{rfe|DT|50|The Crimson Knight Dukemon - Save Your Beloved Ones!|b}})''' ** '''[[Justimon: Accel Arm]]''' ** '''[[Justimon: Critical Arm]]''' ==Card Game Evolution== * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Evolves From Tamers|Any Digimon that also Evolves from non-White Tamers from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{Note|[[BT18-078 (DCG)]] Duskmon can change any Tamer's Color into any other Color, except White.|n=DuskTamerColor}} * [[Blitzmon]]{{rfc|BT18|063 (DCG)}} * [[Bolgmon]]{{rfc|BT18|067 (DCG)}} * [[Gigasmon]]''[[BT7-061 (DCG)|BT7-061]]'' * [[Grottemon]]''[[BT7-060_(DCG)|BT7-060]]'' * [[Kaiser Greymon]]{{rfc|BT18|095 (DCG)}} * [[Magna Garurumon]]{{rfc|BT18|097 (DCG)}} * [[Mercuremon]]{{rfc|BT12|066 (DCG)}} * [[Rhino Kabuterimon]]{{rfc|BT18|070 (DCG)}} * [[Sephirothmon]]{{rfc|BT12|065 (DCG)}} * [[Susanoomon]]''[[BT7-112 (DCG)|BT7-112]]'' =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure: Our War Game!}}=== Ryo is one of the multiple people across the world that watched [[Agumon (Adventure)|War Greymon]] and [[Gabumon (Adventure)|Metal Garurumon]] battle [[Diablomon (Adventure)|Diablomon]] and gave them encouragement through e-mails. {| |[[File:Akiyama Ryo's cameo, our war game.png|thumb|Akiyama Ryo as seen in ''Digimon Adventure: Our War Game!''.]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}=== In a flashback, Ryo is shown fighting [[Millenniumon (Adventure)|Millenniumon]] alongside [[Ichijouji Ken]] and [[Wormmon (Adventure)|Wormmon]]. After being defeated, Millenniumon tried to infect Ryo with a [[Dark Seed]] but Ken took the blow instead, starting his later transformation into the Digimon Kaiser. {| |[[File:Ken's flashback ZT.jpg|thumb|Akiyama Ryo as seen in ''Digimon Adventure 02''.]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers}}=== {| |[[File:Akiyama Ryo DT.jpg|thumb|Akiyama Ryo as seen in ''Digimon Tamers''.]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: The Runaway Digimon Express}}=== {| |[[File:Akiyama Ryo in Digimon Tamers Runaway Locomon.png|thumb|Akiyama Ryo as seen in ''Digimon Tamers: The Runaway Digimon Express''.]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== Although Ryo does not appear himself, [[Justimon: Blitz Arm|Justimon]], his [[Evolution/Anime#Digimon Tamers|Matrix Evolved]] form with [[Cyberdramon (Tamers)|Cyberdramon]], appears alongside [[Patamon (Adventure)|Angemon]] and [[Tailmon (Adventure)|Angewomon]] among those brought by the [[Old Clock Shop Man]] to fight against [[Quartzmon (Xros Wars)|Quartzmon]]. ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Ryo's Story!}}=== Before Ryo met the [[Tamer#Digimon Tamers|Tamers]], in the desert plain on the [[Digital World (Tamers)|Digital World]], Ryo and [[Cyberdramon (Tamers)|Cyberdramon]] fought [[Megadramon]]. With Ryo hiding behind a rock, Cyberdramon hit Megadramon with his {{ATO|Cyber Nail}} while telling them to bully a Digimon who could fight back instead of destroying defenceless villages. Megadramon told him that no one could defeat them and shot a cliff with their Darkside Attack ({{ATO|Genocide Attack}}), causing an avalanche to fall on top of Ryo. Cyberdramon blocked the avalanche with his body, causing both of them to be buried, while Megadramon took the chance to leave while laughing. Cyberdramon was knocked out, causing Ryo to break down, thinking there was no way out and realizing he was tired of all the fighting. His memories of how it all began came back at that point, of how he didn't come from the [[Digimon Tamers]] Earth but from the Earth of [[Digimon Adventure]]. There, in, his first adventure, he teamed up with Tai Kamiya's ([[Yagami Taichi (Adventure)|Yagami Taichi]]) [[Agumon (Adventure)|Agumon]] to defeat the evil Millenniummon ([[Millenniumon (Adventure)|Millenniumon]]). Millenniumon then came back and Ryo fought him alongside [[Ichijouji Ken]]. Ryo was sure he would not come back, but he did, having digivolved ([[Evolution|evolved]]) into Moon=Millenniummon ([[Moon Millenniumon]]). By that point, Ryo had been trained by Azulongmon ([[Qinglongmon (Adventure)|Qinglongmon]]) to defend the [[Digital World]], but Moon=Millenniummon tricked him and opened a portal in time that hurled him to the ancient Digital World while he travelled to the future. In the past, Ryo met [[Monodramon]] and [[ENIAC]], the world's first computer, who taught him about the existence of multiple [[Digital World (concept)|Digital Worlds]] and Earths and how Moon=Millenniummon was desperate to conquer them all. ENIAC then sent Ryo and Monodramon to the future to face Moon=Millenniummon in his final digivolved form: Xeedmillenniummon ([[Zeed Millenniumon]]), who Monodramon faced by shooting beams from his hands. Monodramon then forced a DNA Digivolution (Jogress Evolution) between himself and Xeedmillenniummon after realizing that it was the only way to defeat him. Together, they became Cyberdramon, and then he and Ryo ended up on an alternate Earth where Digimon was simply a TV cartoon and a [[Card Slash|card game]]. In the present, as Cyberdramon woke up and freed himself from the rocks, Ryo explained that the part of Cyberdramon that was Xeedmillenniummon made him hard to control so he had to bring him to the Digital World they now lived in. However, he couldn't go on any further. Cyberdramon rescued Ryo and told him not to give up when he was around. With his spirits renewed, Ryo got on Cyberdramon's back and they flew away to find and get revenge on Megadramon, who they found as he was destroying a [[Mushmon]] village, and immediately slashed them then shot them with a beam from his hand, finally defeating them. The Mushmon then hailed Ryo and Cyberdramon as heroes and invited them to a feast, which made Ryo realize that they needed to protect the weak and innocent of the Digital World, which became Ryo's true purpouse. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== Akiyama Ryo appears in an omake of the manga titled "Hot-Blooded! The Shining Battle!!". ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers Original Story: Message in the Packet}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Series 1-4: Original Story Special ~with~}}=== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure: Anode Tamer}} & {{hdr|Digimon Adventure: Cathode Tamer|Cathode Tamer}}=== On Christmas Day, 1999, Ryo is summoned to the [[Digital World]] by [[Agumon (Adventure)|Agumon]] who begs for him to save the [[Chosen Children]] who have been kidnapped by [[Millenniumon (Adventure)|Millenniumon]] who has also revived their past enemies. After a series of fierce battles, and rescuing the majority of the Chosen Children he prepares for his final confrontation with Millenniumon, to save [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure)|Yagami Taichi]]. He succeeds and after bidding his friends a tearful goodbye, he returns home. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers}}=== Ryo and [[Ichijouji Ken]] are watching the events of [[Digimon Adventure: Our War Game]] online when they are alerted by [[V-mon]] that another [[Diablomon]] has appeared in the Digital World. Ken and Ryo head to the Digital World where they learn that Diablomon is simply a trap set by Millenniumon who had survived his initial battle with Ryo. Ken meets his own partner, [[Wormmon (Adventure)|Wormmon]], when he and Ryo are separated and the two go on separate adventures attempting to rebuild the [[Digimental|Digimentals]]. Once completed, Ryo battles Millenniumon and once again, he succeeds in defeating him. However, in his last moments he sends out his [[Dark Seed|Dark Seeds]] and aims them at Ryo. Ken shoves Ryo out of the way and takes a Dark Seed in the neck which would eventually lead to his transformation into the Digimon Kaiser. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: D1 Tamers}}=== While Ken remains sick in bed, Ryo is once again invited back to the Digital World, this time, for a Digimon tournament in which the winner would receive one of the [[Four Holy Beasts]] as a partner. After battling [[Izumi Koushiro]], [[Wallace]], [[Tachikawa Mimi]], [[Ishida Yamato]], and Taichi it is revealed to have been a scam to train Ryo to prepare him for yet another battle with Millenniumon. Betrayed Ryo becomes mute and heads in for his battle. Though he succeeds, Millenniumon offers Ryo the chance to come with him through time and detonates his palace. Ryo disappears and Ken continues to hope to one day meet with him again{{Note|'''''Seki Hiromi:''''' "''[...] At the same time Ryo defeated Millenniumon in D-1 Tamers, he was thrown beyond the space-time and lost his memory. That's why after that he met Takato and the others.''"{{ref|{{url|i.imgur.com/7520ZPx.jpg ''V Jump, May 2002 issue.''}}}}|n=D-1}}. ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: Brave Tamer}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== Ryo was introduced as a usable Tamer on January 26, 2021.[https://dmo.gameking.com/News/EventView.aspx?idx=502 GameKing: "New Tamers and Fanglongmon have joined into the Digital World!!!"] ==={{hdr|Digimon New Century}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Akiyama_ryo |s1=DCG }} =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Ryou.gif{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Tamers]] |i2=Akiyama_ryo_acrylickeychain.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c2=[[Digimon Tamers]]

Amano Nene.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Amano Nene |image=Nene amano.png |imagesize=x320 |image2=Amano nene2.png |imagesize2=x320 |kan=天野ネネ |dub=Nene Amano{{ref|''[[Digimon Xros Wars|Digimon Fusion]]''}}''[[BT10-092 (DCG)|BT10-092]]'' [https://twitter.com/digimon_tcg_EN/status/1568992764817203205 (English Ver.)] |age= |school= |pd=[[Sparrowmon (Xros Wars)|Sparrowmon]], [[Mervamon (Xros Wars)|Mervamon]], [[Shademon (Xros Wars)|Luminamon]]{{ref|''[[Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)]]'' continuity}}, [[Dark Knightmon (Xros Wars)|Dark Knightmon]] (formerly) |du= |army=[[Twilight]]; later joins [[Xros Heart]] |dv=Black [[Xros Loader]]; later becomes Light Violet
[[Darkness Loader]] ([[Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|manga]] only, as [[Shademon (Nene Ver.)]]) |vjp=[[Kuwashima Houko]] (桑島 法子) |ven=[[Melissa Fahn]] |family=[[Amano Yuu]] (Younger brother, ''Anime + Super Digica Taisen'')
[[Amano Kotone]] (Younger sister,''Manga'')
Unnamed father (anime only) }} '''Amano Nene''' is one of the protagonists of ''[[Digimon Xros Wars]]''. =Profile= Nene is a mysterious girl who resides within the Digital World who comes and goes as she pleases. She is the General of the [[Twilight]] Army and specializes in information and reconnaissance. She keeps close tabs on both [[Aonuma Kiriha]] and [[Kudou Taiki]] and their teams. Upon being questioned about her stalking habits, she says that she likes strong boys who can fight. Nene often serves as a walking tutorial for [[Xros Heart]], informing [[Taiki]] about the [[Code Crown]]s, [[Zone (Xros Wars)|Zone]]s, and various functions of his [[Xros Loader]]. On several instances of Taiki becoming endangered, she has informed Kiriha of his whereabouts and situation, prompting the other boy to rush off to his rescue. [[File:XWM03_mantaraymonfleet.png|right|thumb|Sushi anyone?]] Although she doesn't openly flaunt it, Nene is extremely strong, possessing Digimon with high firepower such as [[Sparrowmon]]. In [[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 03|"Xros Girls!! The Mysterious Beauty Nene!!"]], she is shown to be sunbathing in the [[Island Zone]]... while Sparrowmon completely tears [[Neptunemon]]'s army apart. =Evolution= {{Box Xros |Base=[[Amano Nene]] |X1=[[Shademon (Nene Ver.)]] |X1b=[[Amano Nene]] + [[Shademon]] |X2=[[Moon Millenniumon]] |X2b=[[Amano Nene]] + [[Shademon]] (inside [[Zeed Millenniumon]]) |X3=[[Luminamon (Nene Ver.)]] |X3b=[[Amano Nene]] + [[Luminamon]] }} ==Card Game Evolution== * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Evolves From Tamers|Any Digimon that also Evolves from non-White Tamers from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{Note|[[BT18-078 (DCG)]] Duskmon can change any Tamer's Color into any other Color, except White.|n=DuskTamerColor}} * [[Blitzmon]]{{rfc|BT18|063 (DCG)}} * [[Blizzarmon]]''[[BT7-023 (DCG)|BT7-023]]'' * [[Bolgmon]]{{rfc|BT18|067 (DCG)}} * [[Calamaramon]]''[[BT12-025 (DCG)|BT12-025]]'' * [[Chackmon]]''[[BT7-021 (DCG)|BT7-021]]'' * [[Daipenmon]]{{rfc|BT18|026 (DCG)}} * [[Garummon]]''[[BT4-027 (DCG)|BT4-027]]''''[[BT7-022 (DCG)|BT7-022]]'' * [[Gigasmon]]''[[BT7-061 (DCG)|BT7-061]]'' * [[Grottemon]]''[[BT7-060 (DCG)|BT7-060]]'' * [[Kaiser Greymon]]{{rfc|BT18|095 (DCG)}} * [[Luminamon (Nene Ver.)]]{{rfc|BT19|102 (DCG)}} * [[Magna Garurumon]]{{rfc|BT18|097 (DCG)}} * [[Mercuremon]]{{rfc|BT12|066 (DCG)}} * [[Ranamon]]''[[BT12-024 (DCG)|BT12-024]]'' * [[Rhino Kabuterimon]]{{rfc|BT18|070 (DCG)}} * [[Sephirothmon]]{{rfc|BT12|065 (DCG)}} * [[Shademon]]{{rfc|BT19|068 (DCG)}} * [[Susanoomon]]''[[BT7-112 (DCG)|BT7-112]]'' * [[Wolfmon]]''[[BT4-025 (DCG)|BT4-025]]'' =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars}} & {{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Evil Death Generals and the Seven Kingdoms|The Evil Death Generals and the Seven Kingdoms}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== A year after Bagramon's defeat, she is living in Hong Kong and became an idol. Amano Yuu.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Amano Yuu |image=Amano yuu.png |imagesize=x320 |image2=Amano yuu2.png |imagesize2=x320 |kan=天野ユウ |dub=Ewan Amano{{ref|''[[Digimon Xros Wars|Digimon Fusion]]''}}
Yuu Amano''[[BT10-093 (DCG)|BT10-093]]'' [https://twitter.com/digimon_tcg_EN/status/1568992764817203205 (English Ver.)] |age= |school=Shinonome East Middle School (東雲東中学校) |pd=[[Damemon (Xros Wars)|Damemon]], [[Dark Knightmon (Xros Wars)|Dark Knightmon]] (formerly) |du= |army=[[Twilight]]; later joins [[Xros Heart]]
[[Bagra Army]]''[[BT10-093 (DCG)|BT10-093]]'' |dv=Black [[Xros Loader]]; [[Darkness Loader]], later becomes Yellow [[Xros Loader]]{{Note|Yuu has used as many [[Digivice]]s as [[Akiyama Ryou]]; although he has only two different Digivice models (he had claimed two Xros Loaders and a Darkness Loader), whereas Ryou has used three separate models (a Digivice, a [[D-3]] and a [[D-Ark]]).|n=Digivices}} |vjp=[[Oki Kanae]] (沖 佳苗) |ven=[[Cindy Robinson]] |family=[[Amano Nene]] (Older sister), Unnamed father }} '''Amano Yuu''' is an antagonist, later turned protagonist, in ''[[Digimon Xros Wars]]'', and a protagonist in ''[[Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time]]''. =Profile= Yuu is the younger brother of [[Amano Nene]]. While growing up, he loved other people and creatures and didn't want to hurt anyone, but had to hold himself back to prevent hurting others and came to resent it. Yuu's first encounter with the Digital World was several years prior to the commencement of the series; while hiding from everyone in sorrow from the death of his grandmother, [[Damemon (Xros Wars)|Damemon]] first appeared before him, and [[Dark Knightmon (Xros Wars)|Skull Knightmon]] drew him into digital space, had him register his black Xros Loader, and promised to come back for him one day when the "[[Bagra Army|game]]" was "ready" for him. =Card Game Evolution= * [[Agnimon]]{{rfc|BT18|011 (DCG)}} * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Evolves From Tamers|Any Digimon that also Evolves from non-White Tamers from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{Note|[[BT18-078 (DCG)]] Duskmon can change any Tamer's Color into any other Color, except White.|n=DuskTamerColor}} * [[Blitzmon]]{{rfc|BT18|063 (DCG)}} * [[Bolgmon]]{{rfc|BT18|067 (DCG)}} * [[Duskmon]]{{rfc|BT18|078 (DCG)}} * [[Gigasmon]]''[[BT7-061 (DCG)|BT7-061]]'' * [[Grottemon]]''[[BT7-060 (DCG)|BT7-060]]'' * [[Kaiser Greymon]]{{rfc|BT18|095 (DCG)}} * [[Kaiser Leomon]]''[[BT7-073 (DCG)|BT7-073]]'' * [[Luminamon (Nene Ver.)]]{{rfc|BT19|102 (DCG)}} * [[Löwemon]]''[[BT7-068 (DCG)|BT7-068]]'' * [[Magna Garurumon]]{{rfc|BT18|097 (DCG)}} * [[Mercuremon]]{{rfc|BT12|066 (DCG)}} * [[Raihimon]]{{rfc|BT18|081 (DCG)}} * [[Rhino Kabuterimon]]{{rfc|BT18|070 (DCG)}} * [[Sephirothmon]]{{rfc|BT12|065 (DCG)}} * [[Susanoomon]]''[[BT7-112 (DCG)|BT7-112]]'' * [[Velgrmon]]{{rfc|BT18|079 (DCG)}} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars}} & {{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Evil Death Generals and the Seven Kingdoms|The Evil Death General and the Seven Kingdoms}}=== [[Amano Nene]] was led to believe that Yuu was lost or trapped somewhere in the [[Digital World (Xros Wars)|Digital World]], as discussed in [[XW19]], and so was working with [[Dark Knightmon (Xros Wars)|Skull Knightmon]] to try and find him, or so she said; in reality, Skull Knightmon was entirely aware of Yuu's whereabouts and used that knowledge against Nene to coerce her into helping him. Once he came into possession of the [[Darkness Loader]], however, he went back on his deal and refused to return Yuu. During [[Kudou Taiki]]'s absence from the Digital World, Skull Knightmon began allowing Yuu to "play the game" as a human general to lead the [[Bagra Army]]. Yuu was under the mistaken impression that the Digital World was little more than a game by nature, that no-one would actually die, and were he to kill Taiki or Nene they would still be alive and well in the [[Real World]]. Taiki and Nene's best efforts to convince him otherwise failed, if partially because of the influence of Damemon and Skull Knightmon, and he simply continued to cause the deaths of Digimon en masse gleefully. He first revealed himself to [[Xros Heart]] in [[Honey Land]] in [[XW37]]. His fight with Taiki in [[XW47]] caused him to have doubts about what he'd been led to believe, and with the death of Damemon from wounds sustained in the battle of [[Hell's Field]], he fully realised the magnitude of his actions, but was then captured by Dark Knightmon and used to fuel the Darkness Loader. He was rescued by Taiki and, in the final battle against Darkness Bagramon in the real world, transformed the Darkness Loader into a yellow Xros Loader and led the [[Xros Heart]] charge against the bulk of Bagramon's forces as a [[General]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== In the year since, he has joined Taiki's [[Xros Heart]] street basketball team along with [[Akashi Tagiru]]. He is in the same class as Tagiru, and thinks him to be an airhead at times. As a result of deeply regretting his actions under Skull Knightmon's influence and his desire to atone for them, he objects strongly to anyone who treats anything Digimon-related such as Digimon hunting as merely a game, trivial or to be exploited for personal gain, and is very vocal about his distaste; he has come into conflict with Tagiru and [[Mashimo Hideaki]] on this matter on several occasions. He currently lives in a massive apartment alone, as Nene has since moved to Hong Kong to work as an idol and his other relatives are overseas at the moment. ===={{hdr|Collection}}==== {| border="1" class="wikitable" style=" border: 1px solid #88a; border-collapse: collapse;" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="5" margin: auto; align="center" |- !|Digimon !!Ep |- ||[[File:Damemon.jpg|70px]] [[Damemon (Xros Wars)|Damemon]] || [[XW59]] |- ||[[File:Ballistamon.jpg|70px]] [[Ballistamon (Xros Wars)|Ballistamon]] || [[XW63]] and [[XW73]] (Briefly borrowed from [[Kudou Taiki]]) |- ||[[File:Dorulumon.jpg|70px]] [[Dorulumon (Xros Wars)|Dorulumon]] || [[XW63]] and [[XW73]] (Briefly borrowed from [[Kudou Taiki]]) |- ||[[File:Superstarmon.jpg|70px]] [[Superstarmon]] || [[XW63]] |- ||[[File:Star armies XW76.png|70px]] [[Star Armies]] || [[XW63]] |- ||[[File:Rookchessmon.jpg|70px]] [[Rook Chessmon (Black)]] || [[XW74]] |- |} ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|Digimon Xros Wars}}=== Yuu appears in one of the omakes. ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Amano_yuu |s1=DCG }} ==={{hdr|Super Digica Taisen}}=== Amano Yuu is one of the [[General]]s in the [[Digimon Xros Wars Super Digica Taisen|Super Digica Taisen]] card game. =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Amano_yuu.png{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Xros Wars: The Evil Death Generals and the Seven Kingdoms]] |i2=Amano_yuu_back.gif{{!}}200x200px |c2=[[Digimon Xros Wars: The Evil Death Generals and the Seven Kingdoms]] |i3=Yuu Amano.png |c3=[[Digimon Xros Wars: The Evil Death Generals and the Seven Kingdoms]] |i4=Yuu Amano SDT.png{{!}}200x200px |c4=[[Digimon Xros Wars Super Digica Taisen]] |i7=Amano_yuu2.png{{!}}200x200px |c7=[[Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time]] |i8=Amano_yuu3.png{{!}}200x200px |c8=[[Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time]] }} ==Miscellaneous== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Amano yuu xw death generals character sheet.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Xros Wars: The Evil Death Generals and the Seven Kingdoms]]

Beast Spirit of Darkness.wikitext:
{{Item
|name=Beast Spirit of Darkness
|image=Spirit darkness k.png
|imagesize=320
|kan={{j2|闇のビーストスピリット|Yami no Bīsuto Supiritto}}
|dub=Beast Spirit of Darkness
}}

The '''Beast Spirit of Darkness''' (闇のビーストスピリット ''Yami no Bīsuto Supiritto''), also known as the '''Spirit of Darkness K''' (闇のスピリットK ''Yami no Supiritto Kei'') is one of the twenty [[Legendary Spirits]]. It contains the power of [[Ancient Sphinxmon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]], and allows its user to evolve into the Beast Form [[Hybrid]] [[Digimon]] [[Kaiser Leomon]].

=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}===
[[Image:Beast Spirit of Darkness.JPG|right|thumb|The Beast Spirit of Darkness in ''Digimon Frontier''.]]
The '''Beast Spirit of Darkness''' (闇のビーストスピリット ''Yami no Biisuto Supiritto'') was created from [[Ancient Sphinxmon]]. It was found at some point by [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]], who corrupted it into the [[Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness]] and forced on [[Kimura Kouichi]], who then became [[Velgrmon]]. It purified again after being scanned by [[Minamoto Kouji]] when Kouichi accepted that it and he were nor inherently evil. It then allowed him to spirit evolve into [[Kaiser Leomon]].

Beast Spirit of Earth.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Beast Spirit of Earth |image=Spirit earth b.png |imagesize=320 |kan={{j2|土のビーストスピリット|Tsuchi no Bīsuto Supiritto}} |dub=Beast Spirit of Earth }} The '''Beast Spirit of Earth''' (土のビーストスピリット ''Tsuchi no Bīsuto Supiritto''), also known as the '''Spirit of Earth B''' (土のスピリットB ''Tsuchi no Supiritto Bī'') is one of the twenty [[Legendary Spirits]]. It contains the power of [[Ancient Volcamon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]], and allows its user to evolve into the Beast Form [[Hybrid]] [[Digimon]] [[Gigasmon]]. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== [[Image:Beast Spirit of Earth.JPG|right|thumb|The Beast Spirit of Earth in ''Digimon Frontier''.]] The Beast Spirit of Earth was created from [[Ancient Volcamon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]]. At some point it was found by [[Grottemon]] and allowed him to slide evolve to [[Gigasmon]]. Beast Spirit of Light.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Beast Spirit of Light |image=Spirit light b.png |imagesize= |kan={{j2|光のビーストスピリット|Hikari no Bīsuto Supiritto}} |dub=Beast Spirit of Light }} The '''Beast Spirit of Light''' (光のビーストスピリット ''Hikari no Bīsuto Supiritto''), also known as the '''Spirit of Light B''' (光のスピリットB ''Hikari no Supiritto Bī'') is one of the twenty [[Legendary Spirits]]. It contains the power of [[Ancient Garurumon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]], and allows its user to evolve into the Beast Form [[Hybrid]] [[Digimon (species)|Digimon]] [[Garummon]]. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== According to [[Baromon]], the Beast Spirit of Light is one of the Spirits that was given to [[Seraphimon (Frontier)|Seraphimon]] after the Warrior Ten defeated [[Lucemon (Frontier)|Lucemon]].{{rfe|DF|38|Endless Death Match! The Prelude of Lucemon's Revival}} When [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]] turned evil and began his war, the Beast Spirit of Light was buried on a cliffside and guarded by the three [[Digital World (Frontier)#Guardian deities|guardian deity statues]]. According to a local legend, the "spirit of the legendary warrior" would be resurrected when "the three eyes" shone ({{ETYK|{{j2|三つの目が光り時、伝説の闘士の魂が蘇る|Mittsu no me ga hikari toki, densetsu no toushi no tamashī ga yomigaeru}}}}) — the "eyes" being three large rubies, one embedded in each deity statue, although one of the rubies had gone missing over time. When complete, the rubies emit lasers that all converge on the location of the Beast Spirit and release it from the ground. A [[Gottsumon]] who lived [[Digital World (Frontier)#Gottsumon Village|nearby]] had studied the guardian deities and hunted for the third ruby, so that he could use it to Spirit Evolve and protect his home. The ruby is uncovered when [[Grottemon (Frontier)|Grottemon]]'s (as [[Gigasmon]]) {{ATO|Earthquake}} splits the ground open, and Gottsumon takes it to the statues. When [[Minamoto Kouji]] (as [[Wolfmon]]) and Grottemon bring the fight to the deity statues, he passes the ruby to Kouji and tells him the legend. Kouji climbs one of the statues and fits the ruby in its eye, triggering the statues to release the Beast Spirit of Light. Kouji scans the Spirit and uses it to [[Evolution#Digimon Frontier|Spirit Evolve]] into [[Garummon]]. However, at first, Kouji is unable to control his Garummon form and runs wild, and when Gigasmon attacks him to take advantage of his inability to control himself, the clash between the two destroys both the cliffside and the guardian deities.{{rfe|DF|10|An Uncontrollable Beast Spirit!? Garmmon's Evolution}} Going forward, Kouji continues to use the Spirit without further difficulty controlling it. In his second fight with [[Kimura Kouichi|Duskmon]], Seraphimon's [[DigiTama]] releases some of its power to enable Kouji to combine the Beast Spirit with the [[Human Spirit of Light]] to become [[Beowolfmon]].{{rfe|DF|27|Double Spirit Miracle! Beowolfmon is Born}} During the battle against Cherubimon at the [[Rose Morning Star]], Cherubimon subdues Kouji and takes his D-Scanner, along with all the Spirits in it, but [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]] steals all of the D-Scanners and Spirits back under the pretense of purifying Cherubimon and returns them to the Chosen Children. When Ofanimon upgrades Kouji's D-Scanner, he becomes able to use this Spirit with nine other Spirits to Hyper Spirit Evolve into [[Magna Garurumon]].{{rfe|DF|35|Spirits Become One! Takuya and Kouji's Ultimate Evolution!}} In their last battles with Lucemon, the Chosen Children gain the ability to combine all twenty Spirits, including the Beast Spirit of Light, to become [[Susanoomon]].{{rfe|DF|48|Light and Darkness are One! Kouichi's Last Wish}} {| |[[File:Beast Spirit of Light.JPG|x225px|right|thumb|The Beast Spirit of Light in ''Digimon Frontier''.]] |[[File:DF10 Beast Spirit Statues 1.JPG|right|thumb|The Beast Spirit's guardian deities in ''Digimon Frontier''.]] |[[File:DF10 Beast Spirit Statues 2.JPG|right|thumb|The guardian deities releasing the Beast Spirit in ''Digimon Frontier''.]] |} ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory}}=== The Beast Spirit of Light is an item that, once the player obtains it, allows any of the compatible Adult Digimon ([[Wolfmon]], [[Sangloupmon]], [[Zubaeagermon]], and [[Bao Hackmon]]) to evolve into Garummon, as long as they also meet the other evolution requirements. The item is not consumed in the evolution, so Garummon can be evolved into any number of times. This Spirit, along with the [[Beast Spirit of Fire]], is obtained in the "Territory Capture: Dinosaurists" case in Chapter 13. The Dinosaurists Leader's Garmmon and [[Vritramon]] drop these Spirits when they are defeated. ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Beas_spirit_of_light |s1=HC }} =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Spirit light b.png{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Profile]] }} |2=misc |t2= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Garummon early.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=Early designデジモンウェブ公式 (@digimonweb_net) [https://twitter.com/digimonweb_net/status/1110376621041573888 "「デジモンフロンティアBlu-ray BOX」4月2日(火)に発売！今日の #デジモンフロンティア 掘り出しイラストはガルムモンを公開。台座状態のイラストとガルムモン初期デザインと決定稿です。初期ネーム候補はブレードガルルモン・セイバーガルルモンとありますね😄 #デジモン #Digimon] March 26 2019, 3:03 AM UTC ±00:00. Tweet.
(concept art) }} }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Spirit light b vpet dscan.gif |c1=[[D-Scanner Toy|D-Scanner]] |i2=Spirit light b vpet tector.gif |c2=[[D-Tector Toy|D-Tector]] }} =Additional Information= {{R}} [[Category:Legendary Spirits]]

[[Image:Beast Spirit of Thunder.JPG|right|thumb|The Beast Spirit of Thunder in ''Digimon Frontier''.]]
The Beast Spirit of Thunder was created from [[Ancient Beatmon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]]. It was hidden in the ocean. A [[Whamon]] accidentally swallowed it, then got stuck in a cave. As quiescent would have it, the Legendary Chosen happened to save a battle against [[Grottomon]] in this cave. Whamon tried to help by shooting Grottomon with water from his spout and released the Beast Spirit of Thunder at the same time. [[Shibayama Junpei]] then took it and used it to spirit evolve into [[Bolgmon]].

Behemoth.wikitext:
{{S2 |name=Behemoth, also spelled '''Behemosu''' |kan=ベヒーモス |ipa= |rom=Behīmosu |da={{DigiAlph|be|hi|-|mo|su}} |da2={{DigiAlph2|b|e|h|e|m|o|t|h}} |da2= |image=Behemoth.jpg |imagesize=320 |l1= |l1ref= |a1= |a1ref= |t1= |t1ref= |f1= |w= |wref= |s1= |p=Misc |un=Digimon Series Memorial Book: Digimon Animation Chronicle |e=A cursed motorcycle that has its own will and takes over the consciousness of its rider. It often attacked a village of [[Tuchidarumon]] and was feared as the "{{NN|Beast of Iron}}". After losing its rider, it was given to [[Impmon (Tamers)|Beelzebumon]] by [[Zhuqiaomon]], and became his favorite vehicle. |j=自らの意思を持ち、乘った者の意識を乗っ取る呪われたバイク。ツチダルモンの村をたびたび襲い、「{{NNK|鉄の獣}}」と恐れられていた。乘り手を失った後、スーツェーモンからベルゼブモンに与えられ、その愛車となった。 |u= |ety={{HEB}} {{ETY|Behemoth}} (בהמות)
1} An evil creature from the Judeo-Christian mythology; it comes from the Hebraic ''בהמות'', meaning monster or animal.
2} A {{url|microship.com/behemoth-computer-history-museum|computer-controlled bicycle}} from the USA. |yd=2001 |ydr= |ad={{hdr|Digimon Tamers - Episode 27|Digimon Tamers: "Impmon Evolves! The Shudder of Beelzebumon the Dark Lord"}} |md={{hdr|Digimon ReCollection}} |gd={{hdr|Digimon Masters}} |cd=Bo-644 |dlf=y }} =Attack Techniques= {{T |name=??? |ref={{--}} |kan={{--}} |rom={{--}} |desc={{--}} }} =Evolution= ==Evolves From== ==Evolves To== =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers}}=== This motorcycle was terrorizing the [[Digital World]] before [[Impmon (Tamers)|Beelzebumon]] became its owner. A [[Tsuchidarumon]] village was beset by this motorcycle. When [[Guilmon (Tamers)|Guilmon]] decided to drive that motorcycle, a [[Choromon]] appeared, saying that it was a possessed driver before Guilmon. Choromon also told that Behemoth controls its driver. [[Leomon (Tamers)|Leomon]] helped Guilmon to throw off the motorcycle, and, it suddenly fell. A short time later, Beelzebumon came, and Behemoth found its driver. {| |[[File:DT27 cap.jpg|thumb|Beelzebumon driving Behemoth from ''Digimon Tamers''.]] |[[File:Beelzebumon Behemoth.jpg|thumb|Beelzebumon driving Behemoth from ''Digimon Tamers''.]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: The Runaway Digimon Express}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== Beelzebumon can be seen in the silhouetted in the background riding Behemoth in one scene. ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon ReCollection}}=== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon World 3}}=== [[Beelzebumon]] rides Behemoth in the opening of the game. ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== Behemoth is available as a "riding mode" item, which when equipped allows the Tamer to ride with their Beelzebumon on the back of the motorcycle. ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon All-Star Rumble}}=== One of [[Impmon (Tamers)|Beelzebumon]]'s attacks involves it summoning Behemoth to drive into the enemy Digimon. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth}}=== The [[Beelzebumon]] of the [[Seven Great Demon Lords]] rides Behemoth. Raisable Beelzebumon also ride Behemoth after winning a battle. Beelzebumon: Blast Mode rides Behemoth instead of walking. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory}}=== The [[Beelzebumon]] of the [[Seven Great Demon Lords]] rides Behemoth. Raisable Beelzebumon also ride Behemoth after winning a battle. [[Beelzebumon: Blast Mode]] rides Behemoth instead of walking. ==={{hdr|Digimon ReArise}}=== [[Beelzebumon: Blast Mode]] summons and rides Behemoth when it uses its Behemoth×Death Slinger attack. ==={{hdr|Digimon New Century}}=== [[Beelzebumon]] rides Behemoth. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Loader Toy|Digimon Xros Loader}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |s1=HC |s2=BS }} =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Behemoth2.jpg{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Collectors]] |i2=Behemoth3.jpg{{!}}200px |c2=[[Digimon Collectors]] |i3=Beelzebumon Behemoth gem.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c3=[[G.E.M. Big Death-Stars.wikitext:
The '''Big Death-Stars''' (ビッグデスターズ ''Biggu Desutāzu'') are a group of six Digimon who are themed after the elements which constitute the {{w|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Wuxing}} and the {{w|yin and yang|Moon}}, each representing one of days of the week. Black.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Black |image=Blackwargreymonx_re.jpg |imagesize=x320 |kan=ブラック |dub= |hp=[[Kuga Yuuya]] }} '''Black''' is the [[Partner Digimon]] of [[Kuga Yuuya]] in [[Digimon World Re:Digitize]]. =Evolution= {{Box Evo |Baby1= |Baby2= |Child= |Adult= |Perfect= |Ultimate='''[[Black War Greymon (X-Antibody)]]''' |Ultimate2=[[Imperialdramon: Fighter Mode]] (with ''Imperial Dragon's Ancient Cannon''){{ref|''[[Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode]]''}} }} =Profile= Black is greatly loyal to his [[Tamer]], [[Kuga Yuuya]]. He was modified by Yuuya through a [[GIGO Company|GIGO]] backdoor to become the strongest Digimon in order to serve as a warden of the [[Digital World]], and became even stronger thanks to obtaining the [[X-Antibody]] before the events of the game. Black rarely speaks and just silently follows orders, only voicing his opinions if he sees Yuuya acting oddly. =Appearances= ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize (Manga)|Digimon World Re:Digitize}}=== As [[Mikagura Mirei]] observed the Digital Space inside GIGO Server with her [[Partner Digimon]], [[Angewomon (Re:Digitize)|Angewomon]] and [[Lady Devimon (Re:Digitize)|Lady Devimon]] in "''[[Digimon World Re:Digitize - Chapter 01|Digital Monster]]''", she was distracted by the appearance of a Digimon's signal and warned her Digimon to be careful. A [[Black War Greymon (X-Antibody)]] called Black then appeared in front of Angewomon and Lady Devimon, as his [[Tamer]], Yuuya, pointed out how he had finally found the [[hacker]]. He commended Mirei's hacking abilities, as he had noticed there was someone lurking in the server but she had made it difficult for him to find her. However, as the one who'd lead [[GIGO Company]] someday, he couldn't allow her to do as she pleased and would dispose of her. Their battle would later interrupt [[Taiga]] and [[Rindou Akiho|Nyanko Tamer's]] battle, as it caused a Colosseum wall to suddenly explode, startling Nyanko Tamer, and Angewomon and Lady Devimon fell from said explosion. Black then appeared from the explosion, as Yuuya pointed out he had Mirei cornered. Continuing in "''[[Digimon World Re:Digitize - Chapter 02|Complication!]]''", Black let loose a blast from its mouth at Angewomon and Lady Devimon, though they both managed to avoid it. Taiga, Nyanko Tamer, [[Digimaru|Agumon]] and [[Digitorin]] got sent flying by the shock wave it caused. Black then charged at Angewomon, to Mirei's fear. Yuuya was confident that meant one of Mirei's Partner Digimon was down, but Black was stopped by a barrage of fireballs from Digitorin. Nyanko Tamer then screamed at Yuuya and Mirei for barging into their match, shocking Taiga and [[Nicolai Petrov]], who observed everything alongside Taiga from his computer and found her scary, as did Taiga. Yuuya realized the damage was giving him temporary restrictions. Mirei made an exit and commanded her Digimon to escape through it, as Yuuya would lose all track of her once the exit closed, and thanks to the restrictions, they succeeded in doing so. Taiga, Nyanko Tamer, Agumon and Digitorin looked at the exit, as the former pointed out Angewomon and Lady Devimon had disappeared. Black turned to the [[Tamers]] left in the Colosseum and, in a sound-only connection, Yuuya pointed out that their interference was very irksome and he had no time to play along with them, which Nyanko Tamer found very rude. However, for Yuuya, both Digital Monsters and their low-level battle really were nothing but a game. Nyanko Tamer couldn't let that go unchallenged and Yuuya asked her if she wanted to test him, warning her that she should be careful as Black and him were a little irritated. Black then charged in and swiped at Agumon and Digitorin. Akiho reacted at the blow, while Taiga decided he had to help Digitorin, as did Nico, who sent in his Partner, [[Sashenka]], through his wrist-mounted [[List of Digivices|Digivice]]. Sashenka rescued Digitorin and Agumon [[Evolution|Warp Evolved]] into [[War Greymon]]. However, Yuuya was unfazed, as he thought that no matter how many people joined or evolved, they were no match for him and would teach them that in an instant. Black retracted his arms and legs, confusing Taiga, then let loose a massive shockwave. War Greymon shielded Sashenka and Digitorin with his body. The attack caused explosions all over the Colosseum and left War Greymon badly wounded. Taiga flew towards his partner, as Yuuya asked everyone if they understood their powerlessness as he was a chosen one unlike them. With his hand over War Greymon, Taiga pointed out that it had to be a mistake for someone like Yuuya, who only flaunted around his power, to be chosen. Yuuya pointed out that the scenary around them was the proof of his status and his complaints were those of a loser, then commanded Black to put an end to the fight, aiming at Nico, Nyanko Tamer, Sashenka and the KOed Digitorin. An emergency announcement then sounded off, that the battlefield building Colosseum would be updated to the digitower interior set up within the Digital World. Black then charged at his enemies. Nico challenged him, Nyanko Tamer pointed out he was in no shape to fight, and Nico acknowledged she was right but there were times where he had to fight no matter what. Taiga was worried as they'd all die at this rate and begged War Greymon to lend them his power. This awoke his Partner Digimon who, after a roar, shot a {{ATO|Gaia Force}} at Black, to Yuuya's surprise as he should've been near-death. The attack caused massive damage to Black, shocking Yuuya as it shouldn't have had so much power, and he had Black retreat afterwards. ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Encode}}=== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== ==TCG== ==={{hdr|Digimon Card Game}}=== Black is shown in the cards [[BT11-074 (DCG)|BT11-074]] (as [[Black War Greymon (X-Antibody)]]) alongside [[Kuga Yuuya]]. =Image Gallery= {{IG |w=200 |h=200 |bg=white |i1=Blackwargreymonx_re.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon World Re:Digitize|Re:Digitize]]
(Black War Greymon (X-Antibody)) |i2=Imperialdramon fighter re.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c2=[[Digimon World Re:Digitize|Re:Digitize]]
(Imperialdramon: Fighter Mode) }} =Additional Information= {{R}} {{NavboxReDigitize}} [[Category:Digimon World Re:Digitize characters]] [[Category:Partner Digimon]]

Black Digitron.wikitext:
{{Item
|name=Black Digitron
|image=Blackdigitron.jpg
|imagesize=x320
|kan=ブラックデジトロン
|rom=Burakku Dejitoron
}}

'''Black Digitron''' (ブラックデジトロン, ''Burakku Dejitoron'') is a mysterious secretion that is said to influence the evolution of a Digimon into a darker colored [[subspecies]]. In recent years, it has been confirmed that if it is mixed in with someone in some form or other, they will temporarily take on a black appearance, and during that time, their power will increase.{{refd|Omegamon Zwart|omegamon_zwart}}

In the case of [[Black Gaogamon]] and [[Black Mach Gaogamon]], the influence of Black Digitron seems to have lowered their speed and agility, but increased endurance.{{refd|Black Gaogamon}}{{refd|Black Mach Gaogamon}} Its introduction into [[Omegamon Alter-S]] caused it to lose its sense of self, leaving it in a berserk state.{{refd|Omegamon Alter-B|omegamon_alter-b}}

Thus far, no Digimon known to have Black Digitron mixed in with them were confirmed to have been infected by [[Regulusmon]]'s [[Gulus Realm Burst|GRB]] (Gulus Realm Burst) contagion.{{refd|Regulusmon|regulusmon}}

==Digimon with Black Digitron==

*[[Black Gaogamon]]

*[[Black Rapidmon]]
*[[Omegamon Alter-B]]
*[[Omegamon Zwart]]

|rom=Bureibu Sunacchā

The '''Brave Snatcher''' (ブレイブスナッチャー, ''Bureibu Sunacchā'') is a device that appears in ''[[Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time]]''. It is required to [[Hunter|hunt]] [[Quartzmon (Xros Wars)|Quartzmon]] and creating it requires power from 6 different types of [[List of Digivices|Digivice]] (specifically, [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure)|Yagami Taichi]]'s [[Digivice]], [[Motomiya Daisuke]]'s [[D-3]], [[Matsuda Takato]]'s [[D-Arc]], [[Kanbara Takuya]]'s [[D-Scanner]], [[Daimon Masaru]]'s [[Digivice Burst]], and [[Kudou Taiki]]'s [[Digimon Xros Loader]]). It is made from residual data left by [[Bagramon (Xros Wars)|Bagramon]] after he was defeated by [[Shoutmon|Shoutmon X7 Superior Mode]], and resembles his artificial right arm.

In order to defeat Quartzmon, [[Akashi Tagiru]] [[DigiXros|DigiXrossed]] the powered-up but broken Brave Snatcher with his [[Partner Digimon|Partner]], [[Gumdramon (Xros Wars)|Arresterdramon: Superior Mode]], forming [[Xros Up Arresterdramon Superior Mode (Brave Snatcher)]], who succeeded at the task and returned Quartzmon to a [[Digitama]]. It's unknown what happened to the Brave Snatcher afterwards.

Chronomon's Treasure.wikitext:
[[File:Treasure of chronomon orb DSTS.jpg|thumb|right]]
'''Chronomon's Treasure''', shortened in-game as '''Treasure''' (秘宝 ''aHihō''), is a source of sacred power guarded by [[Junomon (Time Stranger)|Junomon]] in ''[[Digimon Story: Time Stranger]]''. This orb of power contains the data of [[Chronomon (Time Stranger)|Chronomon]] that grants its carrier the ability to manipulate time.

=Appearances=
==Video Games==
==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Time Stranger}}===
Some time after compromising [[Iliad]]'s [[Energy Vein]], the [[Titans]] invaded the [[Guardian Palace]] for the treasure. They were initially unsucessful, but [[Titamon (Time Stranger)|Titamon]] appears personally and knocks Junomon down her throne. Junomon hands the treasure to [[Yuki Dan|Dan]]/[[Yuki Kanan|Kanan]] and [[Misono Inori]] so she could have [[Aegiomon (Time Stranger)|Aegiomon]] left behind to defend Iliad from the Titans for herself. However, Aegiomon's strong bonds with Inori prevents him from agreeing with Junomon's deal. Out of a fit of rage, Junomon then opened numerous rifts that drawn countless Digimon from the Digital World to the human world alongside Yuki, Inori and the treasure, with Aegiomon jumping in to protect his partner.

Junomon's actions results in Shinjuku being attacked by Rogue Digimon, and the party moves towards the treasure trying to stop it from falling into the Titan's hands. They were too late as Titamon reached the treasure first. After being defeated in its [[Titamon + Skull Baluchimon]] form, it transforms into [[Berserk Titamon]] with its power, granting it the ability to use {{AT|Senjin Kishinzan|Thousand-Dust Fierce God Slash}} without charging. The treasure's power was nullified by [[Monica Simmons]]' new EMP device and it was defated.

At this moment however, the treasure's power ran amok and it destroyed the Shinjuku Government building in an explosion, killing Titamon alongside it. Aegiomon then came into contact with the treasure, granting him the ability to travel through time, an ability that he used to send himself, Inori and Yuki back to the present day.

[[Category:Digimon Story: Time Stranger]]

Code Key of Lust.wikitext:
{{UC}}
[[File:Code_key_of_lust.png|150px|thumb|right]]
The '''Code Key of Lust''' (愛欲のコードキー, ''Aiyoku no Kōdo Kī'') is one of the seven [[Code Key]]s in ''[[Digimon Savers: Another Mission]]''.

=={{hdr|Digimon Savers: Another Mission}}==
After [[Kagura Yuma]] was captured by two [[Pico Devimon]] and sent to the [[Dark Area]] by her own brother, [[Kagura Tsukasa]], her lusting for her parents' love along with the Code Key of Lust transformed her into [[Lilithmon]]. After Lilithmon was defeated by [[Daimon Masaru|Masaru]], [[Thoma H. Norstein|Thoma]], [[Yoshino]], [[Ikuto]] and their Partner Digimon, Yuma was released. [[Katsura Kosaburou|Kosaburou]] and his Partner, [[Piyomon (Another Mission)|Piyomon]] tried to steal the Code Key of Lust, but Thoma and [[Gaomon (Savers)|Gaomon]] were prepared and intercepted Piyomon when she was about to take the Code Key. After Kosaburou and Piyomon escape, Kagura arrives to where the battles took place saying that he will take the Code Key of Lust as "evidence". After Kagura's betrayal is revealed, he uses the Code Key of Lust, along the Code Keys of [[Code Key of Sloth|Sloth]], [[Code Key of Greed|Greed]], [[Code Key of Gluttony|Gluttony]], [[Code Key of Envy|Envy]] and [[Code Key of Wrath|Wrath]] to unlock the Seal to the Dark Area and free [[Lucemon: Falldown Mode|Lucemon]].

The '''Crest of Friendship''' (友情の紋章, ''Yūjou no Monshou'') is one of the nine [[Crest|Crests]]. It is engraved with a pattern resembling the {{w|taijitu}} enclosed in an eye.
=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure}}===
The Crest of Friendship was located in [[DA18]], found by [[Ishida Yamato]] and [[Izumi Koushiro]] in a well near [[Piccolomon]]'s home along with the [[Crest of Knowledge]]. It was first activated in [[DA23]] during the fight with [[Vagimon]] and [[Digitamamon]] in response to their torture of [[Kido Jou]], causing [[Gabumon (Adventure)|Garurumon]] to super-evolve into [[Were Garurumon]] for the first time.  During the conflict with the [[Dark Masters]], being the bearer of the Crest of Friendship cased Yamato some distress stemming from his perceived inability to truly get along with others and understand friendship, causing him to leave the Chosen and travel alone for a time.

Its physical form was later destroyed by [[Apocalymon]], and in May 2000 Yamato surrendered its power to free one of the [[Four Holy Beasts]] from the bonds placed upon them by the [[Dark Masters]].

==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure:}}===
The Crest of Friendship belongs to [[Ishida_Yamato_(Adventure:)|Ishida Yamato]]. Like the other Crests in Adventure:, it does not exist in a physical form, and instead appears in Yamato's [[Digivice:]]'s screen when Yamato shows the trait the Crest embodies. It first appeared in War Game.

A giant Crest of Friendship, in the same shape as the ones in ''Digimon Adventure'', appeared in the Tree of Knowledge alongside the other seven Crests in "''[[Digimon Adventure: - Episode 51|The Mystery Hidden Within the Crests]]''". [[Wisemon]] explained that the Crests predated Digimon themselves and were a complete mystery, which led the [[Chosen Children]] to separate to answer said mystery.

In "''[[Digimon Adventure: - Episode 56|The Gold Wolf of the Crescent Moon]]''", after Yamato and [[Gabumon (Adventure:)|Were Garurumon: Sagittarius Mode]] managed to reawaken the gold sword fighter's weapon, which then caused the latter to evolve into [[Cres Garurumon]] to fight [[Zanbamon]], Yamato's Digivice: projected the Crest of Friendship as it turned completely blue.

==Manga==
==={{hdr|Digimon Universe Appli Monsters (Manga)|Digimon Universe Appli Monsters}}===

==={{hdr|Digimon ReCollection}}===
[[Nozomi Riko]] has half of the [[Crest of Courage]] and half of the Crest of Friendship on the back of her jacket, identical to how they appear on [[Omegamon|Omegamon's]] chest.

==Video Games==
==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}===

==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure (Game)|Digimon Adventure}}===
[[Ishida Yamato]] and [[Izumi Koshiro]] found their [[Crests]], the Crest of Friendship and the [[Crest of Knowledge]], in a well inside [[Piccolomon's Forest]] in Episode 23, "''The Fairy! Piccolomon''", after their [[Tags]] alerted them said Crests were near them.

During the fight against [[Digitamamon]] in Episode 21, "''Oh Friend! Were Garurumon''", Yamato's refusal to give up, born from his belief in his friends, resonated with the Crest of Friendship, allowing [[Gabumon (Adventure)|Garurumon]] to [[Evolution/Video Games#Digimon Adventure|super evolve]] into [[Were Garurumon]] for the first time.

Through Garurumon, the Crest of Friendship healed and reenergized [[Agumon (Adventure)|Agumon]] after his fight with [[Piemon]] in Episode 61, "''The Clown from Hell, Piemon''".

[[Apocalymon (Adventure)|Apocalymon]] destroyed all the physical Crests with their {{AT|Death Claw}} in Episode 62, "''The Final Dark Digimon''". However, after the [[Chosen Children]] regained the will to fight, the Crests reappeared, making them realize the real Crests were in their hearts all along; not only their own, but their combined qualities gathering inside each of their hearts, allowing them to evolve once again.

==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}===
The Chip of Friendship item is imbued with the power of the Crest of Friendship. [[Taiga]] obtains three of them after defeating [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure)|Yagami Taichi]], [[Ishida Yamato]] and [[Omegamon]] in the [[Village of Beginnings|Colosseum]] Heavenly Hall Free Battle: "The Final Test". It greatly increases [[Digimaru|Digimaru's]] Defense and Speed.

{{Item

The '''Crest of Hope''' (希望の紋章, ''Kibou no Monshou'') is one of the nine [[Crest|Crests]]. It is engraved with a pattern resembling a single ray of light beaming down from a small sun.
=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure}}===
The Crest of Hope was located accidentally in [[DA19]] on a cliff wall, whereupon it was collected by [[Takaishi Takeru]]. It was the final Crest to be activated, doing so for the first time in [[DA52]] while Takeru and [[Yagami Hikari]] were fleeing from [[Piemon]]; its activation allowed [[Patamon (Adventure)|Angemon]] to super-evolve to [[Holy Angemon]] for the first time and eventually seal away Piemon with his {{ATO|Heaven's Gate}}.  Its physical form was later destroyed by [[Apocalymon]], and in May 2000 Takeru surrendered its power to free [[Qinglongmon]] of the [[Four Holy Beasts]] from the bonds placed upon them by the [[Dark Masters]].

==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure:}}===
The Crest of Hope belongs to [[Takaishi Takeru_(Adventure:)|Takaishi Takeru]]. Like the other Crests in Adventure:, it does not exist in a physical form, and instead appears in Takeru's Digivice's screen when Takeru shows the trait the Crest embodies.

A giant Crest of Hope, in the same shape as the ones in ''Digimon Adventure'', appeared in the Tree of Knowledge alongside the other seven Crests in "''[[Digimon Adventure: - Episode 51|The Mystery Hidden Within the Crests]]''". [[Wisemon]] explained that the Crests predated Digimon themselves and were a complete mystery, which led the [[Chosen Children]] to separate to answer said mystery.

After Takeru managed to get [[El Doradimon]] to climb back up to [[Cloud Continent]] with the help of [[Gravimon]] and [[Patamon (Adventure:)|Holy Angemon]], who projected a [[Seraphimon]] aura, in "''[[Digimon Adventure: - Episode 61|A Place to Return To]]''", his [[Digivice:]] turned yellow and projected the Crest of Hope.

==Manga==
==={{hdr|Digimon Universe Appli Monsters (Manga)|Digimon Universe Appli Monsters}}===

==Video Games==
==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}===

==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}===

==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure (Game)|Digimon Adventure}}===
The [[Chosen Children]] found the Crest of Hope as a cliff wall in Episode 24, "''Perfect Evolution! Metal Greymon''" as they searched for [[Nanomon]], who sent them an e-mail asking to be rescued. As with the other [[Crests]], it glowed then shrunk in the presence of [[Takaishi Takeru]], taking its true form as his Crest. The Chosen Children finding said Crest was part of Nanomon's deal, as he had promised them to give them information on where the Crests were.

In Episode 61, "''The Clown from Hell, Piemon''", Takeru refused to give up hope despite [[Piemon]] turning everyone else except [[Yagami Hikari]] into dolls and cornering them. The Crest of Hope resonated with this, allowing [[Patamon (Adventure)|Angemon]] to [[Evolution/Video Games#Digimon Adventure|super evolve]] into [[Holy Angemon]] for the first time, the last of the eight to obtain his [[Perfect]] evolution.

==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}===
The Chip of Hope item is imbued with the power of the Crest of Hope. [[Taiga]] obtains two of them after defeating [[Takaishi Takeru]] and [[Patamon (Adventure)|Holy Angemon]] in the [[Village of Beginnings|Colosseum]] 40th floor Free Battle: "A Boy's Hope". It greatly increases [[Digimaru|Digimaru's]] Speed and Brains.

The '''Crest of Kindness''' (優しさの紋章, ''Yasashisa no Monshou'') is one of the nine [[Crest|Crests]]. It is engraved with the pattern of a stylized ornate rose.
=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}===
The Crest of Kindness came into existence separately from the other eight Crests, and ended up in the possession of [[Ichijouji Ken]], its destined bearer.  As the [[Digimon Kaiser]], Ken used it to fuel his base, and it became engulfed in darkness; the [[Four Holy Beasts]] somehow from afar used it to create the [[Digimental of Miracles]].   After the Digimental was retrieved, used and depleted by [[Motomiya Daisuke]] to armor-evolve [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] into [[Magnamon]] to defeat [[Chimairamon]], it reverted to the form of the Crest of Kindness and was reclaimed by Ken, now free from his Digimon Kaiser persona and on the path to atoning for his actions.  Ken later used it to find the [[Digitama]] of the deceased [[Wormmon (Adventure)|Wormmon]] in the [[Village of Beginnings]], and some time thereafter returned it to the ruins of the base to prevent a catastrophic meltdown.

Being that Ken never owned a [[Tag]], he was never able to use it for evolution.

The '''Crest of Knowledge''' (知識の紋章, ''Chishiki no Monshou'') is one of the nine [[Crest|Crests]]. It is engraved with a pattern constituting a pair of concentric circles joined by an arch, vaguely reminiscent of binoculars.
=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure}}===
The Crest of Knowledge was located in [[DA18]], found by [[Izumi Koushiro]] and [[Ishida Yamato]] in a well near [[Piccolomon]]'s home, along with the [[Crest of Friendship]]. It was first activated in [[DA26]] while Koushiro and the depleted [[Tentomon (Adventure)|Bubbmon]] attempted to escape from the clutches of [[Vademon]]; it enabled Bubbmon to quickly evolve back into [[Kabuterimon]] before enabling him to super-evolve to [[Atlur Kabuterimon (Red)|Atlur Kabuterimon]] for the first time.  Its physical form was later destroyed by [[Apocalymon]], and in May 2000 Koushiro surrendered its power to free one of the [[Four Holy Beasts]] from the bonds placed upon them by the [[Dark Masters]].

==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure:}}===
The Crest of Knowledge belongs to [[Izumi Koshiro (Adventure:)|Izumi Koshiro]]. Like the other Crests in Adventure:, it does not exist in a physical form, and instead appears in Koshiro's Digivice's screen when Koshiro shows the trait the Crest embodies. It also appears in the other Digivices if they're being used to call Koushiro's computer.

A giant Crest of Knowledge, in the same shape as the ones in ''Digimon Adventure'', appeared in the Tree of Knowledge alongside the other seven Crests in "''[[Digimon Adventure: - Episode 51|The Mystery Hidden Within the Crests]]''". [[Wisemon]] explained that the Crests predated Digimon themselves and were a complete mystery, which led the [[Chosen Children]] to separate to answer said mystery.

In "[[Digimon Adventure: - Episode 59|''Bolt, Herakle Kabuterimon'']]", after Koshiro realized his friendship with [[Tentomon (Adventure:)|Tentomon]] was knowledge, his [[Digivice:]] shone fully in purple and projected the Crest of Knowledge. This allowed [[Atlur Kabuterimon]] to evolve into [[Herakle Kabuterimon]], giving him the strength to easily defeat [[Nanomon]] and [[Gran Kuwagamon]].

==Manga==
==={{hdr|Digimon Universe Appli Monsters (Manga)|Digimon Universe Appli Monsters}}===
A [[Clockmon]]-shaped clock appears alongside many other clocks and the Crest of Knowledge as part of [[Timemon (Appli Monsters)|Timemon's]] {{ATO|Time Leap}} in "''[[Digimon Universe Appli Monsters - Chapter 06|Minerva and Leviathan!!]]''".
{|
|[[File:Digimon Universe - Crest of Knowledge.png|thumb|right|The Crest of Knowledge]]
|}

The Crest of Knowledge appears carved on the sides of [[Sage Tower]]'s Battle Arena in battles with animations on.

It greatly increases [[Digimaru|Digimaru's]] MP and Brains.
