Dark Area.wikitext:
{{FArticle|83}}
[[File:Dark_area.jpg|thumb|right|260px]]
The '''Dark Area''' (ダークエリア, ''Dāku Eria''), also known as '''Hell'''{{ref|[[Beelzebumon (X-Antibody)|''Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode'': Beelzebumon (X-Antibody)]]}} (地獄, ''Jigoku'') and the '''Underworld'''{{refd|Beelzebumon (X-Antibody)|beelzebumon_x}} (冥界, ''Meikai''), is a jet-black, abyssal "graveyard of deleted data"{{refd|Demon}} that is governed by [[Plutomon]]{{ref|[[Plutomon|''Digimon Collectors'': Plutomon]]}} and located within a spatial distortion of the [[Digital World]]{{refd|Bakemon}}, below the [[Dark Web]], the deepest part of the [[Net Ocean]], the place where [[Leviamon]] sleeps{{refd|Leviamon}}, with each Digital World having its own Dark Area.<ref name=dp>''[[Digimon Profile/Report 035|The "Dark Area" Present in the Digital World]]''</ref> How it came to be within the Digital World is a mystery whose secrets are known to be held by [[Grand Dracumon]].{{refd|Grand Dracumon}} It is also serves as the place to which Digimon are ultimately transmitted when their life span finishes or they are defeated in battle, and is guarded and supervised by [[Anubimon]], who constantly surveys the arriving data; if their data is good, it will reset them back to a [[Digitama]], but if they are evil, it imprisons them within the eternal darkness.{{refd|Anubimon}} It is connected to the [[Cocytus]].

The crimson flames of the underworld known as "El Evangelio" are sentient flames, capable of changing their form at will, that are used to impose judgment upon and offer salvation to the souls of the sinners that fall into the Dark Area. Under normal circumstances, a Demon Lord type Digimon would be completely eradicated by the flames{{refd|Beelzebumon X-Antibody}}, however, the Seven Great Demon Lords seem to have the ability to summon and control these flames with varying levels of proficiency.{{ref|[[Digimon_Chronicle_X#Digital_Monster_X_Ver.2_arc_.28chapters_5_to_11.29|Digimon Chornicle X: The Movements of the Seven Great Demon Lords]]}} [[Cerberusmon]], the "Watchdog of Hell", had been entrusted with protecting the gates of Hell.{{refd|Cerberusmon}}{{ref|St-735}} The "Grudgeful Hand" is a mysterious entitie born from the malice data that overflows the Dark Area. It served as the source of evil that forced [[Piemon]] to absorb [[Vamdemon]] and become [[Voltobautamon]]. This digimon has neither Piemon's nor Vamdemon's sense of self, and acts according to the "Grudgeful Hand"'s will.{{refd|Voltobautamon}}

=Areas within the Dark Area=
 	
The Dark Area is one of the many Layers (Dimensions) of the Digital World, but this place is also divided into many sub-layers. The "[[Evil Forest]]" spreads vast across the Dark Area and is inhabited by [[Phascomon]].{{refd|Phascomon}} [[Yatagaramon]] and [[Yatagaramon (2006 Anime Version)]] both have the ability to send their opponents to the Dark Area by summoning "{{AT|Haguro}}" with their attacks.{{refd|Yatagaramon}}{{refd|Yatagaramon (2006 Anime Version)}} Somewhere unknown also lies the [[Seven_Great_Demon_Lords#Gate_of_Deadly_Sins|Gate of Deadly Sins]]. The lowest layer of the Dark Area bears many names, but it is formally known as the [[Cocytus]]. This place is connected to all Dark Areas from all Digital Worlds and is the stronghold of the [[Seven Great Demon Lords]], [[Gulfmon]] and [[Grand Dracumon]].<ref name=dp>''[[Digimon Profile/Report 035|The "Dark Area" Present in the Digital World]]''</ref>

=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}===
[[Image:Dark Area.jpg|thumb|right|The Dark Area depicted in Digimon Frontier.]]
The Dark Area is a dark orb of energy at the center of the Digital World, where [[Lucemon]] was banished after being defeated by the [[Warrior Ten]]. From within it, Lucemon was still able to recreate the [[Royal Knights]] to gather the energy necessary to free him. After becoming [[Lucemon Falldown Mode]], he returns to it briefly to recruit the “corrupted souls”, who were also imprisoned there, to open a gateway to the real world. The Chosen Children arrive and battle Lucemon Falldown Mode before he enters the portal and defeats him. However, when he is scanned, he leaves behind a black [[Digitama]] that assimilates the Dark Area as it evolves into [[Lucemon Satan Mode]]. Lucemon Satan Mode carried the Dark Area in its arms, while inside the Dark Area was [[Lucemon Larva]], who controlled Lucemon Satan Mode's movements. On his second attempt [[Susanoomon]] breaks into the Dark Area and knocks Lucemon Larva out of it causing the Dark Area to fall apart.

==Manga==
==={{hdr|Digimon Next}}===
[[Image:Haguro Digimon Next.png|thumb|right|"Haguro" as seen in [[Digimon Next]].]]
The Dark Area is the [[Digital World]]'s hell, and once one falls in, they can't come back alive. "Haguro" (羽黒 lit. "Feather-black") is one of its districts and can be summoned by [[Peckmon (Next)|Yatagaramon]]. "Haguro" is described as one of the Hell Blocks of the Dark Area, if you touch the darkness covering this area, you will be sucked into the deepest layer of the Dark Area. When [[Kahara Shou]] and Yatagaramon confront [[Tatsuno Tsurugi]], [[Agumon (Next)|Geo Greymon]], [[Kitajima Ami]], and [[Pitchmon (Next)|Pitchmon]] in Light City's underground warehouse, Yatagaramon uses his {{AT|Haguro}} attack, which is immediately detected by [[Andromon]]'s computers. Geo Greymon attempts to attack Yatagaramon with {{AT|Mega Flame}}, but the attack is sucked by the Haguro's darkness. Eventually, Geo Greymon starts being sucked to the Dark Area. Although Geo Greymon tells Tsurugi to stay away, the boy refuses to let Geo Greymon fall into the Dark Area alone and jumps with him, both being completely sucked by the jet-black abyss. Geo Greymon then evolves to [[Rize Greymon]] and emits a light that breaks Haguro, allowing both to come out.

==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|Digimon Xros Wars}}===
The Dark Area is indirectly referenced as the primary source of the residual vital data from which [[Bagramon (Xros Wars)|Bagramon]] created his [[Troopmon]] soldiers and [[Tactimon (Xros Wars)|Tactimon]].

==={{hdr|Digimon Chronicle X}}===
The Dark Area exists on a separate layer to the [[Digital World]], which meant that it was unaffected by the [[Project Ark]] used by [[Yggdrasill]] in its attempt to kill most of the existing Digimon, due to overpopulation. The Dark Area was created by [[Grand Dracumon]], who plans to go to the old Digital World, defeat Digimon, force them to undergo [[Evolution#Death-X-Evolution|Death-X Evolution]], and then send them to the Dark Area. [[Anubimon]] judges the Digimon that are transferred to the Dark Area, deciding whether they are good or evil. If they are good, Anubismon allows them to be reborn as [[Digitama|Digitamas]], but if they're evil, Anubimon seals them in the Dark Area. Eventually, the sealed Digimon attempt to break out of the Dark Area, leading Anubimon to ask the [[Royal Knight]] [[Examon]] for help. Despite managing to kill a large amount of the evil Digimon, Examon is defeated by [[Lilithmon (X-Antibody)]], and the remaining evil Digimon escape the Dark Area.

==Novel==
==={{hdr|Digimon Chronicle X (Web Novel)|Digimon Chronicle X}}===

==Video Games==
==={{hdr|Digimon Savers: Another Mission}}===
The Dark Area is a mysterious area that hasn't been analyzed yet, and it's possibly behind the strange events that occur between the [[Digital World]] and [[Real World]]. [[Kagura Tsukasa]] first warns the [[DATS]] members while they are in Rage Caverns that their Digimon may not evolve into the same Digimon due to the activity in the Dark Area, hence being responsible for the multiple evolution routes the Digimon can take. Somewhere else, [[Demon]] is standing next to what appears to be a seal on the door to the Dark Area. Subsequently, Kagura explains that all the malice, sadness and twisted feelings in the world have gathered in the Dark Area, thus theorizing the negative feelings of the children who were captured may be connected with the Dark Area and must have created the [[Seven Great Demon Lords|Demon Lord Digimon]] with the help of the [[Code Key|Code Keys]]. It is later on unveiled that Kagura's actual agenda was collecting 6 of the Code Keys in order to unlock the last seal in the Dark Area, where the [[Lucemon: Falldown Mode|Lord of Darkness]] was sealed. After [[Lucemon: Falldown Mode|Lucemon]] is defeated, he absorbs Kagura, revealing Kagura himself as the final [[Code Key of Pride]], considering that the first six were necessary to free him from his physical imprisonment in the Dark Area itself and the Code Key of Pride was necessary for Lucemon to leave the boundaries of the Dark Area as well as send his power out beyond.

==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}===
The Dark Area is a world of darkness that can be accessed through a trolley in the Train Garage. In the Dark Area, time doesn't pass and the {{w|autopilot}} can't be used. [[Leviamon]] lives in the Dark Area, and a [[Chaosmon]] is in the train station. At some point, it is revealed that Re:Digitize Decode's [[Digital World]] had their unique distortions that lead to the Dark Area and their own unique guardians to this universe.

==={{hdr|Digimon Fortune}}===
[[Noah]] is an orbital elevator that connects the Digital World to the Dark Area.

==={{hdr|Digimon World -next 0rder- International Edition}}===
The [[Seven Great Demon Lords]] lived in the Dark Area and after agreeing a pack of non-aggression towards each other grew bored and each created a [[Seven Great Demon Lords#Tower of the Seven Deadly Sins|Tower]]. However, the Grudgeful Hand absorbed the Towers and destroyed the Dark Area, causing the Demons to flee to the Dimensional Sea, which then lead them to the -next 0rder- [[Digital World]].

==={{hdr|Digimon ReArise}}===
[[File:Dark Area ReArise.png|thumb|right|The Dark Area from ''Digimon ReArise''.]]
In the past, when a large amount of Digital Points opened up in the [[Digital World]], bringing a large amount of [[Spiral]], the [[Deva]] and [[Four Holy Beasts]] used the power of the Four Holy Beasts [[DigiCore]]s to open a distortion to attract all the Spiral into the same place so that they could deal with them all at the same time. Once all the Spiral arrived in the same place, they used the distortion to send them all to the Dark Area, which was enough to get rid of all the Spiral for a long period of time. When Spirals once again flood the Digital World, [[Caturamon]] tries this plan again, though [[Sinduramon]] was against the idea as creating such distortions causes harm to the Digital World as the Holy Beasts were no longer around to mitigate the effect the distortion would have on the Worlds. Caturamon enlists the help of [[Ofanimon]] of the [[Three Archangels]], but they are unable to execute the plan due to Ofanimon not having enough power to mitigate the effects of the distortion.

==={{hdr|Digimon New Century}}===

[[Lord Knightmon]] and [[Dynasmon]] are told by [[Omegamon]] to go to the Dark Area on a mission given by [[Yggdrasill]], in order to search for the "{{NN|Immortal King}}" of the Dark Area, someone who is no less dangerous than the [[Seven Great Demon Lords]], but they don't work well together. Each one of them attempted a different way to reach the Dark Area and search for it. Lord Knightmon looked for a [[Cerberumon]] to use {{AT|Hell's Gate}}, but Dynasmon looked for a [[Yatagaramon]] and failed the "Threefold Trials" to be sent there. In the Dark Area, Lord Knightmon finds [[Anubimon]] that says it can't judge if Lord Knightmon's soul is good or bad, it's too much data, maybe an existence comparable to the Demon Lords who reside in the [[Cocytus]]. 
[[File:Dark Area New Century.png|thumb|right|The Dark Area from ''Digimon New Century''.]]
The Cocytus is the world of the Demon Lords, the deepest layer of the Dark Area that not even Anubimon can reach. Only the Demon Lords and their subordinates can come and go freely. So, they need to go to the "[[Evil Forest]]" to look for [[Nightmare Soldiers]]. The [[Royal Knights]] cause massive destruction in the Dark Area and fight an [[Astamon]] to enter the Cocytus, they put fear in them with the idea that if you are killed in the Dark Area, there's no further afterlife, there's no concept of death there after all. Now in the Cocytus they look for the Demon Lords. But choosing the one that might help them is hard. 

[[Leviamon]]'s domain is the [[Net Ocean]] and they aren't able to pass through it. They don't want to deal with either [[Lilithmon]] or [[Barbamon]], so they look for [[Demon]] and [[Beelzebumon]]. Lord Knightmon looks for Beelzebumon, he says that looking for someone immortal doesn't mean they are really powerful. In the Dark Area, there are Digimon that predate even his existence as Demon Lord, Digimon who live forever eating [[Digicore|Digicores]] and have lost their sanity. Their bodies have decayed to the point of being unrecognizable, but although they live forever and eat Digicore, they have lost their potential to evolve and no longer grow. There's a Virus in the Digital World and Lord Knightmon wonders if such power can save them. Demon doesn't explain things, but Dynasmon can deduce certain key aspects from his encounter. The Cocytus predated the Dark Area being formed and is the source of darkness that created many Digimon, including the source that triggered the "Fall of Heaven" that led to the birth of the Demon Lords. 

Digimon that the Seven Great Demon Lords kill become data they absorb, with no reincarnation for them, but there are Digimon that are immune to this. The ones who truly are immortal can bypass this and the "{{NN|Immortal King}}" might be one of them, and that is why it's difficult for them to deal with it. When they finally find the "{{NN|Immortal King}}", it's [[Grand Dracumon]], but that is a name he hasn't heard for a long time. Most Digimon that know about that predated the very formation of the Dark Area. Lord Knightmon says that Yggdrasill wants to incorporate the Dark Area under its rule and create an army of undead Digimon, but Grand Dracumon would have the assistance of the Royal Knights to defeat the Demon Lords and finally unify the Dark Area ending the division. However, Grand Dracumon says that was Yggdrasill who created the secrets of Immortality, but he wasn't sure. But since Yggdrasill sent the Royal Knights because of the isolation of the Dark Area, he was now sure of that theory. 

As part of his study to uncover the secrets of immortality, Grand Dracumon created an army of Digimon using the data leftover from Yggdrasill, those are the [[Vamdemon-species]] of Digimon,  but they are still flawed and aren't the true manifestation of that power. Overwhelmed by the army of Vamdemons, Lord Knightmon uses the last safeguard given by Yggdrasill, the [[X-Antibody]], and completely destroys the army of undead Digimon and even asks if there's more. Grand Dracumon is surprised by this, the "X-Antibody" far surpasses the power of the "[[Death-X]]" he was researching and is now what he truly wants, but now that Lord Knightmon has [[X-Evolution|X-Evolved]], they aren't on the same level of power anymore. Together with [[Dynasmon (X-Antibody)]], the two defeat Grand Dracumon, they think his title as the "{{NN|Immortal King}}" was false. 

Dynasmon researched in the castle and found residual data from some not-normal Digimon, they were the source of the "immortality" that Grand Dracumon wanted so much. Death-X was originally something created by Yggdrasill but was abandoned by it due to its failures. Grand Dracumon found the leftovers and studied them in an attempt to uncover the secrets of immortality, but failed in his attempts to really unlock the power of Death-X. They are still unsure about everything since they still can't understand the intentions of [[God]]. They want to return and give the damaged data and report on the mission, but they still need to find a way out of the Dark Area. And so ends the search for the "{{NN|Immortal King}}".

==={{hdr|Digimon Battle Terminal 02}}===
The “Unknown Island” once appeared in the surface layer of the Digital World. This place seems to be a physical manifestation of the Dark Area which surfaced in the middle of the Digital World. Within the island there are two main areas: The [[Trash Hole]], a hole so deep that goes to the center of the world, in which [[Huanglongmon]] can be found, and the “[[Demon's Valley]]”, some kind of giant crack in the ground from which [[Ogudomon]] surfaced during the [[L&R Wars]].

==Trivia==
[[File:Hells field.jpg|thumb|right|Hell's Field in ''Digimon Xros Wars''.]]
* In ''[[Digimon Xros Wars]]'', there's an area called “[[Hell's Field]]”, a pocket dimension underneath [[Bright Land]], in which evil Digimon are actually empowered, while good Digimon are weakened. Although no official connection has been established between the two, it presents many similarities that may suggest it could be directly based on the traditional depiction of the Dark Area.
* There's a dark forest called “[[Evil Forest]]” spread by the Dark Area.{{refd|Phascomon}}
* [[Astamon]] is denominated a prince of the Dark Area.{{refd|Astamon}}
* Some Digimon who committed evil deeds are sent to the Dark Area while still alive, and may transform into [[:Category:Fallen Angel Type|Fallen Angels]] or [[:Category:Demon Lord Type|Demon Lord]] Digimon.
* The [[Seven Great Demon Lords]] inhabit the Dark Area{{ref|''[[Digimon Chronicle X]]''}}, and with the exception of [[Beelzebumon]], [[Lilithmon]] and [[Leviamon]] (who only slept nearby), all members of the group were once sealed there.
* The "{{EQ|w=Gehenna|Gehenna}}" that [[Lucemon: Satan Mode]] carries act like a miniature of the Dark Area.
* The Dark Area is responsible for the multiple evolution routes the Digimon can take in the continuity of ''[[Digimon Savers: Another Mission]]''.
* In the ''[[Digimon_Xros_Loader_Toy#Quest_Mode_Zones|Digimon Xros Loader]]'', the Twelfth Zone of the Quest Mode takes inspiration from the core concept of the Dark Area.

Dark Tower.wikitext:
[[Image:Dark Tower ZT.JPG|thumb|right|A Dark Tower in ''Digimon Adventure 02''.]]
'''Dark Towers''' (ダークタワー ''Dāku Tawā'', ''Dub: Control Spire'') are large black obelisk structures that were constructed throughout the [[Digital World]] by the [[Ichijouji Ken|Digimon Kaiser]] to block evolution and power [[Evil Ring]]s. 

=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}===
The Digimon Kaiser used hundreds of Dark Towers to both control many digimon with [[Evil Ring]]s, block evolution and to represent claimed territory. Once destroyed, their effects are negated.

[[Archnemon (Adventure)|Archnemon]] has the ability to convert Dark Towers into artificial digimon. The more towers that go into its creation, the more powerful it is. One for a Adult, ten for an Perfect, and one hundred for a Ultimate, [[Black War Greymon (Adventure)|Black War Greymon]] was created from one hundred towers.

DigiMemory (Next).wikitext:
{{IT|DigiMemory (''Next'')}}
The eight {{nihongo|'''DigiMemories'''|「デジメモリ」|"Dejimemori"}}, also written as {{nihongo|'''Sekai no Kioku'''|「世界の記憶」||lit. "The world's memories"}}, are powerful items that appear primarily in [[Digimon Next]], but are intertwined with the frequently appearing concepts of {{nihongo|"Fields"|「フィールド」|"Fīrudo"}} and "Species".

=List of DigiMemories=
{{DigimemoryListNext}}

=Appearances=
==Manga==
==={{hdr|Digimon Next}}===
The DigiMemories are a set of eight pendants, each representing a kind of Digimon. [[Barbamon (Next)|Barbamon]] sought to gather all eight DigiMemories, to complete [[N.E.O (Next)|N.E.O]], his supreme Digimon. He then planned to use NEO to control the [[Digital World]] after recreating it in his image.

The DigiMemories were each possessed by a Digimon who embodied that DigiMemory. While contained within the Digimon, the DigiMemory gave them more power than was usual for a Digimon of their level. This extra power was kept as they evolved.

*The '''Dragon DigiMemory''' was possessed by [[Tatsuno Tsurugi]]'s [[Agumon (Next)|Agumon]], but was taken from him in [[Victory Greymon]] form by N.E.O. 

*The '''Beast DigiMemory''' was possessed by [[Inui Yuu]]'s [[Gaomon (Next)|Gaomon]]. It was taken by N.E.O after defeating Gaomon in [[Z'd Garurumon]] form. 

*The '''Bird DigiMemory''' was possessed by [[Kahara Shou]]'s [[Peckmon (Next)|Peckmon]]. It was taken by N.E.O when it was created. 

*The '''Water DigiMemory''' was possessed by [[Kitajima Ami]]'s [[Pitchmon (Next)|Pitchmon]]. It was given to N.E.O by Barbamon, along with Pitchmon.

*The remaining four Digi-Memories all belonged to partnerless Digimon, strangely creating a situation where half of the DigiMemories are possessed by [[Art.]] (Artificial) Digimon and the other four are possessed by 'true' Digimon.

*The '''Holy DigiMemory''' was possessed by [[Piccolomon]], but was taken from him by [[Sealsdramon]].

*The '''Insect/Plant DigiMemory''' was possessed by [[Atlur Kabuterimon (Red)|Atlur Kabuterimon]], but was taken from him by Shou's [[Yatagaramon (2006 Anime Version)|Yatagaramon]]. 

*The '''Machine/Mutant DigiMemory''' was possessed by [[Andromon]], and it is unknown how he lost it. It is assumed that [[Chaosdramon]] took it after destroying him.

*Finally, the '''Dark DigiMemory''' belonged to Barbamon. After he was destroyed by Victory Greymon, he passed the DigiMemory and his data to N.E.O.

N.E.O was created as a result of combining all eight DigiMemories. It proved almost unstoppable, even when the spirits of the destroyed DigiMemory holders (minus Barbamon) fought against it. However, after Tsurugi's Victory Greymon impales N.E.O, empowered by the bonds between humans and Digimon, N.E.O realizes that feelings are important and that everything wants to live, so it releases the DigiMemories, which rebuild the Digital World.

The '''Digimental''' (デジメンタル ''Dejimentaru'') is a [[Digimental]] that appears in ''[[Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01]]''.

=Overview=
==Design==
The Digimental is a rounded rectangular pendant, mounted on a chain, whose body fits in the palm of one's hand.<ref name="V-39">[[V-39|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': ''"Neo VS Alias III"'']]</ref> A large crystal is embedded in its center.

==Effects==
Legend has it that the Digimental's crystal harbors a fearsome power which can be used to evolve a Digimon into a [[Ultimate#Super Ultimate|Super Ultimate]] form.<ref name="V-31" /> The Digimental is capable of bringing out a Digimon's full potential;<ref name="V-39" /> when a Digimon uses it, it gains an exponential increase in power without experiencing any impacts on its cognitive abilities.<ref name="V-31">[[V-31|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': ''"The Legendary Power - Digimental"'']]</ref>

The Digimental requires a massive amount of energy in order to be used: to be precise, three times the battle energy of a single Digimon and its [[Tamer]]. It can be charged with energy either by the occurrence of three such Tamer battles, regardless of whether or not the Digimental itself is physically present for them;<ref name="V-39" /> or by being made to shine by the hearts of Tamers.<ref name="V-58">[[V-58|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Thank You!!"]]</ref> When it is charging with battle energy, its crystal reacts by glowing with a strange light;<ref name="V-26">[[V-26|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Masks Square Offense and Defense"]]</ref> afterward, something can be seen stirring inside it.<ref name="V-27">[[V-27|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Neo Attacks!!!"]]</ref> Alternatively, the Digimental 

When used, the Digimental physically fuses into the resulting Digimon's body.<ref name="V-53" /> Once the Digimon devolves from its Super Ultimate state, it is released from the Digimon's body, its energy having been entirely depleted.<ref name="V-55" />

Only a pure-hearted individual is able to activate the Digimental and use its power. This includes simply having a sincere, pure motivation, regardless of how abhorrent one's goals and actions in pursuit of that motivation may be.<ref name="V-55">[[V-55|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Truth"]]</ref>

=Appearances=
==Manga==
==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}===
[[Lord Holy Angemon]] knew of the legend of the Digimental, but he originally believed it to be no more than a legend and to not actually exist.<ref name="V-31" />

The Digimental is the centerpiece of [[Saiba Neo]]'s plan to raise [[Arkadimon (V-Tamer)|Arkadimon]]; as he threatens Lord Holy Angemon during his attack on [[Holy Angel Castle]], he intends to use it to not only evolve Arkadimon into its [[Ultimate#Super Ultimate|Super Ultimate]] form, but also to let him retain control over Arkadimon after its evolution;<ref name="V-31" /> however, Neo, with his ability to use the Digimental to raise a Super Ultimate Digimon, is unwittingly a pawn in [[Demon (V-Tamer)|Demon]]'s master plan to hijack the power of a Super Ultimate Digimon as his own.<ref name="V-55" /> Even before entering the [[Digital World]], Neo had learned of the Digimental and began collecting data on it. He transmitted that data from his computer in the real world to the Digital World.<ref name="V-39" />

Due to Neo's transmission of its data, the Digimental manifests in the Digital World along with his sister, [[Saiba Rei]];<ref name="V-25" /> although he had always intended to eventually bring her to the Digital World as well, Neo had not intended to send the Digimental with Rei, and speculates that this happened because she may have been looking at his computer at the time.<ref name="V-39" /> When she wakes up, she finds the Digimental next to her and, not knowing what it is, takes to wearing it as a pendant.<ref name="V-25">[[V-25|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Alias 1 - Sigma"]]</ref>

When it first arrives in the Digital World, the Digimental is depleted of energy. Neo calls the [[Alias III]] to the Digital World and sends them to fight [[Yagami Taichi (V-Tamer)|Yagami Taichi]] and [[Zeromaru]]; although his instructions to them are to distract Taichi and buy Neo time for his attack on Holy Angel Castle,<ref name="V-28">[[V-28|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Alias 2 - Mari"]]</ref> his unspoken ulterior motive is purely to generate enough battle energy from the three of them to recharge the Digimental.<ref name="V-39" /> During Taichi and Zeromaru's battle with [[Sigma]] and [[Piemon]], Rei notices the Digimental reacting as the two exchange commands to their Digimon,<ref name="V-26" /> and she comments to Taichi on this, and the continued stirring from within the gemstone, after the battle.<ref name="V-27" />

Having pinpointed its location by analyzing all of the data in the Digital World, Neo sends [[Fujimoto Hideto]] to retrieve it while he attacks Holy Angel Castle.<ref name="V-31" /> Hideto tracks it and finds it in Rei's possession, and despite Taichi and Zeromaru's attempt to intervene, takes both it and Rei back to [[Demon Castle]].<ref name="V-32">[[V-32|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': ''"Alias 3 - Hideto"'']]</ref> Neo takes possession of the Digimental from Rei,<ref name="V-33">[[V-33|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "SERIOUS MIND"]]</ref> and witnesses it accrue the third and final required charge of battle energy from Hideo's battle with Taichi en route to Demon Castle, emboldening him to put the next phase of his plan into action and depose Demon.<ref name="V-38">[[V-33|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Earthquake! Demon Castle!!"]]</ref> When the Alias III attempt to convince him to put a stop to the plan, he declares that he has no further need of their services with the Digimental fully charged, and reveals to them both how he manifested it in the Digital World and his ulterior motive in using the Alias III to recharge it.<ref name="V-39" />

At the start of the final battle at Holy Angel Castle, Neo does not see any need to use the Digimental against Taichi and Zeromaru, but once they subdue [[Arkadimon Ultimate]] using their knowledge of its {{ATO|Dot Matrix}}, he changes his mind and activates it, using its charged power to evolve Arkadimon into [[Arkadimon Super Ultimate|its Super Ultimate form]];<ref name="V-53">[[V-53|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "The Super Ultimate Appears"]]</ref> as Jijimon later observes, it is his sincere wish to make a better future for Rei that, despite the cruelty that he inflicts in the pursuit of that wish, enables him to use it.<ref name="V-55" />

When Rei and Hideto force Neo to realize the truth of what he has done, all against Rei's wishes, Neo has a mental breakdown that causes Arkadimon to revert to its Ultimate state and release the depleted Digimental. Shortly after, Demon takes control of Arkadimon and reveals his exploitation of Neo to fulfill his own plan and take [[Demon Super Ultimate|a Super Ultimate Form]],<ref name="V-55" /> which amplifies his own {{EQO|Dark Virus}} power to manipulate emotions to the point that it shuts down Zeromaru/[[Ulforce V-dramon]]'s emotion-based [[Ulforce|{{EQO|Ulforce}}]].<ref name="V-56">[[V-56|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Dark Virus"]]</ref>

As Demon overwhelms Zeromaru, Taichi runs for the Digimental, refusing to give up despite Demon's advantage. The terrain it is on had been destablized by Arkadimon and Demon's attacks, and it collapses under Taichi as he grabs the Digimental, only for Neo to save him from falling into the crevasse.<ref name="V-57" />

Taichi asks the assembled [[Tamer]]s to lend Zeromaru their strength, and their hearts cause the Digimental to shine again,<ref name="V-58" /> enabling Taichi to activate it and evolve Zeromaru into [[Ulforce V-dramon Future Mode]].<ref name="V-57">[[V-57|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Future"]]</ref> Once Zeromaru devolves from its Future Mode state after destroying Demon, the Digimental is released from his body and returns to Taichi.<ref name="V-58" />

Digimental of Courage.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Digimental of Courage |image=Digimental courage.png |imagesize=320 |kan={{j2|勇気のデジメンタル|Yūki no Dejimentaru}} |dub=Digi-Egg of Courage }} The '''Digimental of Courage''' (勇気のデジメンタル ''Yūki no Dejimentaru''; Dub: ''Digi-Egg of Courage''), also known as the '''Digimental of Flame''' (炎のデジメンタル ''Honō no Dejimentaru''){{rfc|St|135}}, is a [[Digimental]] that is used to induce [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]. =Overview= ==Design== Like other [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] [[Digimental]]s, the Digimental of Courage is a bulky, loosely egg-shaped object. Its shell has a similar shape and a near-identical flame pattern to the armor worn by two Digimon who are created by using it, [[Fladramon]] and [[Shadramon]]; it is also tipped with a horn identical to Fladramon's. Its corresponding [[Crest]], the [[Crest of Courage]], is inscribed on its surface. ==Effects== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental#Properties]]''''' The Digimental of Courage contains the elemental attribute of Flame {{ETYK|{{j2|炎|Honō}}}}, and it is this elemental power from which Digimon who use this Digimental to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] draw their power.''デジモン大図鑑―デジタルワールド研究白書'' [''Digimon Visual Dictionary: Digital World Research White Paper'']. {{w|Futabasha}}. pp. 215-217. November 15, 2002. ISBN 978-4575163537. Armor Digimon who have used the Digimental of Courage are granted fire-based abilities, and their skill at hand-to-hand combat is heightened''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report012/ Digi-Basics: "Armors" and "Digimentals"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 012|Translation on Wikimon]]) like blazing flames, allowing them to attack their foes with intense power.{{refd|Fladramon}} Like other Digimentals, the Digimental of Courage was created in ancient times using now-lost technology. [[Fla Wizarmon]] |} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}=== [[Image:Digimental of Courage.JPG|right|thumb|The Digimental of Courage in its resting place.]] The Digimental of Courage is one of the first five Digimentals that the [[Four Holy Beasts]] chose to provide to a new group of [[Chosen Child#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Chosen Children]] in order to stop the [[Ichijouji Ken|Digimon Kaiser]]. They had it placed in a cave in the Digital World, sealing away both [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]], asleep, and three [[D-3]]s, to be unsealed and awoken once the intended Chosen Child arrived.{{rfe|ZT|37|Gigantic Ultimate, Qinglongmon}} [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure)|Yagami Taichi]], [[Agumon (Adventure)|Agumon]], [[Patamon (Adventure)|Patamon]] and [[Tailmon (Adventure)|Tailmon]] discover the cave and the Digimental of Courage while fleeing the Digimon Kaiser's slaves. Having noticed the [[Crest of Courage]] inscribed on it, he attempts to take it, but is not only totally unable to make it budge, but his touch inadvertently releases the D-3s and sends them to the Real World. Later, once [[Yagami Hikari]], [[Takaishi Takeru]], and [[Motomiya Daisuke]] arrive, Taichi and the Digimon bring them to the Digimental. None of the other Chosen Children are able to budge it from the ground at all, but Daisuke, as its intended owner, successfully takes it and releases V-mon. When an [[Evil Ring]] [[Monochromon]] attacks, following V-mon's instructions, Daisuke activates it to [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] V-mon into [[Fladramon]], becoming the first to circumvent the Digimon Kaiser's block on natural evolution using Armor Evolution. Once V-mon devolves after freeing the Monochromon, the Digimental of Courage automatically transmits itself from Daisuke's D-3 to his [[D-Terminal]], where it remains stored when not in use.{{rfe|ZT|01|The Inheritor of Courage|b}} ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 1: Digimon Hurricane Landing!!/Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals}}=== [[Motomiya Daisuke]] uses the Digimental of Courage to [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] into [[Fladramon]] in both [[Lopmon (Adventure)|Chocomon]]'s attack on the rural road, and in the final encounter at Summer Memory. ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown}}=== In the drama CD's ''omake'', [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure)|Yagami Taichi]] attempts to use the Digimental of Courage, but it fails and has no effect other than hurting [[Agumon (Adventure)|Agumon]]'s tooth.[[Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown|''Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown'': "Surging Armor Evolution"'']] ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== [[Motomiya Daisuke]] uses the Digimental of Courage as his opening gambit in the final fight with [[Parallelmon]]. He [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] into [[Fladramon]] and baits Parallelmon into reaching for him, then V-mon sheds the Digimental's armor to leave Parallemon open to being attacked by [[Zeromaru]].[[V-Tamer Double Tamer|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': Double Tamer!! The Great Super-dimensional Battle!!]] ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Courage is an obtainable item of the Armor category. Equipping it on a Digimon has three possible effects: * If equipped by a [[V-mon]] (including [[V-mon (Adventure)|the temporary partner V-mon]]), it teaches V-mon the {{ATO|Fire Rocket}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique the next time that a battle is won. After this point, as long as the Digimental is equipped, V-mon can use {{ATO|Fire Rocket}} to briefly Armor Evolve into [[Fladramon]], who performs the technique before immediately reverting back into V-mon. * It increases the equipper's resistance to Fire techniques. * Like any other [[Digimental]], having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Courage is the only Digimental that the player can receive in an already complete state: it can be found in a chest in the Sea Cave 2 dungeon on [[Akiyama Ryo]]'s side. Courage Fragments can also be found in the Detached Cave and Machine Base dungeons, both on [[Ichijouji Ken]]'s side, and rebuilt into more Digimentals of Courage at [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop. Alternatively, the player may receive it by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: D-1 Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Courage is an obtainable item of the [[Digimental]] category. Equipping it on a Digimon has two possible effects: * If equipped by a [[V-mon]] (including [[V-mon (Adventure)|the temporary partner V-mon]]), it grants access to the {{ATO|Fire Rocket}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique in V-mon's sixth technique slot. When used, V-mon briefly Armor Evolves into [[Fladramon]], who performs {{ATO|Fire Rocket}} before immediately reverting back into V-mon. * Like any other Digimental, having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Courage can be purchased from [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop at any point in the game, or received by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon World: Digital Card Arena}}=== The Digimental of Courage is an unlockable which is separately acquired for three Partner Cards. Once it has been acquired, the player is given the option to, when playing any of these Partner Cards, use the Digimental to Armor Evolve them free of charge: * [[V-mon]] → [[Fladramon]]
[[Digimental#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Unlock order slot: Digimental #1]] * [[Patamon]] → [[Baromon]]
[[Digimental#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Unlock order slot: Digimental #2]] * [[Wormmon]] → [[Shadramon]]
[[Digimental#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Unlock order slot: Digimental #1]] ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story}}=== The Digimental of Courage is a key item. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following evolutions: * [[XV-mon]] → [[Fladramon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 30 or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so Fladramon can be evolved into any number of times. It can be redeemed from Mr. Gure as a reward for accruing 6400 Tamer Points. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Sunburst}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight}}=== The Digimental of Courage is a key item. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following Armor Evolutions in in the Special Evolution menu of the DigiLab: * [[V-mon]] → [[Fladramon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 22 or higher; Dragon EXP is 2500 or higher * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Allomon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 21 or higher; Attack is 110 or higher * [[Tailmon]] → [[Lynxmon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 29 or higher; Speed is 130 or higher * [[Wormmon]] → [[Shadramon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 30 or higher; Dark EXP is 6000 or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so these Digimon can be evolved into any number of times. It is found in the Chaos Area of Palette Amazon. ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== Appears as a collectable card. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory|Hacker's Memory}}=== The Digimental of Courage is a key item in both games. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following evolutions: * [[V-mon]] → [[Fladramon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 14 or higher; Attack is 40 or higher; Speed is 40 or higher; Camaraderie is 25% or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so Fladramon can be evolved into any number of times. In ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth]]'', the Digimental of Courage is dropped by the Aggressive Hacker's Fladramon after defeating it in Kowloon Level 4 during Chapter 7. In ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory]]'', the Digimental of Courage is dropped by the Passionate Hacker's Fladramon after defeating it in the final round of the Gold Cup, from Chapter 6 onward. ==={{hdr|Digimon ReArise}}=== [[V-mon (ReArise)|V-mon]] uses the Digimental of Courage to Armor Evolve to [[Fladramon]]. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-3 Toy|D-3}}=== The Digimental of Courage is a collectible item that the player uses in battle to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] their Digimon. All six playable Digimon are capable of using it: * [[V-mon]] → [[Fladramon]] * [[Wormmon]] → [[Shadramon]] * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Allomon]] * [[Tailmon]] → [[Lynxmon]] * [[Armadimon]] → [[Boarmon]] * [[Patamon]] → [[Baromon]] To obtain the Digimental of Courage, the player must first collect three fragments of it via the Search function. Its fragments are available in the following Areas:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DThreeEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * [[D-3 Version 1]]: Area 3 * [[D-3 Version 2]]: Areas 1 and 3 * [[D-3 Version 3]]: Areas 1 and 2 Alternatively, the player may receive it via trade with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal}}=== Digimentals of Courage may be created through the fusion of the following combinations of either Digimon from the Reference Book, or other Digimentals:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DTermEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * Digimon: [[Metal Greymon]] + [[Paildramon]] * Digimon: [[War Greymon]] + [[Imperialdramon: Dragon Mode|Imperialdramon]] * Digimentals: [[Digimental of Friendship]] + [[Digimental of Knowledge]] + [[Digimental of Hope]] Digimentals of Courage may be traded with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (International)}}=== The Digi-Egg (Digimental) of Courage is a collectible item that is used in battle to Armor Digivolve ([[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]]) Veemon ([[V-mon]]) into Flamedramon ([[Fladramon]]). Unlike the Japanese D-3 line, none of the other playable Digimon may use it. Using the Digi-Egg of Courage does not incur a D-Power point cost and can be done without limitation, but if used, there is a small chance that it may break after a battle, regardless of its outcome, requiring it to be re-obtained. It is obtained by random chance in "Get Digi-Eggs" map events. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (International)}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Spirit}}=== ==={{hdr|D-3 Ver. 15th}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Digimental_of_courage |s1=HC }} ==={{hdr|Digimon Card Game}}=== The Digimental of Courage appears in the artwork of certain cards that also show [[Fladramon]]. {|class="wikitable" !Card number !Card name |- |[[BT8-012 (DCG)|BT8-012]] (Parallel 2) |[[Fladramon]] |- |[[BT8-110 (DCG)|BT8-110]] |Armor Texture!! |} =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Digimental courage.png{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Profile]] |i2=Digimental courage.jpg{{!}}200px |c2=[[Digimon Collectors]] }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Digimental courage vpet d3.gif |c1=[[D-3 Toy|D-3]] & [[D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal]] |i2=Digimental courage vpet spirit.gif |c2=[[D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (international)]], [[D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (international)]], & [[D-Spirit]] }} =Additional Information= {{R | }} =See Also= * [[Crest of Courage]] * [[Digimental Evolution Chart]] [[Category:Digimentals|C]]

Digimental of Fate.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Digimental of Fate |image=Digimental fate.png |imagesize= |kan={{j2|運命のデジメンタル|Unmei no Dejimentaru}} |dub=Digi-Egg of Destiny{{note|Some English localization materials, such as the ''[[Digimon: The Movie]]'' recut of ''[[Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 1: Digimon Hurricane Landing!!/Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals|Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals]]'', collectively refer to this Digimental and the [[Digimental of Miracles]] as "the Golden Digi-Eggs," giving them a special status separate from the other Digimentals.|n=GOLDEN}} }} The '''Digimental of Fate''' (運命のデジメンタル ''Unmei no Dejimentaru''; Dub: ''Digi-Egg of Destiny''{{note|n=GOLDEN}}) is a [[Digimental]] that is used to induce [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]. =Overview= ==Design== Like other [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] [[Digimental]]s, the Digimental of Fate is a bulky, loosely egg-shaped object. It is shaped like an golden ball topped with a single wing, and bears a passing resembles to one of the Digimon that it creates, [[Rapidmon Armor]]. A symbol is inscribed on its surface; the ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth]]'' games call this symbol the "Crest of Fate," although such a [[Crest]] otherwise generally does not exist.''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth]] ==Effects== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental#Properties]]''''' The elemental attribute of the Digimental of Fate is unknown.''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report012/ Digi-Basics: "Armors" and "Digimentals"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 012|Translation on Wikimon]]) It appears to be capable of granting Digimon who [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] using it exceptional power compared to most other Digimentals: the two [[Armor]] Digimon most frequently associated with it, [[Rapidmon Armor]] and [[Gold V-dramon]], are both capable of channeling up to [[Ultimate]]-class power,{{refd|Rapidmon Armor|rapidmon_gold}}{{refd|Gold V-dramon|goldvdramon}} although Rapidmon is not able to do so consistently unless it [[X-Antibody#X-Evolution|X-Evolves]] into [[Rapidmon (X-Antibody)]] and has the [[X-Antibody]] further draw out the Digimental of Fate's power.{{refd|Rapidmon (X-Antibody)|rapidmon_x}} ==Possible Armor Evolutions== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental Evolution Chart]]''''' {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;font-size:90%;" |- !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution |- | * '''[[V-mon]]'''{{refd|Gold V-dramon|goldvdramon}} !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Goldvdramon.jpg|150px]]

[[Rapidmon Armor]] |- | *[[Clear Agumon]]{{rfc|St|572}} *[[Plotmon]]{{rfc|Bo|461}} !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Maildramon.jpg|150px]]
[[Maildramon]] | *[[Hagurumon]]{{rfc|St|571}} *[[Wormmon]]{{rfc|St|571}} !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Kongoumon.jpg|150px]]

The Golden Digimentals|Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals}}=== During the final confrontation with [[Lopmon (Adventure)|Chocomon's]] [[Cherubimon (Vice)]] form at Summer Memory in the summer of 2002, [[Seraphimon]] and [[Holydramon]] manifest the Digimental of Fate, along with the [[Digimental of Miracles]], and pass the Digimental of Fate on to [[Wallace]]. With [[Motomiya Daisuke]]'s guidance, Wallace activates it and [[Evolution/Anime#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Armor Evolves]] [[Terriermon (Adventure)|Gummymon/Terriermon]] into [[Rapidmon Armor|Rapidmon]], who together with [[Magnamon]] stands up to and, ultimately, euthanizes Cherubimon. {| |[[Image:Digimental of Fate.JPG|thumb|[[Wallace]], de-aged, receives the Digimental of Fate.]] |} ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== The Digimental of Fate appears as a collectable card. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory|Hacker's Memory}}=== The Digimental of Fate is a key item in both games. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following evolutions: * [[Terriermon]] → [[Rapidmon Armor]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 50 or higher; Attack is 100 or higher; Defense is 120 or higher; Intelligence is 120 or higher; Ability is 20 or higher; Camaraderie is 50% or higher * [[Guardromon (Gold)]] → [[Rapidmon Armor]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 50 or higher; Attack is 100 or higher; Defense is 120 or higher; Intelligence is 120 or higher; Ability is 20 or higher; Camaraderie is 50% or higher
''Hacker's Memory'' and compilation rereleases only It is not consumed in the evolution, so Rapidmon Armor can be evolved into any number of times. In ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth]]'', the Digimental of Fate is one of the rewards for completing the post-game case "Great Challenge ①," in which the player must defeat a Rapidmon Armor; in solo releases of ''Cyber Sleuth'', aside from the Digimental, evolving into Rapidmon Armor is also locked until this case is cleared, but this restriction is lifted in compilation rereleases. In ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory]]'', the Digimental of Fate is dropped by Aloof Hacker Kazuyoshi's Rapidmon Armor after defeating it in the EDEN Free Area during the "Beat Aloof Hacker Kazuyoshi!" Blacklisted Hacker case. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (International)}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Spirit}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Digimental_of_fate |s1=HC }} ==={{hdr|Digimon Card Game}}=== The Digimental of Fate appears in the artwork of certain cards that also show [[Rapidmon Armor]]. {|class="wikitable" !Card number !Card name |- |[[BT16-093 (DCG)|BT16-093]] |The Sparkle of Fate!! |} =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Digimental fate.png{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Profile]] |i2=Digimental fate.jpg{{!}}200px |c2=[[Digimon Collectors]] }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Digimental fate vpet spirit.gif |c1=[[D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (international)]] & [[D-Spirit]] }} =Additional Information= {{R}} =See Also= * [[Digimental of Miracles]] * [[Digimental Evolution Chart]] [[Category:Digimentals|F]]

Digimental of Friendship.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Digimental of Friendship |image=Digimental friendship.png |imagesize= |kan={{j2|友情のデジメンタル|Yūjou no Dejimentaru}} |dub=DigiEgg of Friendship }} The '''Digimental of Friendship''' (友情のデジメンタル, ''Yūjou no Dejimentaru''; Dub: ''DigiEgg of Friendship'') is one of the [[Digimental]]s. It resembles a shoe patterned after the armor of [[Lighdramon]], and is emblazoned with the [[Crest of Friendship]]. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}=== [[Image:Digimental of Friendship.JPG|right|thumb|The Digimental of Friendship in its resting place.]] The Digimental of Friendship was discovered in a crater near a cliff by the [[Chosen Children]]. While initially confused as to who it was for given they all had Digimentals, they each took turns attempting to lift it, but [[Motomiya Daisuke]] was reluctant to try. Later, during a fight with the [[Ichijouji Ken|Kaiser]]-possessed [[Agumon (Adventure)|Metal Greymon]], Daisuke got around his fear of hurting Agumon and activated it, armor evolving [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] into [[Lighdramon]] and allowing them to free Metal Greymon. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Digimon Hurricane Touchdown!! Supreme Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals}}=== ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown}}=== When [[Kido Jou]] ran into the Chosen and knocked over all their D-Terminals, Daisuke's D-Terminal ended up with [[Inoue Miyako]]. She later used it to armor evolve [[Hawkmon (Adventure)|Hawkmon]] into [[Rinkmon]]. ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World: Digital Card Arena}}=== The Digimental of Friendship can be obtained for the [[V-mon]] Partner Card to be able to Armor Evolve it in-battle to [[Lighdramon]], being its second Digimental. It can be obtained from [[Wormmon (Adventure)|Wormmon]] in [[Dark City]]'s Battle Cafe after clearing Dark City's first Battle Arena if V-mon was the first Partner Card chosen by [[Player (Digimon World)|the player]] or from Omegamon (the fusion of [[Agumon (Adventure)|War Greymon]] and [[Gabumon (Adventure)|Metal Garurumon]]) in [[Sage Tower]]'s Battle Café after clearing Sage Tower's last Battle Arena if V-mon is the player's third Partner Card. ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story}}=== The Digimental of Friendship is an obtainable item which can be used to evolve a [[Guardromon]] at level 24+ into [[Kenkimon]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Sunburst}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight}}=== The Digimental of Friendship is an obtainable item which can be used to evolve an [[Armadimon]] at level 24+ with 2300+ Machine EXP into [[Kenkimon]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== Appears as a collectable card. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-3 Version 1|D-3 Digital Detect & Discover}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Digimental_of_friendship |s1=HC |s2=AE }} ==={{hdr|Digimon Card Game}}=== The Digimental of Friendship appears in the artwork of certain cards that also show [[Lighdramon]]. {|class="wikitable" !Card number !Card name |- |[[BT8-053 (DCG)|BT8-053]] (Parallel 2) |[[Lighdramon]] |- |[[BT8-110 (DCG)|BT8-110]] |Armor Texture!! |} ==Potential evolutions using this Digimental== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental Evolution Chart]]''''' {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;font-size:90%;" |- !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution |- | *'''[[V-mon]]''' *[[Renamon]] *[[Sethmon]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Lighdramon.jpg|150px]]

Digimental of Hope.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Digimental of Hope |image=Digimental hope.png |imagesize= |kan={{j2|希望のデジメンタル|Kibou no Dejimentaru}} |dub=Digi-Egg of Hope }} The '''Digimental of Hope''' (希望のデジメンタル ''Kibou no Dejimentaru''; Dub: ''Digi-Egg of Hope'') is a [[Digimental]] that is used to induce [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]. =Overview= ==Design== Like other [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] [[Digimental]]s, the Digimental of Hope is a bulky, egg-shaped object. Its shell bears a very strong resemblance to the barrel armor of one of the Digimon that it creates, [[Pegasmon]]; it is also topped with Pegasmon's ears and a tuft of its mane. Its corresponding [[Crest]], the [[Crest of Hope]], is inscribed on its surface, in the same place as on Pegasmon's barrel armor. ==Effects== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental#Properties]]''''' The Digimental of Hope contains the elemental attribute of Holy {{ETYK|{{j2|神聖|Shinsei}}}}, and it is this elemental power from which Digimon who use this Digimental to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] draw their power.''デジモン大図鑑―デジタルワールド研究白書'' [''Digimon Visual Dictionary: Digital World Research White Paper'']. {{w|Futabasha}}. pp. 215-217. November 15, 2002. ISBN 978-4575163537. Armor Digimon who have used the Digimental of Hope are granted holy powers, with which they can exhibit absolute strength over evildoers.''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report012/ Digi-Basics: "Armors" and "Digimentals"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 012|Translation on Wikimon]]) ==Possible Armor Evolutions== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental Evolution Chart]]''''' {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;font-size:90%;" |- !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution |- | * '''[[Patamon]]'''{{rfe|ZT|03|Digimental Up}} !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Pegasmon.jpg|150px]]

[[Sheepmon]] |- |} =Appearances= ==Anime & Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}=== [[File:Digimental of Hope.jpg|right|thumb|The Digimental of Hope in its resting place.]] The Digimental of Hope is one of the first five Digimentals that the [[Four Holy Beasts]] chose to provide to a new group of [[Chosen Child#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Chosen Children]] in order to stop the [[Ichijouji Ken|Digimon Kaiser]]. They had it placed in a cave in the Digital World, together with the [[Digimental of Light]].{{rfe|ZT|37|Gigantic Ultimate, Qinglongmon}} The Chosen Children's [[D-3]]s detect the Digimentals on their third expedition into the Digital World, and [[Takeru Takaishi]], [[Yagami Hikari]] and [[Hida Iori]] track them to the cave using Iori's D-3 while the others hold off the Digimon Kaiser's forces. Although they are skeptical of their ability to claim the Digimentals due to the failure of other members of the 1999 Chosen Child group to do so, approaching the Digimentals triggers a reaction in their [[Digivice]]s that transforms them into D-3s of their own. Takeru takes the Digimental of Hope, causing [[Patamon (Adventure)|Patamon]] to immediately [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] into [[Pegasmon]]. Iori later reflects that he feels that there is a difference between the Digimentals of Hope and Light, and the other three Digimentals belonging to himself and the other new Chosen Children, Patamon and [[Tailmon (Adventure)|Tailmon]] evidently became able to Armor Evolve instead of a new Digimon being released.{{rfe|ZT|03|Digimental Up}} ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown}}=== After the [[Chosen Child#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Chosen Children]]'s [[D-Terminal]]s are mixed up by [[Kido Jo]] colliding with the group, [[Motomiya Daisuke]] hurriedly takes [[Takaishi Takeru]]'s D-Terminal and, without knowing whose D-Terminal it is, initiates an Armor Evolution. Because of this, [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] uses Takeru's Digimental of Hope to [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] into [[Sagittarimon]].[[Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown|''Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown'': "Surging Armor Evolution"'']] ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Hope is an obtainable item of the Armor category. Equipping it on a Digimon has three possible effects: * If equipped by a [[Patamon]], it teaches Patamon the {{ATO|Shooting Star}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique the next time that a battle is won. After this point, as long as the Digimental is equipped, Patamon can use {{ATO|Shooting Star}} to briefly Armor Evolve into [[Pegasmon]], who performs the technique before immediately reverting back into Patamon. * It increases the equipper's resistance to Beam techniques. * Like any other [[Digimental]], having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Hope must be rebuilt by taking three Hope Fragments—which can be found in the Black Ship dungeon on [[Ichijouji Ken]]'s side—to [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop. Alternatively, the player may receive it by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: D-1 Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Hope is an obtainable item of the [[Digimental]] category. Equipping it on a Digimon has two possible effects: * If equipped by a [[Patamon]], it grants access to the {{ATO|Shooting Star}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique in Patamon's sixth technique slot. When used, Patamon briefly Armor Evolves into [[Pegasmon]], who performs {{ATO|Shooting Star}} before immediately reverting back into Patamon. * Like any other Digimental, having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Hope can be purchased from [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop at any point in the game, or received by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon World: Digital Card Arena}}=== The Digimental of Hope is an unlockable which is separately acquired for one Partner Card. Once it has been acquired, the player is given the option to, when playing the following Partner Card, use the Digimental to Armor Evolve it free of charge: * [[Patamon]] → [[Pegasmon]]
[[Digimental#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Unlock order slot: Digimental #1]] ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Savers: Another Mission}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== Appears as a collectable card. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-3 Toy|D-3}}=== The Digimental of Hope is a collectible item that the player uses in battle to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] their Digimon. All six playable Digimon are capable of using it: * [[V-mon]] → [[Sagittarimon]] * [[Wormmon]] → [[Bullmon]] * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Moosemon]] * [[Tailmon]] → [[Goatmon]] * [[Armadimon]] → [[Sheepmon]] * [[Patamon]] → [[Pegasmon]] To obtain the Digimental of Hope, the player must first collect three fragments of it via the Search function. Its fragments are available in the following Areas:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DThreeEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * [[D-3 Version 1]]: Areas 1 and 3 * [[D-3 Version 2]]: Area 1 * [[D-3 Version 3]]: Area 3 Alternatively, the player may receive it via trade with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal}}=== Digimentals of Hope may be created through the fusion of the following combinations of either Digimon from the Reference Book, or other Digimentals:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DTermEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * Digimon: [[Holy Angemon]] + [[Seraphimon]] * Digimon: [[Holy Angemon]] + [[Angewomon]] * Digimentals: [[Digimental of Friendship]] + [[Digimental of Knowledge]] + [[Digimental of Sincerity]] Digimentals of Hope may be traded with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (International)}}=== The Digi-Egg (Digimental) of Hope is a collectible item that is used in battle to Armor Digivolve ([[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]]) [[Patamon]] into Pegasusmon ([[Pegasmon]]). Unlike the Japanese D-3 line, none of the other playable Digimon may use it. Using the Digi-Egg of Hope does not incur a D-Power point cost and can be done without limitation, but if used, there is a small chance that it may break after a battle, regardless of its outcome, requiring it to be re-obtained. It is obtained by random chance in "Get Digi-Eggs" map events. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (International)}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Spirit}}=== ==={{hdr|D-3 Ver. 15th}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Digimental_of_hope |s1=HC |s2=AE }} =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Digimental hope.png{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Profile]] |i2=Digimental hope.jpg{{!}}200px |c2=[[Digimon Collectors]] }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Digimental hope vpet d3.gif |c1=[[D-3 Toy|D-3]] & [[D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal]] |i2=Digimental hope vpet spirit.gif |c2=[[D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (international)]], [[D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (international)]], & [[D-Spirit]] }} =Additional Information= {{R}} ==See also== * [[Crest of Hope]] * [[Digimental Evolution Chart]] [[Category:Digimentals|H]]

Digimental of Kindness.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Digimental of Kindness |image=Digimental kindness.png |imagesize= |kan={{j2|優しさのデジメンタル|Yasashisa no Dejimentaru}} |dub=Digi-Egg of Kindness }} The '''Digimental of Kindness''' (優しさのデジメンタル ''Yasashisa no Dejimentaru''; Dub: ''Digi-Egg of Kindness'') is a [[Digimental]] that is used to induce [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]. =Overview= ==Design== Like other [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] [[Digimental]]s, the Digimental of Kindness is a bulky, loosely egg-shaped object. It is the only Digimental which does not resemble the body or armor of any of the [[Armor]] Digimon who are created using it. Instead, it resembles a rose. Its corresponding [[Crest]], the [[Crest of Kindness]], is inscribed on its surface. ==Effects== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental#Properties]]''''' The Digimental of Kindness is considered to be shrouded in mystery, and its elemental attribute is unknown. It has been observed that many of the [[Armor]] Digimon that result from using it take the form of cute animals.''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report012/ Digi-Basics: "Armors" and "Digimentals"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 012|Translation on Wikimon]]) ==Possible Armor Evolutions== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental Evolution Chart]]''''' {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;font-size:90%;" |- !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution |- | * [[Wormmon]]''[[D-3 Version 1]]'' !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Pucchiemon.jpg|150px]]

[[Pucchiemon (Green)]] |} =Appearances= ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown}}=== In order to snap [[Motomiya Daisuke]] out of his "Daisuke Kaiser" persona despite the [[Dark Tower]] preventing him from using conventional evolution, [[Ichijouji Ken]], with the support of [[Takenouchi Sora]], [[Tachikawa Mimi]], [[Yagami Hikari]] and their Digimon, miraculously transforms his [[Crest of Kindness]] into the Digimental of Kindness through the power of his self-confidence and affection for his friends. He activates the Digimental and [[Evolution/Anime#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Armor Evolves]] [[Wormmon (Adventure)|Wormmon]] into [[Pucchiemon]], who purifies Daisuke's heart[[Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown|''Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown'': "Daisuke, Darkness Evolution!"'']] and assists the others in fighting [[Pukumon]].[[Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown|''Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown'': "Surging Armor Evolution"'']] ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Kindness is an obtainable item of the Armor category. Equipping it on a Digimon has two possible effects: * It increases the equipper's resistance to Gas techniques. * Like any other [[Digimental]], having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. Despite its abilities in other media, the Digimental of Kindness does not have any [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]-related effect at all in ''Tag Tamers''. The Digimental of Kindness must be rebuilt by taking three Kindness Fragments—which can be found in the Ocean Cave dungeon on [[Ichijouji Ken]]'s side, as well as both Ken and [[Akiyama Ryo]]'s sides' versions of the Water Cave—to [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop. Alternatively, the player may receive it by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story}}=== The Digimental of Kindness is a key item. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following evolutions: * [[Galgomon]] → [[Prairiemon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 27 or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so Prairiemon can be evolved into any number of times. It is found in a treasure chest in Packet Swamp. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Sunburst}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight}}=== The Digimental of Kindness is a key item. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolutions]] in the Special Evolution menu of the DigiLab: * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Tocanmon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 20 or higher; Defense is 105 or higher * [[Patamon]] → [[Prairiemon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 27 or higher; Attack is 135 or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so these Digimon can be evolved into any number of times. It is found in the Chaos Area of Resistor Jungle. ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== The Digimental of Kindness appears as a collectible card. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-3 Toy|D-3}}=== The Digimental of Kindness is a collectible item that the player uses in battle to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] their Digimon. All six playable Digimon are capable of using it: * [[V-mon]] → [[Kangarumon]] * [[Wormmon]] → [[Pucchiemon]] * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Tocanmon]] * [[Tailmon]] → [[Opossumon]] * [[Armadimon]] → [[Chamelemon]] * [[Patamon]] → [[Prairiemon]] In the [[D-3 Version 2]], the player can obtain the Digimental of Kindness by first collecting three fragments of it via the Search function in Area 2.[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DThreeEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. However, there is no way to obtain the Digimental of Kindness in a [[D-3 Version 1]] or [[D-3 Version 3|Version 3]] alone, unlike most other Digimentals. It can only be traded onto the device from the D-3 Version 2 or another compatible game or device, such as the [[D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal]] or ''[[Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers]]''. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal}}=== Digimentals of Kindness may be created through the fusion of the following combinations of either Digimon from the [[Digimon Reference Book|Reference Book]], or other Digimentals:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DTermEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * Digimon: [[Wormmon]] + [[Stingmon]] * Digimentals: [[Digimental of Courage]] + [[Digimental of Friendship]] + [[Digimental of Light]] Digimentals of Kindness may be traded with compatible games and devices. Notably, [[Bandai]] advertised the D-Terminal, and its ability to trade Digimentals with the [[D-3 Toy|D-3]], as a way for D-3s to access this Digimental.[https://web.archive.org/web/20021015032451/http://www.digimon.channel.or.jp/city/gallery/ddd/armar_vmon.html "Ｄ－３デジモンフローチャート"] [''"D-3 Digimon Flowchart"'']. ''[[Digimon Web]]''. Date unknown. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (International)}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Spirit}}=== ==={{hdr|D-3 Ver. 15th}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Digimental_of_kindness |s1=HC }} =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Digimental kindness.png{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Profile]] |i2=Digimental kindness.jpg{{!}}200px |c2=[[Digimon Collectors]] }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Digimental kindness vpet d3.gif |c1=[[D-3 Toy|D-3]] & [[D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal]] |i2=Digimental kindness vpet spirit.gif |c2=[[D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal]] (international) & [[D-Spirit]] }} =Additional Information= {{R | }} =See Also= * [[Crest of Kindness]] * [[Digimental Evolution Chart]] [[Category:Digimentals|K]]

Digimental of Knowledge.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Digimental of Knowledge |image=Digimental knowledge.png |imagesize= |kan={{j2|知識のデジメンタル|Chishiki no Dejimentaru}} |dub=Digi-Egg of Knowledge }} The '''Digimental of Knowledge''' (知識のデジメンタル ''Chishiki no Dejimentaru''; Dub: ''Digi-Egg of Knowledge'') is a [[Digimental]] that is used to induce [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]. =Overview= ==Design== Like other [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] [[Digimental]]s, the Digimental of Knowledge is a bulky, loosely egg-shaped object. It is shaped like and patterned after the body armor of one of the Digimon created by using it, [[Digmon]], and has the tip of one of its drills poking out of its top. Its corresponding [[Crest]], the [[Crest of Knowledge]], is inscribed on its surface. ==Effects== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental#Properties]]''''' The Digimental of Knowledge contains the elemental attribute of Earth {{ETYK|{{j2|土|Tsuchi}}}}, and it is this elemental power from which Digimon who use this Digimental to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] draw their power.''デジモン大図鑑―デジタルワールド研究白書'' [''Digimon Visual Dictionary: Digital World Research White Paper'']. {{w|Futabasha}}. pp. 215-217. November 15, 2002. ISBN 978-4575163537. Armor Digimon who have used the Digimental of Knowledge are granted earth-based abilities, are able to control the earth, and specialize in underground combat.''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report012/ Digi-Basics: "Armors" and "Digimentals"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 012|Translation on Wikimon]]) In one case, [[Searchmon]], another effect of the Digimental of Knowledge is granting the Digimon an inquisitive nature.{{refd|Searchmon}} The power of the Digimental of Knowledge can also cause mutant-like [[evolution]]s when mass outbreaks of [[Kunemon]] occur. [[Flybeemon]], [[Butterflamon]], [[Honeybeemon]] and [[Mothmon]] are all known to be born in this way.{{refd|Flybeemon}}{{refd|Butterflamon}}{{refd|Mothmon}} All known Armor Digimon created using the Digimental of Knowledge are [[:Category:Insect Type|Insect]] Digimon. ==Possible Armor Evolutions== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental Evolution Chart]]''''' {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;font-size:90%;" |- !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution |- | * '''[[Armadimon]]'''{{rfe|ZT|02|Digital Gate Open}}''[[D-3 Version 3]]'' !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Digmon.jpg|150px]]
