[[Jiko Tyumon]] |} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}=== [[Image:Digimental of Knowlage.jpg|right|thumb|The Digimental of Knowledge on its altar.]] The Digimental of Knowledge is one of the first five Digimentals that the [[Four Holy Beasts]] chose to provide to a new group of [[Chosen Child#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Chosen Children]] in order to stop the [[Ichijouji Ken|Digimon Kaiser]]. They had it placed in a Mayan pyramid in the [[Digital World (Adventure)|Digital World]], together with the [[Digimental of Love]], sealing away [[Armadimon (Adventure)|Armadimon]] to be awoken once the intended Chosen Child arrived.{{rfe|ZT|37|Gigantic Ultimate, Qinglongmon}} During their second expedition into the Digital World, [[Tentomon (Adventure)|Tentomon]] and [[Piyomon (Adventure)|Piyomon]] lead [[Hida Iori]], [[Inoue Miyako]], [[Izumi Kōshirō]] and [[Takenouchi Sora]] to the pyramid and the Digimentals when they notice their [[D-3]]s reacting. Despite being the owner of the [[Crest of Knowledge]], Kōshirō is unable to lift the Digimental of Knowledge, but realizes that Iori might be able. Iori successfully takes the Digimental and releases Armadimon and, following [[Hawkmon (Adventure)|Hawkmon]]'s instructions, uses it to [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] Armadimon into [[Digmon]].{{rfe|ZT|02|Digital Gate Open}} ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 1: Digimon Hurricane Landing!!/Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals|Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals}}=== [[Hida Iori]] uses the Digimental of Knowledge to [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] [[Armadimon (Adventure)|Armadimon]] into [[Digmon]] in the final encounter with [[Lopmon (Adventure)|Chocomon]] at Summer Memory. ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown}}=== After the [[Chosen Child#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Chosen Children]]'s [[D-Terminal]]s are mixed up by [[Kido Jo]] colliding with the group, [[Yagami Hikari]] hurriedly takes [[Hida Iori]]'s D-Terminal and, without knowing whose D-Terminal it is, initiates an [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]. Because of this, [[Tailmon (Adventure)|Tailmon]] uses Iori's Digimental of Knowledge to Armor Evolve into [[Butterflamon]].[[Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown|''Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown'': "Surging Armor Evolution"'']] In the drama CD's ''omake'', [[Izumi Kōshirō]] uses the Digimental of Knowledge to Armor Evolve [[Tentomon (Adventure)|Tentomon]] into [[Jiko Tyumon]]. ==Manga== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Knowledge is an obtainable item of the Armor category. Equipping it on a Digimon has three possible effects: * If equipped by a [[Armadimon]] (including [[Armadimon (Adventure)|the temporary partner Armadimon]]), it teaches Armadimon the {{ATO|Gold Rush}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique the next time that a battle is won. After this point, as long as the Digimental is equipped, Armadimon can use {{ATO|Gold Rush}} to briefly Armor Evolve into [[Digmon]], who performs the technique before immediately reverting back into Armadimon. * It increases the equipper's resistance to Blow techniques. * Like any other [[Digimental]], having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Knowledge is the only Digimental that the player can receive in an already complete state: it can be found in a chest in the Sea Cave 2 dungeon on [[Akiyama Ryo]]'s side. Knowledge Fragments can also be found in the Detached Cave and Machine Base dungeons, both on [[Ichijouji Ken]]'s side, and rebuilt into more Digimentals of Knowledge at [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop. Alternatively, the player may receive it by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: D-1 Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Knowledge is an obtainable item of the [[Digimental]] category. Equipping it on a Digimon has two possible effects: * If equipped by a [[Armadimon]] (including [[Armadimon (Adventure)|the temporary partner Armadimon]]), it grants access to the {{ATO|Gold Rush}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique in Armadimon's sixth technique slot. When used, Armadimon briefly Armor Evolves into [[Digmon]], who performs {{ATO|Gold Rush}} before immediately reverting back into Armadimon. * Like any other Digimental, having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Knowledge can be purchased from [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop at any point in the game, or received by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon World: Digital Card Arena}}=== The Digimental of Knowledge is an unlockable which is acquired for one Partner Card. Once it has been acquired, the player is given the option to, when playing the following Partner Card, use the Digimental to [[Evolution/Video Games#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Armor Evolve]] it free of charge: * [[Armadimon]] → [[Digmon]]
[[Digimental#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Unlock order slot: Digimental #1]] ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World 3}}=== The Digimental of Knowledge is a key item, which can be used at certain holes in the ground on the overworld to summon a [[Digmon]] in order to carry [[Atsushi|the player]] through the [[Underground]]. The Digimental is found in the [[Zhuque Underground Lake]], and is accessible after defeating the [[Zhuque Chief]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Savers: Another Mission}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== Appears as a collectable card. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-3 Toy|D-3}}=== The Digimental of Knowledge is a collectible item that the player uses in battle to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] their Digimon. All six playable Digimon are capable of using it: * [[V-mon]] → [[Honeybeemon]] * [[Wormmon]] → [[Searchmon]] * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Flybeemon]] * [[Tailmon]] → [[Butterflamon]] * [[Armadimon]] → [[Digmon]] * [[Patamon]] → [[Mothmon]] To obtain the Digimental of Knowledge, the player must first collect three fragments of it via the Search function. Its fragments are available in the following Areas:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DThreeEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * [[D-3 Version 1]]: Areas 2 and 3 * [[D-3 Version 2]]: Areas 1 and 2 * [[D-3 Version 3]]: Areas 1 and 3 Alternatively, the player may receive it via trade with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal}}=== Digimentals of Knowledge may be created through the fusion of the following combinations of either Digimon from the Reference Book, or other Digimentals:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DTermEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * Digimon: [[Atlur Kabuterimon]] + [[Shakkoumon]] * Digimon: [[Herakle Kabuterimon]] + [[Vikemon]] * Digimentals: [[Digimental of Courage]] + [[Digimental of Friendship]] + [[Digimental of Love]] Digimentals of Knowledge may be traded with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (International)}}=== The Digi-Egg (Digimental) of Knowledge is a collectible item that is used in battle to Armor Digivolve ([[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]]) Armadillomon ([[Armadimon]]) into [[Digmon]]. Unlike the Japanese D-3 line, none of the other playable Digimon may use it. Using the Digi-Egg of Knowledge does not incur a D-Power point cost and can be done without limitation, but if used, there is a small chance that it may break after a battle, regardless of its outcome, requiring it to be re-obtained. It is obtained by random chance in "Get Digi-Eggs" map events. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (International)}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Spirit}}=== ==={{hdr|D-3 Ver. 15th}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Digimental_of_knowledge |s1=HC |s2=AE }} ==={{hdr|Digimon Card Game}}=== The Digimental of Knowledge appears in the artwork of certain cards that also show [[Digmon]]. {|class="wikitable" !Card number !Card name |- |[[BT8-110 (DCG)|BT8-110]] (Parallel) |Armor Texture!! |} =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Digimental knowledge.png{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Profile]] |i2=Digimental knowledge.jpg{{!}}200px |c2=[[Digimon Collectors]] }} |2=game |t2= {{IG |w=128 |h=128 |i1=DW3 Digimental knowledge.png |c1=[[Digimon World 3]] }} }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Digimental knowledge vpet d3.gif |c1=[[D-3 Toy|D-3]] & [[D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal]] |i2=Digimental knowledge vpet spirit.gif |c2=[[D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (international)]], [[D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (international)]], & [[D-Spirit]] }} =Additional Information= {{R}} ==See Also== * [[Crest of Knowledge]] * [[Digimental Evolution Chart]] [[Category:Digimentals|K]]

Digimental of Light.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Digimental of Light |image=Digimental light.png |imagesize= |kan={{j2|光のデジメンタル|Hikari no Dejimentaru}} |dub=DigiEgg of Light }} The '''Digimental of Light''' (光のデジメンタル ''Hikari no Dejimentaru''; Dub: ''DigiEgg of Light'') is one of the [[Digimental]]s. It resembles the claw armor of [[Nefertimon]], and is decorated with the [[Crest of Light]]. =Appearances= ==Anime & Manga== [[Image:Digimental Ligth.PNG|right|thumb|The Digimental of Light.]] ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}=== The Digimental of Light was hidden in a cave. Hikari and Takeru found it, and Hikari used it to evolve Tailmon into Nefertimon. ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown}}=== When [[Kido Jo]] bumped into five of the [[Chosen Children]] by accident, Takeru grabbed Hikari's D-Terminal by mistake. When used, Patamon Armor Evolved into Manbomon. ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World: Digital Card Arena}}=== The Digimental of Light can be obtained for the [[Tailmon]] and [[Wormmon]] Partner Cards to be able to Armor Evolve them in-battle to [[Nefertimon]] and [[Coatlmon]] respectively, being the first Digimental for the first and second Digimental for the second. * For Tailmon, it can be obtained from [[Yagami Hikari]] in [[Sky City]]'s Battle Café after clearing the Battle Arena if Tailmon is the player's second Partner Card, or from [[Agumon (Adventure)|Metal Greymon]] in [[Sage Tower]]'s Battle Café after clearing the Battle Arena if Tailmon is the player's third Partner Card. * For Wormmon, it can only be obtained from Omegamon (the fusion of [[Agumon (Adventure)|War Greymon]] and [[Gabumon (Adventure)|Metal Garurumon]]) in Sage Tower's Battle Café after clearing [[Sage Tower]]'s last Battle Arena if Wormmon is the player's third Partner Card. ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Savers: Another Mission}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Sunburst}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== Appears as a collectable card. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-3 Version 1|D-3 Digital Detect & Discover}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Digimental_of_light |s1=HC |s2=AE }} =Possible Armor Evolutions= : ''See also: '''[[Digimental Evolution Chart]]''''' {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; Digimental of Love.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Digimental of Love |image=Digimental love.png |imagesize= |kan={{j2|愛情のデジメンタル|Aijou no Dejimentaru}} |dub=Digi-Egg of Love }} The '''Digimental of Love''' (愛情のデジメンタル ''Aijou no Dejimentaru''; Dub: ''Digi-Egg of Love'') is a [[Digimental]] that is used to induce [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]. =Overview= ==Design== Like other [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] [[Digimental]]s, the Digimental of Love is a bulky, vaguely egg-shaped object. It resembles the head armor of two of the Digimon created by using it, [[Holsmon]] and [[Sethmon]], including short wing-like protrusions. Its corresponding [[Crest]], the [[Crest of Love]], is inscribed on its surface, in the same place and color as it appears on Holsmon. ==Effects== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental#Properties]]''''' The Digimental of Love contains the elemental attribute of Wind {{ETYK|{{j2|風|Kaze}}}}, and it is this elemental power from which Digimon who use this Digimental to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] draw their power.''デジモン大図鑑―デジタルワールド研究白書'' [''Digimon Visual Dictionary: Digital World Research White Paper'']. {{w|Futabasha}}. pp. 215-217. November 15, 2002. ISBN 978-4575163537. [[Armor]] Digimon who have used the Digimental of Love are granted wind-based abilities, are able to flap their wings to soar through the sky like a gust of wind running through the air,{{refd|Holsmon}} and specialize in aerial combat.''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report012/ Digi-Basics: "Armors" and "Digimentals"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 012|Translation on Wikimon]]) The Digimental of Love contains the duality of love and hatred. [[Sethmon]] is an example of the power of the Digimental of Love being too great for a Digimon to control, and consequently being converted into the power of hatred; it and its polar opposite, [[Holsmon]], are thought to embody the duality of the Digimental.{{refd|Sethmon}} ==Possible Armor Evolutions== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental Evolution Chart]]''''' {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;font-size:90%;" |- !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution |- | * '''[[Hawkmon]]'''{{rfe|ZT|02|Digital Gate Open}}''[[D-3 Version 2]]'' !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Holsmon.jpg|150px]]
[[Holsmon]] | * [[V-mon]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Sethmon.jpg|150px]]
[[Sethmon]] |- | * [[Wormmon]]''[[D-3 Version 1]]'' !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Owlmon.jpg|150px]]
[[Owlmon]] | * [[Tailmon]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Swanmon.jpg|150px]]
[[Swanmon]] |- | * [[Armadimon]]''[[D-3 Version 3]]'' !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Pteranomon.jpg|150px]]
[[Pteranomon]] | * [[Patamon]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Pipismon.jpg|150px]]
[[Pipismon]] |- | * [[Piyomon]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Digimon_noimage.jpg|150px]]
[[Kyukanchoumon]] |} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}=== [[Image:Digimental of Love.jpg|right|thumb|The Digimental of Love on its altar.]] The Digimental of Love is one of the first five Digimentals that the [[Four Holy Beasts]] chose to provide to a new group of [[Chosen Child#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Chosen Children]] in order to stop the [[Ichijouji Ken|Digimon Kaiser]]. They had it placed in a Mayan pyramid in the [[Digital World (Adventure)|Digital World]], together with the [[Digimental of Knowledge]], sealing away [[Hawkmon (Adventure)|Hawkmon]] with it to be awoken once the intended Chosen Child arrived.{{rfe|ZT|37|Gigantic Ultimate, Qinglongmon}} During their second expedition into the Digital World, [[Tentomon (Adventure)|Tentomon]] and [[Piyomon (Adventure)|Piyomon]] lead [[Inoue Miyako]], [[Hida Iori]], [[Izumi Kōshirō]] and [[Takenouchi Sora]] to the pyramid and the Digimentals when they notice their [[D-3]]s reacting. Despite being the owner of the [[Crest of Love]], Sora is unable to lift the Digimental of Love, but realizes that Miyako might be able. Miyako successfully takes the Digimental and releases Hawkmon and, following his instructions, uses it to [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] him into [[Holsmon]].{{rfe|ZT|02|Digital Gate Open}} ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 1: Digimon Hurricane Landing!!/Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals|Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals}}=== [[Inoue Miyako]] uses the Digimental of Love to [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] [[Hawkmon (Adventure)|Hawkmon]] into [[Holsmon]] in the final encounter with [[Lopmon (Adventure)|Chocomon]] at Summer Memory. ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown}}=== After the [[Chosen Child#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Chosen Children]]'s [[D-Terminal]]s are mixed up by [[Kido Jo]] colliding with the group, [[Hida Iori]] hurriedly takes [[Inoue Miyako]]'s D-Terminal and, without knowing whose D-Terminal it is, initiates an [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]. Because of this, [[Armadimon (Adventure)|Armadimon]] uses Miyako's Digimental of Love to Armor Evolve into [[Pteranomon]].[[Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown|''Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown'': "Surging Armor Evolution"'']] In the drama CD's ''omake'', [[Takenouchi Sora]] uses the Digimental of Love to Armor Evolve [[Piyomon (Adventure)|Piyomon]] into [[Kyukanchoumon]]. ==Manga== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Love is an obtainable item of the Armor category. Equipping it on a Digimon has three possible effects: * If equipped by a [[Hawkmon]], it teaches Hawkmon the {{ATO|Tempest Wing}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique the next time that a battle is won. After this point, as long as the Digimental is equipped, Hawkmon can use {{ATO|Tempest Wing}} to briefly Armor Evolve into [[Holsmon]], who performs the technique before immediately reverting back into Hawkmon. * It increases the equipper's resistance to Bubble techniques. * Like any other [[Digimental]], having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Love must be rebuilt by taking three Love Fragments—which can be found in the Black Ship 2 dungeons on [[Akiyama Ryo]]'s side, and both Ryo and [[Ichijouji Ken]]'s versions of the Water Cave dungeon—to [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop. Alternatively, the player may receive it by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: D-1 Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Love is an obtainable item of the [[Digimental]] category. Equipping it on a Digimon has two possible effects: * If equipped by a [[Hawkmon]], it grants access to the {{ATO|Tempest Wing}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique in Hawkmon's sixth technique slot. When used, Hawkmon briefly Armor Evolves into [[Holsmon]], who performs {{ATO|Tempest Wing}} before immediately reverting back into Hawkmon. * Like any other Digimental, having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Love can be purchased from [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop at any point in the game, or received by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon World: Digital Card Arena}}=== The Digimental of Love is an unlockable which is separately acquired for one Partner Card. Once it has been acquired, the player is given the option to, when playing the following Partner Card, use the Digimental to [[Evolution/Video Games#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Armor Evolve]] it free of charge: * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Holsmon]]
[[Digimental#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Unlock order slot: Digimental #2]] ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Savers: Another Mission}}=== The Digimental of Love is a key item. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to unlock the following evolutions: * [[Falcomon (Savers)|Falcomon]]: [[Pteranomon]]
Other evolution unlock requirements: [[Peckmon]] has been unlocked; Defense is 250 or higher; Level is 21 or higher; number of defeated [[:Category:Metal Empire|Metal Empire]] Digimon is 10 or higher; highest damage inflicted is 600 or higher It is found in a chest on the second floor of Sea Precipice Jerapilus. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Sunburst}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight}}=== The Digimental of Love is a key item. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following [[Evolution/Video Games#Digimon Story:Sunburst & Moonlight|Armor Evolutions]] in in the Special Evolution menu of the DigiLab: * [[Wormmon]] → [[Owlmon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 21 or higher; Spirit is 110 or higher * [[Patamon]] → [[Pipismon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 21 or higher; Speed is 100 or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so these Digimon can be evolved into any number of times. This Digimental is a reward for clearing the Union Headquarters quest '''Investigate Limit Valley''' {{ETYK|リミットバレーをちょうさ}} (Dub: ''Explore Limit Valley''). ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== Appears as a collectable card. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-3 Toy|D-3}}=== The Digimental of Love is a collectible item that the player uses in battle to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] their Digimon. All six playable Digimon are capable of using it: * [[V-mon]] → [[Sethmon]] * [[Wormmon]] → [[Owlmon]] * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Holsmon]] * [[Tailmon]] → [[Swanmon]] * [[Armadimon]] → [[Pteranomon]] * [[Patamon]] → [[Pipismon]] To obtain the Digimental of Love, the player must first collect three fragments of it via the Search function. Its fragments are available in the following Areas:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DThreeEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * [[D-3 Version 1]]: Areas 1 and 3 * [[D-3 Version 2]]: Area 3 * [[D-3 Version 3]]: Areas 1 and 3 Alternatively, the player may receive it via trade with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal}}=== Digimentals of Love may be created through the fusion of the following combinations of either Digimon from the Reference Book, or other Digimentals:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DTermEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * Digimon: [[Garudamon]] + [[Silphymon]] * Digimon: [[Hououmon]] + [[Valkyrimon]] * Digimentals: [[Digimental of Courage]] + [[Digimental of Hope]] + [[Digimental of Light]] Digimentals of Love may be traded with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (International)}}=== The Digi-Egg (Digimental) of Love is a collectible item that is used in battle to Armor Digivolve ([[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]]) [[Hawkmon]] into Halsemon ([[Holsmon]]). Unlike the Japanese D-3 line, none of the other playable Digimon may use it. Using the Digi-Egg of Love does not incur a D-Power point cost and can be done without limitation, but if used, there is a small chance that it may break after a battle, regardless of its outcome, requiring it to be re-obtained. It is obtained by random chance in "Get Digi-Eggs" map events. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (International)}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Spirit}}=== ==={{hdr|D-3 Ver. 15th}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Digimental_of_love |s1=HC |s2=AE }} ==={{hdr|Digimon Card Game}}=== The Digimental of Love appears in the artwork of certain cards that also show [[Holsmon]]. {|class="wikitable" !Card number !Card name |- |[[BT8-110 (DCG)|BT8-110]] (Parallel) |Armor Texture!! |} =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Digimental love.png{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Profile]] |i2=Digimental love.jpg{{!}}200px |c2=[[Digimon Collectors]] }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Digimental love vpet d3.gif |c1=[[D-3 Toy|D-3]] & [[D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal]] |i2=Digimental love vpet spirit.gif |c2=[[D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (international)]], [[D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (international)]], & [[D-Spirit]] }} =Additional Information= {{R}} ==See also== * [[Crest of Love]] * [[Digimental Evolution Chart]] [[Category:Digimentals|L]]

Digimental of Miracles.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Digimental of Miracles |image=Digimental miracles.png |imagesize= |kan={{j2|奇跡のデジメンタル|Kiseki no Dejimentaru}} |dub=Digi-Egg of Miracles{{note|Some English localization materials, such as the ''[[Digimon: The Movie]]'' recut of ''[[Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 1: Digimon Hurricane Landing!!/Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals|Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals]]'', collectively refer to this Digimental and the [[Digimental of Fate]] as "the Golden Digi-Eggs," giving them a special status separate from the other Digimentals.|n=GOLDEN}} }} The '''Digimental of Miracles''' (奇跡のデジメンタル ''Kiseki no Dejimentaru''; Dub: ''Digi-Egg of Miracles''{{note|n=GOLDEN}}) is a [[Digimental]] that is used to induce [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]. =Overview= ==Design== Like other [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] [[Digimental]]s, the Digimental of Miracles is a bulky object. It is made of [[Chrome Digizoid]],{{refd|Magnamon}} is boxy and angular in shape, and bears a very strong resembles to the armor of one of the Digimon that it creates, [[Magnamon]], including having identical feet. A symbol is inscribed on its top; the ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth]]'' games call this symbol the '''Crest of Miracles''' {{ETYK|{{j2|奇跡の紋章|Kiseki no monshō}}}}, although such a [[Crest]] otherwise generally does not exist.''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth]]'' ==Effects== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental#Properties]]''''' The Digimental of Miracles contains the elemental attribute of Metal {{ETYK|{{j2|メタル|Metaru}}}}, and it is this elemental power from which Digimon who use this Digimental to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] draw their power.''デジモン大図鑑―デジタルワールド研究白書'' [''Digimon Visual Dictionary: Digital World Research White Paper'']. {{w|Futabasha}}. pp. 215-217. November 15, 2002. ISBN 978-4575163537. [[Armor]] Digimon who have used the Digimental of Miracles are granted metal-based abilities and tremendous defensive prowess.''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report012/ Digi-Basics: "Armors" and "Digimentals"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 012|Translation on Wikimon]]) It is fittingly known as a "Miracle Item" {{ETYK|{{j2|ミラクルアイテム|Mirakuruaitemu}}}}, as the miraculous power that it bestows upon its users enables them to overcoming any predicament that they may face.{{refd|Magnamon}} It appears to be capable of granting exceptional power compared to most other Digimentals: the Armor Digimon most frequently associated with it, [[Magnamon]], possesses power equal to or even surpassing that of [[Ultimate]] Digimon.{{refd|Magnamon}} ==Possible Armor Evolutions== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental Evolution Chart]]''''' {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;font-size:90%;" |- !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution |- | * [[Guardromon (Gold)]]''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory]] * '''[[V-mon]]'''{{rfe|ZT|20|Transcendent Evolution! Golden Magnamon|b}} * [[Terriermon]]{{rfc|St|551}} * [[XV-mon]]{{ref|''[[Digimon Story]]''}} !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Magnamon.jpg|150px]]
[[Magnamon]] | * [[Stingmon]]{{ref|''[[Digimon Story]]''}} * [[Wormmon]]''[[D-3 Version 1]]'' !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Kongoumon.jpg|150px]]
[[Kongoumon]] |- | * [[Clear Agumon]]{{rfc|St|553}} * [[Hawkmon]]''[[D-3 Version 2]]'' !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Peacockmon.jpg|150px]]
[[Peacockmon]] | * [[Plotmon]]{{rfc|Bo|461}} * [[Tailmon]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Maildramon.jpg|150px]]
[[Maildramon]] |- | * [[Armadimon]]''[[D-3 Version 3]]'' * [[Clear Agumon]]{{rfc|St|552}} !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Elephamon.jpg|150px]]
[[Elephamon]] | * [[Patamon]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Rhinomon.jpg|150px]]
[[Rhinomon]] |- |} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}=== During the [[Chosen Child#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Chosen Children]]'s final raid on the [[Ichijouji Ken|Digimon Kaiser]]'s mobile base, the [[Four Holy Beasts]] deem it necessary to provide the group with the power of the Digimental of Miracles in order to lead the Kaiser/Ichijouji Ken back from the darkness. To this end, just as [[Wormmon (Adventure)|Wormmon]] shows [[Motomiya Daisuke]] the base's power source—the [[Crest of Kindness]], tainted by darkness and turned into a blackened version of this Digimental—the Holy Beasts transform the Crest into the true Digimental of Miracles, which disconnects itself from the base, emerges from its blackened shell, and unleashes a wave of light that tears apart the power core surrounding it and empowers [[Chibimon]] to evolve back into [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]].{{rfe|ZT|20|Transcendent Evolution! Golden Magnamon|b}}{{rfe|ZT|37|Gigantic Ultimate, Qinglongmon}} The Digimental of Miracles levitates into Daisuke's hands as he hears a mysterious voice, and Daisuke activates it to [[Evolution/Anime#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Armor Evolve]] V-mon into [[Magnamon]].{{rfe|ZT|20|Transcendent Evolution! Golden Magnamon|b}} Using the Digimental's power and with Wormmon's help, Magnamon defeats the Kaiser's [[Chimairamon]], leading to Ken snapping out of the Kaiser persona. After he devolves from Magnamon, the Digimental of Miracles reverts into the Crest of Kindness.{{rfe|ZT|21|Goodbye, Ken...|}} [[Yagami Hikari]] observes that the Digimental of Miracles must be special compared to the other Digimentals, and [[Tailmon (Adventure)|Tailmon]] realizes that its appearance from the Crest of Kindness, and use in bringing Ken back from the darkness, is what [[Wizarmon (Adventure)|Wizarmon]] had meant by the words ''"kindness will release the golden light."''{{rfe|ZT|21|Goodbye, Ken...|}} {| |[[Image:DigimentalMiracles.jpg|thumb|The Digimental of Miracles appears before [[Motomiya Daisuke]].]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 1: Digimon Hurricane Landing!!/Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals|Digimon Adventure 02: Vol. 2: Transcendent Evolution!! The Golden Digimentals}}=== During the final confrontation with [[Lopmon (Adventure)|Chocomon's]] [[Cherubimon (Vice)]] form at Summer Memory in the summer of 2002, [[Seraphimon]] and [[Holydramon]] manifest the Digimental of Miracles, along with the [[Digimental of Fate]], and pass the Digimental of Miracles on to [[Motomiya Daisuke]]. Daisuke activates it and [[Evolution/Anime#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Armor Evolves]] [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] into [[Magnamon]], who together with [[Rapidmon Armor|Rapidmon]] stands up to and, ultimately, euthanizes Cherubimon. ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== During the final battle with [[Parallelmon]], [[Inoue Miyako]], [[Hida Iori]], [[Takaishi Takeru]] and [[Yagami Hikari]] use their [[D-3]]s to send their strength, feelings and encouragement to [[Motomiya Daisuke]]'s D-3, along with those of all the other Tamers that Parallelmon had captured, in the form of the Digimental of Miracles. Once he and [[Yagami Taichi (V-Tamer)|Yagami Taichi]] turn the tide against Parallelmon, Daisuke uses the Digimental to [[Evolution/Anime#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Armor Evolve]] [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] into [[Magnamon]], to finish Parallelmon off.[[V-Tamer Double Tamer|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Double Tamer"]] ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Miracles is an obtainable item of the Armor category. Equipping it on a Digimon has three possible effects: * If equipped by a [[V-mon]] (including [[V-mon (Adventure)|the temporary partner V-mon]]), it teaches V-mon the {{ATO|Plasma Shoot}} technique the next time that a battle is won. After this point, as long as the Digimental is equipped, V-mon can use {{ATO|Plasma Shoot}} to briefly Armor Evolve into [[Magnamon]], who performs the technique before immediately reverting back into V-mon. * It increases the equipper's resistance to Mind techniques. * Like any other [[Digimental]], having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Miracles must be rebuilt by taking three Miracles Fragments—which can be found in the Water Cave 2, Fire Cave 2 and Plume Cave 2 dungeons on [[Akiyama Ryo]]'s side, the Machine Base dungeon on [[Ichijouji Ken]]'s side, and both sides' versions of Death Crevasse—to [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop. Alternatively, the player may receive it by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: D-1 Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Miracles is an obtainable item of the [[Digimental]] category. Equipping it on a Digimon has two possible effects: * If equipped by a [[V-mon]] (including [[V-mon (Adventure)|the temporary partner V-mon]]), it grants access to the {{ATO|Plasma Shoot}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique in V-mon's sixth technique slot. When used, V-mon briefly Armor Evolves into [[Magnamon]], who performs {{ATO|Plasma Shoot}} before immediately reverting back into V-mon. * Like any other Digimental, having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Miracles can be purchased from [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop after purchasing the shop's second and final upgrade from [[Clockmon]], or received by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon World: Digital Card Arena}}=== The Digimental of Courage is the [[V-mon]] Partner Card's third unlockable [[Digimental]]. Once it has been acquired, the player is given the option to, when playing V-mon, use the Digimental to Armor Evolve it free of charge. If V-mon was chosen as the player's first or third partner, the Digimental of Miracles is obtained in the post-game; after 100% completing the post-game content and scoring 300 wins, [[Black War Greymon (Adventure)|Black War Greymon]] will give it to the player if spoken with in [[Beginner City]]'s Battle Café. If V-mon was not chosen as a partner, it is obtained by winning nine repeated matches against [[Motomiya Daisuke]], including being challenged to win using Daisuke's own deck in the ninth match. ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story}}=== The Digimental of Miracles is a key item. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following evolutions: * [[XV-mon]] → [[Magnamon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 30 or higher * [[Stingmon]] → [[Kongoumon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 30 or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so these Digimon can be evolved into any number of times. The Digimental of Miracles is obtained by defeating [[Platinum Scumon]] in Drain Tunnel as part of the main story, as it is required to gain entry into the Chrome Mine. Along the way, the player is misled into accepting a useless bootleg, the '''Dizomentan of Miracles''' (きせきのデゾメンタン ''Kiseki no dezomentan''; Dub: ''Digi-Eff of Miracles''), from [[Pandamon]], and does not realize this until they try using it to enter the Chrome Mine. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Sunburst}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight}}=== The Digimental of Miracles is a key item. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following Armor Evolutions in in the Special Evolution menu of the DigiLab: * [[V-mon]] → [[Magnamon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 40 or higher; Holy EXP is 10000 or higher * [[Wormmon]] → [[Kongoumon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 32 or higher; Attack is 190 or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so these Digimon can be evolved into any number of times. It is found in the Chaos Area of Login Mountain. ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== During the [[Village of Beginnings|Colosseum]] 40th floor Free Battle: "Radiance of Miracles", [[Motomiya Daisuke]] uses the Digimental of Miracles to [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] into [[Magnamon]]. After his defeat, the Digimental of Miracles resonates with [[Taiga|Taiga's]] [[List of Digivices#Digimon Adventure Re:Digitize Decode|Digivice]], which registers its data, unlocking Magnamon as an [[Evolution/Video_Games#Digimon_World_Re:Digitize_.26_Decode|evolution]] for [[Digimaru]]. It also appears as a collectable card. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory|Hacker's Memory}}=== The Digimental of Miracles is a key item in both games. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following evolutions: * [[V-mon]] → [[Magnamon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 60 or higher; Attack is 120 or higher; Defense is 145 or higher; Ability is 40 or higher; Camaraderie is 50% or higher * [[Guardromon (Gold)]] → [[Magnamon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 60 or higher; Attack is 120 or higher; Defense is 145 or higher; Ability is 40 or higher; Camaraderie is 50% or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so Magnamon can be evolved into any number of times. In ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth]]'', the Digimental of Miracles is one of the rewards for completing the post-game case "Great Challenge ①," in which the player must defeat a Magnamon; in solo releases of ''Cyber Sleuth'', aside from the Digimental, evolving into Magnamon is also locked until this case is cleared, but this restriction is lifted in compilation rereleases. In ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory]]'', the Digimental of Miracles is dropped by Aloof Hacker Kazuyoshi's [[XV-mon]] after defeating it in the EDEN Free Area during the "Beat Aloof Hacker Kazuyoshi!" Blacklisted Hacker case. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-3 Toy|D-3}}=== The Digimental of Miracles is a collectible item that the player uses in battle to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] their Digimon. All six playable Digimon are capable of using it: * [[V-mon]] → [[Magnamon]] * [[Wormmon]] → [[Kongoumon]] * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Peacockmon]] * [[Tailmon]] → [[Maildramon]] * [[Armadimon]] → [[Elephamon]] * [[Patamon]] → [[Rhinomon]] Unlike most other Digimentals, there is no way to obtain Digimental of Miracles in a D-3 by itself. It can only be traded onto the device from a compatible game or device, such as the [[D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal]] or ''[[Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers]]''. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal}}=== Digimentals of Miracles may be created through the fusion of the following combinations of either Digimon from the [[Digimon Reference Book|Reference Book]], or other Digimentals:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DTermEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * Digimon: [[Seraphimon]] + [[Holydramon]] * Digimentals: [[Digimental of Purity]] + [[Digimental of Hope]] + [[Digimental of Kindness]] Digimentals of Miracles may be traded with compatible games and devices. Notably, [[Bandai]] advertised the D-Terminal, and its ability to trade Digimentals with the [[D-3 Toy|D-3]], as a way for D-3s to access this Digimental.[https://web.archive.org/web/20021015032451/http://www.digimon.channel.or.jp/city/gallery/ddd/armar_vmon.html "Ｄ－３デジモンフローチャート"] [''"D-3 Digimon Flowchart"'']. ''[[Digimon Web]]''. Date unknown. ==={{hdr|D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (International)}}=== The Digi-Egg (Digimental) of Miracles is a collectible item that is used in battle to Armor Digivolve ([[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]]) Veemon ([[V-mon]]) into [[Magnamon]]. Unlike the Japanese D-3 line, none of the other playable Digimon may use it. Using the Digi-Egg of Miracles does not incur a D-Power point cost and can be done without limitation, but if used, there is a small chance that it may break after a battle, regardless of its outcome, requiring it to be re-obtained. On the D-3 Digivice Version 1, the player may be given a chance to obtain the Digi-Egg of Miracles after defeating the final boss, Azulongmon ([[Qinglongmon]]). If other prerequisites are met, [[Baihumon]] appears for a bonus battle, and defeating it awards the player with the Digi-Egg for use in their next lap of the quest mode.SnowFox102. [http://web.archive.org/web/20010625084636/https://www.angelfire.com/anime2/ad3faq/bosses.html "Bosses"]. ''The AD-3 FAQ - The source for information on the English D-3!'' April 4, 2001. (Archived by the Internet Archive Wayback Machine.) ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (International)}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Spirit}}=== ==={{hdr|D-3 Ver. 15th}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Digimental_of_miracles |s1=HC |s2=BT }} ==={{hdr|Digimon Card Game}}=== The Digimental of Miracles appears in the artwork of certain cards that also show [[Magnamon]]. {|class="wikitable" !Card number !Card name |- |[[BT13-040 (DCG)|BT13-040]] (Parallel 2) |[[Magnamon]] |} =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Digimental miracles.png{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Profile]] |i2=Digimental miracles.jpg{{!}}200px |c2=[[Digimon Collectors]] }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Digimental miracles vpet d3.gif |c1=[[D-3 Toy|D-3]] & [[D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal]] |i2=Digimental miracles vpet spirit.gif |c2=[[D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (international)]], [[D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (international)]], & [[D-Spirit]] }} =Additional Information= {{R}} =See Also= * [[Digimental of Fate]] * [[Crest of Kindness]] * [[Digimental Evolution Chart]] [[Category:Digimentals|M]]

Digimental of Purity.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Digimental of Purity |image=Digimental purity.png |imagesize= |kan={{j2|純真のデジメンタル|Junshin no Dejimentaru}} |dub=Digi-Egg of Sincerity
DigiEgg of Purity''[[Digimon Story: Sunburst]]'' & ''[[Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight]]'' }} The '''Digimental of Purity''' (純真のデジメンタル ''Junshin no Dejimentaru''; Dub: ''Digi-Egg of Sincerity'') is a [[Digimental]] that is used to induce [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]. =Overview= ==Design== Like other [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] [[Digimental]]s, the Digimental of Purity is a bulky object. It resembles two leaves—like those used as shoulder armor by one of the Digimon that it creates, [[Shurimon]]—folded together into an egg shape, placed on top of Shurimon's {{EQO|Kusanagi}}. Its corresponding [[Crest]], the [[Crest of Purity]], is inscribed on its surface. ==Effects== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental#Properties]]''''' The Digimental of Purity contains the elemental attribute of Plant {{ETYK|{{j2|草木|Kusaki}}}}, and it is this elemental power from which Digimon who use this Digimental to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] draw their power.''デジモン大図鑑―デジタルワールド研究白書'' [''Digimon Visual Dictionary: Digital World Research White Paper'']. {{w|Futabasha}}. pp. 215-217. November 15, 2002. ISBN 978-4575163537. [[Armor]] Digimon who have used the Digimental of Purity are granted plant-based abilities, including being able to blend in with nature,''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report012/ Digi-Basics: "Armors" and "Digimentals"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 012|Translation on Wikimon]]) and to conceal themselves within the wind like leaves fluttering, emerging from the enemy's blind spots to land precise attacks.{{refd|Shurimon}} It is common for users of the Digimental of Purity to evolve into [[:Category:Mutation Type|Mutation]] Digimon.{{refd|Frogmon}} ==Possible Armor Evolutions== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental Evolution Chart]]''''' {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;font-size:90%;" |- !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution |- | *[[Aquilamon]]''[[Digimon Story]]'' *[[Fairimon]]{{rfc|Bo|711}} *'''[[Hawkmon]]'''{{rfe|ZT|14|Shurimon's Hurricane}}''[[D-3 Version 2]]'' * Any [[Adult]] or [[Armor]] Digimon with the ability "Grappling"{{rfc|Bo|711}} !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Shurimon.jpg|150px]]
[[Shurimon]] | * [[V-mon]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Yaksamon.jpg|150px]]

[[Nohemon]] | * [[Tailmon]] * [[Tailmon (X-Antibody)]]{{rfc|Bo|328}} * [[Monodramon]]{{rfc|St|649}} !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Kabukimon.jpg|150px]]
[[Kabukimon]] |- | * [[Armadimon]]''[[D-3 Version 3]]'' !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Frogmon.jpg|150px]]

[[Cheer Galmon]] |} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}=== [[Image:Digimental of Purity.jpg|thumb|right|The Digimental of Purity.]] The Digimental of Purity is part of the second wave of three [[Digimental]]s that the [[Four Holy Beasts]] make available to the [[Chosen Child#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Chosen Children]] to access, once they learn that they have the capacity to use more than one Digimental due to their [[D-Terminal]]s. They have it buried in the vicinity of [[Digitamamon]]'s restaurant.{{rfe|ZT|37|Gigantic Ultimate, Qinglongmon}} When Digitamamon attacks [[Tachikawa Mimi]] while under the control of an [[Evil Spiral]], [[Inoue Miyako]] snaps and launches into a tirade against him, bluntly speaking her mind about everything she has observed and felt through the entire day about both Digitamamon and her own nature. The Digimental of Purity reacts to what [[Hida Iori]] describes as the innocent cry of her heart, and emerges from the ground in front of Miyako. She uses it to [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] [[Hawkmon (Adventure)|Hawkmon]] into [[Shurimon]].{{rfe|ZT|14|Shurimon's Hurricane}} ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown}}=== In the drama CD's ''omake'', [[Tachikawa Mimi]] uses the Digimental of Purity to Armor Evolve [[Palmon (Adventure)|Palmon]] into [[Cheer Galmon]].[[Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown|''Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown'': "Surging Armor Evolution"'']] ==Manga== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Purity is an obtainable item of the Armor category. Equipping it on a Digimon has three possible effects: * If equipped by a [[Hawkmon]], it teaches Hawkmon the {{ATO|Kusanagi}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique the next time that a battle is won. After this point, as long as the Digimental is equipped, Hawkmon can use {{ATO|Kusanagi}} to briefly Armor Evolve into [[Shurimon]], who performs the technique before immediately reverting back into Hawkmon. * It increases the equipper's resistance to Devilish techniques. * Like any other [[Digimental]], having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Purity must be rebuilt by taking three Purity Fragments—which can be found in the Water Cave dungeon on [[Ichijouji Ken]]'s side, and the Sea Cave 2, Flame Cave 2, Firebreathing Cave 2 and Death Crevasse dungeons on [[Akiyama Ryo]]'s side—to [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop. Alternatively, the player may receive it by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: D-1 Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Purity is an obtainable item of the [[Digimental]] category. Equipping it on a Digimon has two possible effects: * If equipped by a [[Hawkmon]], it grants access to the {{ATO|Kusanagi}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique in Hawkmon's sixth technique slot. When used, Hawkmon briefly Armor Evolves into [[Shurimon]], who performs {{ATO|Kusanagi}} before immediately reverting back into Hawkmon. * Like any other Digimental, having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Purity can be purchased from [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop after purchasing the shop's first upgrade from [[Clockmon]], or received by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon World: Digital Card Arena}}=== The Digimental of Purity is an unlockable which is separately acquired for one Partner Card. Once it has been acquired, the player is given the option to, when playing the following Partner Card, use the Digimental to [[Evolution/Video Games#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Armor Evolve]] it free of charge: * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Shurimon]]
[[Digimental#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Unlock order slot: Digimental #1]] ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story}}=== The Digimental of Purity is a key item. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following evolutions: * [[Aquilamon]] → [[Shurimon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 27 or higher * [[Wizarmon]] → [[Ponchomon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 27 or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so these Digimon can be evolved into any number of times. The Digimental is a reward for completing Onegai Quest #25, '''Go Get the DM of Purity''' {{ETYK|じゅんしんのＤＭをとりにいく}} (Dub: ''DM of Sincerity''), in which an [[Aquilamon]] from the player's Farm asks them to retrieve it from Chrome Mine. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Sunburst}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight}}=== The Digimental of Purity is a key item. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following [[Evolution/Video Games#Digimon Story:Sunburst & Moonlight|Armor Evolutions]] in in the Special Evolution menu of the DigiLab: * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Shurimon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 22 or higher; Insect/Plant EXP is 800 or higher * [[Patamon]] → [[Ponchomon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 23 or higher; Dark EXP is 2000 or higher * [[Tailmon]] → [[Kabukimon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 31 or higher; Insect/Plant EXP is 2000 or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so these Digimon can be evolved into any number of times. It is found in the Chaos Area of Limit Valley. ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== Appears as a collectable card. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-3 Toy|D-3}}=== The Digimental of Purity is a collectible item that the player uses in battle to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] their Digimon. All six playable Digimon are capable of using it: * [[V-mon]] → [[Yaksamon]] * [[Wormmon]] → [[Nohemon]] * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Shurimon]] * [[Tailmon]] → [[Kabukimon]] * [[Armadimon]] → [[Frogmon]] * [[Patamon]] → [[Ponchomon]] To obtain the Digimental of Purity, the player must first collect three fragments of it via the Search function. Its fragments are available in the following Areas:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DThreeEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * [[D-3 Version 1]]: Area 1 * [[D-3 Version 2]]: ''(TBA)'' * [[D-3 Version 3]]: Areas 1 and 2 Alternatively, the player may receive it via trade with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal}}=== Digimentals of Purity may be created through the fusion of the following combinations of either Digimon from the Reference Book, or other Digimentals:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DTermEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * Digimon: [[Lilimon]] + [[Silphymon]] * Digimon: [[Rosemon]] + [[Valkyrimon]] * Digimentals: [[Digimental of Love]] + [[Digimental of Light]] + [[Digimental of Kindness]] Digimentals of Purity may be traded with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (International)}}=== The Digi-Egg of Sincerity (Digimental of Purity) is a collectible item that is used in battle to Armor Digivolve ([[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]]) [[Hawkmon]] into [[Shurimon]]. Unlike the Japanese D-3 line, none of the other playable Digimon may use it. Using the Digi-Egg of Sincerity does not incur a D-Power point cost and can be done without limitation, but if used, there is a small chance that it may break after a battle, regardless of its outcome, requiring it to be re-obtained. It is obtained by random chance in "Get Digi-Eggs" map events. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (International)}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Spirit}}=== ==={{hdr|D-3 Ver. 15th}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Digimental_of_purity |s1=HC }} =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Digimental purity.png{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Profile]] |i2=Digimental purity.jpg{{!}}200px |c2=[[Digimon Collectors]] }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Digimental purity vpet d3.gif |c1=[[D-3 Toy|D-3]] & [[D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal]] |i2=Digimental purity vpet spirit.gif |c2=[[D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (international)]], [[D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (international)]], & [[D-Spirit]] }} =Additional Information= {{R}} ==See also== * [[Crest of Purity]] * [[Digimental Evolution Chart]] [[Category:Digimentals|P]]

Digimental of Sincerity.wikitext:
{{Item |name=Digimental of Sincerity{{note|See [[Crest of Sincerity#Additional Information|notes]] on [[Crest of Sincerity]] for commentary on meaning and translation of the word {{ETYK|誠実}} ''seijitsu''.|n=MEANING}} |image=Digimental faith.png |imagesize= |kan={{j2|誠実のデジメンタル|Seijitsu no Dejimentaru}} |dub=Digi-Egg of Reliability
Digi-Egg of Sincerity''[[Digimon World 3]]''
DigiEgg of Honesty''[[Digimon Story: Sunburst]]'' & ''[[Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight]]'' }} The '''Digimental of Sincerity''' (誠実のデジメンタル ''Seijitsu no Dejimentaru'';{{note|n=MEANING}} Dub: ''Digi-Egg of Reliability'') is a [[Digimental]] that is used to induce [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]]. =Overview= ==Design== Like other [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] [[Digimental]]s, the Digimental of Sincerity is a loosely egg-shaped object. It is thinner than other Digimentals, and is shaped like and patterned after the body armor of one of the Digimon created by using it, [[Submarimon]], albeit stripped of almost all body parts and features. Its corresponding [[Crest]], the [[Crest of Sincerity]], is inscribed on its surface. ==Effects== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental#Properties]]''''' The Digimental of Sincerity contains the elemental attribute of Water {{ETYK|{{j2|水|Mizu}}}}, and it is this elemental power from which Digimon who use this Digimental to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] draw their power.''デジモン大図鑑―デジタルワールド研究白書'' [''Digimon Visual Dictionary: Digital World Research White Paper'']. {{w|Futabasha}}. pp. 215-217. November 15, 2002. ISBN 978-4575163537. Armor Digimon who have used the Digimental of Sincerity are granted water-based abilities and demonstrate their true power underwater.{{refd|Submarimon|sabmarimon}} They are capable of swimming freely and specialize in underwater combat''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report012/ Digi-Basics: "Armors" and "Digimentals"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 012|Translation on Wikimon]]) and activities.{{refd|Depthmon}} ==Possible Armor Evolutions== : ''See also: '''[[Digimental Evolution Chart]]''''' {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;font-size:90%;" |- !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Base Digimon !style="background: #262626; width:150px;"| Armor Evolution |- | * '''[[Armadimon]]'''{{rfe|ZT|16|Submarimon's Escape from The Bottom of the Sea}}''[[D-3 Version 3]]'' * [[Seahomon]]{{ref|''[[Digimon Collectors]]''}} !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Submarimon.jpg|150px]]

[[Urashimamon]] |} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}=== [[File:Digimental of Sincerity.jpg|right|thumb|The Digimental of Sincerity.]] The Digimental of Sincerity is part of the second wave of three [[Digimental]]s that the [[Four Holy Beasts]] make available to the [[Chosen Child#Digimon Adventure original continuity|Chosen Children]] to access, once they learn that they have the capacity to use more than one Digimental due to their [[D-Terminal]]s. They have it placed in a coastal oil rig.{{rfe|ZT|37|Gigantic Ultimate, Qinglongmon}} The Digimental of Sincerity is the final regular Digimental that any of the Chosen Children obtain, and even before they do, [[Hida Iori]] excitedly anticipates that it will be his. They track it to the oil rig with their [[D-3]]s, and while they are trapped in the oil rig by an [[Evil Spiral]]-possessed [[Mega Seadramon]], [[Takaishi Takeru]] digs it out in Iori's absence and the group confirms that none of them are able to take it. Once Iori returns with [[Kido Jo]] and [[Gomamon (Adventure)|Ikkakumon]], he is initially reluctant to take it, due to his actions trapping the others in the oil rig and his guilt over lying to get Jo's help, which he sees as unworthy of the [[Crest of Sincerity]]. Jo talks him through this by reflecting on the morality of lying for the sake of helping others, and Iori takes the Digimental and uses it to [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] [[Armadimon (Adventure)|Armadimon]] into [[Submarimon]].{{rfe|ZT|16|Submarimon's Escape from The Bottom of the Sea}} ==Manga== ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown}}=== In the drama CD's ''omake'', [[Kido Jo]] uses the Digimental of Sincerity to [[Evolution/Anime#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] [[Gomamon (Adventure)|Gomamon]] into [[Urashimamon]].[[Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown|''Digimon Adventure 02: Armor Evolution to the Unknown'': "Surging Armor Evolution"'']] ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: Tag Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Sincerity is an obtainable item of the Armor category. Equipping it on a Digimon has three possible effects: * If equipped by a [[Armadimon]], it teaches Armadimon the {{ATO|Submarine Attack}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique the next time that a battle is won. After this point, as long as the Digimental is equipped, Armadimon can use {{ATO|Submarine Attack}} to briefly Armor Evolve into [[Submarimon]], who performs the technique before immediately reverting back into Armadimon. * It increases the equipper's resistance to Water techniques. * Like any other [[Digimental]], having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Sincerity must be rebuilt by taking three Sincerity Fragments—which can be found in the Sea Cave 2, Flame Cave 2, Firebreathing Cave 2 and Death Crevasse dungeons, all on [[Akiyama Ryo]]'s side—to [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop. Alternatively, the player may receive it by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: D-1 Tamers}}=== The Digimental of Sincerity is an obtainable item of the [[Digimental]] category. Equipping it on a Digimon has two possible effects: * If equipped by an [[Armadimon]], it grants access to the {{ATO|Submarine Attack}} [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolution]] technique in Armadimon's sixth technique slot. When used, Armadimon briefly Armor Evolves into [[Submarimon]], who performs {{ATO|Submarine Attack}} before immediately reverting back into Armadimon. * Like any other Digimental, having it equipped on Digimon will, depending on their [[Digitama]] line, cause them to evolve into a different [[Adult]], [[Perfect]] or [[Ultimate]] Digimon than they would have without a Digimental equipped. The Digimental of Sincerity can be purchased from [[Centalmon]]'s Digimental Shop after purchasing the shop's first upgrade from [[Clockmon]], or received by trading with compatible video and LCD games. ==={{hdr|Digimon World: Digital Card Arena}}=== The Digimental of Sincerity is an unlockable which is separately acquired for two Partner Cards. Once it has been acquired, the player is given the option to, when playing any of the following Partner Cards, use the Digimental to Armor Evolve them free of charge: * [[Armadimon]] → [[Submarimon]]
[[Digimental#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Unlock order slot: Digimental #2]] * [[Tailmon]] → [[Tylomon]]
[[Digimental#Digimon World: Digital Card Arena|Unlock order slot: Digimental #2]] ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World 3}}=== The Digimental of Sincerity is a key item, which can be used at certain pier points to summon a [[Submarimon]] in order to carry [[Atsushi|the player]] through underwater routes. The Digimental is found inside the [[Sincerity Altar]] in [[Bios Swamp]]. To get inside and get the Digimental, the player must blow a hole in the back of the Altar using a TNT Chip, which is obtained by completing a fetch quest involving [[Sepikmon]] at the [[Tranquil Swamp]] and [[Baromon]] at [[Protocol Ruins]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Savers: Another Mission}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Sunburst}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight}}=== The Digimental of Sincerity is a key item. Having it is one of the evolution requirements to perform the following Armor Evolution in in the Special Evolution menu of the DigiLab: * [[Tailmon]] → [[Tylomon]]
Other evolution requirements: Level is 27 or higher; Aquan EXP is 2200 or higher It is not consumed in the evolution, so Tylomon can be evolved into any number of times. The Digimental is found in the Chaos Area between Palette Amazon and Loop Swamp. ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== Appears as a collectable card. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-3 Toy|D-3}}=== The Digimental of Sincerity is a collectible item that the player uses in battle to [[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]] their Digimon. All six playable Digimon are capable of using it: * [[V-mon]] → [[Depthmon]] * [[Wormmon]] → [[Archelomon]] * [[Hawkmon]] → [[Orcamon]] * [[Tailmon]] → [[Tylomon]] * [[Armadimon]] → [[Submarimon]] * [[Patamon]] → [[Mantaraymon]] To obtain the Digimental of Sincerity, the player must first collect three fragments of it via the Search function. Its fragments are available in the following Areas:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DThreeEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * [[D-3 Version 1]]: Area 2 * [[D-3 Version 2]]: Area 2 * [[D-3 Version 3]]: Area 3 Alternatively, the player may receive it via trade with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal}}=== Digimentals of Sincerity may be created through the fusion of the following combinations of either Digimon from the Reference Book, or other Digimentals:[http://lcd.withthewill.net/DTermEgg "Digimentals"]. ''File Island''. September 7, 2016. * Digimon: [[Zudomon]] + [[Shakkoumon]] * Digimon: [[Plesiomon]] + [[Vikemon]] * Digimentals: [[Digimental of Friendship]] + [[Digimental of Knowledge]] + [[Digimental of Light]] Digimentals of Sincerity may be traded with compatible games and devices. ==={{hdr|D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (International)}}=== The Digi-Egg of Reliability (Digimental of Sincerity) is a collectible item that is used in battle to Armor Digivolve ([[Evolution#Armor Evolution|Armor Evolve]]) Armadillomon ([[Armadimon]]) into [[Submarimon]]. Unlike the Japanese D-3 line, none of the other playable Digimon may use it. Using the Digi-Egg of Reliability does not incur a D-Power point cost and can be done without limitation, but if used, there is a small chance that it may break after a battle, regardless of its outcome, requiring it to be re-obtained. It is obtained by random chance in "Get Digi-Eggs" map events. ==={{hdr|D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (International)}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Spirit}}=== ==={{hdr|D-3 Ver. 15th}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Digimental_of_sincerity |s1=HC }} =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Digimental faith.png{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Profile]] |i2=Digimental reliability.jpg{{!}}200px |c2=[[Digimon Collectors]] }} |2=game |t2= {{IG |w=128 |h=128 |i1=DW3 Digimental sincerity.png |c1=[[Digimon World 3]] }} }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Digimental sincerity vpet d3.gif |c1=[[D-3 Toy|D-3]] & [[D-Terminal Toy|D-Terminal]] |i2=Digimental sincerity vpet spirit.gif |c2=[[D-3 Digivice Toy (International)|D-3 Digivice (international)]], [[D-Terminal Toy (International)|D-Terminal (international)]], & [[D-Spirit]] }} =Additional Information= {{R}} ==See also== * [[Crest of Sincerity]] * [[Digimental Evolution Chart]] [[Category:Digimentals|S]]

Digimon.wikitext:
'''Digimon''' (デジモン ''Dejimon'') can refer to: 

* '''[[Digimon (franchise)]]''': Intellectual property that began as a series of Virtual Pets centered around the creatures of the same name.
* '''[[Digimon (species)]]''': Mysterious lifeforms that are the main focus of the eponymous Digimon franchise.

[[Category:Disambiguation pages]]

Digimon (species).wikitext:
{{Dis|For the intellectual property, see [[Digimon (franchise)]]}}
{{UC}}
[[File:Modokibetamon seekers.jpg|frameless|right|A 'hololized' Modoki Betamon at Abadin Electronics Corp.]]
'''Digimon''' (デジモン, ''Dejimon''), short for '''Digital Monster''' (デジタルモンスター, ''Dejitaru Monsutā''), are mysterious lifeforms that are the main focus of the eponymous [[Digimon|''Digimon'' franchise]]. The vast majority of Digimon reside within the [[Digital World]], a region of pseudo-cyberspace that was created on the [[Network]], and originated from the [[Net Ocean]], the birthplace of life within the Digital World. There are many different [[species]] of Digimon, some of which have their own [[subspecies]], as well as unconfirmed and new generations that have yet to be revealed.

Digimon have been depicted in many different continuities, but some of the settings have differed slightly in each work. That is because interpretations of Digimon differ from creator to creator. For instance, in ''[[Digimon Adventure]]'', Digimon are depicted as creatures that have existed from ancient times, and they are half bodies of “Spirits” that have bonds with humans. On the other hand, in LCD games ''[[Digital Monster]]'' and ''[[Digimon Tamers]]'', Digimon are distinctive entities that self-evolve, and are seen as AI programs in the Digital World created by humans.

In the setting of ''[[Digimon Survive]]'', due to existing without a connection to digital data, Digimon are instead referred to by humans as [[Kemonogami]], while they have no name for themselves. However, a year after the ending of the Moral and Truth routes, their world is sustained through the belief created in the internet to replace their previous religion, thus through digital data, which causes them to be rechristened by [[Minase Akiharu]] as Digital Monsters, or Digimon for short, and their previously-unnamed world as the Digital World.

==Origins==
One day, a virus possessing [[artificial intelligence]] spread onto computers throughout the world. This virus evolved in a way similar to living organisms, changing its appearance and nature on the Network, becoming the "digital lifeforms" that came to be known as "Digital Monsters", which were first discovered on there in 1997. A [[hacker|scientist]] interested in this gave the virus, which was only a program, a skeleton called {{w|Wire-frame model|wireframe}}, and a skin called {{w|Texture mapping|texture}}. Thus the Digimon were born as digital creatures with graphics.

==Characteristics==
===Evolution===
:''See [[Evolution]]''

Digimon evolve with varying conditions. Methods generally being either age, collecting data, winning battles, or drawing energy off a human. Digimon have six "natural" stages of evolution, as well as others obtained through artificial help. Due to the influence of the [[Dark Area]], the evolutionary trees of any Digimon have been altered, enabling them to evolve into many different forms.

While the anime generally presents Digimon as having conceptually "matching" evolution lines, in other aspects of the franchise, Digimon also evolve depending on how they're raised - including what time they're awake, how much they've fought, how much they've trained, how much they've eaten, and other factors. An [[Agumon]] who has been raised properly will evolve into the powerful [[Greymon]], while one who has been starved to death will evolve into the weak and disgusting [[Numemon]]. Evolution can be temporary or permanent depending on the circumstances. Generally in the anime, Digimon evolve for a short period of time before reverting to smaller, cuter forms. In the games, Digimon usually retain their larger forms after evolving.

===Nomenclature===
===Digicore===
:''Main article: [[Digicore]]''
A Digicore is a program said to be found at the center of a Digimon's body.

===Gender===
Digimon don´t have physical sexual organs, as they are made of data, they reproduce not through sexual reproduction but through a universal system of data recycling, copying/pasting, and data merging. Even though these words are oftentimes used as synonyms, gender and sex aren't the same. Gender being something more psychological, how one identifies and acts, they do have physical representation of male/female archetypes as of secondary sex characteristics such as [[Lady Devimon]] or [[Angemon]]. 
 
The creators of the games and anime have said that many Digimon may follow a male or female archetype. 

In [[Digimon Championship]] it is stated "Digimon are not differentiated between male and female, and do not mate". In [[Digimon Tamers]], [[Renamon (Tamers)|Renamon]] clearly says "Digimon don't have sex/gender", the word Seibetsu (性別) means "gender" or "sex", but in this context, this could only mean "sex" , not "gender". 

Digimon of the same species can act as female, male or other. As one example, the [[Piyomon]] from [[Piyomon (Adventure)|Adventure]] is identified as female, while the one from [[Piyomon (Savers)|Savers]] is identified as male. They have different evolutionary routes (Adventure's evolving to [[Birdramon]], and Savers' evolving to [[Aquilamon]]) but both of them evolve into [[Garudamon]], a Digimon with masculine characteristics. As another example, in [[Digimon World 4]] there is an [[Ofanimon]] that referred to himself as male.

Another example is the Renamon from Tamers who self-identified as genderless in the beginning of the series, and later on, as female. [[Chiaki J. Konaka]] (Digimon Tamers' screenwriter) stated "Eventually, Renamon would become [[Sakuyamon]], a very feminine digimon. I thought that I would start Renamon out as a "genderless" digimon, and have that be a large part of its personality." On the other hand, the [[Renamon (Another Mission)|Renamon]] from [[Digimon Savers: Another Mission]] is treated as male, this is so even when he evolves into forms with female secondary sex characteristics like [[Lilithmon]]. 

In the anime, [[Jijimon]] and [[Babamon]] state they are married. This could happen as in the Digimon world, Digimon can live and behave like couples even so they do not have sexual differences.

=Xros Unit Substitute Digimon=
These Digimon can be used as a substitute for certain Xros Unit in a DigiXros:
* [[Atlur Ballistamon]]{{rfc|BT19|051 (DCG)}} (Can act as Ballistamon for a DigiXros.)
* [[Omega Shoutmon]]{{rfc|BT11|015 (DCG)}} (Can act as Shoutmon for a DigiXros.)
* [[Raptor Sparrowmon]]{{rfc|BT19|061 (DCG)}} (Can act as Sparrowmon for a DigiXros.)
* [[Scumon]] (Can substitute for any Xros Unit in a DigiXros.)<ref group="N" name="ScuXros">[[BT11-043 (DCG)]] can turn [[BT10-111 (DCG)]] into a Scumon whose effect can treat it as a substitute for any DigiXros.</ref>
* [[Shooting Starmon]]{{rfc|BT19|035 (DCG)}} (Can act as Starmons for a DigiXros.)
* [[Shoutmon (King Ver.)]]{{rfc|BT10|111 (DCG)}} (Can substitute for any Xros Unit in a DigiXros.)
* [[Yaeger Dorulumon]]{{rfc|BT19|038 (DCG)}} (Can act as Dorulumon for a DigiXros.)

The '''Digimon Pendulum''' (デジモンペンデュラム ''Dejimon Pendyuramu''), common abbreviation "PenOG", is the second in the series of the [[List of Virtual Pets|Digimon Virtual Pet]] line. Each version has a roster of 20 Digimon and additional functions such as the Pendulum training and Jogressing. It evolves through 6 stages after hatching from the Digimon egg, [[Digitama]], beginning with the [[Baby I]] Stage and ending in the [[Ultimate]] Stage.

=Set-Up and Game Play=
Once activated it will prompt you for a time to put on the clock. Input the times using the A button to adjust the hour and the B button to adjust the minute, and your egg will hatch shortly. You will see a total of 8 Icons on the border, some different than the V-Pet.

==Scale== 
To view the Digimon's status you will see numerous screens. These include the Age/Weight, the Hunger Status, the Strength Status, the Win Ratio screen, and the Digimon Points (DP) Gauge. 1 Digimon Year = 24 hours. Weight variations will take place in the following way where G = Gigabytes.
* Food +1G
* Protein +2G
* Training -2G
* Battle -4G 

One addition to the Pendulum Series is the Jogress function. You will see a B-J- in one of the menu screens. The '-'s will be substituted with a O or a X depending on your Digimon. The 'B' refers to 'Battle' and the 'J' refers to 'Jogress'. The 'X' means that Battle or Jogress is not possible and the 'O' means that the function can be carried out.

==Meat==
Two options are available here. The top selection is a piece of meat which will increase the Digimon's hunger. The second selection is the Protein Pill, which will increase your Digimon's Strength.

==Training==
This is where your Digimon will train. A successful training session will increase your Digimon's strength naturally. Training will decrease your DP level, and DP is necessary to train. The Pendulum series offers a new Training method where you must shake the Pendulum and a counter will determine the strength of your shots. You will release a series of 5 shots, you will either shoot a single blast or a Super hit (double shot). You will get points for every successful Super Hit and a failed training session if you get no Super Hits. If the training results in a very successful Mega Hit (Super Hits all five rounds), the Strength meter will fill completely.

==Connection==
For the Pendulums there are now two functions for this selection.

* '''Battle''': This will allow you to fight another compatible virtual pet to increase your Win Ratio. The more battles and higher your Win Ratio the more likely it will be you will reach the Perfect Stage. Baby Digimon cannot battle, and for the other Digimon, DP is necessary. You will fight your opponent and shoot up to three shots against your opponent. Best two out of three will win.

* '''Jogress''': If you have two compatible Digimon, you may Jogress allowing one or both of them to evolve to the next level. An Error sign will appear on the screen if there is an incompatibility. Full DP is required to use the Jogress function. 

==Toilet==
This will wipe the screen clear when your Digimon poops approximately every three hours. Your Digimon will become sick if it poops 8 times without having the mess cleaned up.

==Lights== 
Select this function to turn the lights off when your Digimon is ready to sleep. You must do this within 30 minutes (or before the 'Call' light goes out), or it will count as a care mistake and your Digimon will wake up one hour later. A full 8 hours' sleep is necessary to refill the DP Gauge.

==Bandage== 
This is where you provide Medical attention for your Digimon. If he becomes sick (the 'c') above its head use the top selection and if he is dying (the skull), use the bottom selection. Sometimes, your Digimon will refuse the treatment, and you will have to keep trying until it accepts. If your Digimon gets the sickness/dying status too many times or is left untreated, it may die. Your Digimon can still get injured in battle even if it wins.

==The "Call" icon==
When the Call icon is lit up, you have 20 minutes or less to respond before it goes out or you get a care mistake. Your Digimon will only call for attention when its hearts run out or it gets sick or is dying. It will also do this when its time to turn out the lights.

==Other Options==
*'''Sound Option''': To disable the sounds press the A and C buttons at the same time. If the Digimon dies, the Pendulum will beep regardless of whether or not the sound has been turned off.

*'''Pause Option''': While the Pendulums have no pause feature, you can simulate one by bringing up the clock and hitting A and C to 'change the clock' then leave it on with the time blinking. This will keep your Digimon suspended while you tend to other things.

Beelzebumon Color |lang1=Japanese |rdate1=21 Jun 2018 |dim= |op=is the remake of the original Digimon Pendulum Virtual Pet for the 20th Anniversary of the device. It contains all of the previous Digimon from the Pendulum generation [[List of Virtual Pets|Digimon Virtual Pet line]] but not all are obtainable in one device. The Pendulum generation are now divided into two halves and each half is exclusive to a specific device. It maintains most of the features from [[Digital Monster Ver.20th]] such as raising two Digimon at once but also adds a lot more new features such as [[Copymon]] appearing as a partner for each Digimon on screen and a pause option with a freezer. The [[Jogress]] features makes a comeback and is now able to even combine with copied Digimon. Same as the previous 20th, each color variation contain some exclusive Digimon which are unobtainable on different colors. }} =Versions= There are 4 colors of the Digimon Pendulum Ver.20th, each of which has their own exclusive Digimon that can be acquired. Wave 1 consists of the Original Silver Black and the Original Silver Blue colors, while Wave 2 consists of the Dukemon and Beelzebumon colors. Six available eggs are based on the original six versions of the Digimon Pendulum, with version X.5 Digimon included in the same egg groups as the version X.0 Digimon. Additional Digimon that were not featured in the original versions were also added to the Wind Guardians and Virus Buster eggs.
