[[Dokamon]], [[Musimon]], [[Hackmon]], [[Offmon]], and [[Gatchmon]] → [[Poseidomon]], [[Ouranosmon]], [[Hadesmon]], [[Rebootmon]], and [[Gaiamon (Appmon)|Gaiamon]] | [[Digimon Universe Appli Monsters - Chapter 11|''Digimon Universe Appli Monsters'': ''"What Links Us Together!!"]]'' |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Dreamers}}=== In ''Digimon Dreamers'', evolution follows similar rules to the virtual pets. Evolution is one-way and permanent. There is no way for [[Tamer]]s to trigger their partner to evolve at will, although their bond with their Partner Digimon helps to give their Digimon the necessary energy. [[Pulsemon (Dreamers)|Pulsemon]]'s main goal is to evolve, which motivates him both both train hard and to partner up with [[Kodou Ritsu]]. However, his hometown, Asunaro Village, was inflicted with a curse of unknown origin that supposedly stops the Digimon born there from evolving. A side effect of the curse is that those afflicted experience a phenomenon called '''Localized Evolution''' {{ETYK|{{j2|部分進化|Bubunshinka}}}}:[[Pulse-10|''Digimon Dreamers'': "Steal Pulsemon!"]] a form of evolution that appears to only affects random body parts at a time. [[Bokomon]] notes that Localized Evolution is actually a symptom that a Digimon—in his case, Pulsemon—has indeed gained sufficient energy to evolve through his bond with Ritsu, and that the energy is trying to break through the Asunaro Village curse to fully evolve the Digimon. Pulsemon experiences Localized Evolution for the first time in his fight with [[Orgemon]], only his head evolves into that of [[Bulkmon]] at first while the rest of his body remains that of Pulsemon,[[Pulse-05|''Digimon Dreamers'': "The Pulse of Evolution"]] and then random parts of his body continue to spontaneously evolve into those of Bulkmon while previously-evolved ones devolve back into Pulsemon's. Ritsu and Pulsemon manage to briefly evolve Pulsemon into a complete Bulkmon to finish Orgemon off, although it does not last and he reverts to Pulsemon immediately.[[Pulse-06|''Digimon Dreamers'': "Inner Bulkmon"]] Going forward, Pulsemon continues to only be capable of Localized Evolution, although he manages to make resourceful use of it in battle.[[Pulse-07|''Digimon Dreamers'': "Sandiramon in the Shrine"]] Thanks to being overloaded by [[Wizarmon (Dreamers)|Wizarmon's]] electricity, he later breaks out of the curse, fully evolving into Bulkmon as well as awakening his special [[Digicore]].[[Pulse-22|''Digimon Dreamers'': "Wizarmon's Future"]] However, this was also only temporary, and he returned to Pulsemon soon after.[[Pulse-24|''Digimon Dreamers'': "Kodou Ritsu"]] Dr. [[Vademon]] takes an interest in Localized Evolution for the benefit of his research into Super [[Chimairamon]], under the belief that it is a natural ability that can be controlled at will, so [[Espimon]] attempts to capture Pulsemon to sell that information to the doctor. However, once he sees it in action and gets an explanation from Pulsemon and Ritsu, he dismisses it as useless for Dr. Vademon's purposes due to its origin from the curse. He advises Ritsu and Pulsemon to travel to [[Witchelny]] to learn more about the curse.[[Pulse-11|''Digimon Dreamers'': "Spy Digimon Espimon"]] Wizarmon has the potential to evolve into [[Mistymon]][[Pulse-21|''Digimon Dreamers'': "The World of 0 and 1"]] and [[Medieval Dukemon]].[[Pulse-22|''Digimon Dreamers'': "Wizarmon's Future"]] Unlike the other Digimon, [[Bell]] can go back and forth between her [[Child]] and [[Adult]] forms seemingly at will.[[Pulse-25|''Digimon Dreamers'': "Pounding Heart"]] ==={{hdr|Digimon Liberator}}=== ''Digimon Liberator'' follows the rules of the [[Digimon Card Game]] and thus, evolution works in the same way as it does in the DCG while inside a card battle. Outside of battle, members of the Debug Team can evolve their [[Partner Digimon]] through their [[Unique Emblem]]s. When an evolution is initiated, the text "DIGIVOLVE" appears in dark red (regardless of the language of the manga) and the evolving Digimon fades into a double-helix DNA structure, before taking its new form. Afterwards, if in a card battle, its name (in a black box with white letters), numerical level (in a black circle with a white number) and evolution cost (in a yellow box with black letters) appear. These evolutions were shown in ''Liberator'': *[[Kazama Shoto]] vs [[Kinosaki Arisa]]:[[Digimon Liberator - Episode 01|''Digimon Liberator'': "DigitalGate Open"]] **Arisa evolves [[Junkmon]]{{note|While not specified, this Junkmon is likely [[ST19-02 (DCG)|ST19-02]], as it's the Junkmon in [[ST-19|ST-19 Fable Waltz]], itself centered around Arisa.|n=Junkmon}} into [[Tobucatmon]]{{note|While not specified, this Tobucatmon is likely [[ST19-07 (DCG)|ST19-07]], as it's the Tobucatmon in ST-19 Fable Waltz, itself centered around Arisa. Furthermore, both of them possess the {{dcghl|e=Jamming}} effect.|n=Tobucatmon}} **Shoto evolves an unseen Digimon into [[ST18-07 (DCG)|ST18-07]][https://digimoncard.com/digimon_liberator/jp/column/tips03/ ''Battle Coverage 1 VS. Ice Knight''] [[Cockatrimon]] **Arisa responds by evolving another unseen Digimon into [[Ex-Tyranomon]]{{note|While not specified, this Ex-Tyranomon is likely [[ST19-10 (DCG)|ST19-10]], as it's the Ex-Tyranomon in ST-19 Fable Waltz, itself centered around Arisa.|n=Ex-Tyranomon}}, then either the same or another into [[Shin Monzaemon]]{{note|While not specified, this Shin Monzaemon is likely [[ST19-13 (DCG)|ST19-13]], as it's the Shin Monzaemon in ST-19 Fable Waltz, itself centered around Arisa.|n=Shin Monzaemon}}. In all cases, the evolution is not shown on-screen, only the result *[[NPC]] [[Freezing Knight]] vs Shoto: **Freezing Knight evolves his [[EX7-016 (DCG)|EX7-016]] [[Blucomon]] into [[EX7-020 (DCG)|EX7-020]] [[Paledramon]] in the first turn of its battle against Shoto **Shoto evolves his [[ST18-01 (DCG)|ST18-01]] [[Fluffymon]] in the Raising Area into [[ST18-05 (DCG)|ST18-05]] [[Muchomon]], then his [[ST18-04 (DCG)|ST18-04]] [[Pteromon]] into a newly-drawn [[ST18-08 (DCG)|ST18-08]] [[Galemon]][[Digimon Liberator - Episode 02|''Digimon Liberator'': "Out of Control"]] **Freezing Knight evolves his EX7-016 Blucomon in the Raising Area into a EX7-020 Paledramon, then into [[EX7-021 (DCG)|EX7-021]] [[Crys Paledramon]] **In response, Shoto evolves his ST18-08 Galemon in the Battle Area into [[ST18-10 (DCG)|ST18-10]] [[Grand Galemon]], then one of his ST18-05 Muchomon into another ST18-08 Galemon **After attacking with EX7-021 Crys Paledramon, Freezing Knight evolves it into [[EX7-023 (DCG)|EX7-023]] [[Hexeblaumon]] **Shoto evolves his [[Partner Digimon]], ST18-04 [[Pteromon (Liberator)|Pteromon]], into ST18-08 Galemon, then [[ST18-10 (DCG)|ST18-10]] Grand Galemon, all in the Raising Area **Freezing Knight evolved an unknown Digimon in the Raising Area to [[EX7-017 (DCG)|EX7-017]] [[Yuki Agumon]] **Shoto evolved his Partner Grand Galemon into [[ST18-12 (DCG)|ST18-12]] [[Zephagamon]] *Arisa vs [[Violet Inboots]]:[[Digimon Liberator - Episode 04|''Digimon Liberator'': "Fable Waltz"]] **Arisa evolves her [[Kyaromon]] in her Raising Area into Junkmon{{note|n=Junkmon}} and Tobucatmon{{note|n=Tobucatmon}} **Violet evolves her [[Caprimon]]{{note|While not specified, this Caprimon is likely [[EX7-005 (DCG)|EX7-005]], as it's the only Caprimon that can gain Memory and supports the [[Three Musketeers]].|n=Caprimon}} in her Raising Area into [[Toy Agumon]]{{note|While not specified, this Toy Agumon is likely [[EX7-008 (DCG)|EX7-008]], as it's the only Toy Agumon that supports the Three Musketeers.|n=Toyagumon}} **After attacking, Arisa evolvs Tobucatmon into Pandamon and Shin Monzaemon **Violet moves her Toy Agumon out of her Raising Area and evolves it into [[Tankmon]]{{note|While not specified, this Tankmon is likely [[EX7-043 (DCG)|EX7-043]], as it's the only Tankmon with a {{dcghl|e=Revert 1}} effect with a cost of returning three cards from hand to deck and that supports the Three Musketeers.|n=Tankmon}}. After resolving its effect, she uses her [[Revolmon|Revolmon's]]{{note|While not specified, this Revolmon is likely [[BT6-060 (DCG)|BT6-060]], as both effects Violet uses are found in it and no other Revolmon card.|n=Revolmon}} effect to Warp Evolve it into [[EX7-013 (DCG)|EX7-013]] [[Magna Kidmon]] with a cost of 6 **Arisa evolves her [[Shoemon]]{{note|While not specified, this Shoemon is likely [[EX7-024 (DCG)|EX7-024]], as it's the only Shoemon that reduces its evolution cost if evolving into a Puppet Digimon.|n=Shoemon}} into [[Shoeshoemon]]{{note|While not specified, this Shoeshoemon is likely [[EX7-025 (DCG)|EX7-025]], as it's the only Shoeshoemon that can play a Tamer Card at no cost upon evolving.|n=Shoeshoemon}}. She then evolves the Kyaromon in her Raising Area into another Shoemon and her Shoeshoemon into [[EX7-027 (DCG)|EX7-027]] [[Chaperomon]] **Violet evolved her Caprimon{{note|n=Caprimon}} in her Raising Area into Toy Agumon, then her Tankmon into [[Megadramon]]{{note|While not specified, this Megadramon is likely [[EX7-011 (DCG)|EX7-011]], as it's the only Megadramon card that places an Option Card as an evolution source.|n=Megadramon}} and [[EX7-048 (DCG)|EX7-048]] [[Gundramon]] ==={{hdr|Digimon ReCollection}}=== Due to the setting of ''ReCollection'', in which all properties of Digimon existed as in the real world, every rule of any previous setting could be active in it. [[Nozomi Mayu]] has been shown to evolve his [[Partner Digimon]], [[Agumon (ReCollection)|Agumon]], into his [[Adult]] form at will, but evolving to [[Perfect]] required a [[Card Slash]] as it would in ''[[Digimon Tamers]]''. During Agumon's evolution to [[Geo Greymon]], he went through an anime-style evolution sequence, with his name (in roman capital letters, "AGUMON") shown in the background. Unlike in the anime, this is shown to be happening in reality rather than stock footage, as said name is also shown behind Mayu. This is not shown on any other evolution. Both evolutions from the protagonist also show bits of data flying upwards. Other Digimon have also been shown evolving: *The [[Greymon]] Mayu and [[Geo Greymon]] fought evolved into [[Metal Greymon]] in response to Geo Greymon's own evolution into [[Rize Greymon]]. After being defeated, it became a [[Digivice]]. *[[Keramon (ReCollection)|Keramon's]] resentment over being abandoned and forgotten by humans triggered its evolution into [[Chrysalimon]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Paradox}}=== [[Wanya]] is shown evolving whenever he's overwhelmed by strong emotions. However, evolving in anger causes him to become a berserker in an unusual and unstable evolution that leaves him as a wireframe of his evolved stage, [[Dark Tyranomon]], though this can be stabilized and completed by Reiji. When a Digimon evolves, it first appears as a wireframe of its evolved stage, before being covered in its textures, similar to ''[[Digimon World]]''. If an evolution is unstable, said textures won't form until it is stabilized. ==={{hdr|Digimon Knuckles}}=== As explained by [[Gankoomon (Knuckles)|Gankoomon]], a Digimon's basic instinct is to battle, win, and evolve. Only the ones with the most experience in battle can evolve into the [[Perfect]] and [[Ultimate]] [[level]]s. No actual evolutions have been seen so far. {| |[[File:Digimon Knuckles - Evolution explanation.png|thumb|right|Gankoomon's explanation of evolution, showing a [[V-dramon]] evolving into [[Aero V-dramon]] and [[Ulforce V-dramon]]]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Seekers ~Witch of the Crossroads~‎}}=== Evolution presumably works the same way as it does in other ''[[Digimon Seekers]]'' material. Through his bond with his [[Tamer]], [[Xu Yulin]], [[Ryudamon (Seekers)|Ryudamon]] is shown to evolve straight into his [[Perfect]] form, [[Hisyaryumon]], with his [[Adult]] form, [[Ginryumon]], only shown during the evolution. When an evolution happens, the evolving Digimon glows then creates a massive pillar of light with the [[Digimoji]] {{Dmoji|a=e|d|i|g|i|v|o|l|u|t|i|o|n}} (DIGIVOLUTION) written high on it (this is consistent with other Seekers material, that also refer to evolution using the English dub term). The Digimon is shown in skipped intermediate stages inside the pillar, then takes its final form once the pillar fades away. ==Novel== ==={{hdr|Digimon Seekers}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Liberator (Web Novel)|Digimon Liberator}}=== ''Digimon Liberator'' follows the rules of the [[Digimon Card Game]] and thus, evolution works in the same way as it does in the DCG while inside a card battle. So far, it's unknown if evolution can happen outside of one, or how it would work if it can. When an evolution is initiated, the evolving Digimon is enveloped in bright light, and transformed into particles of emerald green. These particles then reassemble into its new form. Monsters]]''''' ==={{hdr|Digimon Survive}}=== :''Main article: '''[[Evolution/Video Games#Digimon Survive]]''''' ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster}}=== The concept of Digimon evolution was introduced in the original [[Digital Monster]] virtual pet series. All Digimon undergo evolution through up to five Evolution Stages, ending in [[Perfect]]. What a Digimon evolves into, and whether it is able to evolve, depends on several different criteria based on Evolution Stage: * '''Baby I to Baby II:''' Automatically occurs after approximately 60 minutes of real time have passed since the Baby I Digimon hatched. * '''Baby II to Child:''' Each Baby II Digimon can evolve into any of the device's Child Digimon depending on how many care mistakes have been made in raising it. Evolving into Child generally occurs within 20 and 44 hours after the Baby II Digimon has evolved from Baby I. * '''Child to Adult:''' Each Child Digimon can evolve into one of four Adult Digimon, depending on four factors: number of care mistakes made, how many times it have been given training, how often it has been overfed, and how many times its sleep has been interrupted. If the Child Digimon has sustained 20+ care mistakes or injuries, it will not evolve into Adult. Evolving into Adult generally occurs within 20 and 44 hours after the Child Digimon has evolved from Baby II. All models of the Digital Monster feature one "dud" Adult Digimon that is evolved into when the Digimon does not meet the criteria of the other Adult evolution options (respectively: [[Numemon]], [[Vegimon]], [[Scumon]], [[Nanimon]], and [[Raremon]]). * '''Adult to Perfect:''' All Adult Digimon are bound by the same requirements to evolve to Perfect: having fought a minimum of 15 battles, with a minimum win ratio of 80%. Each Adult can only evolve into one particular Perfect. Evolving into Perfect is not guaranteed; if it does occur, it generally occurs within 44 and 124 hours after the Adult Digimon has evolved from Child. Unlike later virtual pets and other ''Digimon'' products, the Digimon that can be evolved into are generally rather random and have no real link to each other (similarities in species or element, etc.) ==={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum}}=== The [[Digimon Pendulum]] virtual pet series introduces three important new evolution concepts, all of which became standard for both subsequent virtual pets and the franchise as a whole: the [[Ultimate]] Evolution Stage, Jogress Evolution, and [[Field]]s. Each version of the Digimon Pendulum is themed around one Field, so almost all featured Digimon within each have some elemental or species trait in common. Evolution requirements are otherwise similar to those of the Digital Monster series. * '''Baby I to Baby II:''' Automatically occurs after approximately 60 minutes of real time have passed since the Baby I Digimon hatched. * '''Baby II to Child:''' Each Baby II Digimon can evolve into any of the device's Child Digimon depending on how many care mistakes have been made in raising it. Evolving into Child generally occurs within 6 and 12 hours after the Baby II Digimon has evolved from Baby I. * '''Child to Adult:''' Each Child Digimon can evolve into one of several Adult Digimon, depending on two factors: number of care mistakes made, and how many times it have been given training. If the Child Digimon has sustained 20+ care mistakes, illnesses or injuries, it will not evolve into Adult. Evolving into Adult generally occurs within 20 and 32 hours after the Child Digimon has evolved from Baby II. * '''Adult to Perfect:''' Adult Digimon become able to evolve into one particular Perfect Digimon if they meet a minimum set of criteria: minimum win ratio of 40%, particpating in a minimum number of battles, being given training a minimum number of times, and making as few care mistakes as possible. Higher win ratios improve the odds of being able to evolve to Perfect. Evolving to Perfect is not guaranteed; if it does occur, it generally occurs within 60 and 76 hours after the Adult Digimon has evolved from Child.
Alternatively, Digimon may undergo Jogress Evolution to evolve into certain Perfect Digimon. * '''Perfect to Ultimate:''' Certain Perfect Digimon become able to evolve into Ultimate Digimon if they meet a minimum set of criteria: minimum win ratio of 60%, and no care mistakes have been made. Evolving to Ultimate is not guaranteed; if it does occur, it generally occurs within 68 and 86 hours after the Perfect Digimon has evolved from Adult.
Alternatively, Digimon may undergo Jogress Evolution to evolve into certain Ultimate Digimon. In the Digimon Pendulum, Jogress Evolution works based on the participating Digimon's [[attribute]]s. Jogressing together two Digimon of a particular combination of attributes will result in each participating Digimon evolving into an appropriate Digimon from their respective virtual pet's roster. The actual identity of the particpating Digimon generally does not matter, with one exception: Jogressing [[War Greymon]] and [[Metal Garurumon]] in the [[Digimon Pendulum ZERO]] will form [[Omegamon]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum Progress}}=== The [[Digimon Pendulum Progress]] introduces time of day as a determiner of what a Digimon will evolve into: training the Digimon in morning, mid-day or evening results in different evolutions. Evolution lines are much freer, and excluding cases where Jogress Evolution is required, any Digimon on the device can evolve into any other Digimon of the next Evolution Stage as long as it meets the evolution criteria. * '''Baby I to Baby II:''' Automatically occurs after approximately 60 minutes of real time have passed since the Baby I Digimon hatched. * '''Baby II to Child:''' Each Baby II Digimon can evolve into any of the device's Child Digimon depending on what time of day it is trained in. * '''Child to Adult:''' Each Child Digimon can evolve into any of the device's Adult Digimon, depending on two factors: what time of day it is trained in, and how many care mistakes have been made in raising it. * '''Adult to Perfect:''' Each Adult Digimon can evolve into any of the device's Perfect Digimon (excluding Jogress exclusives), depending on three factors: what time of day it is trained in, how many care mistakes have been made in raising it, and how many battles it has participated in.
Alternatively, Digimon may undergo Jogress Evolution to evolve into certain Perfect Digimon. * '''Perfect to Ultimate:''' Each Perfect Digimon can evolve into any of the device's Ultimate Digimon (excluding Jogress exclusives) if it meets their respective combinations of these minimum requirements: what time of day it is trained in, how many care mistakes have been made in raising it, how many times its sleep has been disturbed, how many battles it has won, and its battle win ratio.
Alternatively, Digimon may undergo Jogress Evolution to evolve into certain Perfect Digimon. The rules of Jogress Evolution are somewhat different from the Digimon Pendulum. This time, players are required to Jogress together specific species of Digimon to get a particular Digimon, rather than accepting any Digimon of a given attribute. Many of the Digimon required come not only from other models of Pendulum Progress, but even from the [[D-Scanner Toy|D-Scanner]]. Also, some Digimon who are obtainable through Jogressing can also be evolved into by non-Jogress ways. ==={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum X}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Accel}}=== The [[Digimon Accel]] series features the DNA mechanic: collectible points that represent eight Digimon species archetypes that the player feeds to their Digimon to direct its evolution. From the Child stage onwards, the majority of evolution options require specific combinations of DNA types and quantities of points to be fed to evolve into that form. (The only exceptions are one "dud" evolution option per Evolution Stage per version, which are evolved into when the requirements for other Digimon are not met.) Feeding DNA costs 5 DP per DNA point consumed. Aside from the evolution from Baby I to Baby II, the passing of real time is no longer a factor in a Digimon's ability to evolve. * '''Baby I to Baby II:''' Automatically occurs after approximately 60 minutes of real time have passed since the Baby I Digimon hatched. * '''Baby II to Child:''' Each Baby II Digimon can evolve into any of the device's Child Digimon depending on the DNA that has been fed to it. * '''Child to Adult:''' Each Child Digimon can evolve into one of several Adult Digimon, depending on the DNA that has been fed to it. The amounts of DNA required to evolve into each Adult differ between Children. * '''Adult to Perfect:''' Each Adult Digimon can evolve into one of several Perfect Digimon, depending on the DNA that has been fed to it. The amounts of DNA required to evolve into each Perfect differs between Adults. An estimated minimum win ratio of 50% is required to evolve to Perfect.
Alternatively, certain Adult Digimon may undergo Jogress Evolution to evolve into certain Perfect Digimon. * '''Perfect to Ultimate:''' Each Perfect Digimon can evolve into one of several Ultimate Digimon, depending on the DNA that has been fed to it. The amounts of DNA required to evolve into each Ultimate differs between Perfects. Depending on the Ultimate Digimon, a minimum win ratio of between 60% and 80% is required to evolve to Ultimate.
There are also several special ways of evolving to Ultimate: **Certain Perfect Digimon may undergo Jogress Evolution to evolve into certain Ultimate Digimon. **In the Justice Genome, Evil Genome, and Nature Genome versions, playing multiplayer battles with Digimon Pendulum X devices adds points to the Accel's [[X-Antibody]] gauge. When this gauge reaches 20 points, any Digimon from the Child to Ultimate levels evolves into a [[Royal Knights|Royal Knight]] X-Antibody Digimon. Each of these three versions has a different Digimon. This feature is absent from the Ultimate Genome version, which instead uses connectivity with the [[Digimon Mini]] to unlock the [[DORUmon]] family. ** In the Ultimate Genome version, there are four collectible items which, when used, trigger evolution into exclusive Ultimate Digimon. The Digimon Accel also features the '''Blast Mode''' mechanic. When the player's Digimon is Perfect or Ultimate, if the player scores within a specific range on the RPM gauge before a battle, their Digimon will undergo Blast Evolution into a "Blast Mode" version of themself, signified by a changed appearance in their sprites. This consumes 30 DP each time. Being in Blast Mode doubles the Digimon's attack power and replaces their Hyper Hit attack with a One-Hit KO attack. ==={{hdr|Digimon Mini}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Twin}}=== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster Ver.20th}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum Ver.20th}}=== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster X}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum Z}}=== ==={{hdr|Vital Bracelet Digital Monster}}=== ==TCG== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster Card Game}}=== : ''Main article: '''[[Digital Monster Card Game#Evolution Phase]]''''' In the ''[[Digital Monster Card Game]]'', every Digimon card of Level IV (Adult) or higher lists a specific set of '''Evolution Requirements''' that includes the Digimon (usually 2-3) that it can be evolved from, and the required costs ("Evolution Requirements") to evolve each Digimon into the new card. Once every turn, players have an opportunity to evolve their current Digimon. This is done in their turn's Preparation Phase by playing the Digimon card to be evolved into their Evolution Box, face-down, and then playing all of the stated costs for that card in the Evolution Requirements Box. The types of Evolution Requirements that are asked for depend on the card's level. * '''Level IV''': For each Digimon to evolve from, the card will typically list a combination of ● ("Regular Growth," played oriented horizontally) and × ("Irregular Growth," played oriented vertically). This means that, for the total number of symbols listed, you draw that many cards from your Net Ocean and, without looking at their faces, play them face-down in the Evolution Requirements box in the right combination. * '''Perfect''' and '''Ultimate''': These cards typically have one of two Evolution Requirements: ** A "Winning Percentage!" Option Card. There are three "Winning Percentage!" types - 40%, 60%, and 80% - and, as stated in these cards' Effects, the type listed by the card is the minimum winning percentage that can be used. That is, you can use a "Winning Percentage: 80%" card to evolve into a Digimon whose Evolution Requirement asks for "Winning Percentage: 40%!" or "Winning Percentage: 60%!" The effects also require the user to send one card from their hand to the Dark Area (discard it) after evolution is completed. ** Jogress Evolution. The card will list several pairs of Digimon who can be Jogressed into the card, and in each case, either one of those Digimon must currently in the Digimon box, while the other is played in the Evolution Requirements Box. On occasion, Evolution Requirements will mandate that a given Digimon card can only be evolved from a Digimon card that belongs to a specific Field, Attribute, or Level. For example, [[Starter Ver. 6]] mostly features [[:Category:Virus Busters Field Digimon Cards|Digimon cards that belong to the Virus Busters Field]], and many of these require that they be evolved specifically from Digimon cards that also belong to the Virus Busters field, instead of any other field. There are Option cards that can circumvent standard Evolution Requirements, such as "Aim for the Strongest Evolution!" ([[St-56]]), which can be used instead of all other Evolution Requirements to evolve a Digimon. However, some Digimon cards have a special ability that prevents ignoring Evolution Requirements like this. Some cards have Appearance Requirements or Fusion Requirements, instead of Evolution Requirements. Fusion Requirements work similarly to some Evolution Requirements and ask for specific Option Cards, such as cards that feature [[Digimental]]s or [[Legendary Spirits]], to fuse with the player's current Digimon. Appearance Requirements have unique effects that sometimes do not require a specific current Digimon at all. ==={{hdr|Digital Monster Card Game Alpha}}=== ==={{hdr|Super Digica Taisen}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Jintrix}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Universe Appli Monsters Card Game}}=== ==={{hdr|Battle Spirits}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Card Game}}=== [[File:DCG Fun Digica Evolution.jpg|thumb|right|Episode 1 of ''Fun Digica'' demonstrates Evolution Base Effects. [[Garudamon]] gets two Evolution Base Effects from the [[Koromon]] and [[Agumon]] cards that it evolved from.]] In the ''[[Digimon Card Game]]'', evolution is determined by card color. Any Digimon of a given color can evolve into any other Digimon of a higher Evolution Stage that shares its color. Certain cards, such as [[BT3-027 (DCG)|BT3-027 Paildramon]], also allow for evolution from another color in addition to the card's own color, increasing the amount of Digimon that can evolve into them. Furthermore, some Digimon can evolve from Digimon of the same level (for example, [[BT5-029_(DCG)|BT5-029 Were Garurumon: Sagittarius Mode]] can evolve from a Level 5 Digimon like itself). Some cards also have specific evolution clauses, allowing them to evolve from the Digimon they specify (be it specific species or those that follow a certain criteria) even if they don't fit the colour requirements for the card. While Digimon can be played from the hand regardless of their level, evolving them properly gives some benefits over just playing a card from the hand: * Evolving a Digimon has significantly lower Memory cost than directly playing it. The cost is even lower if evolving from a Digimon of the same level, whenever possible. * Upon evolving a Digimon, the player that evolved must draw a card, increasing their options. This can be done as many times in a turn as the player's Memory, deck and hand allow. * Many Digimon cards also have an '''Evolution Base Effect''', which is a special effect that the card passes on to any Digimon cards that subsequently evolve from it, so as a Digimon evolves, they can acquire more Evolution Base Effects. Digimon cards can normally only evolve from other Digimon cards. However, cards that depict characters from ''[[Digimon Frontier]]'' allow Tamer cards to evolve into Digimon as well. Some Hybrid Digimon (such as [[BT4-011 (DCG)|BT4-011 Agnimon]]) have effects that treat Tamer cards as Level 3 Digimon cards. Some Tamer cards that feature the [[Chosen Children]] of ''Frontier'' even have their own effects that allow them to evolve into specific Hybrid Digimon, such as [[BT7-085 (DCG)|BT7-085 Kanbara Takuya]], whose effect lets it evolve into [[Kaiser Greymon]] by treating itself as a Level 5 Digimon card in the process. [[ST-9]] and [[ST-10]] introduce '''Jogress Evolution''' to the game. Digimon who possess the Jogress keyword (such as [[ST10-06 (DCG)|ST10-06 Mastemon]]) can be evolved in one of two ways, either through the normal evolution method, or by stacking cards that fit specific requirements (in ST10-06 Mastemon's case, a [[Digimon_Card_Game_Colors_and_Levels#Yellow_Lv.5_Digimon|Yellow Level 5 Digimon]] and a [[Digimon_Card_Game_Colors_and_Levels#Purple_Lv.5_Digimon|Purple Level 5 Digimon]]), and then stacking the Jogress Evolved card itself on top of them, for no Memory cost. Digimon may have extra effects that trigger if Jogress Evolved, making it more valuable as an evolution method despite the cost of using two Digimon cards instead of one. [[BT-10]] introduces '''[[DigiXros]]''' to the game. Digimon who possess the DigiXros keyword can have their Memory cost lowered by adding the Digimon they specify into their Evolution Base from the player's hand or field, by an amount specified in the clause itself. The DigiXros keyword isn't locked to Digimon born from DigiXros in other media, and the Digimon they require may not be consistent with the [[DigiXros Unit|DigiXros Units]] of other media, either. Much like in the ''Digimon Xros Wars'' [[Digimon Xros Wars|anime]] and [[Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|manga]], however, this is not considered an evolution. [[BT-13]] introduces '''Burst Evolution''' to the game. Only [[Burst Mode]] Digimon, all of which were [[Partner Digimon]] in ''[[Digimon Savers]]'', have the keyword, and it allows them to evolve from their non-Burst Mode forms at no cost by returning their specific Tamer from ''Savers'' to the hand. However, the Burst Mode must be trashed at the end of the turn, returning to its previous stage. [[ST-15]] and [[ST-16]] introduce '''Blast Evolution''' to the game. Only ACE Digimon have the keyword. Blast Evolution allows the Digimon to evolve without paying a cost. Every Blast Evolution keyword comes paired with the Counter keyword, thus Blast Evolution can only be triggered during the opponent's turn. Every card with Blast Evolution also has Overflow, meaning its user will get a Memory penalty once that Digimon is removed from the field. [[BT-16]] introduces '''[[Partition]]''' to the game. Only Digimon that have the Jogress keyword may have this keyword. Partition allows Digimon that fit the requirements specified in the keyword itself to be played from the Partitioned Digimon's Evolution Base without paying their cost if it's removed from the Battle Area other than by the player's own effects or by battle. It can only be activated if every Digimon specified in the keyword exists in the Partitioned Digimon's Evolution Base. [[EX-06]] introduces '''Blast Jogress''' to the game. It works as a combination of Blast and Jogress Evolution, with all of their advantages and disadvantages. However, Blast Jogress forces the player to use specific Digimon for the Jogress, rather than any Digimon of a specific colour and level. =Additional Information= {{R}} [[Category:Evolution]][[Category:Terminology]]

=Appearances= ==Anime== ===''Digimon Adventure'' original continuity=== In ''[[Digimon Adventure]]'', ''[[Digimon Adventure 02]]'', ''[[Digimon Adventure tri.]]'', ''[[Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna]]'' and ''[[Digimon Adventure 02: The Beginning]]'', the ability to evolve is mostly tied to both the strength of the bond between the [[Chosen Children|Chosen Child]] and their Digimon, and to the maturity and psychological growth of the Chosen Children themselves. For example: * The [[Crests]] that are required for Super Evolution embody a trait that each child displayed when they were younger, and unlocking Super Evolution requires bringing out the best of those qualities in themselves for the right reasons, rather than misusing them.{{rfe|DA|45|The Clashing Ultimates! War Greymon VS Metal Garurumon}} * The ability to Jogress requires that each pair of Chosen Children "synchronize" with each other, and truly understand their thoughts and feelings.{{rfe|ZT|26|Jogress Evolution, Two Hearts become One}} * [[Takenouchi Sora]], [[Izumi Kōshirō]], [[Tachikawa Mimi]], [[Kido Jo]], [[Takaishi Takeru]], and [[Yagami Hikari]]'s attainment of Ultimate Evolution is linked to a cryptic message in [[Digimoji]] that is emailed to Kōshirō from an unknown address in the Digital World:[[Digimon Adventure tri. - Chapter 2|''Digimon Adventure tri.'': "Determination"]] {|style="font-size:small;" |- | :: ''To seek true power...''
''Know the darkness...''
''And make it through...'' |{{j2|真なる力を求めるには…|Shin-naru chikara wo motomeru ni wa...}}
{{j2|暗黒を知り…|Ankoku wo shiri...}}
{{j2|超えてゆけ…|Koete-yuku...}} |- |} :As the message hints, each of them is tested by a personal crisis that puts their philosophies and what they hold dear in jeopardy, and after learning from and navigating the experience, they become able to Ultimate Evolve their partner Digimon.[[Digimon Adventure tri. - Chapter 3|''Digimon Adventure tri.'': "Confession"]] * [[Agumon -Yuki no Kizuna-]] and [[Gabumon -Yujo no Kizuna-]] are the result of the deep bond that the two Digimon share with [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure)|Yagami Taichi]] and [[Ishida Yamato]].''[[Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna]]'' Evolution can also be triggered, especially for the first time, when a Chosen Child is in danger. Their partner Digimon will evolve to protect them. The Chosen Children become aware of this, and on one occasion [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure)|Yagami Taichi]] unsuccessfully tries to exploit this to force [[Greymon]] to Super Evolve.{{rfe|DA|16|Dark Evolution! Skull Greymon}} Higher levels of evolution also often require some sort of intervention by some other force. For instance, the Chosen Children initially require physical Crests to unlock Super Evolution. [[Agumon (Adventure)|Agumon]] and [[Gabumon (Adventure)|Gabumon]] need to be shot with arrows of hope and light by Angemon and Angewomon to fulfill a prophecy and unlock their Ultimate forms,{{rfe|DA|38|Revival! The Devil Venom Vamdemon}} and it takes one of [[Qinglongmon]]'s [[Digicore]]s to grant [[Paildramon]] the ability to evolve to [[Imperialdramon: Dragon Mode]], and to enable the original eight partner Digimon to Super Evolve again after surrendering their Crest power.{{rfe|DA|39|Everyone Advance Out! Imperialdramon}} Evolving requires a great amount of energy, so if a Digimon does not have the energy left to do so, the evolution will fail.{{rfe|DA|49|Farewell Numemon}} Higher levels require higher amounts of energy; as a result, when partner Digimon Super Evolve to [[Perfect]] or higher, it is common for them to devolve to their [[Baby II]] forms when they are finished, and when they Jogress to Perfect or higher, they usally revert to their [[Baby I]] forms. The exception is [[Tailmon (Adventure)|Tailmon]], who usually just devolves back to Tailmon and only devolves to [[Plotmon]] after Jogressing or other significant expenditures of her power, or [[Nyaromon]] on two exceptionally taxing occasions. She attributes this to her having greater self-discipline than the other partner Digimon.{{rfe|DA|38|Revival! The Devil Venom Vamdemon}} The energy requirements also mean that generally, the Digimon cannot evolve if they are hungry,{{rfe|DA|16|Dark Evolution! Skull Greymon}} and that they must rest to recover the energy needed to evolve the next time.{{rfe|DA|38|Revival! The Devil Venom Vamdemon}} It is possible for a partner Digimon to be attacked while evolving, which causes the evolution to fail.''[[Digimon Adventure: Our War Game!]]'' Several independent Digimon also undergo evolution in the various series: * As foretold in a prophecy, [[Vamdemon (Adventure)|Vamdemon]] is resurrected in the evolved form of [[Venom Vamdemon]] following his initial defeat by [[Tailmon (Adventure)|Angewomon]] in August 1999.{{rfe|DA|37|All Perfects Attack! Sparkling Angewomon}}{{rfe|DA|38|Revival! The Devil Venom Vamdemon}}
Three years later, on December 31, 2002, Vamdemon's spirit (possessing [[Oikawa Yukio]]) evolves again into [[Belial Vamdemon]] after he consumes the [[Dark Seed]]s implanted in several children.{{rfe|ZT|48|Terror! Belial Vamdemon}} * After his defeat by [[Yagami Taichi]] and [[Metal Greymon]], [[Etemon (Adventure)|Etemon]] is eventually reborn as [[Metal Etemon]].{{rfe|DA|46|Metal Etemon Strikes Back}} * [[Leomon (Adventure)|Leomon]] gains the ability to Warp Evolve into [[Saber Leomon]] due to his repeated exposure to the light of the Chosen Children's Digivices. However, he has poor control over his evolution and cannot maintain the Saber Leomon form for long.{{rfe|DA|47|Oh Wind! Oh Light! Saber Leomon}} * A [[Kuramon]] that hatches on the Internet evolves by eating data and causing havoc worldwide, going through the forms of [[Tsumemon]], [[Keramon]], [[Infermon]] (skipping the Adult level), and [[Diablomon]]. That Diablomon is later reborn, and the many Kuramon that it spawns all fuse together in the real world to evolve into [[Armagemon]].''[[Digimon Adventure 02: Diablomon Strikes Back]]'' * [[Hackmon (Adventure tri.)|Hackmon]] is able to freely evolve into [[JESmon]] and devolve back into Hackmon.[[Digimon Adventure tri. - Chapter 5|''Digimon Adventure tri.'': "Coexistence"]] {| class="wikitable" border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:90%" |- ! colspan=5 align="center" | Types of evolution in the ''Digimon Adventure'' original continuity |- ! style="width:16%" | Type ! (''Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna'') | ''[[Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna]]'' |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers}}=== In ''[[Digimon Tamers]]'', the source of all evolution is the Digi-Entelechy, or "light of evolution". The existence of the Digi-Entelechy is what enables Digimon to evolve. Before the start of the series, the Digi-Entelechy is transformed into [[Culumon (Tamers)|Culumon]] by the [[Digignome]]s in accordance with the wishes of [[Qinglongmon]], who wanted to prevent any evolution from occurring to avoid provoking the [[D-Reaper (Tamers)|D-Reaper]].{{rfe|DT|48|True Enemy Gets to Move! The Battle of the Four Holy Beasts}} As a result, normally no Digimon evolve at all, but sometimes Culumon accidentally causes evolutions to occur around him just by emotionally reacting to events around him (sadness, curiosity, etc.) As the series progresses, the Tamers become able to trigger evolutions at will by [[Card Slash|Slashing]] certain cards. They use the "Super Evolution Plug-In S" card ([[St-100]]) to evolve their Digimon to the Adult level, and [[Blue Card]]s to evolve them to the Perfect Level. Sometimes, when they do this, it is shown that the slashing of the card has triggered Culumon into using his powers on the partner Digimon. Devolution is also significantly harder in ''Tamers''. Unlike in other series, where a partner Digimon is likely to devolve as soon as the action is over, it is common for the Tamers' partner Digimon to remain in their evolved forms (mainly Adult) for hours at a time afterward with no way to force themselves to devolve, even if their Tamer is no longer present. [[Lee Jianliang]] theorizes that it is entirely possible for their Digimon to remain evolved indefinitely, but that after a while it becomes too hard to maintain an evolved form, so they devolve into a more stable form.{{rfe|DT|25|Enter the Digital World! Goodbye to Our City}} Initially, it is impossible for a Digimon to maintain an evolved form while inside the [[D-Reaper Zone]].{{rfe|DT|45|Stand Up to the D-Reaper, Charge into the Zone!}} [[Mizuno Gorou]] develops the [[Red Card]] algorithm to counteract this by altering the wavelengths of Digimon to be closer to those of the D-Reaper itself.{{rfe|DT|51|Dreaming Power is Our Future}} Several independent Digimon evolve in the series. This is usually caused by Culumon's power. * A [[Dark Tyranomon]] in the Digital World evolves into [[Metal Tyranomon]] and destroys a [[Maildramon]] that was chasing Culumon. The cause of this evolution is unknown, although it coincides with this exact evolution and fight outcome occurring in a [[Digital Monster Card Game]] match played between [[Matsuda Takato]] and [[Shiota Hirokazu]].{{rfe|DT|01|Guilmon is Born! The Digimon that I Created}} * A Wild One [[Goblimon]] who Realizes in Shinjuku evolves into [[Fugamon]] when Culumon unintentionally activates some of its power.{{rfe|DT|02|You are my Friend - Introducing Terriermon!}} * [[Impmon (Tamers)|Impmon]] evolves into [[Beelzebumon]] for the first time when he makes a deal with [[Caturamon]] to be endowed with [[Zhuqiaomon]]'s power, in exchange for eliminating the Tamers and their Partner Digimon.{{rfe|DT|27|Impmon Evolves! The Shudder of Beelzebumon the Dark Lord}} The next time that Impmon takes this form, he does it through the power of his rekindled bond with his Tamers, [[Ai and Makoto]]. * While playing with some [[Digignome]]s, Culumon unintentionally causes three [[Woodmon]] to evolve into [[Jyureimon]].{{rfe|DT|29|Here is the Ghost Castle! The Great Escape of Stray Culumon}} * Culumon unintentionally causes hundreds of [[Chrysalimon]] who are fighting Beelzebumon to fuse into one giant [[Infermon]].{{rfe|DT|30|Urgent Message from the Digital World, Culumon is...}} * With the assistance of the Digignomes, Culumon intentionally uses its {{ATO|Shining Evolution}} to cause many Digimon around the Digital World to evolve to the Ultimate level to help fight the D-Reaper. These Digimon include three [[Diablomon]] (from the fusion of hundreds of Chrysalimon), a [[Metal Seadramon]], a [[Mugendramon]], a [[Gran Kuwagamon]], a [[Griffomon]], at least five [[Pukumon]], a [[Hououmon]], a [[Boltmon]], a [[Plesiomon]], and a [[Marin Angemon (Tamers)|Marin Angemon]] (who becomes [[Kitagawa Kenta]]'s partner Digimon).{{rfe|DT|40|Evolutionary Radiance - Shining Evolution}} {| class="wikitable" border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:90%" |- ! colspan=5 align="center" | Types of evolution in ''Digimon Tamers'' |- ! style="width:16%" | Type ! Description ! style="width:20%" | Digimon's Call ! style="width:200px" | Example ! style="width:15%" | First Appearance |- ! id="Evolution (Tamers)" | Evolution

'''[[Dukemon: Crimson Mode]]''' | [[Digimon Tamers - Episode 43|''Digimon Tamers'': ''"Connected Hearts: The Revival of Beelzebumon"]]'' |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== ''[[Digimon Frontier]]'' focuses on the use of the twenty [[Legendary Spirits]] to undergo Spirit Evolution into [[Hybrid]] Digimon. Both human [[Chosen Children]] and ordinary Digimon are capable of Spirit Evolution. The series focuses on six human Chosen Children who use Spirits. Digimon are also capable of using the Spirits to become Hybrid Digimon, and this occurs on several occasions: * [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]] created four of the five [[Evil Warriors]] ([[Mercuremon (Frontier)|Mercuremon]], [[Ranamon (Frontier)|Ranamon]], [[Grottemon (Frontier)|Grottemon]], and [[Arbormon (Frontier)|Arbormon]]) by bonding Spirits to Digimon. He also tried to bond the Spirits of Darkness to various Digimon, but none of these attempts succeeded;{{rfe|DF|33|The New Warriors of Darkness! Löwemon & Kaiser Leomon}} the broken data remains of these Digimon inhabit the dungeons of Cherubimon's castle at the [[Rose Morning Star]].{{rfe|DF|35|Spirits Become One! Takuya and Kouji's Ultimate Evolution!}} ** On one occasion, Mercuremon also fuses with the scanned data of [[Seraphimon (Frontier)|Seraphimon]] to evolve into [[Black Seraphimon]], in a process that he announces as "Advent of the Fallen Angel" {{ETYK|{{j2|堕天使降臨|Datenshi kōrin}}}}.{{rfe|DF|28|Takuya's Fusion Evolution, Aldamon's Explosive Attack!}} * A [[Shamamon]] finds the [[Beast Spirit of Fire]] in the temple where he works as a fortune teller, and is involuntarily bonded to it and evolves into [[Vritramon]]. He runs wild until Takuya (as Agnimon) subdues and purifies him and takes the Beast Spirit.{{rfe|DF|11|Defeat Me! Legendary Warrior Vritramon Runs Wild}} Conventional Digimon evolution still exists in the series, although it is downplayed and the concept of [[Evolution Stage]]s is not mentioned. Several independent Digimon characters undergo evolution: * After being hit by [[Minamoto Kouji]], a [[Pagumon]] evolves into [[Raremon]] to take revenge.{{rfe|DF|02|Wolfmon of Light, The Underground Labyrinth Battle!}} * A [[Candmon]] evolves into [[Wizarmon]] after one of its allies was frozen by [[Himi Tomoki]] as [[Chackmon]]. It devolves into Candmon after being defeated and purified by [[Kanbara Takuya]] as [[Agnimon]].{{rfe|DF|03|Bullying is Unforgivable! Evolve, Chackmon of Ice}} * The Three [[Mushmon]] Brothers evolve together into a single [[Woodmon]] after they're beaten by [[Orimoto Izumi]] as [[Fairimon]]. They return to Mushmon after being defeated and purified by [[Wolfmon]].{{rfe|DF|04|My Painful Kick! Female Warrior Fairymon}} * A [[Tunomon]] from the Digimon elementary school is able to evolve into [[Gabumon]] at will. However, because evolving scared its classmates, it refused to do so, and stayed on its Tunomon form, only evolving to save Izumi from drowning and then to save its classmate, [[Caprimon]], from getting killed by a log swept by a strong current. After the latter event, the others stop fearing Gabumon, and Tunomon decides to keep evolving until it becomes as strong as the Warrior Ten, though it still stays as Tunomon rather than Gabumon.{{rfe|DF|08|Save Everyone! Evolve, Tunomon}} * A [[Gottsumon]] who had befriended Kouji evolves into [[Insekimon]] so that he can help Kouji (as [[Magna Garurumon]]) fight [[Lord Knightmon]]. He devolves back to Gottsumon when Lord Knightmon severely injures him.{{rfe|DF|44|Fight Together! Gottsumon and Kouji's Vow}} * [[Murmukusmon]] is able to Slide Evolve between the forms of [[Darcmon]], [[Hippogriffomon]], and Murmukusmon at will.{{ref|''[[Digimon Frontier: Revival of the Ancient Digimon!!]]''}} In the ''[[Digital Monster 25th Anniversary Book –Digimon Device & Dot History–]]'' artbook, it was stated that Slide Evolution is when a Digimon evolves to another Digimon species of the same level (unlike Mode Change, which is the same type of Digimon - e.g. [[Metal Greymon (Vaccine)]] Mode Changing to [[Metal Greymon: Alterous Mode]]). Darcmon, Hippogriffomon, and Murmukusmon are all different levels which goes against this claim - though it's worth nothing that ''Frontier'' doesn't use levels in the first place. Kouichi's Last Wish"]]'' |- |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Savers}}=== A key theme of ''[[Digimon Savers]]'' is the influence of human emotions on Digimon. [[Digisoul]] is a power that comes from human emotion, and Digimon respond to that power. Tamers must learn to focus their emotions and Digisoul, and direct it toward a clear purpose, in order to evolve their partner Digimon.{{rfe|DS|29|Resurrected Digivices, A New Shining Light}} Evolution is initiated by manifesting their Digisoul as an aura around themselves, and then charging it into their [[Digivice iC]]. ''Savers'' also features a high number of independent Digimon, usually enemies, who evolve. Many of these are Digimon who have infiltrated the real world and are feeding on the negative emotions of humans, whose dark wishes they are often trying to fulfill. * A [[Kunemon]] uses [[Takashi]]'s resentment at having to look after the chickens at [[Daimon Chika]]'s school to evolve into [[Flymon]].{{rfe|DS|02|Burn, Digisoul of Anger, The Flymon Hidden in Darkness'''}} * [[Agumon (Savers)|Agumon's]] {{ATO|Baby Burner}} attack accidentally causes three [[Petit Meramon]] to fuse together and evolve into [[Meramon]].{{rfe|DS|03|Genius Thoma has Returned! Beat Meramon}} * A [[Drimogemon]] evolves into [[Digmon]] in the Digital World.{{rfe|DS|05|Rush into the Digital World! Drimogemon's Trap}} * An [[Elecmon]] that has fed on power lines evolves into [[Garurumon (Black)|a black Garurumon]].{{rfe|DS|06|The Masaru-Agumon Combo Dissolves?! Squalling Garurumon}} * A [[Keramon]] that was manipulating chart data for [[Hanamura Neon]] evolves into [[Chrysalimon]] when Neon is enraged.{{rfe|DS|08|Yoshino gets her Cinderella Story?! Chrysalimon's Shadow}} * When [[Mercurimon (Savers)|Mercurimon]] tries to force [[Piyomon (Savers)|Piyomon]] to forget about Chika, he evolves into [[Aquilamon]] and runs wild. He evolves again into [[Garudamon]] in his mad desperation to find Chika.{{rfe|DS|13|Masaru's New Power, Evolve! Rize Greymon}} Later, after being reborn, Piyomon's bond with Chika allows him to evolve into Garudamon again.{{rfe|DS|45|One-on-one Match Between Man and Man! Masaru VS Suguru}} * Mercurymon's servant, [[Gottsumon (Savers)|Gottsumon]], is able to evolve into his "true form", [[Insekimon]].{{rfe|DS|23|Once More, To the Digital World, Insekimon's Great Rampage}} {| class="wikitable" border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:90%" |- ! colspan=5 align="center" | Types of evolution in ''Digimon Savers'' |- ! style="width:16%" | Type ! Thoma VS Nanami"]]'' |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars}}=== The primary focus of ''[[Digimon Xros Wars]]'' is on its main gimmick of [[DigiXros#Anime|DigiXrossing]], where humans use [[Xros Loader]]s to temporarily combine their Digimon allies. The series features DigiXros combinations of more and more Digimon as it goes on. Later in the series, it is common for both Super Evolutions and DigiXrossing to be used together to create even stronger composite Digimon. Antagonist access to DigiXrossing is mostly limited to [[DigiXros#Forced DigiXros (anime)|Forced DigiXrossing]], where a [[Darkness Loader]] is used to force a DigiXros to occur regardless of whether any of the constituent Digimon want it. Before the Darkness Loader is introduced, antagonists [[Evolution/Anime#Absorption|absorb]] other Digimon. ''Xros Wars'' does not use the concept of [[Evolution Stage]]s. Instead, evolution is characterized as a permanent growth into new forms that Digimon undergo as they age, which takes a very long time. Unlike other ''Digimon'' series, evolution as a power available to the human cast and their partner Digimon is entirely absent from the series until {{epn|XW|30|A New Journey!! The Great Tokyo Showdown!!}}, where Super Evolutions are introduced as the ability to skip over all of the time that is normally needed to evolve and immediately become the mature form that the Digimon will have in the future. As a result, each partner Digimon only has one conventional evolved form (not counting Mode Changes). During the first season of ''Xros Wars'', some Digimon transform into new, more powerful forms. These are not treated by the story as conventional evolutions, but rather as being caused by special circumstances, although both examples are considered normal evolution in both prior and subsequent material. * After being mortally wounded and donning the goddess's mask, [[Baalmon]] is resurrected as [[Beelzebumon (2010 Anime Version)|Beelzebumon]].{{rfe|XW|14|Warrior Beelzebumon, Dance!}} * Once he reveals his true allegiance to the [[Bagra Army]] and [[Lilithmon (Xros Wars)|Lilithmon]], [[Lucemon]] takes his true form, [[Lucemon: Falldown Mode]].{{rfe|XW|16|Dark Knight Digimon, An Entrance!}} After being defeated in this form, the evil energy of the dark sky temple overwhelms him and he becomes [[Lucemon: Satan Mode]].{{rfe|XW|17|Miraculous Digi Xros! Shoutmon X5 Flies!}} During the ''[[Digimon Xros Wars: The Evil Death Generals and the Seven Kingdoms]]'' season, only the partner Digimon of the protagonists evolve. In the ''[[Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time]]'' season, some independent Digimon evolve as well, mostly those created by [[Quartzmon (Xros Wars)|Quartzmon]]. Despite the lack of human element to them, other than the first one, they are still called Super Evolutions in-series: * Knight Chessmon absorbs the greed from [[Hiroya]] after pushing him towards becoming more and more obsessed with victory, turning from [[Knight Chessmon (White)|White]] to [[Knight Chessmon (Black)|Black]] before Super Evolving into [[Rook Chessmon (Black)|Rook Chessmon]]. Unlike other Super Evolutions, this one appears to be permanent, as it keeps the form after [[Amano Yuu]] captures it.{{rfe|XW|74|The Rare Card is Gone! Invincible Rook Chessmon}} * A [[Tsumemon]] created by Quartzmon evolves into [[Keramon]], [[Chrysalimon]], [[Infermon]] and finally [[Diablomon]] during several fights against the Digimon Hunters, making it the only Digimon seen in the series to evolve multiple times.{{rfe|XW|77|All Will Be Revealed Now! The Secret of the Digimon Hunt!}} * Several [[Vamdemon]] created by Quartzmon evolve into [[Belial Vamdemon]], with three of them instead becoming giant [[Venom Vamdemon]].{{rfe|XW|78|The Gathering of the Legendary Heroes! The Digimon All-star Showdown!!}} The guest characters from prior ''Digimon'' series who appear in ''Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time'' all follow the same rules and mechanisms for evolution from their respective original series. {| class="wikitable" border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:90%" |- ! colspan=5 align="center" | Types of evolution in ''Digimon Xros Wars'' |- ! style="width:16%" | Type ! The Mystery of Metallife Kuwagamon"]]'' |- | colspan="5" style="text-align:center;" | Component Digimon listed in '''bold''' are the lead Digimon of the respective absorption/DigiXros.
Component Digimon listed in (parentheses) are treated as a single [[DigiXros#DigiXros Unit|DigiXros Unit]] for the purposes of the respective DigiXros. |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Universe Appli Monsters}}=== ''[[Digimon Universe Appli Monsters]]'' has its own version of Evolution Stages, called "Grades", but unlike other ''Digimon'' series, there is not quite any directly "evolving" a single [[Appmon]] into a single new form. Instead, all progression to higher Grades revolves around two compatible Appmon (or their [[Appmon Chip]]s) undergoing AppGattai to combine together. Unlocking a [[Buddy Appmon]]'s higher-Grade form via AppGattai creates an Appmon Chip of that form, and as the series progresses, that chip (when activated, for instance in an [[Appmon Band]]) can be used to [[Appliarise]] the Buddy Appmon directly into that form instead of having to AppGattai them again. This basically works how evolution works in other ''Digimon'' series. When an Ultimate AppGattai/Appliarise is performed with a regular [[Appli Drive]], the ability of the Appmon to remain in their Ultimate form is limited to 60 seconds, and a countdown is displayed on the screen of the Appli Drive.{{rfe|APM|19|The Net Ocean is in a Big Pinch! The "Time" has Come, Ultimate App Fusion!!}} The [[Appli Drive DUO]] removes this limitation.{{rfe|APM|28|The Appli Drive DUO! Offmon Appears}} If a Buddy Appmon is exhausted, they are unable to take their Ultimate Grade form until they have rested and recovered the energy needed to do so.{{rfe|APM|24|The Super Gigantic Cometmon Attacks!? Open the Door, Dantemon!}} Several independent Appmon, primarily villains, undergo AppGattai or are Appliarised: * [[Cameramon]] uses [[Shotmon]]'s Appmon Chip to AppGattai into [[Scorpmon]].{{rfe|APM|04|I Will Have You in Costume! Cameramon's Halloween Scandal!}} * [[Mienumon (Appli Monsters)|Mienumon]] uses [[Sakusimon (Appli Monsters)|Sakusimon's]] Appmon Chip to AppGattai into [[Warudamon]].{{rfe|APM|23|Reclaim the 7code Appmon! Showdown—Ultimate VS Ultimate!}} * The completed [[Seven Code PAD]] uses [[Shinkai Haru]]'s [[Appli Drive]] to Appliarise [[Dantemon]].{{rfe|APM|24|The Super Gigantic Cometmon Attacks!? Open the Door, Dantemon!}} A single Digimon appeared in the series: [[Agumon (Appli Monsters)|Agumon]]. It follows conventional Digimon evolution rules to undergo Warp Evolution, instead of Appmon rules.{{rfe|APM|45|Legendary Battle!? Gatchmon VS Agumon!}} {| class="wikitable" border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:90%" |- ! colspan=5 align="center" | Types of evolution in ''Digimon Universe: Appli Monsters'' |- ! style="width:16%" | Type ! Gatchmon VS Agumon!"'']] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure:}}=== Evolution in ''[[Digimon Adventure:]]'' works similarly to how it does in the original ''[[Digimon Adventure]]''. It is generally triggered by the [[Chosen Children]] by their emotional responses and willpower, especially as it relates to their [[Crest]]. Unlike the original ''Adventure'', ''Adventure:'' explores the idea of multiple routes of evolution for partner Digimon, as five of them have unlocked an second, alternate evolution to a given Evolution Stage. However, most of these evolutions have only been used once. When an evolution is triggered, the outer central ring of the [[Digivice:]] will light up in a specific pattern that corresponds with the Evolution Stage that the Digimon is undergoing evolution into. It also lights up when their partner Digimon is gaining a temporary power boost from the Chosen Child's/their own determination and emotional drive. The lights are in the color of the Chosen Child's Crest. During the final battle with [[Negamon (Adventure:)|Negamon]], [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure:)|Yagami Taichi]] and [[Ishida Yamato (Adventure:)|Ishida Yamato]]'s [[Omegamon]] also demonstrates the ability to replace its own {{EQO|Grey Sword}} and {{EQO|Garuru Cannon}} arms with the {{EQO|Garuru Sword}} and {{EQO|Grey Cannon}} arms of [[Omegamon Alter-S]], shortly before it completely Mode Changes into a true Omegamon Alter-S.{{rfe|DA:|67|The End of the Adventure}}https://digimon.net/journal/topic007/ Several independent Digimon have undergone evolutions, including many of the series' villains. A common source of evolution for enemy Digimon is black lightning. * Some [[Algomon (Baby II)|Baby II Algomon]] undergo evolutions into higher levels on several occasions, including one that evolves into [[Algomon (Ultimate)|the Ultimate-form Algomon]].{{rfe|DA:|02|War Game}} [[Algomon (Adventure:)|The one Algomon who is capable of speech]] evolves from Adult to Perfect in the first encounter with it,{{rfe|DA:|57|Contact from the Catastrophe}} then into Ultimate during its last battle.{{rfe|DA:|65|The Great Catastrophe, Negamon}} * One of the [[Eyesmon (Adventure:)|Eyesmon]] evolves into [[Orochimon]],{{rfe|DA:|16|The Jet-black Shadow Invades Tokyo}} and then again into [[Nidhoggmon]].{{rfe|DA:|18|Countdown to Tokyo's Annihilation}} * [[Skull Knightmon (Adventure:)|Skull Knightmon]] Super Evolves into [[Dark Knightmon]].{{rfe|DA:|22|The Unbeatable Blue Sagittarius}} * [[Devimon (Adventure:)|Devimon]] evolves twice in his final fight with the Chosen Children: first into [[Neo Devimon]] using power taken from the [[Real World]],{{rfe|DA:|23|The Messenger of Darkness, Devimon}} and then into [[Done Devimon]] thanks to Dark Knightmon using the power of the [[Millenniumon (Adventure:)|dark crystal]] on him.{{rfe|DA:|24|The Final Stage, Done Devimon}} * A [[Parrotmon]] is evolved by black lightning into [[Crossmon]].{{rfe|DA:|30|The Ultimate, War Greymon}} * Three [[Bakemon]] are evolved by black lightning into [[Metal Fantomon]]. One of them then evolves even further into [[Gokumon]].{{rfe|DA:|31|A New Darkness, Millenniumon}} * An [[Ebidramon]] is evolved by black lightning into [[Gusokumon]].{{rfe|DA:|35|The Glowing Angewomon}} * [[Potamon]] evolves into [[Jyagamon]] and goes into a rampage due to its sorrow at not being able to eat the fries of the haven for travelling Digimon anymore.{{rfe|DA:|39|Jyagamon, Potato Hell}} * A [[Tyumon]] from [[Gerbemon]]'s garbage region evolves into [[Searchmon]] after [[Rare Raremon]]'s defeat.{{rfe|DA:|42|King of Inventors, Gerbemon}} * [[Millenniumon (Adventure:)|Millenniumon]] is resurrected from its [[Moon=Millenniumon]] form when it fuses with an incomplete [[Chimairamon]] (itself evolved from [[Sephirothmon]]).{{rfe|DA:|49|The God of Evil Descends, Millenniumon}} When [[Patamon (Adventure:)|Goddramon]] and [[Tailmon (Adventure:)|Holydramon]] destroy it, its soul survives and evolves into [[Zeed Millenniumon]].{{rfe|DA:|50|The End, The Ultimate Holy War}} * Several [[Soundbirdmon]] fuse together to become [[Deathmon (Adventure:)|Deathmon]], which quickly switches from [[Deathmon|grey]] to [[Deathmon (Black)|black]].{{rfe|DA:|64|The Angels' Determination}} * [[Negamon (Adventure:)|Negamon]] undergoes several evolutions during its final battle with the Chosen Children: into an [[Negamon (Evolved Form)|unnamed evolved state]], and then simultaneously into [[Abbadomon]] and [[Abbadomon Core]].{{rfe|DA:|66|The Last Miracle, the Last Power}} {| class="wikitable" border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:90%" |- ! colspan=5 align="center" | Types of evolution in ''Digimon Adventure:'' |- ! style="width:16%" | Type ! '''[[Were Garurumon: Sagittarius Mode]]''' → '''[[Metal Garurumon]]''' | [[Digimon Adventure: - Episode 30|''Digimon Adventure:'': ''"The Ultimate, War Greymon"'']] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Ghost Game}}=== How evolution works in ''[[Digimon Ghost Game]]'' has not been explained yet, although [[Bokomon (Ghost Game)|Bokomon]] has speculated that evolution may be the result of the combination of the emotions of both a Digimon and their human partner.{{rfe|GG|09|Warped Time}} Evolution appears to be related to a Vitals gauge readout on the human partner's [[Digivice-V-]] or [[Digivice-VV-]], which is displayed and shown filling when an evolution is about to occur. ''Ghost Game'' explores the idea of multiple routes of evolutions for [[partner Digimon]], albeit only for [[Amanokawa Hiro]]'s [[Gammamon (Ghost Game)|Gammamon]]. He currently has access to four different conventional [[Adult]] forms which are used for different situations. Hiro has observed that there is a time limit to how long a [[Perfect]] evolution can be maintained before the Digimon will revert to a lower level.{{rfe|GG|36|Labyrinth of Grief}} When the time is nearly up and devolution from Perfect is imminent, the Vitals gauge readout will sound an alarm.{{rfe|GG|28|Face Taker}} Several independent Digimon undergo evolutions: * When Hiro's group breaks into [[Never-Ever Land]] to confront [[Petermon]] for abducting children and Digimon, [[Kazuma]]'s two [[Hawkmon]] friends both evolve into [[Orcamon]] to protect Kazuma from Petermon. They inspire the other Digimon to evolve as well, including a [[Elecmon (Virus)|Virus Elecmon]] (into [[Thunderballmon]]), a [[Gazimon]] (into [[Hanumon]]), a [[Bearmon]] (into [[Gryzmon]]), a [[Kamemon]] (into [[Gawappamon]]), and a [[Hagurumon]] (into [[Guardromon]]). The Orcamon stay evolved when they return to Tokyo.{{rfe|GG|12|The Land of Children}} * [[Ryudamon (Ghost Game)|Ryudamon]] evolves into [[Ginryumon]] in order to save [[Gammamon (Ghost Game)|Gammamon]] upon learning that he had been kidnapped, in order to repay Gammamon for having previously saved him.{{rfe|GG|48|The White Bride}} * Pucchie the [[Pucchiemon]] evolves into [[Meicrackmon: Vicious Mode]] after spending time with [[Fukatsu Riku]].{{rfe|GG|50|Payback}} * [[Kuzuhamon: Miko Mode]] Mode Changes into [[Kuzuhamon]] to fight [[Canoweissmon]], who interrupted her purification ritual by saving Hiro.{{rfe|GG|56|Impurity}} * [[Lilithmon]]'s servant, [[Cerberumon]], Mode Changes into [[Cerberumon: Werewolf Mode|Werewolf Mode]] to fight [[Angoramon (Ghost Game)|Lamortmon]] and [[Jellymon (Ghost Game)|Thetismon]].{{rfe|GG|57|Ghost Taxi}} {| class="wikitable" border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:90%" |- ! colspan=5 align="center" | Types of evolution in ''Digimon Ghost Game'' |- ! style="width:16%" | Type ! Final Xros Units.wikitext:
In the finales of the [[Digimon Xros Wars|''Digimon Xros Wars'' anime]] and [[Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|manga]], [[Kudou Taiki]] and his allies perform a [[DigiXros|Final Xros]] that creates [[Shoutmon X7: Superior Mode]] by fusing [[Shoutmon (Xros Wars)|Shoutmon]] with many other [[Digimon (species)|Digimon]].

The following lists document every Digimon that is seen to be part of the Final Xros in each story. Due to the significant story divergences between the anime and manga, the Digimon who are seen are not the same between them.

=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Evil Death Generals and the Seven Kingdoms}}===
[[File:XW54 Evo FinalXros Shoutmonx7SM 02.jpg|thumb|right|[[Shoutmon X7: Superior Mode]] in the ''Digimon Xros Wars'' anime.]]
In ''[[Digimon Xros Wars - Episode 54|"Glorious DigiXros, Seize It! Our Future!!"]]'', when the [[Code Crown]] chooses [[Kudou Taiki]] over [[Darkness Bagramon]], [[Shoutmon X7: Superior Mode]] is created in the [[Real World]] to finish the fight. This Final Xros consists of almost every living Digimon, including all of [[Xros Heart]], [[Blue Flare]], and the [[DigiMemory (Xros Wars)|DigiMemories]] (who were already in the Real World to fight Darkness Bagramon); and countless Digimon who are called to the Real World from the [[Digtal World (Xros Wars)|Digital World]] through portals that open in the sky.

The following Digimon are seen on-screen contributing to the Final Xros:{{note|In ''[[Digimon Xros Wars - Episode 54|"Glorious DigiXros, Seize It! Our Future!!"]]'', [[Toei Animation]]'s stock art of Digimon species is used to depict Digimon who contribute to the Final Xros in two contexts:
* In the shots of Digimon coming out of the portals from the [[Digital World (Xros Wars)|Digital World]], the stock art is used for those who are in the far background
* The rings of Digimon that orbit [[Shoutmon X7: Superior Mode]]'s torso are comprised entirely of stock art

Four Holy Beasts.wikitext:
[[Image:Agumon hakase qinglongmon.png|thumb|right]] The {{nihongo|'''Four Holy Beasts'''|(四聖獣|Shiseijū}}; Dub: ''Digimon Sovereign, Four Sovereign Monsters''{{ref|[[Digimon Survive]]}}) are a group of four [[Ultimate]] Holy Beast Digimon who protect the [[Digital World]]{{refd|Qinglongmon}}{{refd|Baihumon}}{{refd|Xuanwumon}}{{refd|Zhuqiaomon}} and govern its stability.{{ref|''{{url|web.archive.org/web/20060117111814/http://www.digimon.channel.or.jp:80/city/card/lineup/starter6-10.html|Digital Monster Card Game - Starter Ver. 6 Advent of the Three Great Angels (HOLY KINGDOM)}}''}} They represent and rule over the cardinal points of the compass — [[Xuanwumon]] for the north,{{refd|Xuanwumon}} [[Zhuqiaomon]] for the south,{{refd|Zhuqiaomon}} [[Qinglongmon]] for the east,{{refd|Qinglongmon}} and [[Baihumon]] for the west.{{refd|Baihumon}} They are led by [[Huanglongmon]], who represents and is enshrined in the Center, from which he supervises the others.{{refd|Huanglongmon}} In turn, each of the Four Holy Beasts has three [[Deva]] as subordinates.{{ref|[[Deva]]}} The Four Holy Beasts have reigned over and protected the Digital World since the age of [[Ancient Digital World|myth]], and have passed into legend as beings which are extremely difficult to track down.{{refd|Qinglongmon}} Baihumon reins over the [[Folder Continent]] and [[File Island]], having being seen in the [[Codec Ruins]] and the [[Register Grassland]]; Xuanwumon lives in the [[Forest of the Gods]], around the [[Script Forest]]; and the [[WWW Continent]] is home to Qinglongmon and Zhuqiaomon, with Qinglongmon living in the [[Assembler Tower]], and Zhuqiaomon in the [[Boot Volcano]].{{ref|[[Digimon Battle Terminal 02]]}}{{ref|''{{url|web.archive.org/web/20130213124236/http://digimon.net/digimon_archive2010/cat-digimon-world/index.html|Digimon Twin - Digital World Data: 01-05 (Digimon Web)}}''}} However, due to [[Lucemon]] descending to the Digital World, Huanglongmon was sealed in the deepest, darkest place within the earth. This caused the Four Holy Beasts to lose their rule and struggle for hegemony, but an equilibrium was established that is still maintained.{{refd|Huanglongmon}} In 2007, the [[Seven Great Demon Lords]] secretly released the crack program [[Tempest]] into the Digital World which affected a number of Digimon, including the Four Holy Beasts, and caused world wide rampage as a consequence that eventually led to the [[L&R War]].{{ref|''{{url|web.archive.org/web/20130213124236/http://digimon.net/digimon_archive2010/cat-digimon-world/index.html|Digimon Twin - Digital World Data: 01-05 (Digimon Web)}}''}} =Members= The Four Holy Beasts are derived from the {{w|Four Symbols (China)|Four Symbols}} of Chinese mythology. In ancient Chinese astronomy, the sky ecliptic is divided into four sections, and each section is assigned a mythological creature. Symbolically and spiritually, the Four Symbols have been culturally important across many countries in East Asia and Southeast Asia. The Four Holy Beasts share several common features:[https://digimon.net/profile/report006/ ''Digimon Profile'' Report: Four Holy Beasts and their Follower Digimon] * Each of the Four Holy Beasts guards over one of the four cardinal directions of the Digital World. * They all possess immense power equal to that of a god.{{refd|Xuanwumon}}{{refd|Baihumon}}{{refd|Zhuqiaomon}}{{refd|Qinglongmon}} * They all possess four shining, red eyes. * Each Holy Beast possesses twelve [[Digicore]]s that float outside of their body, instead of a single Digicore inside their body. * Each Holy Beast is served by three of the twelve [[Deva|Deva]] who share their Attribute. The Deva are assigned to each Holy Beast according to the cardinal direction that corresponds with their respective [[wikipedia:Chinese zodiac|zodiac sign]] based on the [[wikipedia:Earthly Branches|Earthly Branches]] system. {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;width:50%;font-size:90%" |- |style="background:#262626; text-align:center;"|'''Holy Beast''' |style="background:#262626; text-align:center;"|'''Direction''' |style="background:#262626; text-align:center;"|'''Symbol''' |style="background:#262626; text-align:center;"|'''Element''' |style="background:#262626; text-align:center;" colspan="3"|'''Subordinates''' |- !style="background: #fff; color:#000;" valign="top" | [[File:Xuanwumon.jpg|120px]]

[[Majiramon]] |} ==Emperor Enshrined in the Center== [[Huanglongmon]] is derived from the [[wikipedia:Yellow Dragon|Yellow Dragon]], the zoomorphic incarnation of the [[wikipedia:Yellow Emperor|Yellow Emperor]], a legendary sovereign in Chinese religion. It is included alongside the Four Symbols as the ruler of the Center and the embodiment of Earth. Like its origin, Huanglongmon is the emperor who governs and supervises over the Four Holy Beasts and the Digital World. It is enshrined within the center of the four cardinal directions. Huanglongmon also possesses twelve external Digicores, but unlike the Four Holy Beasts, it has eight eyes instead of four.{{refd|Huanglongmon}} {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;width:70%;font-size:90%" |- |style="background:#262626; text-align:center;"|'''Emperor''' |style="background:#262626; text-align:center;"|'''Alternate form''' |style="background:#262626; text-align:center;"|'''Point''' |style="background:#262626; text-align:center;"|'''Symbol''' |style="background:#262626; text-align:center;"|'''Element''' |style="background:#262626; text-align:center;" colspan="4"|'''Subordinates''' |- !style="background: #fff; color:#000;" valign="top" | [[File:Huanglongmon.jpg|120px]]
