[[Qinglongmon]] |} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02}}=== When [[Inoue Miyako|Miyako]] goes on a school trip to Kyoto, she ends up encountering [[Takenouchi Haruhiko]], [[Takenouchi Sora|Sora's]] father, and [[Kido Shuu]], one of [[Kido Jou|Jou's]] older brothers. Haruhiko, a professor at Kyoto University, and Shuu, his student, explain to her about Kyoto's importance to the Digital World, telling her that Kyoto is the place where it is said that the barrier between the two worlds are at their weakest and that's why Digimon were able to pass. They explain to her about the Four Holy Beasts, who guard the four cardinal points of the city and protect it from evil spirits. In [[Digimon Adventure 02 - Episode 37]], while protecting the last of the [[Holy Stones]], the Chosen Children use their [[D-3|D-3s]] on the stone to release Qinglongmon. Qinglongmon explains that the [[Dark Masters]] had sealed away the Four Holy Beasts, but remained sealed even as the Dark Masters were defeated. To break the seal, the Chosen had to return to the Digital World and give up the power of their Crests. ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure tri.}}=== Appears in [[Digimon Adventure tri. - Chapter 4|"Loss"]]. In a flashback the [[Dark Masters]] battle against the [[Four Holy Beasts]]. At the edge of defeat the Holy Beasts evolve. [[Xuanwumon]] is the evolved form of [[Orochimon]]. {| |- |[[Image:Four Holy Beasts tri.jpg|thumb|right|Four Holy Beasts in ''Digimon Adventure tri.''.]] |} ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Ryo's Story!}}=== ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD|Digimon Adventure 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD ~Digimon Mystery File: The Liberation of the Millennial Seal~}}=== [[Gennai]] tries to get help from the Four Holy Beasts to solve the problem in Odaiba. ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure 02: D-1 Tamers}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World 3}}=== While none of the Holy Beasts actually appear in-game, each of the four main cities on each server are named after the cardinal legendary beasts they are named after. ==={{hdr|Digital Monster: D-Project}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story}}=== Each of the Four Holy Beasts is an [[Observer]] over the individual ranks of [[Tamers]] ([[Qinglongmon]] of Beginner Tamers, [[Xuanwumon]] of Bronze Tamers, [[Baihumon]] of Silver Tamers, and [[Zhuqiaomon]] of Gold Tamers), with [[Clavis Angemon]] being in charge of the Platinum rank Tamers. All but Zhuqiaomon are later infected by a virus and are fought as boss battles. ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Crusader}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Fortune}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize}}=== The Four Holy Beasts appear as collectable cards. ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== The Four Holy Beasts appear as collectable cards. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth}}=== After [[Aiba Takumi]] or [[Aiba Ami]] collects all of the Four Holy Beasts medals, the Medal Man rewards them with 10 Exciting Meats and 3 Best Meats. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory}}=== After [[Amasawa Keisuke]] collects all of the Four Holy Beasts medals, the Medal Man rewards him with 10 Exciting Meats and 3 Best Meats. ==={{hdr|Digimon ReArise}}=== [[Xuanwumon]] is a member of the Four Holy Beasts. [[Vikaralamon]] had travelled to the Human World to the ascertain the threat after a large quantity of devices affected the [[Digital World]]. Xuanwumon however considered it to just be a "passing noise" and so travelled to the Human World to retrieve its subordinate. ==={{hdr|Digimon Survive}}=== In Part 9, Truth route, [[Minase Akiharu]] explained to [[Momotsuka Takuma]] how, in the past, five youths from Kamakura were offered to the [[Kemonogami]] to obtain their power. However, one of them had a wicked mind, causing the Kemonogami to turn savage. Due to that, the four strongest Kemonogami now sealed those wild Kemonogami from the four directions. The mural on the first floor of the inner shrine shows [[Huanglongmon]], while the one on the third floor shows these four strongest Kemonogami, which share aspects with the four gods that protected the north, south, east and west in ancient texts. According to Akiharu, the mural depicted how the [[Master]] was sealed by the four Kemonogami, and therefore, they should have clues about the Master since he was now running rampant. To learn about their locations, the team decides to go to the amusement park to meet with [[Jijimon]], who indeed knew about them and told them their true name, the ''Four Holy Beasts'', and how they existed to capture, supress and restrain the Master, but their power had weakened. However, no one knew where they were, as no one had seen them as long as Jijimon had lived. Nonetheless, Jijimon thought the Master would aim to destroy them to obtain his full power, so he could be used as a guide to them. The party met [[Baihumon]] soon after, finding his Western Shrine by accident while escaping from the Master. Baihumon easily killed several [[Kenzoku]], then attacked the party, though this turned out to be just a test from his part, which was ended by the appearance of the ghost of his partner, [[Byakko Boy]]. Byakko Boy explained how he and four more were sacrificed to obtain the power of the god beasts to govern Japan, but one of their own, [[Minase Haruchika]], was swallowed by said power and went berserk. As such, they now bound him, but wouldn't be able to do so much longer due to the fog destroying the world and the souls in it. Byakko Boy accepted Takuma's desire to save [[Minase Miyuki]], who had been taken by Haruchika, and instructed them to meet with the other three Holy Beasts, as those of the Western Shrine acknowledged their power, and the power of all Four Holy Beasts should be able to return the Master to normal. The Western Shrine was now safe, as they had learned of Byakko Boy and Baihumon and recovered their power to supress Haruchika, but the attacks on the other shrines would be even more fierce, and the other shrines were weakening in the order of north, east, south. While he could not tell them where the other shrines were, the fog would show them the way. Afterwards, they cleared the fog from the amusement park and gave [[Labramon (Survive)|Labramon]] the ability to evolve into Baihumon herself. Afterwards, the party met [[Xuanwumon]] in Part 10, thanks to [[Renamon (Survive)|Renamon]] disguised as Haru leading them to the Northern Shrine. However, he was already under attack by the Kenzoku, and was besides himself in pain. Renamon took the chance to reveal themselves and attack Akiharu to sacrifice him to the Master, thus giving him the power to defeat Xuanwumon, thinking that with the power he'd regain he'd have no more use for Miyuki and would free her. However, they're beaten as [[Taomon]], and the fight gave Xuanwumon enough time to get rid of the Kenzoku that attacked him. Angered at the situation, Xuanwumon attacked the party, thinking they were responsible for the Kenzoku. As Taomon pointed out to Takuma, they had a chance to win as Xuanwumon could not use his attack technique, as it had a charge time and he had used it against the Kenzoku, and furthermore, all Four Holy Beasts were weakened. However, Xuanwumon was still covered in a barrier that woudn't let any attack hit him. Takuma refused to give up in spite of this, and wished to find a way to calm Xuanwumon down and make him recognize their strength. Taomon then joined the party, as making the Four Holy Beasts recognize them would allow them to save Miyuki, and taunted Xuanwumon by pointing out that his barrier was a cheap trick, much like what Taomon did earlier. Despite recognizing it as a provocation, Xuanwumon nonetheless lowered his barrier and swore to crush Taomon and the rest with his full strength. Xuanwumon refused to give up and listen to Takuma even after being beaten, and he only stopped after the appearance of [[Genbu Boy]]'s ghost. While the ghost recognized their strength and how hard they trained, much like the five sacrifices did in the past, Xuanwumon arrogantly pointed out he had just been careless and he wouldn't lose again, though Genbu Boy wasn't as sure of that. Xuanwumon finally listened to Takuma's request to save Miyuki, though he deferred judgement to his partner, who trusted Takuma. As such, they agreed to do what they could, although Genbu Boy knew it wouldn't be enough, and instead offered them knowledge: how humans and Kemonogami were once two halves of a single soul, and how they grew stronger together than they could on their own. He also explained how the world was filled with things that couldn't be quantified with their own values, and Minase Haruchika gained his power by being the first to understand that. Genbu Boy finally asked the party if they were prepared to face the unimaginable difficulties coming their way, which Takuma and the rest resolutely confirmed. Thanks to that answer, and how stunned he was by their progress, Genbu Boy entrusted the party with Xuanwumon's power, giving [[Floramon (Survive)|Floramon]] the ability to evolve into Xuanwumon. They also cleared the fog from the second island. In Part 11, [[Garurumon (Survive)|Garurumon]] brought the party to [[Qinglongmon]]'s shrine, however, only Takuma, Akiharu, [[Agumon (Survive)|Agumon]] and Garurumon himself (now [[Gabumon]]) entered it. Unlike the previous two times, they immediately met [[Seiryū Boy]], who sensed Baihumon and Xuanwumon's "breaths" on Agumon but not Gabumon, who had just joined the team. Takuma asked him for his help, but Qinglongmon thought it'd be a waste of his power to give it to weak humans, when it should be used to supress the Master. He challenged Akiharu to pass his trial, and refused to allow Takuma to help. While they cleared the trial, Qinglongmon just followed it with his "final trial", which Garurumon also cleared after evolving into [[Metal Garurumon]]. Seiryū Boy acknowledged that Takuma and the rest may be able to defeat "him" if they could defeat Qinglongmon, with the Professor believing this "him" was one of the boys offered as a sacrifice. Seiryū Boy explained their past - how they became god beasts and united the world to create one devoid of hatred, but the Master could not abandon hatred, because he was betrayed and offered as a sacrifice by his own sister, a maiden he idolized. The others tried to stop him, but they could only supress his powers and barely so at that. As such, he entrusted the heroes to return Haruchika to his senses, and from that, save the world. Takuma and Akiharu accepted their mission to save Haruchika, and [[Shakomon (Survive)|Shakomon]] was given the power to evolve into Qinglongmon, as well as clearing the fog on the dam and waterway. In Part 12, [[Suzaku Boy]] appeared in the school to [[Tominaga Ryo]], [[Kimishima Saki]] and [[Hinata Minoru]], who are scared of him due to him being a ghost. Agumon realized he was [[Zhuqiaomon]]'s partner, though Takuma wondered why he'd leave his shrine. As the team searched the school, several paranormal phenomena happened, and everyone's [[Partner Digimon|Partner Kemonogami]] left them. Following Agumon, who had gone into an unresponsive state, Takuma found himself in the gym alongside everyone else who had lost their Digimon, and then an earthquake happened, though Renamon thought it had to do with a nearby shrine as they could feel the power of a Holy Beast. Using his camera, Takuma found a weak point in the floor, which hid an old well. After everyone entered the well, its floor broke, making them all fall into the South Shrine. In it, they met with Zhuqiaomon who had taken the form of a ball of light and was surrounded by the Partner Kemonogami. He denied their partnerships, as he thought humans only used Kemonogami. Despite that, Takuma asked him for his help, though Zhuqiaomon didn't believe they could succeed where the Four Holy Beasts had failed. Takuma refused to stand down and asked him to release Agumon, which only angered Zhuqiaomon, who saw it as proof they saw Kemonogami as nothing but tools, and exclaimed that they didn't understand his endless wrath. [[Kayama Shuuji]] asked him to explain said rage, and Zhuqiaomon agreed to do so, relating how a thousand years ago, he and his companions had met and formed bonds with humans, which made him happy, until they were betrayed by Haruchika. They combined their powers to fight him, but at the verge of victory, they were betrayed once again, this time by their own human partners, as they pitied Haruchika and were unable to deal him the finishing blow. Ultimately, the Four Holy Beasts were sacrificed to stop the Master by bounding them to the shrines. He planned to punish the humans for what he saw as decieving his allies, and used the Partner Kemonogami to do so, influenced by his rage. Takuma mananged to get Agumon to come back to his senses, followed by Ryo and Shuuji doing so for everyone else. Zhuqiaomon was forced to recognize the strength of their bond, although he still believed they'd be betrayed eventually, which Agumon denied. Zhuqiaomon decided to just kill everyone himself. After he was beaten, Suzaku Boy appeared in front of everyone, explaining that he wanted to lead everyone to the shrine as they had the blessings of the other Holy Beasts, but his powers were unstable outside hte shrine, causing him to fail to communicate properly. Zhuqiaomon was surprised at the appearance of his partner, and Suzaku Boy explained he had always been there but never had the right to answer Zhuqiaomon's calls. However, the other humans gave him the courage to act, and he apologized to his partner, then explained that there were originally five God Beasts, but Haruchika caused his own, [[Huanglongmon]], to [[Dark Evolution|Dark Evolve]] due to his hatred for his clan. Said Dark Evolution, the Master, fused from Haruchika and Huanglongmon, had immense power and drew in all sorts of malice from both worlds, which he used to try to cross over to the human world. However, he was stopped by the other four God Beasts, who became the Four Holy Beasts. The Master took in the dark emotions that were common of their era, and he planned to use them for revenge against the human world. While he was stopped in the past, he could now make that desire true using Miyuki. The Four Holy Beasts were doing all they could to stop him, but their failure was a matter of time. Takuma explained how they had to beat the Master and calm his rage, so that tragedy would not repeat itself once the were gone, which Suzaku Boy agreed with, and he asked Zhuqiaomon to put his faith in them. Zhuqiaomon finally agreed, as he didn't want them or their partners to pay for the mistakes of the Four Holy Beasts' own partners. As such, Zhuqiaomon gave them his blessing and made them promise not to fail their mission. Said blessing allowed [[Falcomon (Survive)|Falcomon]] to evolve into Zhuqiaomon. ==={{hdr|Digimon New Century}}=== ==V-Pets== ==={{hdr|D-3 Toy|D-3}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Twin}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum Z}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum Z II}}=== ==TCG== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster Card Game}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Battle Terminal 02}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Card Game}}=== ==References== {{R | }} [[Category:Four Holy Beasts| ]]

{{S2 |drbentry= |kan=フカモン |rom=Fukamon |ipa= |da={{DigiAlph|fu|ka|mon}} |da2={{DigiAlph2|f|u|k|a|mon}} |add= |dub= |dref= |ol= |image=Fukamon.jpg |imagesize=320x320 |l1=Baby I |l1ref= |a1= |a1ref= |t1= |t1ref= |s1= |p=NP |e={{NoProfile}} |j= |u= |ety={{JP}} {{ETY|Fuka}}
From {{ETY|Fukafuka}} (フカフカ) meaning "soft and fluffy". |dsgn=Fukamon and [[Mokokomon]] are both inspired by the Kintoun cloud, which appears in art depicting Sun Wukong performing the Cloud Somersault in the Journey to the West. Although both seem to be designed with the intention of evolving into Digimon related to [[Gokuwmon]] like [[Kakamon]], they are also designed to work with all the new Child Digimon introduced in the [[Digimon Pendulum COLOR|Digimon Pendulum COLOR 6 Saiyu Warriors]], namely Kakamon, [[Hujiamon]], and [[Takimon]].[https://digimon.net/topic/detail.php?id=1811 モンモンメモ‼Vol.125 「デジモンペンデュラムCOLOR 6/7」新デジモンについて。] |yd=2025 |ydr= |drbed= |drbedy= |ad= |md= |gd= |vd={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum COLOR}} |cd= }} =Attack Techniques= =Evolution= ==Evolves From== ==Evolves To== * [[Mokokomon]]{{ref|''[[Digimon Pendulum COLOR]]''}} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==Manga== ==Novel== ==Video Games== ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum COLOR}}=== ==TCG== =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Fukamon.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Web]] }} }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Fukamon vpet dpc.gif{{!}}80x80px |c1=[[Digimon Pendulum COLOR]] }} =Additional Information= {{R | }}

{{noun}} From {{ETY|Ami{{FGU|gasa}}}} {{ETYK|編笠|r=アミ{{FGM|ガサ}}}}, which is a straw hat of the type traditionally worn in some Japanese folk dances. |dsgn=Gasamon is modeled after the {{w|Tsukumogami}} (付喪神; lit. Hanimon.wikitext:
{{S2 |drbentry={{DRBEntry|1241|1242|1243|1244|2a=Kokeshimon|3a=Karakurumon|4a=Kaguyamon}} |kan=ハニモン |rom=Hanimon |ipa= |da={{DigiAlph|ha|ni|mon}} |da2={{DigiAlph2|h|a|n|i|mon}} |add= |dub= |dref= |ol={{ZHO}} 埴轮兽{{DD|ha}}
{{CHI}} 埴輪獸{{DD|ha}}
{{KOR}} 하니와몬{{DD|ha}} |image=Hanimon.jpg |imagesize=320x320 |l1=Child |l1ref={{DD|ha}} |a1=Data |a1ref={{DD|ha}} |t1=Puppet |t1ref={{DD|ha}} |s1= |pn=DRB |pe=A Puppet Digimon that primarily inhabits ruinous areas in the [[Digital World: Shambala|Eastern Digital World]]. Its appearance strongly resembles that of a {{w|haniwa}}, and its body is made of clay. It looks sturdy, but it is more brittle than what it is made out to be due to it being composed of clay data. Although it instantly forms cracks and becomes chipped, it can occasionally be seen meticulously repairing itself.{{br}}Despite having an appearance where you cannot tell what it is thinking at first glance, its personality is gentle, and it is also friendly towards other species. However, it absolutely hates the hair on its head being touched, and flies into a rage when touched there, so caution is necessary.{{br}}It possesses holy power, albeit weak, and by dancing together, multiple Hanimon can create a barrier that wards off Dark-species Digimon.{{br}}Its Special Moves are firing strange beams of light from its eyes, both attacking and blinding the enemy ({{AT|Haniwa Flash}}), and a full-force ramming attack that risks shattering its body ({{AT|w=Kofun}} {{AT|Tackle}}). |pj=東方のデジタルワールドの中でも、主に遺跡地帯に生息しているパペット型のデジモン。その姿はハニワによく似ており、身体は土で構成されている。硬そうな見た目ではあるが、土のデータで構成されているために印象より脆い。すぐにヒビが入ったり欠けてしまったりするが、ちまちまと修復している姿が目撃されることもある。
　一見すると何を考えているか分からない見た目をしているが、性格は温厚そのもので、他の種族にも友好的である。ただし、頭部に生えた髪の毛に触れられることを極端に嫌い、触ると怒り出すため注意が必要だ。
　微弱ながらも聖なる力を持っており、複数のハニモンが踊ることで暗黒系のデジモンを退散させる結界を作り出すことができる。
　必殺技は、目から怪光線を放ち、目くらましを兼ねた攻撃を行う『{{ATK|ハニワフラッシュ}}』と、身体が割れてしまう危険性を秘めた全力の体当たり『{{ATK|コフンタックル}}』。 |pu=hanimon |pn2=Anime & Manga |pun2=Digimon Liberator |pe2= |pj2={{fc|ミライが決めたことさね。わらわは従うと決めている|#54139e}}{{br}}デジモンリベレイターβテスト時代、エメラルドコーストに突如出現した竹藪にて発見された新種のデジモン。古風なのか今風なのか判断がつかない独特の喋り方は生まれつきのもの。偉そうな態度を取っているように見えるが、その実は慈愛に満ちた心優しい性格であり、パートナーである観来のことを全面的に信頼している。観来曰く、肝っ玉母ちゃん。 |pu2=https://digimoncard.com/digimon_liberator/jp/character/ |ety={{JP}} {{ETY|Hani}}

'''Hell's Field''' (ヘルズフィールド ''Heruzu Fīrudo'') (Dub: ''Digital Underworld'') is a an area underneath [[Bright Land]].

=Description=
Hell's Field is an area created underneath Bright Land. It contains countless hexagonal fields, each based on a different human interpretation of the underworld from various mythologies, with a white castle and black castle at either end of the field. All communications are disrupted within this area, minus the stones in each of the chess-themed castles.

Himi Tomoki.wikitext:
{{FArticle|63}}{{Char |name=Himi Tomoki |image=Tomoki.png |imagesize= |kan=氷見　友樹 |dub=Tommy Himi, Tommy Yomi{{ref|''[[Digimon Frontier]]'' Iberian dub}} |age=9, 8{{ref|''[[Digimon Frontier]]'' Iberian dub}} |school= |dv=Cyan/Green [[D-Scanner]] |spirits=Ice |vjp=[[Watanabe Kumiko]] (渡辺 久美子) |ven=[[Brianne Siddall]] |family=Father and mother (Unnamed), [[Himi Yutaka]] (Older brother) }} '''Himi Tomoki''' is one of the protagonists in ''[[Digimon Frontier]]''. =Evolution= {{Box Hybrid |Human = '''[[Chackmon]]''' |Human2 = [[Arbormon]]{{ref|''[[D-Scanner Toy|D-Tector Version 3.0]]''}} |Beast = [[Blizzarmon]] |Beast2= [[Petaldramon]]{{ref|''[[D-Scanner Toy|D-Tector Version 3.0]]''}} |Fusion = [[Daipenmon]]{{ref|''[[D-Scanner Toy|D-Tector Version 3.0]]''}} |Ultimate = [[Ancient Megatheriumon]]{{ref|''[[Battle Spirit: Digimon Frontier]]''}} |Ultimate2 = [[Susanoomon]] }} ==Card Game Evolution== * [[Agnimon]]{{rfc|BT4|011 (DCG)}} * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Evolves From Tamers|Any Digimon that also Evolves from non-White Tamers from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{Note|[[BT18-078 (DCG)]] Duskmon can change any Tamer's Color into any other Color, except White.|n=DuskTamerColor}} * [[Blizzarmon]]{{rfc|BT7|023 (DCG)}} * [[Calamaramon]]{{rfc|BT12|025 (DCG)}} * [[Chackmon]]{{rfc|BT7|021 (DCG)}} * [[Daipenmon]]{{rfc|BT18|026 (DCG)}} * [[Fairimon]]{{rfc|BT18|048 (DCG)}} * [[Garummon]]''[[BT4-027 (DCG)|BT4-027]]''''[[BT7-022 (DCG)|BT7-022]]'' * [[Gigasmon]]{{rfc|BT18|014 (DCG)}} * [[Grottemon]]{{rfc|BT18|012 (DCG)}} * [[Jet Silphymon]]{{rfc|BT18|053 (DCG)}} * [[Kaiser Greymon]]{{rfc|BT18|095 (DCG)}} * [[Magna Garurumon]]{{rfc|BT18|097 (DCG)}} * [[Ranamon]]''[[BT12-024 (DCG)|BT12-024]]'' * [[Shutumon]]{{rfc|BT18|049 (DCG)}} * [[Susanoomon]]{{rfc|BT7|112 (DCG)}} * [[Vritramon]]{{rfc|BT7|011 (DCG)}} * [[Wolfmon]]''[[BT4-025 (DCG)|BT4-025]]'' =Profile= Himi Tomoki is a [[Chosen Child]], chosen by [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]] to inherit the Legendary Spirits of Ice. He is the youngest of the group, and starts out as a bit of a crybaby. However, he is also kind-hearted, brave and modest, and quickly begins to mature through his experiences in the [[Digital World]]. He will not tolerate any bullying, which comes from his experiences of being bullied in the [[Real World]]. Tomoki was always spoiled by his parents, who gave in to his every whim and bought him all the toys and candy he desired. His much-older brother, [[Himi Yutaka|Yutaka]], disapproved of his parents' way of raising Tomoki. He refused to pamper Tomoki, telling his younger brother that he'll never learn how to do things by himself if he will rely on other people. Because of this, Tomoki resented Yutaka, and thought his brother did not love him. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== Tomoki was being bullied by [[Katsuharu]] and [[Teppei]], two older boys from his school, when he received [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]]'s call. The two bullies dragged him to Shibuya Station and pushed Tomoki onto one of the trains in the underground terminal against his will. On the train, he met [[Kanbara Takuya]], [[Shibayama Junpei]] and [[Orimoto Izumi]]. Though he wanted to go home at first and almost got himself killed trying to walk on the tracks back to the Real World, seeing Takuya evolve into [[Agnimon]] and saving him changed his mind. Because of this event, Tomoki became very fond of Takuya, thinking of him as a hero and a surrogate older brother. Soon, he, too, found the [[Human Spirit of Ice]] and evolved into [[Chackmon]]. When a group of [[Tocanmon]] stole the children's [[D-Scanner|D-Scans]], it was Tomoki who found them at a [[Nanomon|Nanomon's]] pawn shop. However, Nanomon wanted Tomoki to trade something in for them, and Tomoki set off to find the Toucanmon, who traded the D-Scans in for an old camera. He found them in a frigid area, and when he tried to take the camera, the Tocanmon fell through the ice. He rescued the birds out of the goodness of his heart, but the ungrateful Toucanmon fled with the camera, intending to trade it back for the D-Scans. However, when Nanomon checked the camera, he saw the footage of Tomoki's kind act which was recorded on it, and was so impressed with Tomoki's honesty that he gave him the D-Scans. Additionally, he installed the [[Beast Spirit of Ice]] into Tomoki's D-Scan, which allowed him to evolve into [[Blizzarmon]]. When [[Mercuremon (Frontier)|Sephirothmon]] sucked the Chosen Children into his body, Tomoki eventually found himself alone in the Fire Sphere, where he was attached by a mysterious Digimon. After he escaped, he met a cloaked Digimon named [[Asuramon]], who accompanied him in his search for the other Chosen Children. When Tomoki was tired and thirsty, Asuramon declared his friendship and volunteered to get water for him, which reminded Tomoki of how his pampered behavior angered his brother Yutaka. In the end, it became clear that Asuramon was after Tomoki's Spirits, and he revealed himself to be the same Digimon that attacked Tomoki earlier. When Asuramon mocked Tomoki for not having friends, Tomoki realized what Yutaka meant when he said he should be more self-reliant. After purifying Asuramon's data, he vowed to tell his brother that he loves him as soon as he returns from the Digital World. When they were fighting the [[Royal Knights]], the Chosen Children met four other children who came to the Digital World with the Trailmon. Though they weren't chosen to inherit any Legendary Spirit, these children decided to stay in the Digital World and explore it, and were guarded by an [[Angemon]] sent by Ofanimon. Two of the children were Katsuharu and Teppei, who bullied Tomoki before. Though they tried to bully him some more, he wouldn't stand for it, having grown more self-reliant and confident with time. He ended up saving their lives twice, and they began to respect and like him. ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Revival of the Ancient Digimon!!}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== After [[Magnamon]] ran out of power and devolved to [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] in the fight against [[Quartzmon (Xros Wars)|Quartzmon's]] [[Belial Vamdemon]] and [[Venom Vamdemon]] army in "''[[Digimon Xros Wars - Episode 78|The Gathering of the Legendary Heroes! The Digimon All-star Showdown]]''", [[Ichijouji Ken]] appeared alongside [[Wormmon (Adventure)|Stingmon]], followed by [[Ishida Yamato]], [[Gabumon (Adventure)|Metal Garurumon]], and balls of light containing Chackmon, [[Minamoto Kouji|Wolfmon]], [[Orimoto Izumi|Fairimon]] and [[Shibayama Junpei|Blitzmon]]. The Spirits then Ancient Spirit Evolved with [[Kanbara Takuya|Aldamon]] to become [[Susanoomon]], who kept a Venom Vamdemon in place for [[Imperialdramon: Fighter Mode]] to end the three with his {{ATO|Giga Death}}. Susanoomon would stay in the form for the rest of the fight against Quartzmon. ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier Original Story: Things That Want To Be Told}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Series 1-4: Original Story Special ~with~}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD ~A Train Named Hope~}}=== ==Live Readings== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Another Mysterious Message! The '''Human Spirit of Darkness''' (闇のヒューマンスピリット ''Yami no Hyūman Supiritto''), also known as the '''Spirit of Darkness L''' (闇のスピリットL ''Yami no Supiritto Eru'') is one of the twenty [[Legendary Spirits]]. It contains the power of [[Ancient Sphinxmon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]], and allows its user to evolve into the Human Form [[Hybrid]] [[Digimon]] [[Löwemon]].

=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}===
[[Image:Human Spirit of Darkness.JPG|right|thumb|The Human Spirit of Darkness in ''Digimon Frontier''.]]
The Human Spirit of Darkness was created from [[Ancient Sphinxmon]]. It was found at some point by [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]], who corrupted it into the [[Corrupted Human Spirit of Darkness]] and forced it on [[Kimura Kouichi]], turning him into [[Duskmon]]. The spirit revealed its true form after being purified through [[Minamoto Kouji|Minamoto Kouji's]] [[D-Scanner]], when Kouichi accepted that neither he nor the spirit was inherently evil. It then allowed him to spirit evolve into [[Löwemon]].

The '''Human Spirit of Ice''' (氷のヒューマンスピリット ''Kōri no Hyūman Supiritto''), also known as the '''Spirit of Ice H''' (氷のスピリットH ''Kōri no Supiritto Eichi'') is one of the twenty [[Legendary Spirits]]. It contains the power of [[Ancient Megatheriumon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]], and allows its user to evolve into the Human Form [[Hybrid]] [[Digimon]] [[Chackmon]].

=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}===
[[Image:Human Spirit of Ice.JPG|right|thumb|The Human Spirit of Ice in ''Digimon Frontier''.]]
The Human Spirit of Ice was created from [[Ancient Megatheriumon]] after his battle with [[Lucemon (Frontier)|Lucemon]] in the past. From there it passed to [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]]'s keeping, where she hid it in an icy cave. When [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]] betrayed the [[Digital World (Frontier)|Digital World]] and began his conquest of it, locking [[Seraphimon (Frontier)|Seraphimon]] into an endless sleep and imprisoning Ofanimon in a prison of light, she was able to call Legendary Chosen to the Digital World. From them, the Spirit of Ice chose [[Himi Tomoki|Tomoki]] to possess it, and so he was able to spirit evolve into [[Chackmon]].

It contains the power of [[Ancient Beatmon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]], and allows its user to evolve into the Human Form [[Hybrid]] [[Digimon]] [[Blitzmon]].

=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}===
[[Image:Human Spirit of Thunder.JPG|thumb|right|The Human Spirit of Thunder in ''Digimon Frontier''.]]
The Human Spirit of Thunder was created from [[Ancient Beatmon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]]. After [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]]'s revolt, it was hidden in the [[Factory of Wind]]. It chose [[Shibayama Junpei]] to wield it when it was uncovered. From then on, it allowed him to spirit evolve into [[Blitzmon]].

It contains the power of [[Ancient Mermaimon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]], and allows its user to evolve into the Human Form [[Hybrid]] [[Digimon]] [[Ranamon]]. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== [[Image:Human Spirit of Water.JPG|right|thumb|The Human Spirit of Water in ''Digimon Frontier''.]] The Human Spirit of Water was created from [[Ancient Mermaimon]] after her battle with [[Lucemon (Frontier)|Lucemon]] in the past. From there it passed to [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]]'s keeping. When Cherubimon betrayed the [[Digital World (Frontier)|Digital World]], he transformed it into [[Ranamon (Frontier)|Ranamon]]. When Ranamon is defeated by [[Orimoto Izumi|Izumi]] in [[DF26|''"Ranamon's Tenacity! Female Digimon Personal Combat"'']] it is trapped inside her [[D-Scanner]] until she gives it to [[Minamoto Kouji|Kouji]] in [[DF35|''"Spirits Become One! The '''Human Spirit of Wind''' (風のヒューマンスピリット ''Kaze no Hyūman Supiritto''), also known as the '''Spirit of Wind H''' (風のスピリットH ''Kaze no Supiritto Eichi'') is one of the twenty [[Legendary Spirits]]. It contains the power of [[Ancient Irismon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]], and allows its user to evolve into the Human Form [[Hybrid]] [[Digimon]] [[Fairimon]].

=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}===
[[Image:Human Spirit of Wind.JPG|thumb|right|The Human Spirit of Wind in ''Digimon Frontier''.]]
The Human Spirit of Wind was created from [[Ancient Irismon]] of the [[Warrior Ten]]. After [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]]'s revolt, it was hidden in a huge tree near where some [[Floramon]] lived. It chose [[Orimoto Izumi]] to wield it. She can then use it to spirit evolve into [[Fairimon]]. 

The spirit was stolen by [[Gigasmon]] for a while, but was rightfully returned short after [[Bolgmon]] defeated Gigasmon.

Hybrid.wikitext:
[[File:Ancientgreymon digimonweb.png|thumb|right|[[Agnimon]] and [[Vritramon]], two Hybrid Digimon, pictured with the [[Warrior Ten]] Digimon from whom their [[Legendary Spirits|Spirits]] are derived, [[Ancient Greymon]].]] '''Hybrid''' (ハイブリッド体 ''Haiburiddo-tai'') is an artificial [[Evolution Stage]] comprised of [[Digimon (species)|Digimon]] who have been created using the [[Legendary Spirits]] of the [[Warrior Ten]]. =Overview= ==Origin== In the aftermath of their battle against [[Lucemon]]{{refd|Ancient Greymon|ancientgreymon}} in ancient times, the [[Warrior Ten]] left behind the [[Legendary Spirits]] in various places around the world, intending them to be used in case of some future crisis.''[[Digital Monster Card Game]]'' [[Starter Ver. 7|Starter Ver. 7: "Hybrid Warriors"]] [[Starter Ver. 7#Back Story|(Back Story)]] When humans were summoned to the Digital World to use the Spirits, the result was a new species: Hybrid Digimon.''[[Digital Monster Card Game]]'' [[Booster 15|Booster 15: "Burning Beast Fighter"]] [[Booster 15#Back Story|(Back Story)]] A connection has also been drawn between the origin of Hybrids and the creation of [[Dukemon: Crimson Mode]], believed to have been made possible when the Spirit of a human [[Tamer]] mediated the resonance between [[Dukemon]] and [[ZERO-ARMS: Grani]]. ==Properties== Hybrid Digimon are the result of humans fusing with the [[Legendary Spirit]]s to become Digimon who have been granted the power of the Warrior Ten by the Spirits. They have been characterized as being born when the digital data-based Digimon are "transformative multiplied" by the innate potential of analog humans, and as having unknown power. However, despite the strong association between Hybrids and humans, use of the Spirits is not exclusive to humans, as Digimon are capable of doing so as well. Each Hybrid Digimon has power over one of [[Legendary Spirits#List of Spirits|ten elemental powers]]—Fire, Light, Darkness, Thunder, Wind, Ice, Earth, Wood, Water, and Steel—derived from the Spirit(s) used to create it. Hybrid Digimon generally have the [[:Category:Variable Attribute|Variable]] [[Attribute]], which changes their Attribute in response to the environment. [[Hybrid#Power Level|The power level of Hybrid Digimon varies,]] depending on the type and quantity of spirits that are used to create them, and can fall anywhere between [[Child]]-[[Evolution Stage#Class|class]] to [[Ultimate]]-class power. Hybrid Digimon are all of the [[:Category:Variable Attribute|Variable]] attribute, meaning that their attribute changes in accordance with their current circumstances. Although it is also created using Spirits, [[Susanoomon]] has never been classified as a Hybrid, and is instead a conventional Ultimate Digimon. ==Forms== Hybrids are divided into five categories, called '''Forms''' (形態 ''Keitai''), based on the type and quantity of spirits that are used to create them. {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;font-size:90%;width:70%" |- !style="background: #262626;width:15%;"|Name !style="background: #262626;"|Description !style="background: #262626;width:150px;"|Example !style="background: #262626;width:15%"|First Appearance |- |style="text-align:center;" id="Child Form"|''(unnamed)'' |A half-human, half-beast Hybrid Digimon that is the result of a Human Form Hybrid Digimon losing its power. Despite the power reduction, [[Adult]]-class Digimon are still not a match for it.{{refd|Flamon}}{{refd|Strabimon|storabimon}}
'''Evolution Stage equivalent:''' [[Child]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Storabimon.jpg|150px]]
[[Strabimon]] |[[D-Scanner 1.0]] |- |style="text-align:center;" id="Human Form"|'''Human Form'''
({{j2|人型形態|Ningatakeitai}};
Dub: ''Human Hybrid'') |A Hybrid Digimon created using one Human Spirit. It excels in agility and stability, but [[Human and Beast Digimon|the human side's]] strengths and weaknesses exert a significant influence.
'''Evolution Stage equivalent:''' Varies, usually [[Adult]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Wolfmon.jpg|150px]]
[[Wolfmon]] |[[Digital Monster Card Game]] [[Booster 14|Booster 14: ''"The Radiant Crimson Knight of Heavenly Wings"'']] |- |style="text-align:center;" id="Beast Form"|'''Beast Form'''
({{j2|獣型形態|Jūgatakeitai}};
Dub: ''Beast Hybrid'') |A Hybrid Digimon created using one Beast Spirit. [[Human and Beast Digimon|Its more animalistic nature]] has been unleashed, and while it boasts power that surpasses [[Ultimate]] Digimon, it struggles to control its savage impulses and is prone to running wild.
'''Evolution Stage equivalent:''' Varies, usually [[Adult]] to [[Perfect]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Garummon.jpg|150px]]
[[Garummon]] |[[Digital Monster Card Game]] [[Booster 15|Booster 15: ''"The Super Beast Warrior of Bursting Flames"'']] |- |style="text-align:center;" id="Fusion Form"|'''Fusion Form'''
({{j2|融合形態|Yūgōkeitai}};
Dub: ''Fusion form''/''Hybrid Combo''[[D-Spirit]]) |A Hybrid Digimon created using both the Human Spirit and Beast Spirit of the same element. This "strongest Form" is the result of bringing the Human and Beast Forms into harmony, and fuses the human's intellect with the Digimon's vitality. It has inherited all of the might of one of the Warrior Ten and has therefore acquired unknown powers and transcended legend.{{refd|Aldamon}}{{refd|Beowolfmon}}
'''Evolution Stage equivalent:''' Varies, usually [[Perfect]] to [[Ultimate]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Beowolfmon.jpg|150px]]
[[Beowolfmon]] |[[Digital Monster Card Game]] [[Booster 16|Booster 16: ''"The True Super-Fusion Warriors"'']] |- |style="text-align:center;" id="Transcendent Form"|'''Transcendent Form'''
({{j2|超越形態|Chōetsukeitai}};
Dub: ''Transcendent form''/''Extra Hybrid'') |A Hybrid Digimon created using a specific combination of ten Spirits. It is said to have surpassed even the power of the Warrior Ten.{{refd|Kaiser Greymon|kaisergreymon}}{{refd|Magna Garurumon|magnagarurumon}}
'''Evolution Stage equivalent:''' [[Ultimate]] !style="background: #fff;"|[[File:Magnagarurumon.jpg|150px]]
[[Magna Garurumon]] |[[Digital Monster Card Game]] [[Booster 17|Booster 17: ''"The Transcendental Warrior of Radiant Flame"'']] |- |} ==Power Level== It is common to rate the power levels of the Hybrid Digimon Forms on the scale of conventional Evolution Stages. The mechanics of video and card games often require that all Digimon, including Hybrids, be treated in some way as either equivalent to a conventional Evolution Stage, or treated as an ordinary Digimon who outright belongs to one of these Stages. However, attempts to do so have varied over the years and often disagree with each other. * '''''[[Digimon Frontier]]'':'''
The concept of Evolution Stages is not mentioned at all, but the show presents each Form as a linear progression in power. Human Form Hybrids are depicted defeating Digimon as high-leveled as [[Perfect]].{{rfe|DF|01|The Legendary Warrior! Agnimon of Flame}} Beast Form Hybrids are so much more powerful than Human Form Hybrids that defeating them is impossible without another Beast Form Hybrid.{{rfe|DF|09|Chakkumon's the Enemy!? The Mysterious Forest of Televisions}} Fusion Form Hybrids are likewise significantly more powerful than Beast Form Hybrids, and Transcendent Form Hybrids are significantly more powerful than Fusion Form Hybrids and the only Hybrids who are a match for Ultimate-level opponents like [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]] and the [[Royal Knights]]. [[Duskmon]] and [[Velgrmon]] are major exceptions to this progression, as they are depicted as being a match for Fusion Form Hybrid Digimon.{{rfe|DF|27|Double Spirit Miracle! Beowolfmon is Born}}{{rfe|DF|32|The Past is Revealed! Duskmon's Secret}} * '''[[Digital Monster Card Game]]:'''
Hybrid Digimon (excluding [[Flamon]] and [[Strabimon]], who are just assigned [[Child|Level III (Child)]]) are always marked as belonging to the Hybrid level in the conventional Evolution Stage field of the card, but some Hybrid Digimon cards also denote a [[Evolution Stage#Class|class]]. Human Form Hybrid Digimon are rated as [[:Category:Adult Class|Level IV (Adult) Class]]; Beast Form Hybrid Digimon are rated as [[:Category:Perfect Class|Perfect Class]]; and Fusion Form and Transcendent Form Hybrid Digimon are rated as [[:Category:Ultimate Class|Ultimate Class]]. * '''''[[Digimon Racing]]'':'''
[[Agnimon]]'s three playable forms are all assigned natural Evolution Stages. Flamon is a Baby II Digimon, Agnimon is a Child Digimon, and [[Vritramon]] is an [[Adult]] Digimon. * '''[[Digital Monster Card Game Alpha]]:'''
Hybrid Digimon are always marked as belonging to the Hybrid level in the conventional Evolution Stage field of the card, but each include errata that instructs the player to treat the card as if it belongs to a specified natural Evolution Stage. Human Form Hybrid Digimon are treated as Level IV (Adult) Digimon; Beast Form Hybrid Digimon are treated as Perfect Digimon; and Fusion Form Hybrid Digimon are treated as [[Ultimate]] Digimon. Transcendent Form Hybrids are not present, and neither are Flamon or Strabimon. * '''''[[Digimon Masters]]'':'''
Each Form has its own separate Evolution Stage, which are each classed as roughly equivalent in power to a natural Evolution Stage. The "H-Hybrid" (Human Form Hybrid) stage is Adult-class, but slightly stronger; the "B-Hybrid" (Beast Form Hybrid) stage is Perfect-class; and the "Z-Hybrid" (Transcendent Form Hybrid) stage is Ultimate-class. Fusion Form Hybrids are not present, and neither are Flamon or Strabimon. * '''''[[Digimon Adventure (Game)]]'':'''
Two Hybrid Digimon appear in the game, the Human Form Agnimon and the Transcendent Form Kaiser Greymon. The game's Reference Book gives them no stated Evolution Stage (appearing as "-"). Since Agnimon only appears in a cutscene, its power in the game is unknown, but Kaiser Greymon is treated as equivalent to an Ultimate-level in terms of power. * '''''[[Digimon World -next 0rder-]]'':'''
Agnimon (Human Form Hybrid) is assigned the Perfect level; and Transcendent Form Hybrid Digimon assigned the Ultimate level. No Beast Form or Fusion Form Hybrid Digimon are present, and neither are Flamon or Strabimon. * '''''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory]]'':'''
Agnimon and [[Wolfmon]] (Human Form Hybrids) are assigned the Adult level; Vritramon and [[Garummon]] (Beast Form Hybrids) are assigned the Perfect level; and Transcendent Form Hybrid Digimon are assigned the Ultimate level. Fusion Form Hybrids are not present, and neither are Flamon or Strabimon. * '''[[Digimon Card Game]]:'''
Hybrid Digimon are labeled accordingly in the Evolution Stage field on card errata, but for gameplay purposes they are also folded into the numerical "Level" system of Evolution Stages. Flamon and Strabimon are Lv.3 (Child); Human Form and Beast Form Hybrid Digimon are Lv.4 (Adult); Fusion Form Hybrid Digimon are Lv.5 (Perfect); and Transcendent Form Hybrid Digimon are Lv.6 (Ultimate). * '''[[Vital Bracelet Digital Monster]]:'''
Hybrid Digimon are assigned conventional Evolution Stages based on their Form. Flamon and Strabimon are assigned the Child level; Human Form Hybrid Digimon are assigned the Adult level; Beast Form and Fusion Form Hybrid Digimon are assigned the Perfect level; and Transcendent Form Hybrid Digimon are assigned the Ultimate level. When Hybrid Digimon are incorporated into evolution lines in traditional ''Digimon'' virtual pets, there is little to no consistency of assignment to Evolution Stages. For example, looking at Beast Form Hybrid Digimon, the [[Digimon Pendulum Z]] depicts Vritramon as an Adult Digimon, but the [[Digimon Accel Ultimate Genome]] depicts [[Bolgmon]] as an Ultimate Digimon. ==Terminology== The term "Hybrid"—sometimes given as {{ETYK|{{jap|超融合|ハイブリッド}}}}, i.e. as ''{{w|furigana}}'' for the ''{{w|kanji}}'' 超融合 ''chōyūgō'' (lit. "super fusion")''[[Digital Monster Card Game]]'' [[Booster 14|Booster 14: "Crimson Hylander"]] [[Booster 14#Back Story|(Back Story)]]—refers to how these Digimon can be literal hybrids of humans and Digimon, who are created when a human fuses with a Spirit. However, use of the Spirits [[Hybrid#Properties|is not exclusive to humans]].''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report014/ Digi-Basics: "Hybrids" and "Spirits"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 014|Translation on Wikimon]]) =Additional Information= {{R}} =See Also= * [[:Category:Hybrid Level]] {{Evo}} [[Category:Hybrid Level| ]] [[Category:Evolution]]

Ivan seems to have difficulty containing his inner thoughts, and tends to blurt out what he's thinking without realizing it. After meeting [[Fujieda Yoshino|Yoshino]] (calling her his "Beloved Honey") for the first time, he developed some sort of attraction towards her. After being defeated by [[Rosemon]], it was revealed the he worked for Kurata to earn money for his family. He was last seen in the finale with his siblings, trying to help stop the real world and the [[Digital World]] from colliding. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Savers}}=== =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Ivan.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Savers]] |i2=Biostegomon2.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c2=[[Digimon Savers]]

Kakamon.wikitext:
{{S2 |drbentry= |kan=カカモン |rom=Kakamon |ipa= |da={{DigiAlph|ka|ka|mon}} |da2={{DigiAlph2|k|a|k|a|mon}} |add= |dub= |dref= |ol= |image=Kakamon.jpg |imagesize=320x320 |l1=Child |l1ref= |a1= |a1ref= |t1= |t1ref= |s1= |p=NP |e={{NoProfile}} |j= |u= |ety={{JP}} {{ZHO}} {{ETY|Kaka}}
From {{ETY|{{FGU|Kaka}}zan}} {{ETYK|{{FGM|花果}}山}}; the Japanese name of {{w|Mount Huaguo}}, the mountain that {{w|Sun Wukong}} is said to have been born on in the novel {{w|Journey to the West}}.[https://digimon.net/topic/detail.php?id=1811 モンモンメモ‼Vol.125 「デジモンペンデュラムCOLOR 6/7」新デジモンについて。] |dsgn=Kakamon is inspired by the {{w|Golden snub-nosed monkey}}, found in Southwest China, and the {{w|François' langur}}, a lanky species of primate found in Southeast Asia. Kakamon was designed alongside [[Seiten Gokuwmon]], with [[Gokuwmon]] in mind, as depictions of the Sun Wukong character from Journey to the West. Sun Wukong was born from "stone", however Kakamon seems to subvert that by wearing headphones, in reference to "rock" music. This motif is seen in other Digimon like [[Shoutmon]]. Gokuwmon was introduced during the time frame of [[Digimon Xros Wars]] and has interacted with Shoutmon within that series. Fittingly, Kakamon’s mohawk hair is inspired by the "rocker" aesthetic found in pop culture. Kakamon’s partial dogi seems to be inspired by ''{{w|Dragon Ball}}''’s {{w|Goku|Son Goku}}, another depiction of Sun Wukong; in specific, the dogi Goku wears in his childhood and early adulthood. Bearing this in mind, Kakamon’s name may double as an allusion to ''Kakarot'', another name that the ''Dragon Ball'' character is known by. |yd=2025 |ydr={{Note|n=A|Kakamon's initial artwork was illustrated by [[sasasi]].}} |drbed= |drbedy= |ad= |md= |gd= |vd={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum COLOR}} |cd= }} =Attack Techniques= =Evolution= ==Evolves From== * [[Mokokomon]]{{ref|''[[Digimon Pendulum COLOR]]''}} ==Evolves To== =Appearances= ==Anime== ==Manga== ==Novel== ==Video Games== ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum COLOR}}=== ==TCG== =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Kakamon.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Web]] }} }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Kakamon vpet dpc.gif{{!}}80x80px |c1=[[Digimon Pendulum COLOR]] }} =Additional Information= {{R | }}

(DCG)|BT4-013]]''''[[BT7-011 (DCG)|BT7-011]]''''[[BT12-013 (DCG)|BT12-013]]'' * [[Wolfmon]]{{rfc|BT17|022 (DCG)}} =Profile= Kanbara Takuya is a [[Chosen Child]], chosen by [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]] to inherit the Legendary Spirits of Fire. He is an energetic and adventurous person. He has a good heart and a strong sense of justice, and tries to do what he believes his right. He is hotheaded and stubborn, often acting before he thinks, which causes some conflicts with the other Chosen Children. He seems to have a soft spot for younger children, most likely due to having a younger brother himself. An athletic boy, he is a member of his school's soccer club. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== He was waiting for his younger brother [[Kanbara Shinya|Shinya's]] birthday party to begin when he received Ofanimon's call. Traveling from his home in Jiyuugaoka to Shibuya Station, he was nearly late, but managed to catch a [[Trailmon]] with [[Shibayama Junpei]], [[Orimoto Izumi]] and [[Himi Tomoki]]. When they reached the Fire Terminal in the Digital World, he was the first to find and use the [[Human Spirit of Fire]], evolving into [[Agnimon]]. [[Seraphimon (Frontier)|Seraphimon]] gives him the power to Double Spirit Evolve, with which he becomes [[Aldamon]]. After [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]] upgrades his [[D-Scanner]] he is able to combines his two Sspirits along with the Spirits of Earth, Wood, Wind, and Ice to Hyper Spirit Evolve into [[Kaiser Greymon]]. When he and [[Minamoto Kouji]] (and Izumi, Tomoki and Junpei on the second time) combine with all of the Spirits they become [[Susanoomon]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Revival of the Ancient Digimon!!}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== [[Image:Gogglehead1.png|200px|right]] A silhouette of Takuya appeared on [[Old Clock Shop Man]]'s boat at the end of the {{epn|XW|68|The Great Hunter Gathering! The Digimon Competition at South Island!}}. Takuya returned in {{epn|XW|78|The Gathering of the Legendary Heroes! The Digimon All-star Showdown!!}} and fought against the army of [[Belial Vamdemon]] and [[Venom Vamdemon]] created by [[Quartzmon (Xros Wars)|Quartzmon]] first as [[Aldamon]] and then as [[Susanoomon]]. ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Legendary Skies}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|Digimon Xros Wars}}=== During "''[[XWM22|BONUS TRACK]]''", [[Wizarmon (Adventure)|Wizarmon]] saw other universes. In one of them, he saw Takuya spirit-evolving into [[Agnimon]]. {| |[[File:Wizarmon's vision XW.jpg |thumb|Wizarmon's vision in "''BONUS TRACK''".]] |} ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Original Story - Things I Want to Tell You}}=== Takuya's audio recording is addressed to his family, and he apologizes for leaving so suddenly right before his little brother Shinya's birthday party. He sings a song to try and persuade everyone he is doing well. He lets slip that he went to the Digital World, and demonstrates Spirit Evolving to prove to Shinya he's telling the truth. ==={{hdr|Get the Biggest Fire!!|Digimon Frontier: Get the Biggest Fire!!}}=== Takuya and [[Kouji]] (as a joke) are in a recording studio because they are going to sing the opening for their next season, Frontier 02. They are both exceptionally bad at singing, and eventually quit. ==={{hdr|Digimon Series 1-4: Original Story Special ~with~}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD ~A Train Named Hope~}}=== Takuya talks to [[Tomoki]] on a train about Tomoki's goal to become the student council president as a first year, and speaks about his own goals to be on the top Seria team in Italy. ==Live Readings== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Another Mysterious Message! Who’s the Culprit?}}=== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Battle Spirit: Digimon Frontier}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Racing}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Battle Chronicle}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure (Game)|Digimon Adventure}}=== Takuya, as [[Agnimon]], first appeared in Episode 70, "''Dragon Warrior, Kaiser Greymon''". He exclaimed "you're back", confusing the others who had never seen him before. Agnimon thought they were mocking him and attempted to attack [[Agumon (Adventure)|Agumon]] but [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure)|Yagami Taichi]] got in his way. Agnimon asked who he was, and Taichi acknowledged he didn't understand the situation but was sure Agnimon mistook Agumon for someone else. [[Hackmon (Adventure)|Hackmon]] wanted to take Agnimon on but Taichi saw Agnimon's gentle eyes and knew he didn't want to fight. Agnimon acknowledged his mistake and devolved into Takuya, then apologised. That a human [[Evolution/Video Games#Digimon Adventure|evolved]] into a Digimon was a shock for Taichi and Agumon. Hackmon asked Takuya why he attacked them and Takuya explained that [[Daimon Masaru|a human]] with an [[Agumon (Savers)|Agumon]] attacked him earlier. Taichi thought this meant a copy of Agumon was in the [[Digital Dungeon]] too, which Hackmon didn't find surprising considering they met copies of others. Taichi and Takuya were glad they avoided fighting each other. Taichi offered Takuya to join them since both were trapped in the Digital Dungeon, and Takuya realized their situation was the same and joined up. Takuya as [[Kaiser Greymon]] then joins the party as a guest. Kaiser Greymon can't be controlled, has 9999 HP and infinite SP. He can deal multiple hits to all enemies with {{AT|Kuzuryūjin|Nine-headed Dragon Formation}}, deal damage to one enemy with {{AT|Enryūgeki|Flame Dragon Strike}}, deal damage to all enemies with {{AT|Ryūgonken|Dragon Soul Sword}}, or heal the entire party for 2000 HP with Giga Heal +. He can take two actions per turn and return damage taken with a critical hit thanks to his Continuous Action and Ultra Counter support abilities. Upon reaching Area 2, Taichi asked Takuya about how a human attacked him. Takuya explained he looked like a middle school student and had an Agumon with him. Taichi pointed out Takuya must've been a Digimon at the time, which he confirmed. Taichi and Hackmon were surprised a middle school student would attack a Digimon. Takuya said he had a really strong punch, comparing him to the goalkeeper of his last football match. Taichi was surprised Takuya played football too and viceversa. They both bonded over getting punched by their goalkeepers a lot. Hackmon asked Agumon if football was a kind of human martial arts, which Agumon denied. Upon reaching Area 3, the group was attacked by the black fog once more which took the form of Kaiser Greymon. Takuya wondered who it was and Hackmon explained it was another fake. After defeating it, Takuya and Hackmon were relieved, but Agumon knew it meant they had to part with Takuya. Takuya knew they were together for a short time but he had learned much from them. Both humans were glad they met each other and had fun talking about football. After sepaarting, Takuya thought he had to get fired up. The protagonists met once again in Episode 74, "''Everyone Unite! The Final Battle''". They reunited because they heard about [[Gankoomon (Adventure)|Gankoomon]] and Hackmon. Takuya pointed out a lot happened but it wasn't the time to deal with it and [[Daimon Masaru]] apologised to him for attacking him earlier. [[Kudou Taiki]] pointed out their first priority was to deal with this. However, a quake suddenly hit the Digital Dungeon, followed by the appearance of two [[Diablomon]] and a [[Keramon]]. Taichi realized they wouldn't get to the spatial core so easily. Masaru proposed them all to break in at once, Taiki thought they should conserve their energy, [[Matsuda Takato]] proposed to split into pairs but Taichi pointed out they wouldn't make it in time like that. [[Motomiya Daisuke]] told Taichi to just go ahead and Takuya recognized him as their leader. Hackmon told Taichi to pick who should go with them. If the player refuses every choice, Taichi won't be able to pick, and Hackmon will hurry him up to do it. After stopping the spatial core, upon regaining consciousness, Hackmon found himself back in the normal Digital Dungeon, surrounded by the protagonists. He asked them what happened to the spatial control core. Agumon told him everything was fine and the copies were gone. Gankoomon then arrived and praised everyone. The Chosen Children were ready to go back to their own worlds, each with their own thoughts. Gankoomon granted them a reward: to face them, as a [[Royal Knight]]. After a harsh fight, the team won, with Hackmon being gladly surprised they did. Gankoomon wondered if that was the power of humans allied with Digimon. Hackmon and Gankoomon agreed they still had a lot of training to do. Taichi asked Gankoomon if they could finally go home. Gankoomon said yes, but added they would lose all memories they got in the Digital Space upon leaving it, since if they kept those memories from a space that existed beyond space-time, it would disturb the order of the Digital Field. Hackmon thought it was a shame since they just got to know each other, but the protagonists accepted it, if not without regrets, with Taiki and [[Akashi Tagiru]] feeling [[Digimon Xros Wars - Episode 78|they'd meet again]]. They all then promised to do so, before finally separating. {| |[[File:Digimon Adventure PSP - Takuya.jpg|thumb|Takuya in ''Digimon Adventure''.]] |[[File:DA PSP crossover.jpg|thumb|Takuya among all the future protagonists in the ''Digimon Adventure PSP''.]] |[[File:Digimon Adventure PSP - Crossover Protagonists.jpg|thumb|The protagonists of the crossover arc of ''Digimon Adventure''.]] |- |[[File:Digimon Adventure PSP - Agnimon.jpg|thumb|Agnimon in ''Digimon Adventure''.]] |[[File:Digimon Adventure PSP - Kaiser Greymon.jpg|thumb|Kaiser Greymon in ''Digimon Adventure''.]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon New Century}}=== ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-Scanner Toy|D-Tector}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Kanbara_takuya |s1=HC |s2=DCG }} ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars Super Digica Taisen|Super Digica Taisen}}=== Kanbara Takuya is one of the [[General]]s in the [[Digimon Xros Wars Super Digica Taisen|Super Digica Taisen]] card game. =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Takuya.gif{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Frontier]] |i2=Takuya toei acrylic.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c2=[[Digimon Series: Acrylic de Card]] |i3=Kanbara takuya 20thshop.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c3=[[Digimon 20th Anniversary Project]] |i4=Kanbara takuya agnimon 20thshop.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c4=[[Digimon 20th Anniversary Project]]

[[Wolfmon]]{{rfc|BT17|022 (DCG)}} =Profile= Kouichi is a [[Chosen Child]], though unlike the other Chosen Children who were chosen by [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]], he was manipulated by [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]] and assigned the Legendary Spirits of Darkness. He is also the twin brother of [[Minamoto Kouji‎|Kouji]]. Kouichi is very shy, soft-spoken and withdrawn. Kouichi is the firstborn of the twins, but they were separated when their parents divorced; Kouichi was taken by their mother, while Kouji was taken by their father. Each twin was told that the parents he was separated from was dead, and neither twin was told of his brother's existence. For a long time, Kouichi lived with his mother and maternal grandmother, knowing nothing of his younger twin brother. However, when his grandmother was on her deathbed, she told him of Kouji. After this revelation, Kouichi tracked down his brother and spent an unknown period of time watching him from a distance, trying to get courage up to speak with him. Kouichi's mindset grew gradually darker over this time, as he felt unable to tell anyone of his conflict. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== When Kouji received his message from Ofanimon, Kouichi was in the midst of following him. Kouichi boarded the train to Shibuya after him, and attempted to catch up with and talk to his brother, but he missed the elevator to the underground train station. Attempting to catch up with Kouji, he ran down the stairs, tripped and crashed at the bottom, which proved near-fatal. [[Image:Kouichi dead.JPG|thumb|right]] Kouichi's next memory was of a dark expanse, possibly the borders of the Digital World, where he was tormented by memories of his family and the dark thoughts that had been invading his mind. [[Cherubimon (Virus)|Cherubimon]] appeared and gave him the [[Corrupted Human Spirit of Darkness]], changing him into [[Duskmon]]. Kouichi forgot who he was and served Cherubimon as one of his warriors, though his relationship with the other four was obviously strained. He spent most of his time inside the Venus Rose castle and was known for staring at the moons in a thoughtful manner. [[Mercuremon (Frontier)|Mercuremon]] often spoke of him disdainfully because of this. Eventually, prodded into battle by Mercuremon, he first appeared to the other Legendary Chosen by killing and scanning [[Arbormon (Frontier)|Arbormon's]] data. During a battle, Duskmon badly injured [[Garummon]], forcing him to change back into Kouji. When he heard Kouji's name, he began to remember who he really was and became greatly bothered by it, overwhelming everything in darkness. When he returned to Cherubimon, confused, Cherubimon told him that his father had betrayed him and his mother and that he could take revenge on Kouji for this. Cherubimon then gave him the Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness, turning him into [[Velgmon]]. Losing himself in the darkness again, he was released to the other Children. Ofanimon, working through Kouji's [[D-Scanner]], managed to bring his memories back, but these were quickly twisted by Cherubimon. Velgmon, who was powerful enough to be a threat even to the Fusion Evolution [[Beowulfmon]], seemed unstoppable. But, working together, [[Kanbara Takuya|Takuya]] and Kouji managed to defeat him and took away the corrupted Spirits of Darkness. Now back to his normal self, Kouichi was shy and ashamed for what he had done. When Cherubimon used an illusion of himself to attack the group, he offered Kouichi the chance to join him again, but Kouichi refused. It was then that the Spirits of Darkness were purified and returned to Kouichi, along with his D-Scanner. The [[Human Spirit of Darkness]] changed him into [[Löwemon]] and the [[Beast Spirit of Darkness]] changed him into [[Kaiser Leomon]]. Kouichi continued to travel with the other Legendary Chosen, but soon noticed that there was something strange about him. When the Royal Knights first attacked them, Kouichi was the only one of the Children who remained conscious. Even when they were blown to the Digital World's blue moon, he was the only one whose [[DigiCode]] never appeared, and he never seemed to become injured. During their battle at Akiba Market, [[Lord Knightmon (Frontier)|Lord Knightmon]] told him that this was because he had no data to reveal. Kouichi was simply a spirit pulled from its body, wandering in the Digital World. After hearing this, Kouichi became depressed, but made a point of not revealing this to the others, especially to Kouji. However, he made the younger twin promise that he will go visit their mother in the Real World. In the battle against [[Lucemon (Frontier)|Lucemon: Falldown Mode]] in the [[Dark Area]], Löwemon moved between the Light and Dark orbs of the Dead or Alive technique in order to save the other Children. Kouichi was scanned by Lucemon, but was able to give his Spirits to Kouji beforehand, enabling Kouji and Takuya to evolve into [[Susanoomon]]. As the others were returning to the Real World, Löwemon contacted Kouji through his D-Scanner to tell him that Kouichi was still alive. The moment they returned, the others rushed to find him, but his body had already been moved from the stairs in Shibuya Station. They hurried to the hospital, where Kouichi was in critical condition – his heart had stopped, and the doctors were attempting to revive him. The power of the D-Scanners managed to revive Kouichi, who woke up because of a tear of Kouji's that hit his cheek. When they returned to their lives, the brothers continued to bond with each other, and Kouichi reintroduced Kouji to their mother. ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier Original Story: Things That Want To Be Told}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Series 1-4: Original Story Special ~with~}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD ~A Train Named Hope~}}=== ==Live Readings== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Another Mysterious Message! Who’s the Culprit?}}=== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Battle Spirit}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Battle Chronicle}}=== ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-Scanner Toy|D-Tector}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Kimura_kouichi |s1=DCG }} =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Kouichi.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Frontier]] |i2=Kimura kouichi toei acrylic.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c2=[[Digimon Series: Acrylic de Card]] |i3=Kouichi frontier tokyo character street.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c3=Digimon Frontier X Tokyo Character Street }} ==Miscellaneous== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Kimura kouichi lowemon eyecatch.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Frontier]]

Possibly to make up for this, his evolution sequence seems to be a mix between Human and Beast sequences. *Kouichi is the only Chosen Child in the animated Digimon series to 'die' in battle, although he is revived at the end of the season. *Originally, Crispin Freeman was considered to voice Kouji, but was reconsidered and was cast to play Kouichi instead. =Additional Information= {{R | }} {{NavboxDF}} [[Category:Digimon Frontier characters]]

Kuga Yuuya.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Kuga Yuuya |image=Kuka yuuya2.jpg |imagesize=x320 |kan=久我ユウヤ |dub= |age=14''[[Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode]]'': "The CEO of GIGO Company, Kenzou Kuga, confirmed in the media that his son, Yuuya Kuga (14), has been missing for serveral days now." |pd=[[Black]] (Black War Greymon X-Antibody)
[[DORUmon (X-evolution)|DORUgamon]] (temporarily) |dv= |family=[[Kuga Kenzo]] (father) |vjp=[[Kaida Yuki]] (甲斐田 ゆき) |ven= }} '''Kuga Yuuya''' is an enemy, and later supporting character, in ''[[Digimon World Re:Digitize]]''. He's temporarily playable just before the final boss battles of ''[[Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode]]'' alongside [[Black]]. =Profile= Yuuya is the son of [[Kuga Kenzou]], CEO of [[GIGO Company]], and thus, heir to the position, which he sees as being the head of the world. He's willing to do anything for that, including hacking his [[Partner Digimon|Partner Digimon's]] data, having reinforced [[Black]] through a GIGO backdoor. However, he does this because he considers GIGO to be noble, and is visibly distressed when he learns of the dark side of the company from [[Professor Petrov]]. He feels a sense of responsibility towards the [[Digital World]] due to his position, so he acted as warden to it through Black before being sent into it himself. Beyond Black's overwhelming strength, Yuuya himself is an extremely skilled [[Tamer]], having ranked 2nd at the Digitower, below [[Nyanko Tamer]] and above [[Taiga]] without using Black at all, hence why he was selected by [[Mikagura Mirei]]. Yuuya is a natural loner. He considers himself above others, and thus, is unwilling to team up with them, causing rifts in the Tamers. He dislikes the weak, especially those that won't act by themselves due to fear like [[DORUmon (X-evolution)|DORUmon]], and is extremely harsh with them, if with the intent to make them improve. He's fearless towards even dangerous threats like the [[X-Program]] and [[Vitium]]. [[Nicolai Petrov]] has a one-sided rivalry with him due to Yuuya defeating him in battle early on during their adventure, then insulting his father, Professor Petrov, who he sees as having hurt GIGO's image. He mistrusts Mirei, who can use two Partner Digimon at once and got in his way earlier. Nonetheless, he's capable of respecting those that have proven their worth to him, such as Taiga. =Card Game Evolution= * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Evolves From Tamers|Any Digimon that also Evolves from non-White Tamers from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{Note|[[BT18-078 (DCG)]] Duskmon can change any Tamer's Color into any other Color, except White.|n=DuskTamerColor}} * [[Blitzmon]]{{rfc|BT18|063 (DCG)}} * [[Bolgmon]]{{rfc|BT18|067 (DCG)}} * [[Gigasmon]]''[[BT7-061 (DCG)|BT7-061]]'' * [[Grottemon]]''[[BT7-060 (DCG)|BT7-060]]'' * [[Kaiser Greymon]]{{rfc|BT18|095 (DCG)}} * [[Magna Garurumon]]{{rfc|BT18|097 (DCG)}} * [[Mercuremon]]{{rfc|BT12|066 (DCG)}} * [[Rhino Kabuterimon]]{{rfc|BT18|070 (DCG)}} * [[Sephirothmon]]{{rfc|BT12|065 (DCG)}} * [[Susanoomon]]''[[BT7-112 (DCG)|BT7-112]]'' =Appearances= ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize (Manga)|Digimon World Re:Digitize}}=== Five young children: [[Nicolai Petrov]], [[Rindou Akiho]], [[Kuga Yuuya]], [[Mikagura Mirei]], and [[Taiga]], dived into the [[Digital World]] by entering their passwords and having their accounts confirmed in "''[[Digimon World Re:Digitize - Chapter 01|Digital Monster]]''". As Mirei observed the Digital Space inside GIGO Server with her Partner Digimon, [[Angewomon (Re:Digitize)|Angewomon]] and [[Lady Devimon (Re:Digitize)|Lady Devimon]], she was distracted by the appearance of a Digimon's signal and warned her Digimon to be careful. A [[Black War Greymon (X-Antibody)]] called [[Black]] then appeared in front of Angewomon and Lady Devimon, as his [[Tamer]], Yuuya, pointed out how he had finally found the [[hacker]]. He commended Mirei's hacking abilities, as he had noticed there was someone lurking in the server but she had made it difficult for him to find her. However, as the one who'd lead [[GIGO Company]] someday, he couldn't allow her to do as she pleased and would dispose of her. Their battle would later interrupt Taiga and Nyanko Tamer's battle, as it caused a Colosseum wall to suddenly explode, startling Nyanko Tamer, and Angewomon and Lady Devimon fell from said explosion. Black then appeared from the explosion, as Yuuya pointed out he had Mirei cornered. Continuing in "''[[Digimon World Re:Digitize - Chapter 02|Complication!]]''", Black let loose a blast from its mouth at Angewomon and Lady Devimon, though they both managed to avoid it. Taiga, Nyanko Tamer, [[Digimaru|Agumon]] and [[Digitorin]] got sent flying by the shock wave it caused. Black then charged at Angewomon, to Mirei's fear. Yuuya was confident that meant one of Mirei's Partner Digimon was down, but Black was stopped by a barrage of fireballs from Digitorin. Nyanko Tamer then screamed at Yuuya and Mirei for barging into their match, shocking Taiga and Nico, who observed everything alongside Taiga from his computer and found her scary, as did Taiga. Yuuya realized the damage was giving him temporary restrictions. Mirei made an exit and commanded her Digimon to escape through it, as Yuuya would lose all track of her once the exit closed, and thanks to the restrictions, they succeeded in doing so. Taiga, Nyanko Tamer, Agumon and Digitorin looked at the exit, as the former pointed out Angewomon and Lady Devimon had disappeared. Black turned to the [[Tamers]] left in the Colosseum and, in a sound-only connection, Yuuya pointed out that their interference was very irksome and he had no time to play along with them, which Nyanko Tamer found very rude. However, for Yuuya, both Digital Monsters and their low-level battle really were nothing but a game. Nyanko Tamer couldn't let that go unchallenged and Yuuya asked her if she wanted to test him, warning her that she should be careful as Black and him were a little irritated. Black then charged in and swiped at Agumon and Digitorin. Akiho reacted at the blow, while Taiga decided he had to help Digitorin, as did Nico, who sent in his Partner, [[Sashenka]], through his wrist-mounted [[List of Digivices|Digivice]]. Sashenka rescued Digitorin and Agumon [[Evolution|Warp Evolved]] into [[War Greymon]]. However, Yuuya was unfazed, as he thought that no matter how many people joined or evolved, they were no match for him and would teach them that in an instant. Black retracted his arms and legs, confusing Taiga, then let loose a massive shockwave. War Greymon shielded Sashenka and Digitorin with his body. The attack caused explosions all over the Colosseum and left War Greymon badly wounded. Taiga flew towards his partner, as Yuuya asked everyone if they understood their powerlessness as he was a chosen one unlike them. With his hand over War Greymon, Taiga pointed out that it had to be a mistake for someone like Yuuya, who only flaunted around his power, to be chosen. Yuuya pointed out that the scenary around them was the proof of his status and his complaints were those of a loser, then commanded Black to put an end to the fight, aiming at Nico, Nyanko Tamer, Sashenka and the KOed Digitorin. An emergency announcement then sounded off, that the battlefield building Colosseum would be updated to the digitower interior set up within the Digital World. After the update, Yuuya wondered what happened, as he hadn't heard of any plans for such a large update. Black then charged at his enemies. Nico challenged him, Nyanko Tamer pointed out he was in no shape to fight, and Nico acknowledged she was right but there were times where he had to fight no matter what. Taiga was worried as they'd all die at this rate and begged War Greymon to lend them his power. This awoke his Partner Digimon who, after a roar, shot a {{ATO|Gaia Force}} at Black, to Yuuya's surprise as he should've been near-death. The attack caused massive damage to Black, shocking Yuuya as it shouldn't have had so much power, and he had Black retreat afterwards. After the battle, Mirei wondered about the {{ATO|Gaia Force}} that showed far greater power from its original configuration and War Greymon's mysterious revival, explaining that his Tamer's strong and unique ability to adapt to the Digital World must have temporarily awakened War Greymon's abilities. She found all the people involved interesting: *Nico, the son of [[Professor Petrov]] who developed the Digitize technology. *Akiho, the number one Digital Monster Tamer in the world. *Yuuya, the one with a privileged GIGO account who held overwhelming power but lurked in darkness. *Taiga, the Tamer who showed incredibly strong adaptability to the Digital World. Acknowledging them as the most appropiate to receive an e-mail, she sent it to them: "Before everything returns to the void, digitalize and renew the world..." ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Encode}}=== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Kuga_yuuya |s1=DCG }} =Image Gallery= {{IG |w=200 |h=200 |bg=white |i1=Kuka_yuuya2.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon World Re:Digitize|Re:Digitize]]

Lee Jianliang.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Lee Jianliang |name2=([[Image:Jap.png|Japanese]] Lee Jenrya; [[Image:China.png|20px|Chinese]] Li Jianliang) |image=Jianliang.jpg |imagesize=x320 |kan=李 健良（リー・ジェンリャ） |dub=Henry Wong |age=10 (Dub: 12{{ref|[http://www.konaka.com/alice6/tamers/characters/jen-e.html Chiaki J. Konaka's ''Digimon Tamers Resources'': Jianliang Lee]}}) |pd=[[Terriermon (Tamers)|Terriermon]] |dv=Green [[D-Ark]] |family=[[Lee Jiang-yu]] (Father), [[Lee Mayumi]] (Mother), [[Lee Rinchei]] (Older brother), [[Lee Jaarin]] (Older sister), [[Lee Shaochung]] (Younger sister) |vjp=[[Yamaguchi Mayumi]] (山口 眞弓) |ven=[[Dave Wittenberg]] }} '''Lee Jianliang''' is one of the main protagonists of ''[[Digimon Tamers]]''. He is the partner of [[Terriermon (Tamers)|Terriermon]], as well as [[Lee Shaochung]]'s elder brother. =Profile= Jianliang is half-Chinese and half-Japanese: his father (Jiang-yu) is Chinese and his mother (Mayumi) is Japanese. He is the third oldest of four siblings: he has a 17-year-old brother, Rinchei, and a 15-year-old sister, Jaarin, as well as a 7-year-old sister, [[Lee Shaochung|Shaochung]]. Along with his father, Jianliang is a Tai-chi student, studying art under a Sensei who is a family friend. In a moment of self-reflection, Jianliang confessed that he had been an extremely willful child and that having three siblings forced him to become self-reliant and independent. His study of martial arts was also revealed to be a way of asserting himself to stand out amongst his large family. Once, he got into a serious argument with a neighborhood child and, presumably in a moment of thoughtlessness, injured the child with his Tai-chi skills. Since realizing the error of his ways, Jianliang devoted himself to practicing self-restraint, fearing that he might hurt someone else if he were to lose control of his emotions again. He became responsible, quiet, and a soft-spoken person who conducts himself with maturity, but also tends to blame himself for things beyond his control. When he was first introduced, he was practical and knowledgeable. Being reserved resulted in clashes with the seemingly cold and indifferent [[Makino Ruki|Ruki]]. However, he was willing to help Takato with Digimon-related issues. As the series progressed, he became more open and willing to engage with others, learning that there were battles he has to fight when need be. =Evolution= * '''[[Saint Galgomon]]''' (with '''[[Terriermon]]''''''''[[Digimon Tamers - Episode 37|Confronting Zhuqiaomon! Saint Galgomon, Ultimate Evolution]]''''') ==Card Game Evolution== * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Evolves From Tamers|Any Digimon that also Evolves from non-White Tamers from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{Note|[[BT18-078 (DCG)]] Duskmon can change any Tamer's Color into any other Color, except White.|n=DuskTamerColor}} * [[Arbormon]]''[[BT6-049 (DCG)|BT6-049]]'' * [[Blitzmon]]''[[BT7-046 (DCG)|BT7-046]]'' * [[Bolgmon]]''[[BT7-047 (DCG)|BT7-047]]'' * [[Fairimon]]''[[BT7-035 (DCG)|BT7-035]]'' * [[Garummon]]{{rfc|BT17|023 (DCG)}} * [[Jet Silphymon]]{{rfc|BT18|053 (DCG)}} * [[Kaiser Greymon]]{{rfc|BT18|095 (DCG)}} * [[Kaiser Leomon]]{{rfc|BT18|077 (DCG)}} * [[Löwemon]]{{rfc|BT18|076 (DCG)}} * [[Magna Garurumon]]{{rfc|BT18|097 (DCG)}} * [[Petaldramon]]''[[BT6-050 (DCG)|BT6-050]]'' * [[Raihimon]]{{rfc|BT18|081 (DCG)}} * [[Rhino Kabuterimon]]{{rfc|BT18|070 (DCG)}} * [[Shutumon]]''[[BT7-036 (DCG)|BT7-036]]'' * [[Susanoomon]]''[[BT7-112 (DCG)|BT7-112]]'' * [[Wolfmon]]{{rfc|BT17|022 (DCG)}} =Appearances= ==Anime== ===Background=== Jianliang received his partner long before [[Matsuda Takato|Takato]]. While playing a Digimon computer game, his partner in the game, Terriermon, was defeated by [[Gorimon]]. Slashing a [[Blue Card]] through his card reader transformed the card reader into a D-Ark, and Terriermon was transported from the computer game into Jianliang's arms. Jianliang realized he had hurt many Digimon through the game, and promised Terriermon that he would never make him battle again. Since then, Jianliang was reluctant to get into fights, developing a pacifistic approach to life. ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers}}=== {| |[[File:Jian Liang use Red Card.jpg|thumb|Lee Jianliang from ''Digimon Tamers''.]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: The Adventurers' Battle|The Adventurers' Battle}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: The Runaway Digimon Express|The Runaway Digimon Express}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== Although Jianliang does not appear himself, [[Saint Galgomon]], his Matrix Evolved form with [[Terriermon (Tamers)|Terriermon]], appears alongside [[Shibayama Junpei]] in the form of [[Blitzmon]] among those brought by the [[Old Clock Shop Man]] to fight against [[Quartzmon (Xros Wars)|Quartzmon]]. ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers Original Story: Message in the Packet}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Series 1-4: Original Story Special ~with~}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD|Digimon Tamers 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD ~Days -Information and the Unordinary-~}}=== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: Pocket Culumon}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: Digimon Medley}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: Battle Spirit}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: Battle Evolution}}=== Jianliang serves as [[Terriermon (Tamers)|Terriermon's]] Tamer. As such, he appears in the character select menu alongside his Partner Digimon and when Terriermon evolves into [[Saint Galgomon]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster Card Game Ver.
