Legendary Spirits.wikitext:
[[Image: Agnimon digimon profile.png|thumb|right]] The '''Legendary Spirits''' (伝説のスピリット ''Densetsu no Supiritto''), or '''Spirits''' (スピリット/{{jap|魂|スピリット}} ''Supiritto'') for short, are a set of twenty ancient artifacts that are used to [[Evolution|evolve]] into [[Hybrid]] [[Digimon]]. Each contains the sealed power of a member of the [[Warrior Ten]], a group of ten [[Ultimate]] [[Digimon (species)|Digimon]] who saved the [[Digital World]] in its distant past. The Spirits grant their respective Warrior's power to their users.''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report014/ Digi-Basics: "Hybrids" and "Spirits"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 014|Translation on Wikimon]]) =Overview= ==Origin== The Legendary Spirits are remnants of the [[Warrior Ten]], created in the aftermath of their victory over [[Lucemon]]. Although all ten of them died in the battle, they left behind the Spirits—artifacts which embody both their souls and their power— in various places around the [[Digital World]], to someday be inherited and used in the event that the [[Digital World]] comes under threat from another menace.''[[Digital Monster Card Game]]'' [[Starter Ver. 7|Starter Ver. 7: "Hybrid Warriors"]] [[Starter Ver. 7#Back Story|(Back Story)]] ==Properties== When a human fuses with a Spirit, they form a "new species":''[[Digital Monster Card Game]]'' [[Booster 15|Booster 15: "Burning Beast Fighter"]] [[Booster 15#Back Story|(Back Story)]] [[Hybrid]] Digimon. Both humans and Digimon are capable of using the Spirits. Each Spirit is associated with one of ten elemental powers corresponding to the element of its member of the Warrior Ten. There are two Spirits for each element: * a '''Human Spirit''' {{ETYK|{{j2|ヒューマンスピリット|Hyūman supiritto}}}}, or '''Spirit H''' {{ETYK|{{j2|スピリットＨ|Supiritto eichi}}}}, which creates [[Hybrid#Human Form|Human Form Hybrid Digimon]]; and * a '''Beast Spirit''' {{ETYK|{{j2|ビーストスピリット|Bīsuto supiritto}}}}, or '''Spirit B''' {{ETYK|{{j2|スピリットＢ|Supiritto bī}}}}, which creates [[Hybrid#Beast Form|Beast Form Hybrid Digimon]]. In certain circumstances, multiple Spirits can be combined to create more powerful Hybrid Digimon: using both Spirits of the same element creates [[Hybrid#Fusion Form|Fusion Form Hybrid Digimon]] such as [[Raihimon]], and using ten Spirits in specific combinations creates [[Hybrid#Transcendent Form|Transcendent Form Hybrid Digimon]] such as [[Kaiser Greymon]]. The union of all twenty Spirits creates the [[Ultimate]] Digimon [[Susanoomon]]. It is possible for Spirits to be corrupted into alternate, sinister forms that produce different Hybrid Digimon. The only current corrupted Spirits are the [[Corrupted Human Spirit of Darkness]] and the [[Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness]]. ==Design & Analysis== [[Image:Legendary Spirits Symbols.png|thumb|right|200px|The ten elemental symbols of the Spirits.]] The Spirit items themselves resemble the Hybrid Digimon that they create in some way, such as being stored arrangements of their armor or body. Every Spirit sits on a hexagonal base, which has the word "SPIRIT" ({{DigiAlph2|s|p|i|r|i|t}}) inscribed on it in [[Digimoji]] repeating six times in a pattern around its edge. The elemental attributes of the Spirits are each represented by a symbol that is a stylized/simplified version of the respective {{w|kanji}} for that element. Both the Spirits and their respective Hybrid Digimon often have the respective element's symbol inscribed somewhere on them. In addition, two Digimon created using multiple Spirits have other symbols: * A symbol based on the kanji {{ETYK|炎|honō}} ("flame"), instead of the usual {{ETYK|火|hi}} ("fire" or "flame") symbol, is inscribed on [[Kaiser Greymon]]'s breastplate * A symbol based on the kanji {{ETYK|天|ten}} ("heaven") is inscribed on [[Susanoomon]]'s headgear The concept of the ten elements embodied by the Spirits and their related Digimon follows the common trope of elemental powers widely seen in modern fiction, as well as historical philosophical concepts of elements which comprise or underpin reality, such as the Japanese ''{{w|Godai (Japanese philosophy)|godai}}'', the European and Indian {{w|classical elements}}, and the Chinese ''{{w|Wuxing_(Chinese_philosophy)|Wuxing}}''. In ''[[Digimon Frontier]]'', the groups of Spirits that were originally held by members of the [[Three Archangels]], as noted in ''[[Digimon Frontier - Episode 38|"Endless Death Match! The Prelude of Lucemon's Revival"]]'', may be interpreted as references to particular elemental schemes or cultural perceptions: * Four of the five elements whose Spirits [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]] had—Water, Wood, Metal, Earth, and Darkness—match the phases of the ''Wuxing'', differing only in that Cherubimon had the Spirits of Darkness instead of the Spirits of Fire. * The three elements whose Spirits [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]] had—Fire, Ice and Thunder—match a common combination of elemental powers or magic in modern Japanese popular media, such as ''{{w|Final Fantasy}}''. ==List of Spirits== {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse; font-size:90%; text-align:center;" |- !style="background: #262626;" rowspan="2" width="150px" | Warrior Ten !style="background: #262626;" rowspan="2" width="150px" | Element !style="background: #262626;" colspan="2" | Human Form !style="background: #262626;" colspan="2" | Beast Form !style="background: #262626;" rowspan="2" width="150px" | Fusion Form !style="background: #262626;" rowspan="2" width="150px" | Transcendent Form |- !style="background: #262626;" width="150px" | Spirit !style="background: #262626;" width="150px" | Evolution !style="background: #262626;" width="150px" | Spirit !style="background: #262626;" width="150px" | Evolution |- !style="background: #ffffff;" | [[File:Ancientgreymon.jpg|120px]]

(Japanese LCD game) |- |} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== The Legendary Spirits, which were born from the same data as the [[Digital World (Frontier)|Digital World]] itself, were created in the distant past after the Warrior Ten's final battle with [[Lucemon (Frontier)|Lucemon]]. The Warrior Ten released the Spirits and entrusted them to the [[Three Archangels]]:{{rfe|DF|38|Endless Death Match! The Prelude of Lucemon's Revival}} * [[Seraphimon (Frontier)|Seraphimon]] possessed the Spirits of two elements: Light and Wind * [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]] possessed the Spirits of three elements: Fire, Ice, and Thunder * [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]] possessed the Spirits of five elements: Darkness, Earth, Wood, Steel, and Water After Lucemon corrupted him, Cherubimon bonded four of his five Spirits to Digimon to create the [[Evil Warriors]],{{rfe|DF|34|Decisive Battle!! Rose of Venus, Ofanimon's Rescue Plan}} and corrupted the Spirits of Darkness and bonded them to [[Kimura Kouichi]]'s to create [[Duskmon]].{{rfe|DF|33|The New Warriors of Darkness! Löwemon & Kaiser Leomon}} However, the Beast Spirit of Water does not appear to have been in his possession at this point, as [[Ranamon (Frontier)|Ranamon]] only later finds it in a shipwreck at the bottom of the ocean.{{rfe|DF|15|Cool Beast Evolution! Calmaramon}} Seraphimon and Ofanimon's Spirits were all hidden to protect them from Cherubimon. When she brings the [[Chosen Child#Digimon Frontier|Chosen Children]] into the Digital World, Ofanimon instructs them to search for and collect the remaining Spirits, so that they can use them to [[Evolution#Digimon Frontier|Spirit Evolve]] and resist Cherubimon.{{rfe|DF|02|Wolfmon of Light, The Underground Labyrinth Battle!}} When not in use, the Spirits that they have collected are stored in their [[D-Scanner]]s. A Hybrid Digimon who also possesses the other Spirit of their respective element can undergo Slide Evolution to transform into that Spirit's respective Hybrid Digimon at any time. Both the Chosen Children and the Evil Warriors do this. A recurring theme with the Beast Spirits is that, unlike the Human Spirits, they are very difficult to control. [[Kanbara Takuya]],{{rfe|DF|11|Defeat Me! Legendary Warrior Vritramon Runs Wild}} [[Minamoto Kouji]],{{rfe|DF|10|An Uncontrollable Beast Spirit!? Garmmon's Evolution}} Ranamon{{rfe|DF|15|Cool Beast Evolution! Calmaramon}} and [[Duskmon]]{{rfe|DF|30|Soaring! Warrior of Darkness Velgrmon}} are all overwhelmed by their Beast Spirits and run wild when they first obtain them. [[Himi Tomoki]]{{rfe|DF|17|Blizzarmon, Blow the Snow, Call the Glaciers!}} and [[Shibayama Junpei]]{{rfe|DF|14|Thunder! Even Rock Breaks! Bolgmon's Do-or-Die Challenge}} also have some difficulty, but [[Orimoto Izumi]]{{rfe|DF|16|Strength Does not Matter! The Beautiful Warrior, Shutumon}} and Kouichi{{rfe|DF|33|The New Warriors of Darkness! Löwemon & Kaiser Leomon}} do not. He returns the D-Scanner to Tomoki when he becomes convinced of his honesty, and Tomoki uses the new Spirit to Spirit Evolve into [[Blizzarmon]] and fight Petaldramon. |{{epn|DF|17|Blizzarmon, Blow the Snow, Call the Glaciers!}} |- |rowspan="4" | '''[[Orimoto Izumi]]''' |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:Human Spirit of Wind.JPG|120px]]

When the wreck of a crane breaks the core open and reveals the Spirit, Junpei uses it to Spirit Evolve into [[Blitzmon]] and fight [[Snimon]]. |{{epn|DF|05|Thunder Power Shaking the Ground, Blitzmon!}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:Beast Spirit of Thunder.JPG|120px]]

Even Rock Breaks! Bolgmon's Do-or-Die Challenge}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF14 Human Spirit of Earth.JPG|120px]]

Kouichi then uses the Human Spirit of Darkness to Spirit Evolve into [[Löwemon]] and fight Cherubimon. '''''[[Beast Spirit of Darkness]]''''' |When Kouichi stands up to Cherubimon and his D-Scanner appears, the Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness emerges from Kouji's D-Scanner, transforms back into its true form, and enters Kouichi's D-Scanner. Kouichi then uses the Beast Spirit of Darkness to Slide Evolve from Löwemon into [[Kaiser Leomon]] and finish his fight with Cherubimon. After Lucemon fatally wounds Kouichi in the battle in the Dark Area, Kouichi passes this Spirit on to Kouji.{{rfe|DF|48|Light and Darkness are One! Kouichi's Last Wish}} |{{epn|DF|33|The New Warriors of Darkness! Löwemon & Kaiser Leomon}} |- | colspan="4" style="text-align:center;" | Spirits listed in '''''bold and italics''''' are used by the respective Chosen Child to Spirit Evolve. |} [[Image: Legendary Spirits DF50.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Legendary Spirits, reborn as Digimon.]] The ability to use more than one Spirit at a time requires the power of one of the Three Archangels. During their battles inside [[Sephirothmon]], Seraphimon's [[Digitama]] gives power to Takuya and Kouji's D-Scanners to allow them to Double Spirit Evolve using both the Spirits of their respective elements.{{rfe|DF|27|Double Spirit Miracle! Beowulfmon is Born}}{{rfe|DF|28|Takuya's Fusion Evolution, Aldamon's Explosive Attack!}} In their confrontation with Cherubimon at the Rose Morning Star, Ofanimon upgrades Takuya and Kouji's D-Scanners again to transform them into New D-Scanners, allowing them to Hyper Spirit Evolve using ten Spirits each. When they gain this ability, the group reorganizes their collected Spirits to permanently give the Spirits of Earth and Wood to Takuya, and the Spirits of Water and Steel to Kouji, so that they can be used for their respective Hyper Spirit Evolutions.{{rfe|DF|35|Spirits Become One! Takuya and Kouji's Ultimate Evolution!}} In the group's second battle with Lucemon in the [[Dark Area]], Kouichi gives the Spirits of Darkness back to Kouji when he is killed by Lucemon. The reaction of Kouji's Spirits of Light and the Spirits of Darkness triggers Kouji and Takuya's Ancient Spirit Evolution into [[Susanoomon]] using all twenty Spirits.{{rfe|DF|48|Light and Darkness are One! Kouichi's Last Wish}} In the final battle against Lucemon, the Warrior Ten are reborn as the ten Human Form Hybrids as entities in their own right, who protect and encourage the Chosen Children. As a side effect of the powerful bond established between the Spirits and Chosen Children from all of their time spent traveling together and Spirit Evolving, the Warrior Ten have inherited some of the traits of the Chosen Children (for instance, [[Fairimon]] now speaks Italian, like Izumi), and there is some sort of link between each Chosen Child-Warrior pair (e.g. [[Löwemon]]'s time spent with Kouichi allows him to sense that Kouichi is still alive in the Real World).{{rfe|DF|50|Go Beyond Time! The Beginning of a New Legend}} ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== [[Kanbara Takuya]] uses the Spirits of Fire to become [[Aldamon]] for the fight against [[Quartzmon]]'s forces. Near the end of the battle, [[Himi Tomoki]] sends the rest of the Spirits to Takuya so that he can become [[Susanoomon]] by himself.{{rfe|XW|78|The Gathering of the Legendary Heroes! The Digimon All-star Showdown!!}} {| |[[File:XW78 Spirit Delivery.jpg|thumb|Aldamon receives the rest of the Spirits in ''Digimon Xros Wars''.]] |} ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== In the ''[[V-Tamer Frontier Generation|"Frontier Generation"]]'' special, [[Kanbara Takuya]] and [[Minamoto Kouji]] use their Human Spirits to defeat a [[Megadramon]], following the same Spirit Evolution mechanics as in ''[[Digimon Frontier]]''. Takuya also uses his [[Beast Spirit of Fire]] to Slide Evolve into [[Vritramon]] when fighting [[Metamormon]] with [[Yagami Taichi (V-Tamer)|Yagami Taichi]] and [[Zeromaru]]. ==Video Games== [[Image:Legendary Spirits Symbols DMO.png|thumb|right|200px|Legendary Spirits Symbols as seen in ''Digimon Masters Online'', in the order:
闇 土 火 水 光
鋼 雷 氷 風 木]] ==={{hdr|Digital Monster: D-Project}}=== Five Spirits—the [[Human Spirit of Fire|Human Spirits of Fire]], [[Human Spirit of Light|Light]], [[Human Spirit of Ice|Ice]], [[Human Spirit of Wind|Wind]] and [[Human Spirit of Thunder|Thunder]]— are equippable items which can be obtained by inputting their respective codes into the meat machine. When equipped onto a compatible [[Child]] Digimon, the next time that the Digimon enters a battle, it will evolve into the matching [[Hybrid]] Digimon for its duration. Spirits are single-use only, and after the battle in which they are used, they disappear. The same Spirit can be freely re-acquired from the meat machine, and the player may stockpile multiples of the same Spirits. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Lost Evolution}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory}}=== The four Spirits of Fire and Light appear as items that, once obtained, allow compatible Digimon to evolve into the respective Digimon ([[Agnimon]], [[Wolfmon]], [[Vritramon]], [[Garummon]]) as long as they also meet the other evolution criteria. The item is not consumed in the evolution, so the Digimon can be evolved into any number of times. Kaiser Greymon, Magna Garurumon, and Susanoomon also appear in the game, but their evolution criteria do not involve the Spirits at all aside from the former two evolving only from Vritramon and Garummon. In compilations of both ''Cyber Sleuth'' games—the Japanese Nintendo Switch and PlayStation 4 versions of ''Hacker's Memory'', and the western ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Complete Edition]]''—any of the Digimon evolved using the Spirit items can be transferred back to ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth]]'' after both games have been cleared, but the Spirits are not made available in ''Cyber Sleuth'' so these Digimon cannot be evolved into natively there. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-Scanner Toy}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Tector Toy}}=== The Spirits are collectible items which the player's party of DigiDestined ([[Chosen Child#Digimon Frontier|Chosen Children]]) may use in battles to "[[D-Tector Toy#Spirit On|Spirit On]]" into their respective [[Hybrid]] Digimon. In order to use a Spirit, the matching DigiDestined must currently be present in the party, and the player must have enough Spirits Power points to afford to use the Hybrid Digimon's attacks. Each Spirit comes with a set of stat bonuses which are added to the respective DigiDestined's own base stats, meaning that unlike regular Digimon, Hybrid Digimon stats scale as the game progresses and the player levels up. The Spirits available depend on the device's firmware version: * Only the ten Human and Beast Spirits of Fire, Light, Ice, Wind and Thunder are available in firmware revision A. * Revision B adds the true Human and Beast Spirits of Darkness. * Revision C adds: ** the Human and Beast Spirits of Earth, Water, Wood, and Gold (Steel); unlike in ''[[Digimon Frontier]]'' or any other ''Digimon'' media, they may respectively be used by J.P. ([[Shibayama Junpei]]), Zoe ([[Orimoto Izumi]]), Tommy ([[Himi Tomoki]]) and Koichi ([[Kimura Kouichi]]) just like their usual Spirits ** the "Extra" (Z) Spirits for KaiserGreymon ([[KaiserGreymon]]) and MagnaGarurumon ([[Magna Garurumon]]) ** the ability to become "Combo" ([[Hybrid#Fusion Form|Fusion Form]]) Hybrids and Susanomon ([[Susanoomon]]), albeit not represented with Spirits unlike the Japanese D-Scanners ==={{hdr|D-Spirit}}=== In the Quest mode, the player's goal is to collect Spirits by defeating the [[Hybrid]] bosses of each Area. They begin with one of five Human Spirits, chosen based on which [[Trailmon]] they selected to board at the beginning of the mode. Any Spirits that the player has obtained in Quest Mode may also be used for the "V.S. Spirit" multiplayer battle mode. In the Database, a Human Form, Beast Form or Extra (Transcendent) Form Hybrid Digimon's corresponding Spirit may also be viewed by selecting the "Spirit" option in the Digimon's Digivolve ([[Evolution]]) menu. ==TCG== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster Card Game}}=== All of the Legendary Spirits, including the corrupted forms of the Spirits of Darkness, have one card each in the [[Digital Monster Card Game]]. The Human Spirits (excluding the true [[Human Spirit of Darkness]] but including [[Corrupted Human Spirit of Darkness|the corrupted version]]) were all introduced in [[Starter Ver. 7]], the Beast Spirits (again, excluding the true [[Beast Spirit of Darkness]] but including [[Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness|the corrupted version]]) were introduced in [[Expansion Kit S]], and the true forms of the Spirits of Darkness were introduced in [[Booster 17]]. The Spirits' cards are all Option Cards of the Item Category, and are used as part of the Appearance Requirements of many (but not all) cards featuring Human Form and Beast Form Hybrid Digimon. The Appearance process for these Hybrids is labeled "Spirit Evolution" {{ETYK|{{j2|スピリット進化|Supiritto shinka}}}}, and requires that the Spirit card be played in the player's Support Box to evolve a Digimon of an appropriate level ([[Adult|Level IV/Adult]] for Human Form Hybrids, and [[Perfect]] for Beast Form Hybrids). The Spirit remains in the Support Box until the resulting Digimon is removed from play. In addition to the Appearance Requirements use, each Spirit card also confers a number of additional effects onto the Digimon that used it to Spirit Evolve. Unrelated to Hybrid Digimon, some Human Spirits have an additional effect that allows them to substitute for certain [[Digimental]] cards. Spirit cards are not used to evolve into [[Hybrid#Fusion Form|Fusion Form]] or [[Hybrid#Transcendent Form|Transcendent Form]] Hybrid Digimon. These Digimon all have other Evolution/Fusion/Appearance Requirements instead. In the ''Digital Monster Card Game'', all Spirits are given formulaic, abbreviated names: Human Spirits are "Spirit of ''(x)'' H" {{ETYK|''(x)''のスピリットＨ}} and Beast Spirits are "Spirit of ''(x)'' B" {{ETYK|''(x)''のスピリットＢ}}. Because the Corrupted Spirits of Darkness were released first, they occupy the formulaic H and B initials for Darkness, so the two true Spirits are instead called "Spirit of Darkness L" {{ETYK|闇のスピリットＬ}} and "Spirit of Darkness K" {{ETYK|闇のスピリットＫ}}, taking their initials from the respective Digimon that they create, [[Löwemon]] and [[Kaiser Leomon]]. {| |valign="top"| *[[Human Spirit of Fire|Spirit of Fire H]]: [[St-676]] *[[Human Spirit of Light|Spirit of Light H]]: [[St-677]] *[[Human Spirit of Ice|Spirit of Ice H]]: [[St-678]] *[[Human Spirit of Wind|Spirit of Wind H]]: [[St-679]] *[[Human Spirit of Thunder|Spirit of Thunder H]]: [[St-680]] *[[Corrupted Human Spirit of Darkness|Spirit of Darkness H]]: [[St-681]] *[[Human Spirit of Steel|Spirit of Steel H]]: [[St-682]] *[[Human Spirit of Water|Spirit of Water H]]: [[St-683]] *[[Human Spirit of Wood|Spirit of Wood H]]: [[St-684]] *[[Human Spirit of Earth|Spirit of Earth H]]: [[St-685]] |valign="top"| *[[Beast Spirit of Fire|Spirit of Fire B]]: [[St-739]] *[[Beast Spirit of Wind|Spirit of Wind B]]: [[St-740]] *[[Beast Spirit of Earth|Spirit of Earth B]]: [[St-741]] *[[Beast Spirit of Steel|Spirit of Steel B]]: [[St-742]] *[[Beast Spirit of Ice|Spirit of Ice B]]: [[St-743]] *[[Beast Spirit of Thunder|Spirit of Thunder B]]: [[St-744]] *[[Beast Spirit of Water|Spirit of Water B]]: [[St-745]] *[[Beast Spirit of Light|Spirit of Light B]]: [[St-746]] *[[Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness|Spirit of Darkness B]]: [[St-747]] *[[Beast Spirit of Wood|Spirit of Wood B]]: [[St-748]] |valign="top"| *[[Human Spirit of Darkness|Spirit of Darkness L]]: [[Bo-852]] *[[Beast Spirit of Darkness|Spirit of Darkness K]]: [[Bo-853]] |} =Additional Information= {{R}} [[Category:Items]] [[Category:Legendary Spirits| ]] [[Category:Digital Lifeforms]]

Lili.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Lili (Emilie De Rochefort) |image=Lili2.jpg |imagesize=x320 |kan=リリ (エミリー・ド・ロッシュフォール) |dub= |age= |pd=[[Catherine (Re:Digitize)|Catherine]] (Numemon)
[[Lilithmon]] (temporarily) |dv= |vjp=[[Koshimizu Ami]] (小清水 亜美) |ven= }} '''Emilie De Rochefort''', usually referred to simply as '''Lili''', is a character that can be fought in the [[Village of Beginnings|Colosseum]] in ''[[Digimon World Re:Digitize]]'', where she's partnered with [[Catherine (Re:Digitize)|Catherine]]. In ''[[Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode|Decode]]'', she becomes a supporting character in the '''Scheme of the Demon Lords chapter''' (策謀の魔王篇 ''Sakubō no Maō-hen''), where she temporarily partners up with [[Lilithmon]] of the [[Seven Great Demon Lords]], before using her power to make Catherine one. =Profile= Lili, along with [[Sebastian]], are from the ''{{w|Tekken}}'' series of fighting games. =Appearances= ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize}}=== Lili can be encountered on the first floor of the [[Infinity_Mountain#Digimon_World_Re:Digitize|Colosseum]] after defeating [[Professor Petrov]] and his [[Metal Greymon]]. She can then be challenged for a match against her [[Numemon]], [[Catherine (Re:Digitize)|Catherine]], which she considers beautiful. She and Sebastian re-appear on the 30th floor in a double battle against Catherine (now a [[Monzaemon]]) and Sebastian's [[Holy Angemon]]. After being defeated, Lili laments not being able to show her fighting skills since the Colosseum matches are between Digimon. After obtaining access to the VIP Room on the 40th Floor and talking to both Sebastian and Lili in there, Lili will disguise herself with a pink Monzaemon costume and enter herself as a Digimon in a Colosseum match under the alias "{{w|Kigurumi}} Lili". After being defeated, Lili complains about the costume being heavy and the mask making it hard to see what's in front of her. She'll complain about not being able to fight [[Taiga]] himself because the rules only allow matches between Digimon. Two days after being defeated, she will send a challenge e-mail to Taiga requesting a battle. Catherine has now evolved into [[Lilithmon]], which greatly pleases Lili since her Digimon has "Lili" in their name, but it is at odds with Sebastian's [[Seraphimon]] due to their opposite natures and refuses to cooperate. Lili asks the player to defeat both so they calm down, and afterwards recognizes the player as her rival. As a reward, Lili and Sebastian will give their evolution data to the Taiga, unlocking Lilithmon and Seraphimon as obtainable Digimon. ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== After recruiting [[Geo Greymon]] to the [[Village of Beginnings]], [[Taiga]] meets Lili and [[Sebastian]] in the middle of an argument in the Colosseum. Sebastian thought Lili's love for street fighting and online gaming was unbecoming of her position and threatened to tell her father about it. While Lili was shocked by this, she got immediately distracted by seeing Taiga and gave him the right to challenge her, then left, annoying Sebastian. Lili can be challenged as the second Free Battle on the Colosseum's 1st Floor: "A challenge from Lili". Lili commends Taiga's courage for challenging her instead of running away and decides to show him the strongest Digimon she had raised, [[Catherine (Re:Digitize)|Catherine]] the [[Numemon]], which she finds beautiful. After being defeated, Lili praises Taiga's strength but warns him that's not all she can do. She then gives him approval to call her "Lili" then leaves alongside Sebastian. Sebastian explains to Taiga that Lili had fun and being allowed to call her that is a sign of affection, before they both leave. {| |[[File:Digimon World ReDigitize Decode - Lili.png|thumb|Lili in the Colosseum]] |[[File:Digimon World ReDigitize Decode - A challenge from Lili.png|thumb|"A challenge from Lili"]] |} Lili and Sebastian suddenly challenge Taiga after beating three battles in the Colosseum 30th floor Free Battle: "Lili and Sebastian" (though Taiga is forced into it, without selecting it). Lili wanted Taiga to fight her newly-[[Evolution/Video_Games#Digimon_World_Re:Digitize_.26_Decode|evolved]] Catherine, now a [[Monzaemon]], and wondered how long [[Digimaru]] could hold out. Sebastian wondered when she got that Digimon but would do his best to support her nonetheless alongside [[Angemon (Re:Digitize)|Holy Angemon]]. After being defeated, Lili got frustrated, promised not to play with Taiga ever again, then left. Sebastian assured Taiga he had done nothing wrong before following his mistress. {| |[[File:Digimon World ReDigitize Decode - Lili and Sebastian 1.png|thumb|"Lili and Sebastian"]] |[[File:Digimon World ReDigitize Decode - Lili and Sebastian 2.png|thumb|"Lili and Sebastian"]] |} Taiga meets Lili again in the VIP Room. She tells him she came up with a plan to defeat him, laughs, then tells him to go to the reception desk when he's ready, because she won't lose again, then leaves. Taiga can then enter the 40th floor Free Battle: "Lili's Secret Weapon", where Taiga finds her wearing a red Monzaemon suit under the name "Kigurumi Lili". She explains to Taiga she registered herself as a Digimon to use her martial arts skills. However, the costume ends up limiting her movement and sight. She calls Taiga a coward and tells him to fight her by himself instead of using his Digimon for it. Taiga then explains to her the Colosseum is a place for Digimon to fight. Lili says she still has a plan ready and next time it'll be Taiga's end. Sebastian apologises on her behalf, then they both leave. This awards Taiga the Lovely Plushie item. Taiga can then talk to Sebastian in the VIP Room, where he tells him that Lili has locked herself in her room since then to read. {| |[[File:Digimon World ReDigitize Decode - Kigurumi Lili.png|thumb|Kigurumi Lili]] |[[File:Digimon World ReDigitize Decode - Lili's Secret Weapon.png|thumb|"Lili's Secret Weapon"]] |} However, the next time Taiga talks to Sebastian, the latter tells him that Lili managed to escape from the Colosseum, putting her in danger. They meet again in the [[Dark Area]], where she introduces herself as a hero who was summoned to the [[Digital World]], a girl who wants the best partner, and a martial artist who travels the world, then orders [[Belphemon: Sleep Mode]] to wake up to become her [[Partner Digimon]]. Realizing she was too quiet, she raises her voice more and more, then runs out of patience and kicks Belphemon's clock. This causes the world to shake and [[Shinomiya Rina]] immediately realizes how bad of an idea it was to kick a Demon Lord, though for Lili, it should be a huge honour to get kicked by her. Belphemon then shoots lightning close to Lili, proving Rina's point, though Lili thinks it can't hit her as she's honed her body to the extreme. Belphemon then attacks Taiga and Rina as well, so they send Digimaru and [[V.V.]] to fight Belphemon. Rina thought it calmed down after the fight and wanted to take the chance, but Lili insstead went and kicked it again, causing it to awaken as [[Belphemon: Rage Mode]]. It only woke up once every thousand years, and considered Lili admirable for accomplishing that feat, so as a reward she'd receive its strongest attack. Lili's confidence then left her as she realized how bad the situation was. Rina pushed Lili out of the way, getting hit by Belphemon's attack instead. Having released its overwhelming power, Belphemon returned to its slumber to maintain the order of the world, so it entered its Sleep Mode once again. Lili didn't intend for this to happen and was now indebted to someone whose name she didn't even know, a debt she intended to repay when her time to die came, though she would be in Heaven and Rina may be in Hell so she'd never get the chance to do so. As she went through this monologue, Rina woke up and then shouted at her to shut up. Lili questioned if Rina was a human or a cockroach if she could survive that, but in reality, Rina's [[List of Digivices|Digivice]] absorbed Belphemon's power, completing their mission. Rina wanted to go back to the Digitower as they had no time for Lili, an obnoxious little fake princess, then left running. Lili didn't like being called a fake princess as the Rochefort family was one of the most distinguished houses, so she pursued Rina. The heroes then confronted [[Lilithmon]] in the Digitower. Lili then showed up, telling Lilithmon she couldn't speak of the balance of power as long as she was around, leaving [[Nicolai Petrov]] and [[Kuga Yuuya]] confused. Lili then told them she was back as there was no way she'd miss out on this fun and she intended to get her revenge on Taiga. To do so, she decided to assist Lilithmon, who'd become her Partner Digimon as she had none. While Lilithmon found her insolent, she allowed her to do as she pleased because it wouldn't make much of a difference. Lilithmon then turned to the Decode ability users, who she intended to kill. However, she was defeated, and only then did she realize they had Belphemon's power. She told them they were foolish, as they had played into Barbamon's hands. Lili was worried for Lilithmon, which she wasn't. She thought even getting her power wouldn't be enough for the humans to win, then perished, with her power going into Rina's Digivice. After V.V. caused their exit to collapse, Lili told them not to worry about such trivial things as she had found an emergency exit they should be able to open with their new power. After agreeing to use said exit, Nico told Lili to go back to the Real World as she was in danger there. Accepting Sebastian must be worried too and she was a bad kid, she told Nico she'd go back straight away, though in reality, she only did so because she obtained what she wanted: the evolution item for Lilithmon, which she could use on Catherine to make her truly invincible, so now she had no reason to not go back to the Colosseum where everyone, including Taiga, would bow down to her, the [[Tamer]] with a Demon Lord as a Partner. {| |[[File:Digimon World ReDigitize Decode - Lili 2.png|thumb|Lili in the Dark Area]] |[[File:Digimon World ReDigitize Decode - Lilithmon Digimaru V.V..png|thumb|Lilithmon facing Digimaru and V.V.]] |} Lili then sends Taiga a challenge mail for the Colosseum 40th floor, telling him he'd face a Demon Lord he knew well. This is the Free Battle: "Angels and Demons". There, she boasted of how she finally obtained a beautiful Digimon worthy of being her Partner who also had "Lili" in its name in Lilithmon. Meanwhile, Sebastian would face Taiga with [[Seraphimon]], which had "Se" in its name. However, their Digimon did not get along, with Lilithmon dissing and threatening Seraphimon, who stoically returned her threats. Lili and Sebastian were unable to stop them, so Lili asked Taiga to do it instead while she watched him do it. After winnning, Lili accepts Taiga as her rival and gives him the evolution data from her and Sebastian's Digivices, unlocking Lilithmon and Seraphimon as evolutions for Digimaru, then goes back to the VIP Room. Sebastian apologises for the fight while hoping Lili learned a lesson from the fight and may become more ladylike. Lili can then be fought alongside Lilithmon on the 40th floor Free Battle: "I'll take you on any time". This awards Taiga a Bewitching Hairpin. {| |[[File:Digimon World ReDigitize Decode - Angels and Demons 1.png|thumb|"Angels and Demons"]] |[[File:Digimon World ReDigitize Decode - Angels and Demons 2.png|thumb|"Angels and Demons"]] |[[File:Digimon World ReDigitize Decode - I'll take you on any time.png|thumb|"I'll take you on any time"]] |} =Image Gallery= {{IG |w=200 |h=200 |bg=white |i1=Lili.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon World Re:Digitize|Re:Digitize]]
(character) |i2=Lili2.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c2=[[Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode|Re:Digitize Decode]]
(character) }} {{NavboxReDigitize}} [[Category:Digimon World Re:Digitize characters]]

Makino Ruki.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Makino Ruki |image=Ruki.gif |imagesize=x320 |kan=牧野 留姫 |dub=Rika Nonaka |title=Digimon Queen (デジモンクイーン) |age=10 (Dub: 13{{ref|[http://www.konaka.com/alice6/tamers/characters/ruki-e.html Chiaki J. Konaka's ''Digimon Tamers Resources'': Ruki Makino]}}) |pd=[[Renamon (Tamers)|Renamon]] |dv=Blue [[D-Ark]] |family=[[Makino Rumiko]] (Mother), [[Hata Seiko]] (Grandmother), Father (Unnamed) |vjp=[[Orikasa Fumiko]] (折笠 富美子) |ven=[[Melissa Fahn]] }} '''Makino Ruki''' is one of the main protagonists of ''[[Digimon Tamers]]''. She is the partner of [[Renamon (Tamers)|Renamon]]. =Profile= At the start, Ruki was cold and oftentimes competitive. Her initial goal was for Renamon to defeat as many Digimon as possible so that she can load their data and evolve. Unfriendly toward [[Matsuda Takato]] and [[Lee Jianliang]] in the beginning, she gradually opened up to them and her partner, softening her stance on them while retaining the confidence she held at the beginning. Eventually, she became a close ally to the group. =Evolution= * '''[[Sakuyamon]] (with [[Renamon]]{{rfe|DT|39|Whirling Ultimate Flower! Sakuyamon Evolves|b}})''' ==Card Game Evolution== * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Evolves From Tamers|Any Digimon that also Evolves from non-White Tamers from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{Note|[[BT18-078 (DCG)]] Duskmon can change any Tamer's Color into any other Color, except White.|n=DuskTamerColor}} * [[Blitzmon]]{{rfc|BT18|063 (DCG)}} * [[Bolgmon]]{{rfc|BT18|067 (DCG)}} * [[Fairimon]]''[[BT7-035 (DCG)|BT7-035]]'' * [[Garummon]]{{rfc|BT17|023 (DCG)}} * [[Kaiser Greymon]]{{rfc|BT18|095 (DCG)}} * [[Kaiser Leomon]]{{rfc|BT18|077 (DCG)}} * [[Löwemon]]{{rfc|BT18|076 (DCG)}} * [[Magna Garurumon]]{{rfc|BT18|097 (DCG)}} * [[Raihimon]]{{rfc|BT18|081 (DCG)}} * [[Rhino Kabuterimon]]{{rfc|BT18|070 (DCG)}} * [[Shutumon]]''[[BT7-036 (DCG)|BT7-036]]'' * [[Susanoomon]]''[[BT7-112 (DCG)|BT7-112]]'' * [[Wolfmon]]{{rfc|BT17|022 (DCG)}} =Appearances= ==Anime== ===Background=== Ruki was born to her mother and father when her mother was only 18 years old. However, after several years, her parents divorced, and Ruki seldom saw her father from that point onward. A year before the series started, Ruki participated in the Digimon Grand Prix and got to the final round, but was defeated by [[Akiyama Ryo]], who disappeared shortly afterwards. In the next Grand Prix, where he failed to appear, she was the winner, and was given the title of "the Digimon Queen". Following her victory, Ruki was confronted by a multitude of Digimon, who all wanted her to tame them. Ruki declared that she only wanted a powerful Digimon partner, causing a [[Blue Card]] to appear. The Card, upon being slashed through her Card Reader, caused the Card Reader to transform into a [[D-Ark]] and allow her partner, Renamon, to enter the Real World. Ruki began to locate Digital Fields and destroy the Digimon that emerged in them, believing that their data would give Renamon the power to [[Evolution|Evolve]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: The Adventurers' Battle}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: The Runaway Digimon Express}}=== {| |[[File:TRDE Makino Ruki.png|thumb|Ruki being controlled by [[Parasimon]].]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== {| |[[File:MimiTokiwoKakeruShounenHunterTachi.jpg|thumb|Ruki, [[Renamon (Tamers)|Renamon]], [[Tachikawa Mimi|Mimi]], and [[Palmon (Adventure)|Palmon]] in [[DigiQuartz]].]] |} ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers Original Story: Message in the Packet}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Series 1-4: Original Story Special ~with~}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD|Digimon Tamers 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD ~Days -Information and the Unordinary-~}}=== ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|Digimon Xros Wars}}=== During "''[[XWM22|BONUS TRACK]]''", [[Wizarmon (Adventure)|Wizarmon]] saw other universes. In one of them, he saw [[Matsuda Takato]] travelling through the [[Digital World (Tamers)|Digital World]] alongside [[Guilmon (Tamers)|Guilmon]], Ruki and [[Shioda Hirokazu]]. {| |[[File:Wizarmon's vision XW.jpg |thumb|Wizarmon's vision in "''BONUS TRACK''".]] |} ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: Pocket Culumon}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: Digimon Medley}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: Battle Spirit}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Tamers: Battle Evolution}}=== Ruki serves as [[Renamon (Tamers)|Renamon's]] Tamer. As such, she appears in the character select menu alongside her Partner Digimon and when Renamon evolves into [[Sakuyamon]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon RPG}}=== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster Card Game Ver. Medusamon.wikitext:
{{S2 |drbentry={{DRBEntry|1234|1235|2a=Lamiamon}} |kan=メデューサモン |rom=Medyūsamon |ipa= |da={{DigiAlph|me|dyu|-|sa|mon}} |da2={{DigiAlph2|m|e|d|u|s|a|mon}} |add= |dub= |dref= |ol={{ZHO}} 美杜莎兽{{DD|ma}}
{{CHI}} 梅杜莎獸{{DD|ma}}
{{KOR}} 메두사몬{{DD|ma}} |image=Medusamon.jpg |imagesize=320x320 |l1=Ultimate |l1ref={{DD|ma}} |a1=Virus |a1ref={{DD|ma}} |t1=Dragon Man |t1ref={{DD|ma}} |s1= |p=Digimon Reference Book |e=A Dragon Man Digimon that evolved via data from a {{w|Medusa|mythological monster}}. During battle, the sentient serpent-dragons growing from its head drive the enemy away, so its true form rarely moves and often assumes an elegant stance. Even if someone is able to endure the serpent-dragons' attacks, it can deal with them easily in close combat with its fanged boots and the stinger on its tail.{{br}}It is extremely fond of small, round Digimon, and despite those it has taken an interest in likely being Babies, it will petrify them without mercy, take them home, and line them up as a collection.{{br}}Its Special Move is a kicking-blow that bites the enemy with the fangs on its boots ({{AT|Biting Boot}}). Its "{{AT|Eyes of}} {{AT|w=Gorgon}}", in which it emits a flash from its large eye, petrifying anything the light touches, has the ability to rewrite the target's texture to stone, forcibly {{w|Hang (computing)|freezing}} them, and is a powerful technique that cannot be lifted unless Medusamon touches the opponent directly. In its "{{AT|Raging Serpentine}}", where it unleashes its braided serpent-dragons, the serpent-dragons, who regain their original power by being unraveled, run rampant, so they exhibit high destructive power regardless of whether it is one-against-one or one-against-many. |j=神話に登場する怪物のデータによって進化した竜人型デジモン。戦いの際には頭部から生えた意思を持つ蛇竜が敵を打ち払うため、本体が動くことは少なく優雅に構えていることが多い。たとえ蛇竜の攻撃をしのぐ者がいても、牙を持つブーツと尻尾の毒針で近接戦闘も難なくこなす。
　小さくて丸っこいデジモンに目がなく、気に入った相手は幼年期だろうが容赦なく石化して持ち帰り、コレクションとして並べる。
　必殺技は、ブーツの牙で敵を噛み千切る蹴りの一撃『{{ATK|バイティングブート}}』。大きな一つ目から閃光を放ち、光を浴びたものを石化させる『{{ATK|アイズオブゴルゴーン}}』は、対象のテクスチャを石に書き換えて強制的にフリーズさせる能力であり、メデューサモンが直接相手に触れなければ解除されない強力な技だ。編んでいる蛇竜を解き放つ『{{ATK|レイジングサーペンタイン}}』は、ほどかれたことによって本来の力を取り戻した蛇竜が暴れ回るため、一対一や一対多数を問わず高い殲滅力を発揮する。 |u=medusamon |ety={{GRE}} {{ETY|Medusa}}
{{noun}} From {{ETY|{{w|Medusa}}}} (Ancient Greek: Μέδουσα ''Médousa''), was one of the three {{w|Gorgons}}, in {{w|Greek mythology}}. |yd=2024 |ydr= |drbed=March 27 |drbedy=2025 |ad= |md={{hdr|Digimon Liberator - Episode 08 (Part 1)|Digimon Liberator: "CHAIN OF LIBERATION (Part 1)"}} |gd= |vd= |cd=BT21-029 (DCG) }} =Attack Techniques= {{T |name=Biting Boot |ref={{DD|ma}} |kan=バイティングブート |rom=Baitingu Būto |d=Biting Boots{{DD|ma}} |desc=Delivers a kick that bites the enemy with the fangs on its boots. |name2=Eyes of Gorgon |ref2={{DD|ma}} |kan2=アイズオブゴルゴーン |rom2=Aizu obu Gorugōn |d2=Gorgon Eye{{DD|ma}} |desc2=Emits a flash from its large eye that petrifies anything the light touches. |namel=Raging Serpentine |refl={{DD|ma}} |kanl=レイジングサーペンタイン |roml=Reijingu Sāpentain |dl=Raging Serpentine{{DD|ma}} |descl=Unleashes its braided serpent-dragons. }} =Evolution= ==Evolves From== * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Red Lv.5 Digimon|Any Red Lv.5 Digimon from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{rfc|BT21|029 (DCG)}} * '''[[Lamiamon]]'''{{ref|''[[Digimon Liberator]]''}} ==Evolves To== * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Also Evolves From Red Lv.6|Any Digimon that also Evolves from Red Lv.6 Digimon from the ''Digimon Card Game'']] * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Red Lv.7 Digimon|Any Red Lv.7 Digimon from the ''Digimon Card Game'']] * [[Dorbickmon]]{{rfc|EX3|014 (DCG)}} (with or without [[Digimon Card Game DigiXros#Dorbickmon|certain Digimon from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{rfc|EX3|014 (DCG)}}) =Appearances= ==Anime== ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Liberator}}=== [[Elizamon (Liberator)|Medusamon]] is the [[Partner Digimon]] of [[Owen Dreadnought]]. ==Video Games== ==Virtual Pets== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |s1=DCG }} =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Medusamon.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Reference Book]] }} |2=misc |t2= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Digimonliberator new key visual.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Liberator]]

Menoa Bellucci.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Menoa Bellucci |image=Menoa_bellucci.jpg |imagesize=x320 |kan=メノア・ベルッチ |dub=Menoa Bellucci |age=22 |vjp=[[Matsuoka Mayu]] (松岡 茉優) |ven=[[Erika Harlacher]] |pd=[[Morphomon (Adventure)|'''Morphomon'''/Eosmon]] |dv=[[Digivice]] }} '''Menoa Bellucci''' is the main antagonist of ''[[Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna]]''. =Profile= =Card Game Evolution= * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Evolves From Tamers|Any Digimon that also Evolves from non-White Tamers from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{Note|[[BT18-078 (DCG)]] Duskmon can change any Tamer's Color into any other Color, except White.|n=DuskTamerColor}} * [[Kaiser Greymon]]{{rfc|BT18|095 (DCG)}} * [[Magna Garurumon]]{{rfc|BT18|097 (DCG)}} * [[Susanoomon]]''[[BT7-112 (DCG)|BT7-112]]'' =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna}}=== Menoa Bellucci is a researcher who was once a [[Chosen Child]]. She appears to be kind at first, but turns out to be cruel despite her goals. She lived in Colorado and was a child prodigy, so people looked at her badly, and she felt alone. She met her partner Digimon, [[Morphomon (Adventure)|Morphomon]], when she was 9 years old. She took it everywhere with her, and gave her a butterfly hair clip that she currently uses. At age 14, Menoa decided to move to New York when she was accepted to a university, but when she told the news to Morphomon, she realized that it was disappearing, and left only her butterfly hair clip. Due to the loss of her partner, Menoa spent years studying the Digimon trying to bring Morphomon back, but always unsuccessful. She eventually managed to find Morphomon's data but was unable to use it to bring Morphomon back. After the aurora in August 2010, Morphomon's data suddenly reconfigured into its [[Digitama]] and Menoa claimed that Morphomon, from within her Digitama, telepathically told her to use her data to help save all the [[Chosen Children]] in the World from having to suffer the same fate they had suffered, and so Menoa re-configured Morphomon's data, turning it into the artificial Digimon [[Eosmon]]. She then mass re-produces them to help in her goals of kidnapping the consciousness of every Chosen Child in the World. Eosmon had the ability to transform human consciousness into data and would them take them to [[Never-Land]]. Each Chosen Child would be imprisoned within their own island, be de-aged, and would be trapped in their memories, remaining with their partners forever. Menoa claims she did this so that no one else will suffer the loss of their Digimon partner, as she did. Everyone was freed by Taichi and Yamato and she was arrested after Eosmon's destruction. ==Video Games== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Menoa bellucci |s1=DCG }} =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Menoa_bellucci.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna]] |i2=Menoa Bellucci 9 years old.png{{!}}200x200px |c2=[[Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna]]
(9 years old) |i3=Menoa Bellucci 14 years old.png{{!}}200x200px |c3=[[Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna]]
(14 years old) |i4=Menoa Bellucci Kizuna Lineart.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c4=[[Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna]]
(Lineart) }} =Additional Information= {{R}} [[Category:Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna characters]]

Miasma.wikitext:
[[Image:Jatetsufuujinmaru unleashed.jpg|thumb|The {{EQ|Jatetsufūjin-maru|Snake Sealed by God in Iron}}'s true form unleashed]]
'''Miasma''' (瘴気, ''Shōki'') is a type of {{w|Miasma theory|dark mist}} born from negative emotions that brims with evil and can corrode and disintegrate data in high concentrations. A flapping of the wings of [[Ordinemon]] will spread miasma to its surroundings. The endless miasma that flows from its body would eventually be able to cover the entire world and end all life.{{refd|Ordinemon}}

=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure tri.}}===
[[Meicoomon (Adventure)|Ordinemon]]'s wings are covered in miasma. 

==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure:}}===
[[File:Miasma DA2020-10.jpg|thumb|One of [[Kido Joe (Adventure:)|Kido Joe]]'s books being corroded in a miasma swamp.]]
Several Digimon controlled by [[Devimon (Adventure:)|Devimon]] expel this dark mist from their mouths. In response to this phenomenon, [[Leomon]] is leading a resistance army against evil Digimon infected by the dark miasma. It's mentioned that the miasma overflows evilness. Crossing the miasma is impossible for normal humans or Digimon.

Entire pools and seas of miasma exist in the Digital World. The one that is close to Devimon's Castle is able to form into a Digimon, [[Splashmon Darkness Mode]].'''{{rfe|DA:|21|''The Tide-turning Update''|b}}

The miasma can corrupt living beings such as [[Yagami Taichi]], and said corruption can trigger Dark Evolutions, such as [[Agumon (Adventure:)|Metal Greymon: Alterous Mode]] to [[Mugendramon]]{{rfe|DA:|24|''The Final Stage, Done Devimon''|b}}. However, its power can also be absorbed to cause Mode Changes that only power up the user without corrupting them, such as allowing [[Metal Greymon]] to enter his [[Metal Greymon: Alterous Mode|Alterous Mode]] and [[Were Garurumon]] to do the same with his [[Were Garurumon: Sagittarius Mode|Sagittarius Mode]].{{rfe|DA:|22|''The Unbeatable Blue Sagittarius''|b}}

==Manga==
==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|Digimon Xros Wars}}===
[[Tactimon (Xros Wars)|Tactimon]]'s unleashed {{EQ|Jatetsufūjin-maru|Snake Sealed by God in Iron}} expels large amounts of miasma in the form of a snake. If a Digimon is slashed with this sword, the power of the miasma will prevent regeneration, even blocking the effect of the {{EQ|l=Ulforce|Ulforce|Ultimate Force}}. After being damaged by Tactimon's sword, [[Zeromaru|Ulforce V-dramon]] was unable to heal from the injuries sustained from this battle and remained blind and crippled in the [[Human World]] for several years. [[Starmons (Xros Wars)|Starmon]] was also incapable of being healed by [[Cutemon (Xros Wars)|Cutemon's]] {{ATO|Kizunaol}} due to it. However, both eventually healed from it, thanks to the [[Xros Loader]] and [[Jogress Evolution]], respectively.

[[Vritramon]]{{rfc|BT7|011 (DCG)}} * [[Wolfmon]]{{rfc|BT4|025 (DCG)}}{{rfc|P|030 (DCG)}} =Profile= Minamoto Kouji is a [[Chosen Child]], chosen by [[Ofanimon]] to inherit the Legendary Spirits of Light. He is also the twin brother of [[Kimura Kouichi|Kouichi]]. A lone-wolf, he is quiet and aloof, often keeping to himself. Kouji is the second-born of the twins, but they were separated when their parents divorced; Kouji was taken by their father, while Kouichi was taken by their mother. Each twin was told that the parents he was separated from was dead, and neither twin was told of his brother's existence. Kouji's father remarried when Kouji was eight, and Kouji had a hard time accepting her as his new mother, which led to some conflict between himself and his father. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== Kouji received [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]]'s message while he was out buying flowers for his father and stepmother's third wedding anniversary, fulling intending on apologizing to her and calling her "mother" for the first time. Though he met [[Kanbara Takuya]] in the train to Shibuya and rode the elevator to the underground platform with him, he didn't speak to him and got on a different train than the other children. He received his first Spirit, the [[Human Spirit of Light]], when he saved [[Himi Tomoki‎]] and [[Shibayama Junpei‎]] from a mob of [[Pagumon]] who later fused into [[Raremon]]. During this fight, he also showed himself to be quite skilled in martial arts even without his Spirits, an ability that he shows throughout the series. Kouji spent several days traveling separately from the group and did not actually join the team until the incident at the [[Karatuki Numemon]] village, after which he stuck with Takuya and Tomoki while they were in Toy Town. After they rejoined [[Orimoto Izumi]] and Junpei, Kouji remained with the group. Kouji was the first to discover his [[Beast Spirit of Light|Beast Spirit]], evolving him into [[Garummon]] while he was assisting a young [[Gottsumon]] that eventually became his friend. Though he had a hard time controlling Garummon at first and spent a while running into things, he did not have the trouble that Takuya eventually had with his own. While on the Continent of Darkness, Kouji came face-to-face with [[Duskmon]], the Warrior of Darkness and, logically, [[Wolfmon]]'s opposite. He clashed swords with Duskmon, developing a personal insight on just how strong he was. Later, he argued with Takuya over how they should proceed with the battle, with Kouji favoring caution and Takuya insisting that teamwork can conquer anything. Takuya's plan eventually ended in devastating failure, where [[Agnimon]] was nearly brought down by Duskmon's attack, but Garummon took the blow instead. He was badly wounded and forced to revert to his human form, which proved to be his saving grace – when Duskmon saw Kouji and heard his name, he appeared to be in pain and left the battle. When the team was sucked into [[Mercuremon (Frontier)|Sephirothmon]], Kouji found himself confronted by his past and his relationship with his family many times. While inside Sephirothmon's Wind Sphere, he fought with and was taunted by [[Karatenmon]], who claimed that Kouji's loneliness is self-inflicted, though it could also be that his father often moved, causing him to change schools many times. Later, inside the Darkness Sphere, he was confronted by Duskmon once more. Duskmon used the Sphere's power to look into Kouji's memories, discovering Kouji's strained relationship with his family. Seeing this, Duskmon became even angrier and seemed ready to kill Kouji, but just then the power of [[Seraphimon (Frontier)|Seraphimon]] was released from his egg and fused both of Kouji's spirits, creating the Double Spirit Evolution, [[Beowulfmon]]. He and Duskmon clashed swords, releasing auras of dark and light energy that temporarily revealed the spirit inside. Kouji was surprised to find that inside Duskmon, there was a boy who looked exactly like him – his twin brother, [[Kimura Kouichi]], whose existence he did not know of. Following this, Duskmon leaped out of Sephirothmon and ran away, confused. Kouji followed him, intent on discovering what relationship Duskmon has with him. Eventually, Kouichi's memories were returned to him and their relationship was fully revealed. When Kouji discovered that Duskmon is his brother he refused to fight, but Takuya managed to convince him that the only way to save his brother is to fight him, and together they returned Kouichi to normal. Kouji was confused over the sudden appearance of a twin brother he never knew of, but he was determined to get to know Kouichi and found himself caring for him greatly. Because of their desire to get to know each other and be the brothers they never got to be, Kouji and Kouichi grew close very fast. During the final battle with [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]], Kouji's Spirits of Light used Ofanimon's power to merge with Kouichi's purified Spirits of Darkness, Junpei's Spirits of Thunder and the Spirits of Steel and Water to evolve into the Hybrid [[Magna Garurumon]] who, using his own body as a shield, helped [[Kaiser Greymon]] to defeat the corrupted Cherubimon. The two Hybrids were the only ones who were able to stand up against the [[Royal Knights]] and, later on, against [[Lucemon (Frontier)|Lucemon]]. Just before the battle with [[Lucemon: Falldown Mode]] in the Dark Area, Kouji had a short heart-to-heart with his brother, who has realized that he was not physically in the Digital World, but was just a spirit without his body. Though Kouichi was unable to admit this to Kouji, he did make the younger twin promise that he will go visit their mother in the Real World. Soon afterwards, Kouichi came between Lucemon: Falldown Mode's {{ATO|Dead or Alive}} technique and sacrificed himself to save the others, giving Kouji his Spirits before he was scanned. Kouji was overcome with the power of both Light and Darkness. This, along with the emotions from watching his brother being scanned, activated the ultimate evolution of the Legendary Ten - [[Susanoomon]]. Alongside the others, he defeated Lucemon and returned to the Real World just in time to use the [[D-Scanner]]'s power to revive Kouichi. Returning to his life, Kouji made peace with his father and stepmother, and was reintroduced to his birth mother. ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Revival of the Ancient Digimon!!}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== After [[Magnamon]] ran out of power and devolved to [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] in the fight against [[Quartzmon (Xros Wars)|Quartzmon's]] [[Belial Vamdemon]] and [[Venom Vamdemon]] army in "''[[Digimon Xros Wars - Episode 78|The Gathering of the Legendary Heroes! The Digimon All-star Showdown]]''", [[Ichijouji Ken]] appeared alongside [[Wormmon (Adventure)|Stingmon]], followed by [[Ishida Yamato]], [[Gabumon (Adventure)|Metal Garurumon]], and balls of light containing [[Himi Tomoki|Chackmon]], Wolfmon, [[Orimoto Izumi|Fairimon]] and [[Shibayama Junpei|Blitzmon]]. The Spirits then Ancient Spirit Evolved with [[Kanbara Takuya|Aldamon]] to become [[Susanoomon]], who kept a Venom Vamdemon in place for [[Imperialdramon: Fighter Mode]] to end the three with his {{ATO|Giga Death}}. Susanoomon would stay in the form for the rest of the fight against Quartzmon. ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Legendary Skies}}=== ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier Original Story: Things That Want To Be Told}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Get the Biggest Fire!!}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Series 1-4: Original Story Special ~with~}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD ~A Train Named Hope~}}=== ==Live Readings== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Another Mysterious Message! Minase Miyuki.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Minase Miyuki |image=Miyuki survive.jpg |imagesize=x320 |kan=水無瀬ミユキ |rom=Minase Miyuki |dub=Miyuki Minase |age= |height=140 cm |weight=33.8 kg |blood=O |pd=[[Renamon (Survive)|Renamon]] |vjp=[[Kohara Konomi]] (小原 好美) |family=[[Minase Akiharu]] (younger brother)
[[Minase Yukiha]] (ancestor)
[[Minase Haruchika]] (Yukiha's brother) }} =Profile=
水無瀬ミユキ {{br}}'''年齢：'''-- {{br}}'''身長：'''140cm {{br}}'''体重：'''33.8kg {{br}}'''血液型：'''O '''タクマたちと出会うまで：'''{{br}}ミユキは弟のハルとずっと逃げ続けていた。 口を閉ざし、意思を表さず、息を潜め、各地を{{br}}転々としてようやくこの校舎に辿り着いた。 これからどうしたいのか、どうするべきなのか、{{br}}彼女は弟にもそれを語らず、ただじっとしている{{br}}ばかりだった。 黙したまま、未来への希望も抱くことなく、{{br}}彼女は弟に導かれるだけだった。
廃墟同然の木造校舎で{{br}}弟のハルと共に潜んでいたミユキ。 ほとんど喋らないし、{{br}}何を考えているのかもよくわからない。{{br}}彼女がこの世界で生き残ってこられたのは{{br}}ハルのおかげなんだろうな。
ミユキと僕、アグモンだけが元の世界に{{br}}戻ってきた。 ミユキは別人のようにはっきり喋るようになって{{br}}僕たちにたくさんのことを教えてくれる。 あの霧を操る、異世界の『主』のこと。 ミユキの一族『水無瀬』家に伝わる、{{br}}二つの世界をつなぐ力を持つ『歌』のこと。 ミユキはその『歌』の力を使える{{br}}特別な巫女だから、ずっと『主』に{{br}}狙われているんだ。 それにしても、ミユキが異世界にいる間に{{br}}こっちの世界では何十年も経ってたなんて ……ってことは、ミユキって{{br}}僕たちよりずっと年上ってこと！？
僕は信じられない。{{br}}まさかミユキが敵の手におちて、{{br}}僕らに敵対してくるなんて……！ 彼女がいなければ{{br}}僕らが元の世界に戻ることも、{{br}}二つの世界を救うこともできない。 どうにかして救い出さないと……{{br}}でも、どうやって！？
あの優しかったミユキの心は{{br}}封じられているだけだ。 僕らが何度も呼びかけると、ほんの一瞬だけど、{{br}}『あの』ミユキが覚醒する。 彼女が元に戻るチャンスはまだある……{{br}}だから、僕は諦めるわけにはいかないんだ。
主が数百年も待ち続けた、{{br}}『水無瀬』の力を受け継ぐ巫女。それがミユキ。 でも、僕にはミユキがそんな{{br}}特別な存在だとは思えない。 ミユキはただ弟想いで仲間想いの、{{br}}優しいお姉ちゃんだ。{{br}}主に支配されて、大好きな人たちを傷つけて{{br}}平気な子じゃないんだ！ ミユキ、もう少しだけ待っててほしい。{{br}}僕たちが必ず助けるから！
'''レナモン：''' 50年前、ミユキがこの異世界に{{br}}迷い込んだときに出会ったケモノガミ。{{br}}ミユキの相棒でずっとそなにいたものの、{{br}}ミユキは途中で主に魂の半分を奪われたため{{br}}自我がなくなって、レナモンのことを{{br}}忘れてしまった。{{br}}そのため、唯一反応を示したハルの姿となって{{br}}ずっとミユキを支えてきた。 レナモンがいなければミユキは{{br}}もう生きていなかったかもしれないけど、{{br}}その分本来の姿で接する事ができない辛さは、{{br}}僕も同情するしかない。

Mockingbirdmon.wikitext:
{{S2 |drbentry={{DRBEntry|1238th, 1239|1240|2a=Maquinamon and Mulemon}} |kan=モッキンバーモン |rom=Mokkinbāmon |da={{DigiAlph|mo|kki|n|ba|-|mon}} |da2={{DigiAlph2|m|o|c|k|i|n|g|b|i|r|d|mon}} |add= |dub= |dref= |aln= |ol={{ZHO}} 仿声鸟兽{{DD|ma}}
{{CHI}} 仿聲獸{{DD|ma}} |image=Mockingbirdmon.jpg |imagesize=320x320 |l1=Perfect |l1ref={{DD|ma}} |a1=Vaccine |a1ref={{DD|ma}} |t1=Machine |t1ref={{DD|ma}} |s1= |p=Digimon Reference Book |e=A Machine Digimon modeled after a bird. At first glance, it looks like a large-winged Bird Digimon that flies gracefully, but in actuality, what appears to be its head and legs are all robotic arms with beak-like functions. It can move these arms freely, using them in various capacities like laser shooting or capturing the opponent. In addition, the two giant {{EQ|Search Eyes}} equipped to the base of its wings possess excellent detection capabilities, allowing it to find even Digimon hidden in the shadows or underground.{{br}}It doesn't just excel in maneuverability and combat ability. It also has a cunning side that specializes in mimetic behavior; this includes recording the sounds of the Digimon it encounters, and throwing the enemy off guard by reproducing those sounds when hunting.{{br}}Once it has spotted its prey, it uses its robotic arms to corner them with strange movements far removed from its bird appearance. It is truly a hunter of the sky that makes full use of all of its abilities to track down its prey.{{br}}Its Special Moves are piercing the enemy with its sharp beak and draining their life energy ({{AT|Iron Inhaler}}), and shooting lasers while spinning rapidly in an attempt to capture the enemy with its three arms ({{AT|Tri-Airbots}}). |j=鳥の姿を模したマシーン型デジモン。一見優雅な姿で飛ぶ、大きな翼の鳥型デジモンに見えるが、実際には頭や足に見える部分は全て嘴のような機能を備えたロボットアームである。　ロボットアームは自由自在に動かすことができ、レーザーでの狙撃や相手を捕らえるなど、様々な用途に使われる。他、翼の基部に備えられた二つの巨大な{{EQK|サーチアイ}}は優れた探知能力を持ち、物陰や地中に隠れたデジモンすらも発見できる。
　運動性能や戦闘能力に長けているだけではない。遭遇したことのあるデジモンの声を録音し、狩りの際に再生して敵を油断させるなど、擬態行動を得意とする狡猾な一面もある。
　獲物を見つけた際にはロボットアームを使いこなし、鳥の姿からはかけ離れた奇怪な動きで敵を追い詰めていく。まさに、あらゆる能力を駆使して獲物を付け狙う空の狩人だ。
　必殺技は、鋭い嘴を突き刺し敵の生命エネルギーを吸い取る『{{ATK|アイアンインヘイラー}}』と、高速回転しながら3つのアームで相手を絡め取るようにしてレーザーを放つ『{{ATK|トライエアボッツ}}』。 |u=mockingbirdmon |ety={{ENG}} {{ETY|Mockingbird}}. |yd=2025 |ydr= |drbed=May 15 |drbedy=2025 |ad= |md={{hdr|Digimon Liberator - Episode 14 (Part 1)|Digimon Liberator: "Prelude of Destruction"}} |gd= |vd= |cd= }} =Attack Techniques= {{T |name=Iron Inhaler |ref={{DD|ma}} |kan=アイアンインヘイラー |rom=Aian Inheirā |d=Iron Siphon{{DD|ma}} |desc=Pierces the enemy with its sharp beak and drains their life energy. |namel=Tri-Airbots |refl={{DD|ma}} |kanl=トライエアボッツ |roml=Torai Eabottsu |dl=Tri-Airbots{{DD|ma}} |descl=Shoots lasers while spinning rapidly in an attempt to capture the enemy with its three arms. }} =Evolution= ==Evolves From== * '''[[Mulemon]]'''{{ref|''[[Digimon Liberator]]''}} ==Evolves To== =Appearances= ==Anime== ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Liberator}}=== ==Novel== ==Video Games== ==Virtual Pets== ==TCG== =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Mockingbirdmon.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Reference Book]] }} }} =Additional Information= {{R | }}

Although both seem to be designed with the intention of evolving into Digimon related to [[Gokuwmon]] like [[Kakamon]], they are also designed to work with all three new Child Digimon introduced in the [[Digimon Pendulum COLOR|Digimon Pendulum COLOR 6 Saiyu Warriors]], namely Kakamon, [[Huijamon]], and [[Takimon]].[https://digimon.net/topic/detail.php?id=1811 モンモンメモ‼Vol.125 「デジモンペンデュラムCOLOR 6/7」新デジモンについて。] |yd=2025 |ydr= |drbed= |drbedy= |ad= |md= |gd= |vd={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum COLOR}} |cd= }} =Attack Techniques= =Evolution= ==Evolves From== * [[Fukamon]]{{ref|''[[Digimon Pendulum COLOR]]''}} ==Evolves To== * [[Hujiamon]]{{ref|''[[Digimon Pendulum COLOR]]''}} * [[Kakamon]]{{ref|''[[Digimon Pendulum COLOR]]''}} * [[Kamemon]]{{ref|''[[Digimon Pendulum COLOR]]''}} * [[Takimon]]{{ref|''[[Digimon Pendulum COLOR]]''}} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==Manga== ==Novel== ==Video Games== ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum COLOR}}=== ==TCG== =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Mokokomon.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Web]] }} }} ==Virtual Pets== {{IG |bg=white |w=80 |h=80 |i1=Mokokomon vpet dpc.gif{{!}}80x80px |c1=[[Digimon Pendulum COLOR]] }} =Additional Information= {{R | }}

'''Morphomon''' was the [[Partner Digimon]] of [[Menoa Bellucci]] in ''[[Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna]]''. '''Eosmon''' was born after Menoa modified Morphomon's data.

=Evolution=
{{Box Evo
|Baby1= ?
|Baby2= ?
|Child='''[[Morphomon]]'''
|Adult= '''[[Eosmon (Adult)]]'''
|Perfect= [[Eosmon (Perfect)]]
|Ultimate= [[Eosmon (Ultimate)]]
}}

=Appearances=
==Anime==
==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure: Last Evolution Kizuna}}===
Morphomon became [[Menoa Bellucci]]'s Partner Digimon when Menoa was 9 years old. They spent the next five years together as the best of friends. One day, when Menoa happily told Morphomon that she had skipped enough grades to be allowed to start College and that she could finally live her own life, Morphomon disintegrated into data and ceased to exist in the tearful Menoa's arms.

At some point between Morphomon ceasing to exist and August 2010, Menoa managed to locate Morphomon's data, however any instances of her trying to edit and restore it would result in failure. When the aurora formed the data suddenly reconfigured into Morphomon's [[Digitama]] and Menoa claimed that Morphomon, from within her Digitama, telepathically told her to use her data to help save all the [[Chosen Children]] in the World from having to suffer the same fate they had suffered, and so Menoa reconfigured Morphomon's data, turning it into the artificial Digimon [[Eosmon]]. She then mass reproduces them to help in her goals of kidnapping the consciousness of every Chosen Child around the world so that they could live in [[Never-Land]] with their partners forever so that none of them had to be separated from their Digimon like Menoa was. The Chosen Children were de-aged and brainwashed into thinking this was what they wanted. 

After all the Eosmon (Perfect) merge together into its Ultimate form it easily defeats [[Omegamon]], however it is killed by [[Agumon (Adventure)|Agumon -Yuki no Kizuna-]] and [[Gabumon (Adventure)|Gabumon -Yujo no Kizuna-]]. As it dies, it appears in front of Menoa in its Adult form and smiles at Menoa, and Menoa, remembering that it used to be Morphomon, smiles back and apologizes to Eosmon.

Necromon.wikitext:
{{S2 |drbentry={{DRBEntry|1233}} |kan=ネクロモン |rom=Nekuromon |ipa= |da={{DigiAlph|ne|ku|ro|mon}} |da2={{DigiAlph2|n|e|c|r|o|mon}} |add= |dub= |dref= |ol={{ZHO}} 幽灵巫师兽{{DD|na}}
{{CHI}} 死靈巫師獸{{DD|na}}
{{KOR}} 네크로몬{{DD|na}} |image=Necromon.jpg |imagesize=320x320 |l1=Ultimate |l1ref={{DD|na}} |a1=Virus |a1ref={{DD|na}} |t1=Ghost |t1ref={{DD|na}} |s1= |p=Digimon Reference Book |e=A Ghost Digimon of the highest class that took in data from various ghosts and horrors on the [[Net]]. Its characteristic gravestone-like sword, the "{{EQ|Grave-Blade}}", is a brutal weapon that brings death to those who touch it, despite being blunt. If it encounters a superior Digimon as it wanders around, it will bring them down by stabbing them with the {{EQ|Grave-Blade}} without making a sound. As such, it takes pleasure in absorbing and consuming the fading data of the Digimon it defeats with the lantern it holds.{{br}}The data it absorbs with its lantern is recorded in the "{{EQ|w=Necronomicon}}", the book that it carries in its hand, and can be summoned as an apparition during battle.{{br}}While it possesses the powerful ability to manipulate the dead, it is considerably weak against light and holy power, so it never appears before Digimon that bear such power.{{br}}Its Special Moves are stabbing the {{EQ|Grave-Blade}} into the opponent as if it were an actual gravestone, penetrating their defenses, and completely destroying their [[Digicore]] ({{AT|Soul Striker}}), and summoning the data of the Digimon it has absorbed as apparitions all at once, and annihilating the opponent with an army that will not stop regardless of how injured they are ({{AT|Apparition Legion}}). |j=ネット上のあらゆる幽霊や恐怖のデータを取り込んだ、最上位クラスのゴースト型デジモン。特徴的な墓石のような剣「{{EQK|グレイブレイド}}」は、切れ味がないに等しいなまくらではあるものの、触れた者に死をもたらす凶悪な代物。徘徊した先で優れたデジモンに遭遇すると、音もなく{{EQK|グレイブレイド}}を突き立てて倒してしまう。そうして倒したデジモンの消えゆくデータを、手にしたランタンで吸収し喰らうことを喜びとする。
　ランタンで吸収したデータは手に携えた書物「{{EQK|ネクロノミコン}}」に記録され、戦闘の際に霊体として召喚することが可能だ。
　死者を操る強力な力を持つ一方で、光や聖なる力に著しく弱いため、そうした力を持つデジモンの前には決して姿を現さない。
　必殺技は「{{EQK|グレイブレイド}}」を本物の墓標のように相手に突き立て、防御を貫通しデジコアを完全に破壊する『{{ATK|ソウルストライカー}}』と、取り込んだデジモン達のデータを霊体として一斉に召喚し、たとえ傷つこうと止まらない軍勢で相手を殲滅する『{{ATK|アパリションレギオン}}』。 |u=necromon |ety={{ENG}} {{ETY|Necro}}
{{noun}} From {{w|Necromancy|{{ETY|{{FGU|Necro}}mancer}}}} {{ETYK|{{FGM|ネクロ}}マンサー|Nekuromansā}}. |yd=2025 |ydr= |drbed=January 16 |drbedy=2025 |ad= |md={{hdr|Digimon Liberator - Episode 10 (Part 1)|Digimon Liberator: "OVERWRITE BLACK"}} |gd= |vd= |cd=BT20-079 (DCG) }} =Attack Techniques= {{T |name=Soul Striker |ref={{DD|na}} |kan=ソウルストライカー |rom=Souru Sutoraikā |d=Soul Striker{{DD|na}} |desc=Stabs the {{EQ|Grave-Blade}} into its opponent, penetrating their defenses and completely destroying their [[Digicore]]. |namel=Apparition Legion |refl={{DD|na}} |kanl=アパリションレギオン |roml=Aparishon Region |dl=Apparition Legion{{DD|na}} |descl=Summons the data of the Digimon it has absorbed as apparitions all at once, and annihilates the opponent with an unstoppable army. }} =Evolution= ==Evolves From== * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Purple Lv.5 Digimon|Any Purple Lv.5 Digimon from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{rfc|BT20|079 (DCG)}} * '''[[Fantomon]]'''{{ref|''[[Digimon Liberator]]''}} ==Evolves To== * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Also Evolves From Purple Lv.6|Any Digimon that also Evolves from Purple Lv.6 Digimon from the ''Digimon Card Game'']] * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Purple Lv.7 Digimon|Any Purple Lv.7 Digimon from the ''Digimon Card Game'']] =Appearances= ==Anime== ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Liberator}}=== [[Ghostmon (Liberator)|Necromon]] is the [[Partner Digimon]] of [[Violet Inboots]]. ==Novel== ==Video Games== ==Virtual Pets== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |s1=DCG }} =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Necromon.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Reference Book]] }} |2=misc |t2= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Violet necromon illustration gs.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=Illustration by [[GS]] |i2=Digimonliberator new key visual.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c2=[[Digimon Liberator]]
(Key Visual) }} }} =Additional Information= {{R | }}

[[Vritramon]]{{rfc|BT7|011 (DCG)}} * [[Wolfmon]]{{rfc|BT17|022 (DCG)}} =Profile= Orimoto Izumi is a [[Chosen Child]], chosen by [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]] to inherit the Legendary Spirits of Wind. The only female of the group, she is a spunky and opinionated girl, the kind of person who doesn't go with the crowd, preferring to stick to her ideals. She is an open person who always speaks her mind, sometimes to the point of unintentionally insulting and alienating others. She is aware of [[Shibayama Junpei‎|Shibayama Junpei's]] crush on her, and though she thinks of him as a good friend, she doesn't return his feelings. Izumi was born in Japan, but she moved to Italy with her family when she was younger. The family lived there for two years, in which Izumi found herself fitting in, becoming fluent in Italian and even learning how to make Italian foods. However, as she returned to Tokyo, she found herself unable to understand the Japanese social codes and fit in, something her outspoken personality did not help. As a result, she was shunned by her classmates, and became quite lonely. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== Izumi received [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon's]] message while she was baking a pizza at home. Thinking that it sounded like an adventure, she traveled to Shibuya Station and boarded a [[Trailmon]] with [[Kanbara Takuya]], Shibayama Junpei, and [[Himi Tomoki]]. She was at odds with Takuya at first, but she found her place on the team quickly. When they tried to help a group of Floramon living in Breezy Village, she found her [[Human Spirit of Wind]], evolving into [[Fairimon]]. After running away from the Evil Warriors, a [[Whamon]] that helped the children brought them to a beach. There, they were tricked by a group of [[Tocanmon]] who were working for [[Ranamon (Frontier)|Ranamon]], and stole the boys' D-Scanners while they were swimming. Izumi, who hadn't managed to change into her swimsuit yet, was the only one with a D-Scanner, and had to protect the boys when Ranamon Slide Evolved into [[Calamaramon]]. It was only thanks to Calamaramon's lack of control over her own Beast Spirit that they managed to escape unharmed. The Tocanmon flew to another island, when the children wanted to cross, a group of [[Gomamon]] stopped them and told them it was no use due to whirlpools. The children decided to try and get by the whirlpools using a makeshift raft, but Calmaramon attacked them again. Izumi evolved and tried to stop her, but she was no match and was thrown into a whirlpool. However, she found the [[Beast Spirit of Wind]] in a shell underwater, and used it to evolve into [[Shutumon]]. As she was able to control her Beast Spirit, she fought Calmaramon off easily and the children were able to get to the other island with ease. ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Revival of the Ancient Digimon!!}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== After [[Magnamon]] ran out of power and devolved to [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] in the fight against [[Quartzmon (Xros Wars)|Quartzmon's]] [[Belial Vamdemon]] and [[Venom Vamdemon]] army in {{epn|XW|78|The Gathering of the Legendary Heroes! The Digimon All-star Showdown!!}}, [[Ichijouji Ken]] appeared alongside [[Wormmon (Adventure)|Stingmon]], followed by [[Ishida Yamato]], [[Gabumon (Adventure)|Metal Garurumon]], and balls of light containing [[Himi Tomoki|Chackmon]], [[Minamoto Kouji|Wolfmon]], Fairimon and [[Shibayama Junpei|Blitzmon]]. The Spirits then Ancient Spirit Evolved with [[Kanbara Takuya|Aldamon]] to become [[Susanoomon]], who kept a Venom Vamdemon in place for [[Imperialdramon: Fighter Mode]] to end the three with his {{ATO|Giga Death}}. Susanoomon would stay in the form for the rest of the fight against Quartzmon. ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Legendary Skies}}=== ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier Original Story: Things That Want To Be Told}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Series 1-4: Original Story Special ~with~}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD ~A Train Named Hope~}}=== ==Live Readings== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Another Mysterious Message! Perfect.wikitext:
[[File:Digimon adventure perfect level toei.jpg|thumb|right|[[Zudomon]], [[Atlur Kabuterimon (Red)]], [[Lilimon]], [[Garudamon]], [[Were Garurumon]], and [[Metal Greymon]]: six Perfect Digimon.]]
'''Perfect''' (完全体 ''Kanzentai'', abbreviated 完 ''Kan'';<ref>[[Digital Monster Card Game]]</ref>{{note|Originally translated as '''Ultimate''', before the creation of [[Ultimate]]<ref>[https://imgur.com/FVyXuZh]</ref><ref>[https://i.imgur.com/GAcFiOI.png]</ref> and possibly why the English localisation uses it.|n=ultimate}}{{note|Some Japanese material, such as the [[D-Scanner Toy|D-Scanner]] toy line, borrow the English dub Evolution Stage names as glosses for the Japanese level names whenever text is presented on-screen in English. As a result, this Evolution Stage is also sometimes called '''Ultimate''' in Japanese material. On rare occasions, this also extends to Japanese-language text: in ''[[Digimon Story: Sunburst]] & [[Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight]]'', [[Algomon Perfect]] is named アルゴモン アルティメット ("Algomon Ultimate").|n=ultimate2}} Dub: ''Ultimate''), also referred to as '''Level V''', is the fifth natural [[Evolution Stage]] in [[Digimon (species)|Digimon]] [[evolution]].

=Overview=
The Perfect level is the point where further evolution becomes increasingly rare. The majority of Digimon never evolve any further than the [[Adult]] level, so only the most exceptionally powerful among Adult Digimon ever evolve into Perfect Digimon.<ref name="level">''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report011/ Digi-Basics: Digimon "Level"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 011|Translation on Wikimon]])</ref>

Compared to Adult Digimon, Perfect Digimon possess overwhelming power. In the wild, they typically take on the role of pack leaders.<ref name="level" />

Those few, rare Perfect Digimon who endure countless battles and overcome their own limits can evolve again to reach the [[Ultimate]] level.<ref name="level" />

At the time of the inception of the ''[[Digimon]]'' franchise, Perfect was the highest Evolution Stage. It remained so until October 24, 1998, when the [[Digimon Pendulum]] virtual pet line introduced the Ultimate level. Some ''Digimon'' video games released as late as 2000 (which post-date the launch of the Digimon Pendulum)—such as ''[[Digimon World]]'', ''[[Digimon World: Digital Card Battle]]'', and ''[[Digimon World: Digital Card Arena]]''—also do not feature the Ultimate level, so in these games, Ultimate Digimon who appear are assigned the Perfect level.
