Seven Great Demon Lords.wikitext:
{{FArticle|93}} [[File:SGDL digimonprofile.png|thumb|right]] {{nihongo|'''Seven Great Demon Lords'''|七大魔王|Nana Dai Maō}} is a collective term for the strongest Demon-type Digimon. Usually, they don't interfere with each other, neither cooperate or become hostile with each other regardless of their interests or disagreements. {{ref|''{{url|toy.bandai.co.jp/series/digimon/x-evo/seven.php|Digimon Chronicle X: Seven Great Demon Lords}}''}} They were once high-ranking Angel digimon, especially among beings of goodness. However, they each committed a deadly sin and {{w|Fallen angel|fell}}.{{ref|''{{url|web.archive.org/web/20070129014358/http://www.carddas.com/digimon/data/news/daemon.shtml|Digimon Battle Terminal Digimon News: Who are the Seven Great Demon Lords...?}}''}} When [[Lucemon]] instigated a rebellion against [[God]]{{refd|Lucemon: Falldown Mode|rucemonfalldownmode}}, alongside other Angel digimon that fell,{{refd|Demon}}{{refd|Bagramon}} they were deleted to the [[Dark Area]] by the [[Digital World]]'s [[Yggdrasill|system]] (or perhaps the very human that created the Digital World), and being sealed there as punishment,{{refd|Lucemon: Falldown Mode|rucemonfalldownmode}} continuing to exist as a deadly sin, a desire that they each pursue, subsequently becoming Demon Lords.{{ref|''{{url|web.archive.org/web/20080209230820/http://www.carddas.com/digimon/data/news/daemon.shtml|Digimon Battle Terminal Digimon News: Who are the Seven Great Demon Lords...?}}''}} [[Belphemon: Sleep Mode|Belphemon]] was sealed in the deepest parts of the Dark Area, and as its power was too immense, its data was put in a {{w|sleep mode|sleep state}} by that very same system.{{refd|Belphemon: Sleep Mode|belphemonsleepmode}} Currently, the Seven Great Demon Lords are the chief nobility of the Dark Area,{{refd|Beelzebumon}} residing within the heart of that world, the {{w|Pandæmonium (Paradise Lost)|den of demons}},{{refd|Barbamon}} and each member has the right to command legions of the "[[Nightmare Soldiers]]", if they so wish.{{refd|Beelzebumon}} They are also able to subvert the cycle of reincarnation presided over by [[Anubimon]]—instead of having the chance to be reborn, the data of their victims becomes their own flesh and blood.{{refd|Anubimon}}{{refd|Belphemon: Rage Mode|belphemonragemode}} Among the Seven Great Demon Lords, [[Lucemon: Falldown Mode]] is said to be the strongest.{{refd|Lucemon: Falldown Mode|rucemonfalldownmode}} Despite their might, however, certain other demonic digimon are powerful enough to stand outside their authority, such as [[Grand Dracumon]], who is so strong that even the Seven Great Demon Lords cannot interfere with him.{{refd|Grand Dracumon|granddracumon}} Some other Demon Lords such as [[Belial Vamdemon]] are rumored to be part of this group, but are not members.{{ref|''[[Digimon Battle Chronicle]]''}} The group has a natural rivalry with the [[Royal Knights]].{{ref|''[[Booster 24|Digital Monster Card Game - Booster 24 Advent of the Seven Great Demon Lords (THE EVIL GATE)]]''}} =Members= These are the known members of the Seven Great Demon Lords. There are 7 members in total, each representing one of the {{w|Seven Deadly Sins}}. They are all Demon Lord type Digimon (There are also more [[:Category:Demon Lord Type|Demon Lord type]] Digimon, but only this 7 are mentioned to be members of the Seven Great Demon Lords). (Note: The [[X Digimon]] still count as the same Demon Lord). {|border="1" class="wikitable" style="background: #f8f8ff; width: 42em; font-size: smaller; text-align: left; border: 1px solid #88a; border-collapse: collapse;" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="1" margin: auto; |- !colspan="7" style="background: #7B68EE; text-align: center;" | Seven Great Demon Lords |- |colspan="7" style="background: #ccf; text-align: center; color: black;"|七大魔王 ''Nanadaimaou'' |- |style="background: #e6e9ff; text-align: center; color: black;"|'''Name''' |style="background: #e6e9ff; text-align: center; color: black;"|'''Sin''' |style="background: #e6e9ff; text-align: center; color: black;" colspan="2"|'''Alternate Form''' |- !align="center" |[[Lucemon: Falldown Mode]] |align="center" |Pride |align="center" |[[Lucemon]] |align="center" |[[Lucemon (X-Antibody)|X-Antibody]] |- !align="center" |[[Leviamon]] |align="center" |Envy |align="center" colspan="2"|[[Leviamon (X-Antibody)]] |- !align="center" |[[Demon]] |align="center" |Wrath |align="center" colspan="2"|[[Demon (X-Antibody)]] |- !align="center" |[[Belphemon: Rage Mode]] |align="center" |Sloth |align="center" |[[Belphemon: Sleep Mode]] |align="center" |[[Belphemon (X-Antibody)|X-Antibody]] |- !align="center" |[[Barbamon]] |align="center" |Greed |align="center" colspan="2"|[[Barbamon (X-Antibody)]] |- !align="center" |[[Beelzebumon]] |align="center" |Gluttony |align="center" |[[Beelzebumon: Blast Mode]] |align="center" |[[Beelzebumon (X-Antibody)|X-Antibody]] |- !align="center" |[[Lilithmon]] |align="center" |Lust |align="center" colspan="2"|[[Lilithmon (X-Antibody)]] |} [[Image:Reference_divinecomedy.jpg|thumb|right]] The Seven Great Demon Lords are listed here in order of ascending '''Purgatory Level'''. This ordering is a reference to "the levels/terraces of purgatory" mentioned in the epic poem, ''{{w|Divine Comedy|La Divina Commedia}}'' by {{w|Dante Alighieri}}. In the poem, each level represented a different one of the seven deadly sins and presented ways to atone for each sin. As a person progressed, they would eventually make it to the top of the "{{w|Purgatorio|Mountain of Purgatory}}", reaching {{w|Paradiso (Dante)|Paradise}}. The structure of the "Mountain of Purgatory" analogous to the Seven Great Demon Lords' Purgatory Levels is the following: {|align="left" |- |{{SDGLPurgatoryMount}} |}
===Lucemon: Falldown Mode=== {|align="right" |- |{{SGDLMain |n=Lucemon Falldown Mode |nj=ルーチェモン：フォールダウンモード |i=Lucemon_falldown.jpg |t=Paradise of Loss |d=Lucifer |s=Pride |bc=93292a |bc2=cc5b78 |bc3=e98aa2 |bc4=d64243 |bc5=f7b7c8 }} |} {{SGDLText| The strongest of the "Seven Great Demon Lords", it is an ultimate Demon Lord Digimon which possesses both the divine and the diabolic. It instigated a rebellion in the far-flung past, and so it was sealed in the [[Dark Area]] along with many other Demon Lord Digimon. Its power exceeds that of Ultimates, and is said to rival that of the existence called "God" as well. It has a contradictory existence, in that one side of it loves all things, as God does, while the other is trying to destroy the whole world, as the Devil does. Hence, once it destroys this world, it intends to create a fresh new world. Its Special Moves are, after launching the opponent high into the sky with a wild dance of blows, holding their limbs fixed as it drives them back into the ground ({{AT|Paradise Lost}}), and producing a three-dimensional [[wikipedia:Magic square|magic square]], as a glowing orb of holiness and evilness, which it traps the opponent within ({{AT|Dead or Alive}}). When shut within this magic square, it is a 50/50 decision whether the opponent is either completely annihilated or suffers great damage. }}
===Leviamon=== {|align="left" |- |{{SGDLMain |n=Leviamon |i=Leviamon.jpg |t=Abyss of Despair |d=Leviathan |s=Envy |bc=297693 |bc2=5bc9cc |bc3=8ae6e9 |bc4=3fa6cd |bc5=bbf6f8 }} |} {{SGDLText| Called the "Devil Beast" out of terror of the exceedingly mighty power that this monster possesses, it is counted as one of the "Seven Great Demon Lords" and is the strongest monster existing in the [[Digital World]]. This extensive monster, said to possess gigantic jaws which could guzzle even the Digital World, is reported to sleep in the depths of the [[Net Ocean]] nearest to the [[Dark Area]], and if it is awakened, it will despise any Digimon at the top, and will flee even Angel Digimon, much less other Demon Lord Digimon. However, as the existence of the limits on Digimon are decoded, its own appears similar to nothingness, and it is said that it builds the foundation for the existence of wickedness in the Digital World. Its Special Moves are destroying everything with its gigantic jaws ({{AT|Rostrum|Lat: 'Snout'}}), and mowing everything down with its long tail ({{AT|Cauda|Lat: 'Tail'}}). }}
===Demon=== {|align="right" |- |{{SGDLMain |n=Demon |i=Demon_beast2.jpg |t=Hellfire of Chaos |d=Satan |s=Wrath |bc=935929 |bc2=cc8b5b |bc3=e9b08a |bc4=d68642 |bc5=fcc8ba }} |} {{SGDLText| A Demon Lord Digimon that leads the many Demon and Fallen Angel Digimon. Like [[Devimon]], Demon was originally an Angel Digimon, and a particularly high-ranked one. However, in response to a being of goodness in the [[Digital World]] (possibly the creator of the Digital World), it raged in fury and rebellion, and so was deleted to the [[Dark Area]] (the graveyard of erased data). It has vowed to one day conquer the Digital World, in revenge against the being of goodness. Also, while it led the rebellion, and was the strongest among the Ultimates, it plotted to secretly revive the "Super-Ultimate Digimon". Its Special Move is extremely high-temperature hellfire ({{AT|Flame Inferno}}). Those who incur this technique burn up without a trace. }}
===Belphemon Rage Mode=== {|align="left" |- |{{SGDLMain |n=Belphemon Rage Mode |i=Belphemon rage.jpg |t=Roar of Awakening |d=Belphegor |s=Sloth |bc=2c2993 |bc2=5b74cc |bc3=8a9fe9 |bc4=4642d6 |bc5=bccafa }} |}{{SGDLText|It is said that [[Belphemon Sleep Mode|Belphemon]] awakens from its eternal slumber once in a thousand years, and regains its natural shape. When Belphemon awakens from its slumber it changes into an incarnation of rage, and anything that enters its field of vision becomes a target for destruction. It is said that just by suffering Belphemon Rage Mode's roar, Perfect Digimon and below will have their data disintegrated and instantly die, and not even Ultimate Digimon can escape unharmed. Its Special Moves are black flames cast from the chains coiled around its body ({{AT|Lampranthus}}), and a slash attack which is unleashed from its claws, clad in the flames of hell ({{AT|Gift of Darkness}}). Furthermore, the data of Digimon consigned to oblivion by the Digimon crowned as the [[Seven Great Demon Lords]] is not reincarnated, but is sent to the center of the [[Dark Area]], and becomes the flesh and blood of the Demon Lords.}}
===Barbamon=== {|align="right" |- |{{SGDLMain |n=Barbamon |i=Barbamon2.jpg |t=Schemer of Gold |d=Mammon |s=Greed |bc=642993 |bc2=7e5bcc |bc3=a78ae9 |bc4=9542d6 |bc5=cebbfa }} |} {{SGDLText|One of the "[[Seven Great Demon Lords]]", it is a Demon Lord Digimon which has the appearance of a long-bearded old man. Residing within the central part of the [[Dark Area]], the [[wikipedia:Pandæmonium (Paradise Lost)|den of demons]], it manipulates Fallen Angel Digimon to carry out evil to its limit. It is obsessed with all of the treasure which exists within the Network, so out of avarice, and regardless of the means, it has a cruel personality with a greed that would kill Digimon just for a piece of treasure. It is the foremost and most cunning schemer of the Seven Great Demon Lords, and can easily manipulate the Ultimate [[Deathmon]] in addition to Fallen Angel Digimon. Its Special Moves are using its demonic staff "{{EQ|Death Lure}}" to tempt Digimon into an inescapable living hell, converting them to wickedness, and unleashing all of the Dark Area's evil energies at once as an extremely high-temperature explosion that burns up everything without a trace ({{AT|Pandemonium Lost}}). Furthermore, even [[Belial Vamdemon]]'s Special Move "{{ATO|Pandemonium Flame}}" only releases a small fraction of these energies. }}
===Beelzebumon=== {|align="left" |- |{{SGDLMain |n=Beelzebumon |i=Beelzebumon2.jpg |t=Duelist of Finality |d=Beelzebub |s=Gluttony |bc=7d9329 |bc2=ccc75b |bc3=e9e58a |bc4=b9d642 |bc5=f8f4bb }} |} {{SGDLText|While it possesses the power to command the many Demon Digimon, it is a Demon Lord Digimon that dares to observe a solitary existence. As one of the "[[Seven Great Demon Lords]]", it is said that it could stand at the peak of the dark army, "Nightmare Soldiers", if it cared to, although it is also said that there exists a Demon Lord Digimon which surpasses even Beelzebumon. It possesses its favorite shotgun "{{EQ|Berenjena|Eggplant}}" , and rides on the gigantic motorcycle-type machine, "{{EQ|Behemoth}}". Although its personality is cruel and merciless, it is also exceedingly prideful, so it would never attack the weak. Its Signature Move is raising a claw overhead and then tearing the opponent to pieces ({{AT|Darkness Claw}}). Its Special Move is rapidly firing its two shotguns ({{AT|Double Impact}}). }}
===Lilithmon=== {|align="right" |- |{{SGDLMain |n=Lilithmon |i=Lilithmon.jpg |t=Goddess of Darkness |d=Asmodeus |s=Lust |bc=319329 |bc2=80cc5b |bc3=a9e98a |bc4=4dd642 |bc5=c8f5b2 }} |} {{SGDLText| One of the "[[Seven Great Demon Lords]]", it is a Demon Lord Digimon which has the appearance of a woman. It is thought that it was originally the same kind as [[Ofanimon]], but it fell from heaven and eventually became known as the "Goddess of Darkness". It confounds its opponents with its bewitchingly lovely appearance, and it is said that those who are taken in by its temptations are invariably granted death. Its reputation as the "Goddess of Darkness" is appropriate, as it is tolerant in regards to wickedness, but offers only cold-hearted inhumanity in regards to goodness. The demonic "{{AT|Nazar Nail}}" on its right arm corrodes everything it touches. Its Special Move is rotting the opponent's body with a sigh of darkness ({{AT|Phantom Pain}}). It is said that when you receive this curse your data dissipates from the tips of your body, and dying you suffer from that pain. }}
==Crowns== [[Image:Reference_sevenseals.jpg|150px|right|thumb|The Seven Seals of the Seven Olympian Spirits, also depicted alongside the seals of the Seven Archangels]] Each of the Seven Great Demon Lords has their own crown (冠, ''Kanmuri'') displayed in one of the seven colors of the visible spectrum of light. The center of each crown depicts a {{w|Sigil (magic)|sigil}} of one of the {{w|Olympian spirits|Seven Olympian Spirits}}, a group of seven mythological entities who, under God, ruled over the 196 provinces of Heaven and presided over the natural world for 490 years each''[http://books.google.com/books?id=xAmMNnJlfnoC&pg=PP7&dq=The+new+encyclopedia+of+the+occult+%09+The+new+encyclopedia+of+the+occult Greer, J. M. The New Encyclopedia of the Occult ISBN: 9781567183368]''. They are mentioned in works such as ''{{w|Arbatel de magia veterum}}'' and are each associated with a {{w|Astronomical symbols|celestial body}}, which is also displayed on the crown directly above the Olympian Spirit seal, and corresponds to a particular sin. Within the space between the two concentric circles is text stating a designated '''Level'''; this is "666" for all the crown where "666" is the "{{w|Number of the Beast}}", a concept mentioned in the ''Book of Revelation'', '''System'''; the demon that traditionally represents the respective sin, and '''Code''' which identifies a sin associated with the aforementioned demon. At the bottom of the inner circle is text which specifies a caution for a '''Purgatory Level''' ranging between 1 and 7. The structure of the text is in the following format: :LEVEL:___ SYSTEM:___ CODE:___ (repeated twice) :CAUTION! PURGATORY LEVEL ___ {{SGDL_Crowns}} In [[Digimon Savers: Another Mission]], the crowns are displayed on card keys in the following format: {{SGDL_Codekey}} ===Connections=== [[File:Lucemon_satan2.jpg|left|thumb]] ===={{hdr2|Lucemon: Satan Mode}}==== Having the appearance of the dragon of [[wikipedia:Book of Revelation|Revelation]], it is [[Lucemon]]'s final form. As was told in the Revelation, it carries the [[Seven Great Demon Lords#Crests|Crowns of the Seven Deadly Sins]] upon its head, and it holds the globe of darkness, "[[wikipedia:Gehenna|{{EQ|Gehenna}}]]", which absorbs all attacks. Before the {{EQ|Gehenna}} all attacks are nullified, and it is said that the world will perish when Lucemon takes this form. However, Lucemon's true form ([[Lucemon Larva]]) exists within the {{EQ|Gehenna}}, and Satan Mode is no more than Lucemon's reflection. Therefore, no matter how many times Satan Mode is attacked, those attacks will not reach the true form, Lucemon Larva. Its Special Moves are a flame of destruction which purifies everything ([[wikipedia:Purgatory|{{AT|Purgatorial}}]] {{AT|Flame}}), and a light of annihilation fired from the seven Crowns ({{AT|Divine Atonement}}). [[File:Gate_of_deadly_sins.jpg|right|thumb|The Gate of Deadly Sins]] ===={{hdr2|Ogudomon}}==== [[File:Ogudomon.jpg|left|thumb]] Ogudomon, also known as the '''Super Demon Lord of Atonement''' (贖罪の超魔王, ''Shokuzai no Chō Maō''){{ref|''[[Digimon Collectors]]''}}, is a grotesque Super Demon Lord who has seven legs and eyes, as well as an eighth eye. As it includes the whole of the Digital World's sins, and is a mysterious personification with the ability to atone for them all, the power of a Digimon with even a small amount of malice is counterbalanced, and it is impossible for it to defeat Ogudomon. Its Special Moves are running wild while lustful ({{AT|Oratio Grandiloqua}} {{j2|{Lat: ''Rhapsody''}|羅：狂詩曲 / Ra: Kyoushikyoku}}), and a shock-wave which is played from its oral-area ({{AT|Catedral}} {{j2|{Esp: ''Cathedral''}|西：大聖堂 / Sei: Taiseidou}}). Also, it pulverizes its opponent with its enormous legs ({{AT|Gradus}} {{j2|{Lat: ''Step''}|羅：ステップ / Ra: Suteppu}}). Like Lucemon: Satan Mode, it carries the Crowns of the Seven Deadly Sins upon its head.
===Gate of Deadly Sins=== Although very little is known about it, the seven crowns are also engraved on the Gate of Deadly Sins {{ETYK|大罪の門|Daizai no Mon}}. ===Tower of the Seven Deadly Sins=== The '''Towers of the Seven Deadly Sins''' {{ETYK|七大罪の塔|Nanadaizai no Tou}} are mysterious buildings. In ''[[Digimon Collectors]]'' they begin appearing across the [[Digital World]] and they attract Digimon exhibiting their respective sin, as well as the Protagonist, who is sent to investigate them by the [[Royal Knights]]. Although the protagonist defeats the Great Demon Lords lurking within each tower, this only serves to bring about the manifestation of [[Ogudomon (Collectors)|Ogudomon]]'s tower, as well as the Super Demon Lord itself. In ''[[Digimon World -next 0rder- International Edition]]'' the Seven Great Demon Lords each create one of the Towers whilst living in the [[Dark Area]], however they are absorbed by the Grudgeful Hand, after which it destroys the Dark Area. {{IG |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Tower of the Seven Deadly Sins (Lilithmon) dco.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=Lilithmon's Tower |i2=Tower of the Seven Deadly Sins (Barbamon) dco.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c2=Barbamon's Tower |i3=Tower of the Seven Deadly Sins (Belphemon) dco.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c3=Belphemon's Tower |i4=Tower of the Seven Deadly Sins (Lucemon) dco.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c4=Lucemon's Tower |i7=Tower of the Seven Deadly Sins (Leviamon) dco.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c7=Leviamon's Tower |i8=Tower of the Seven Deadly Sins (Demon) dco.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c8=Demon's Tower |i9=Tower of the Seven Deadly Sins (Beelzebumon) dco.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c9=Beelzebumon's Tower |i10=Tower of the Seven Deadly Sins (Ogudomon) dco.jpg{{!}}200x200px |c10=Ogudomon's Tower }} ===Ogudomon's Swords=== Ogudomon has seven swords stuck in its body, said to contain the abilities of the Seven Great Demon Lords. Each sword is inspired by the physical appearance of each of the Demon Lords, and features the crown of their respective sin floating above it, while embedded in Ogudomon's legs. In [[Digimon Chronicle X]], the Seven [[Diablomon (X-Antibody)|Diablomon X]] serving [[Lucemon (X-Antibody)|Lucemon X]] were able to summon Ogudomon's swords after absorbing the Seven Great Demon Lords, in their [[X-Antibody]] forms, and using them in their battle against the [[Royal Knights]]. {{IG |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Ogudomon's Swords (Lilithmon).jpg{{!}}200x200px |c1=Lust Sword |i2=Ogudomon's Swords (Beelzebumon).jpg{{!}}200x200px |c2=Gluttony Sword |i3=Ogudomon's Swords (Barbamon).jpg{{!}}200x200px |c3=Greed Sword |i4=Ogudomon's Swords (Belphemon).jpg{{!}}200x200px |c4=Sloth Sword |i7=Ogudomon's Swords (Demon).jpg{{!}}200x200px |c7=Wrath Sword |i8=Ogudomon's Swords (Leviamon).jpg{{!}}200x200px |c8=Envy Sword |i9=Ogudomon's Swords (Lucemon).jpg{{!}}200x200px |c9=Pride Sword }}
=Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== The [[Lucemon: Falldown Mode]] card, [[Bo-137t]], which is based on the anime character, states that the [[Lucemon (Frontier)|Lucemon]] of Frontier was a member of the Seven Great Demon Lords. ==={{hdr|Digimon Savers}}=== Belphemon, along with the other great demon lords, almost brought ruin to the Digital World long time ago. However, he was sealed deep inside an ancient ruin in the Digital World. [[Kurata Akihiro|Professor Akihiro Kurata]] found his Digitama and brought it to the real world at some point of time. He nurtured the Digitama that eventually hatched into Belphemon Sleep Mode. Kurata's plan was to use Belphemon as his ultimate weapon for both Digital World and [[Real World|Human World]] domination. {| |[[Image:Sgdl_savers.jpg|thumb|right]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars}}=== [[Beelzebumon (Xros Wars)|Beelzebumon]] is mistakenly stated in its official ''Toei Animation'' profile to be a member of the Seven Great Demon Lords.{{ref|''{{url2|https://web.archive.org/web/20140630180901/http://www.toei-anim.co.jp/tv/digimon_xw2010/character1_30/x/ch14-1.html|Beelzebumon's Profile (Toei Animation)}}''}} ==={{hdr|Digimon Ghost Game}}=== [[Lilithmon]] is a member of the Seven Great Demon Lords.[https://www.toei-anim.co.jp/tv/digimon_ghost_game/character/digimon.php?char=d076 ''Toei Animation'', ''[[Digimon Ghost Game]]'' character list: Lilithmon] ==Manga== [[File:Lilithmon XWM2.jpg|thumb|right|Lilithmon in ''Digimon Xros Wars''.]] ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|Digimon Xros Wars}}=== The group is mentioned by [[Zeromaru|Ulforce V-dramon]] as a powerful Demon Lord-class group of Digimon. In his story, Ulforce V-dramon explains that the [[Royal Knights]] confused [[Homeostasis]]' prophecy of the "Red and Blue Dragon" that will destroy the world with [[Leviamon]] and [[Belphemon]]. Ulforce V-dramon also mentions that the rest of the Demon Lords, except for [[Lilithmon (Xros Wars)|Lilithmon]], didn't wanted to get involved in the war and maintained a neutral position.{{ref|''[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 13|Digimon Xros Wars: "Xros Memories!! The Boys' Old Days"]]''}} ==={{hdr|Digimon Chronicle X}}=== The Seven Great Demon Lords all decided they wanted the X-Antibody for themselves, so take advantage of [[Grand Dracumon]]'s plan to defeat Digimon and send them to the [[Dark Area]]. {| |[[File:Seven Great Demon Lords Chronicle X.jpg|thumb|The Seven Great Demon Lords X-Antibody from ''Digimon Chronicle X''.]] |} ==Novel== ==={{hdr|Digimon Chronicle X (Web Novel)|Digimon Chronicle X}}=== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Savers: Another Mission}}=== As they are the incarnation of the sins, the Seven Great Demon Lords will exist while the sins still exist. {| |- |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[File:Lilithmon another mission.png|255px]] |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[File:Beelzebumon another mission.png|255px]] |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[File:Barbamon another mission.png|255px]] |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[File:Belphemon another mission.png|255px]] |- |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[File:Demon another mission.png|255px]] |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[File:Leviamon another mission.png|255px]] |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[File:Lucemon another mission.png|255px]] |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[File:Lucemon Satan Mode another mission.jpg|255px]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize}}=== The Seven Great Demon Lords appear as a collectable card set. ==={{hdr|Digimon Crusader}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== The Seven Great Demon Lords appear in the '''Scheme of the Demon Lords chapter''' (策謀の魔王篇 ''Sakubō no Maō-hen''). They're meant to be one of the forces that keeps balance in the world, but [[Barbamon]] and [[Demon]] wish to take over both worlds instead, starting the conflict in the chapter. [[Belphemon]], [[Leviamon]] and [[Beelzebumon]] reject Barbamon's plans and decide to help [[Taiga]] and [[Shinomiya Rina]] stop him instead, [[Lilithmon]] wants to stop him but on her own and attacks them as well, while [[Lucemon]] is a victim of Barbamon's schemes and torture, turning him into the rage-driven [[Lucemon: Falldown Mode]], then the world-destroying [[Lucemon: Satan Mode]]. The Seven Great Demon Lords are extremely powerful and can only be stopped with the power of other Demon Lords, thus Taiga and Shinomiya Rina seek to obtain their powers, though this is a trap, as Barbamon wanted them to obtain said powers to force-feed them to Lucemon so he can become Satan Mode. Ultimately, Barbamon is killed by Demon to take over his plans, then Demon dies at the hands of Lucemon who he thought was under his control, and Lucemon is taken down and saved by Taiga and [[Digimaru]], stopping their ambitions. The Seven Great Demon Lords also appear as a collectable card set. ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== The "Towers of the Seven Deadly Sins" that appear alongside the Digital World after [[Arkadimon]] is defeated start to collect the essence of all sins, as a prelude to the advent of [[Ogudomon]]. {| |- |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[Image:SGDL collectors.jpg|510px|Seven Great Demon Lords and Ogudomon from ''[[Gallery:Digimon Collectors/Cutscenes#Cutscene.67|Digimon Collectors]]'']] |} Yggdrasil sent Ogudomon as a way to find superior beings that could defeat it, otherwise the whole world would collapse. The [[Royal Knights]] are unable to do anything against Ogudomon, due to in their words "the meaning of Justice can mean some evil depending on the interpretation", casting doubts on [[Yggdrasill]]'s own judgement itself. {| |- |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[Image:Ogudomon collectors screenshot.jpg|255px|Ogudomon from ''[[Gallery:Digimon Collectors/Cutscenes#Cutscene.67|Digimon Collectors]]'']] |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[Image:Ogudomon collectors screenshot2.jpg|255px|Ogudomon from ''[[Gallery:Digimon Collectors/Cutscenes#Cutscene.67|Digimon Collectors]]'']] |class="toccolours" style="background-color:black;"|[[Image:Ogudomon collectors screenshot3.jpg|255px|Ogudomon from ''[[Gallery:Digimon Collectors/Cutscenes#Cutscene.67|Digimon Collectors]]'']] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Fortune}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth}}=== According to [[Mikagura Mirei]], the Seven Great Demon Lords are multiversal constants, they exist throughout the entire multiverse, and all we see in the franchise are just fractions of the original Demon Lord. They exist in all the parallel universes because of the power they possess. The will of the universe couldn't allow such extraordinary powerful beings to exist, so they were dispersed across the multiverse in order to weaken their powers. It's a kind of seal, and by existing in each world, their power is dispersed. This phenomenon is described as a "providence of the universe", a system that isn't maintained by anyone and simply naturally works. The official English localization of the game explains this as each Demon Lord dividing their existence as a method of self-protection. These fractions are connected with each other and when an avatar is defeated, their powers are sent to all other avatars throughout the multiverse. No matter how many times they are defeated, they will always return as long as there are sins. One of the problems of defeating one of its members is that the one who tries to face one of the Seven Great Demon Lords is committing a "sin" and soon after the fight against the Seven Great Demon Lord the one that faced them will be punished by the Digital World's system to suffer "karma" under certain circumstances. It is also said that they are above all the magical Digimon, being the highest of the high. {| |[[File:SGDL Cyber Sleuth DLC Sayo Mirei Aiba.jpg|thumb]] |[[File:SGDL Cyber Sleuth DLC Sayo Mirei Aiba2.jpg|thumb]] |[[File:SGDL Cyber Sleuth DLC Sayo Mirei Aiba3.jpg|thumb]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Linkz}}=== In "Dragon's Land Chapter 1: The Key of the Dragon Stream" in [[Digimon Linkz]], [[Volcanicdramon]] transforms the Seven Great Demon Lords into [[Mutant Digimon|mutants]] that hold the Key Code, the clues to restores the Digital World. Along with [[Kurihara Hina|Hina]], the Player challenge the seven demons to gather the Key Codes. After collecting the seven Key Codes from each Demon Lord, Hina talks to the player. She says that they have gathered all the key codes but an error occurs and the program cannot be started unless using the IP of the administrator. Surprisingly, that IP matched to Volcanicdramon! ==={{hdr|Digimon World -next 0rder-}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World -next 0rder- International Edition}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon ReArise}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon New Century}}=== ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|Digimon Twin}}=== In 2007, the Seven Great Demon Lords secretly released the crack program [[Tempest]] into the Digital World, that affected the [[Four Holy Beasts]], among other digimon, causing world wide rampage as a consequence that eventually led to the [[L&R War]].{{ref|''{{url|web.archive.org/web/20130213124236/http://digimon.net/digimon_archive2010/cat-digimon-world/index.html|Digimon Twin - Digital World Data: 01-05 (Digimon Web)}}''}} ==={{hdr|Digital Monster X Ver.2}}=== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster X Ver.3}}=== ==TCG== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster Card Game}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Battle Terminal}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Battle Terminal 02}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Card Game}}=== ==Trivia== * Each one of the Seven Great Demon Lords represent one of the {{w|Seven Deadly Sins}} and are based on seven demons from Judeo-Christian mythology, a reference to {{w|Peter Binsfeld}}'s {{w|Classification_of_demons#Binsfeld.27s_classification_of_demons|classification of demons}}, as well as incorporating elements from and references to the epic poem ''{{w|Paradise Lost}}'' by {{w|John Milton}}, and ''{{w|The Lesser Key of Solomon}}'' (''Clavicula Salomonis Regis''). * In the ''[[Digimon Card Game]]'', the '''Gate of Deadly Sins'''「大罪の門 ''Daizai no Mon''」can be seen in the background of the card [[EX2-044 (DCG)|EX2-044]]. * During the "''Hypnos Meeting 2019''", following a fan's request for more details on the origin of the "[[Three Head Officers]]" in the [[Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|manga]], [[Nakashima Yuuki]] revealed that [[Bagramon (Xros Wars)|Bagramon]] went to [[Lucemon]] in search of someone to work alongside [[Tactimon (Xros Wars)|Tactimon]], although Lucemon didn't want to work with someone and only see what would happen as a result of what Bagramon was trying to do, eventually turning down the offer. But [[Lilithmon (Xros Wars)|Lilithmon]], who was watching what was happening and took an interest in Bagramon, offered her help, although she ended up falling in love with Bagramon. http://digitalmado.com/Hypnos/meeting2019/ancer_n.html ==References== {{R | }} [[Category:Seven Great Demon Lords| ]]

Shibayama Junpei.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Shibayama Junpei |image=Junpei.gif |imagesize= |kan=柴山　純平 |dub=J.P. Shibayama |age=12 |school= |dv=Blue/Yellow [[D-Scanner]] |spirits=Thunder |vjp=[[Shitanda Michael]] (四反田 マイケル) |ven=[[Steven Blum]] |family= }} '''Shibayama Junpei''' is one of the protagonists of [[Digimon Frontier]]. =Evolution= {{Box Hybrid |Human = '''[[Blitzmon]]''' |Human2 = [[Grottemon]]{{ref|''[[D-Scanner Toy|D-Tector Version 3.0]]''}} |Beast = [[Bolgmon]] |Beast2 = [[Gigasmon]]{{ref|''[[D-Scanner Toy|D-Tector Version 3.0]]''}} |Fusion = [[Rhino Kabuterimon]]{{ref|''[[D-Scanner Toy|D-Tector Version 3.0]]''}} |Ultimate = [[Ancient Beatmon]]{{ref|''[[Battle Spirit: Digimon Frontier]]''}} |Ultimate2 = [[Susanoomon]] }} ==Card Game Evolution== * [[Digimon Card Game Colors and Levels#Evolves From Tamers|Any Digimon that also Evolves from non-White Tamers from the ''Digimon Card Game'']]{{Note|[[BT18-078 (DCG)]] Duskmon can change any Tamer's Color into any other Color, except White.|n=DuskTamerColor}} * [[Arbormon]]{{rfc|BT6|049 (DCG)}} * [[Blitzmon]]{{rfc|BT7|046 (DCG)}} * [[Bolgmon]]{{rfc|BT7|047 (DCG)}} * [[Fairimon]]{{rfc|BT7|035 (DCG)}} * [[Garummon]]{{rfc|BT17|023 (DCG)}} * [[Gigasmon]]{{rfc|BT7|061 (DCG)}} * [[Grottemon]]{{rfc|BT7|060 (DCG)}} * [[Jet Silphymon]]{{rfc|BT18|053 (DCG)}} * [[Kaiser Greymon]]{{rfc|BT18|095 (DCG)}} * [[Kaiser Leomon]]{{rfc|BT18|077 (DCG)}} * [[Löwemon]]{{rfc|BT18|076 (DCG)}} * [[Magna Garurumon]]{{rfc|BT18|097 (DCG)}} * [[Mercuremon]]{{rfc|BT12|066 (DCG)}} * [[Petaldramon]]{{rfc|BT6|050 (DCG)}} * [[Raihimon]]{{rfc|BT18|081 (DCG)}} * [[Rhino Kabuterimon]]{{rfc|BT18|070 (DCG)}} * [[Sephirothmon]]{{rfc|BT12|065 (DCG)}} * [[Shutumon]]{{rfc|BT7|036 (DCG)}} * [[Susanoomon]]{{rfc|BT7|112 (DCG)}} * [[Wolfmon]]{{rfc|BT17|022 (DCG)}} =Profile= Shibayama Junpei is a [[Chosen Child]], chosen by [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]] to inherit the Legendary Spirits of Thunder. The oldest of the group, Junpei is also tall for his age and of a stocky build. He is loud, abrasive and a bit of a show-off, but in truth, he is a kindhearted person and is always the first to cheer a friend up. He is incredibly intelligent and knowledgeable (though he is not a very good student), and is a talented magician. He seems to carry a large supply of chocolate bars in his jumpsuit. In the past, Junpei never felt like he had any true friends. He was well-liked by his classmates, who were impressed by his elaborate magic tricks, though he was never able to get close to any of them. Though he was surrounded by people, he always felt lonely. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== Junpei received [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon's]] message while he was parking his bicycle at home. Intrigued by the message, he traveled to Shibuya Station and boarded a [[Trailmon Worm|Trailmon]] with [[Kanbara Takuya]], [[Orimoto Izumi]], and [[Himi Tomoki]]. He was immediately infatuated with Izumi, though he didn't think much of the others. Just before they entered the Digital World, their cellphones turned into [[D-Scanner|D-Scanners]]. After Takuya found his [[Human Spirit of Fire]] at the Fire Terminal, Junpei decided that he would rather go back home, and tried to drag a somewhat reluctant Tomoki along. Junpei bribed a [[Pagumon]] with a chocolate bar, so he would tell them where can they find another Trailmon that would take them to the Real World, though the Trailmon ended up speeding off without Junpei and Tomoki. Soon, they were surrounded by many Pagumon who demanded to receive more chocolate. They were chased to the underground and were cornered, but [[Minamoto Kouji]] arrived and saved them, particularly when the Pagumon merged into [[Raremon]] and Kouji had to evolve using his newly-found [[Human Spirit of Light]]. Kouji left after the event, but Junpei and Tomoki decided to stick with Takuya and Izumi anyway. By [[Digimon Frontier - Episode 05]], Junpei was the only Chosen Child who had yet to find his Human Spirit, and was feeling very weak and insecure. The children reached Wind Factory, where they encountered a village of [[Kokuwamon]] that were being enslaved by [[Snimon]] and his [[Goburimon]] soldiers. The children wanted to free them, but Junpei felt helpless because of his lack of Human Spirit. However, he faced Snimon in spite of this, and luckily found the [[Human Spirit of Thunder]] in the factory. With it, he evolved into [[Blitzmon]] and defeated Snimon. After escaping from Seraphimon's castle following the Evil Warriors' attack, the Chosen Children reached a dead end in an underground cavern. As they tried to find a way out, they found a [[Whamon]] in what looked like a small pond, who attacked them in hysterics and hit Junpei, weakening him. [[Grottomon (Frontier)|Grottomon]] and [[Arbormon (Frontier)|Arbormon]] found the children and attacked them. Junpei tried to fight them off as Blitzmon, but his weakness got over him and devolved. Before Grottomon could attack again, Whamon smashed him into a wall with a jet of water fired from his spout, and as he did, he spat out the [[Beast Spirit of Thunder]], which he had eaten. Junpei used the spirit to evolve into [[Bolgmon]] and scanned Grottomon's data, getting the [[Human Spirit of Earth]] and giving Izumi her stolen [[Human Spirit of Wind]] back. When [[Mercuremon (Frontier)|Sephirothmon]] sucked the Chosen Children into his body, Junpei was the first to find himself inside the Earth Sphere. There, he battled a [[Volcamon]], who played on the boy's self-doubts by saying that no one will help him, and that he has no real friends. Junpei managed to defeat Volcamon, but then he found himself battling a shadow version of Blitzmon in an arena, while many copies of the other Chosen Children jeered at him from the stands and yelled that they hate him. Though plagued by memories of his lonely home life, he managed to evolve into Bolgmon and defeat the shadow with his strongest attack. It was then when the other children managed to get into the sphere and showed great concern towards him. From then on, he knew that he had finally made some real friends: the other Chosen Children. ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Revival of the Ancient Digimon!!}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== After [[Magnamon]] ran out of power and devolved to [[V-mon (Adventure)|V-mon]] in the fight against [[Quartzmon (Xros Wars)|Quartzmon's]] [[Belial Vamdemon]] and [[Venom Vamdemon]] army in "''[[Digimon Xros Wars - Episode 78|The Gathering of the Legendary Heroes! The Digimon All-star Showdown]]''", [[Ichijouji Ken]] appeared alongside [[Wormmon (Adventure)|Stingmon]], followed by [[Ishida Yamato]], [[Gabumon (Adventure)|Metal Garurumon]], and balls of light containing [[Himi Tomoki|Chackmon]], [[Minamoto Kouji|Wolfmon]], [[Orimoto Izumi|Fairimon]] and Blitzmon. The Spirits then Ancient Spirit Evolved with [[Kanbara Takuya|Aldamon]] to become [[Susanoomon]], who kept a Venom Vamdemon in place for [[Imperialdramon: Fighter Mode]] to end the three with his {{ATO|Giga Death}}. Susanoomon would stay in the form for the rest of the fight against Quartzmon. ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== ==Drama CDs== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier Original Story: Things That Want To Be Told}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Series 1-4: Original Story Special ~with~}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD|Digimon Frontier 15th Blu-ray BOX Special Drama CD ~A Train Named Hope~}}=== ==Live Readings== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Another Mysterious Message! Shibuya Aoi.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Shibuya Aoi |image=Shibuya aoi2.png |imagesize=x320 |kan=渋谷アオイ |rom=Shibuya Aoi |dub= |age=15 |height=158 cm |weight=44.8 kg |blood=A |pd=[[Labramon (Survive)|Labramon]] |vjp=[[Hondo Kaede]] (本渡楓) |family= }} =Profile= A third-year from another middle school participating in the extracurricular activities camp. She is a hard worker who does her best to deal with the unreasonable demands and complaints of her peers. She is usually quiet, rarely speaking. In the unknown world, she undertakes detailed work such as food management and base management, and she does it well.
渋谷アオイ {{br}}'''年齢：'''15 {{br}}'''身長：'''158cm {{br}}'''体重：'''44.8kg {{br}}'''血液型：'''A '''課外キャンプ参加の理由：'''{{br}}元々アオイは、この課外キャンプの参加は{{br}}乗り気ではなかった…… そんなとき、とある作家のインタビューが{{br}}彼女の目に留まる。 作家になるきっかけは学生時代のキャンプでの{{br}}貴重な経験だ― そう語られた記事に触発された彼女は、{{br}}自分を変えるきっかけになるかもと思い直す。 こうして彼女は改めてキャンプ参加への想いを{{br}}募らせるのだった。
異世界で再開したアオイさんは、{{br}}不思議な生き物を見てもあんまり動じなかった。 ミノルを叱ったり、ラブラモンの心配をしたり{{br}}いつもどおりな感じだ。 まじめで優しい先輩だとは思ってたけど、{{br}}実はけっこう肝が据わってたりして……
『いい子ぶって出しゃばって』 そんなファングモンの言葉が、アオイさんの{{br}}心をくじいてしまった。 でも、みんなを助けたいっていう{{br}}アオイさんの気持ちは本物だったよ。 ラブラモンが進化した『ドーベルモン』の{{br}}りりしさが、それを証明したんだ。
みんなを守れる自分になりたいと、{{br}}初めて『倒すための力』を欲したアオイさん。 その想いにこたえて、ラブラモンが{{br}}『ケルベロモン』に進化した。 その力は想像以上に強く、鋭くて…… アオイさんはこんなに強い力を{{br}}自分に使いこなせるのかって、不安そうだ。 でも、アオイさんならきっと{{br}}『正しい使い方』を見つけられるよね。
アオイさんが、自分の求める力を{{br}}はっきりと思い描いたとき、{{br}}ラブラモンは『アヌビモン』に進化した。 力に溺れず、公正に立ち向かえる強い心。{{br}}アオイさんにしか持ちえない力だ。 その力で、アオイさんはこれからも{{br}}みんなを守り続けるだろう。
この課外キャンプで最初に会ったときと比べて、{{br}}アオイさんは見違えるように強くなった。 そんな彼女のおかげで僕らは{{br}}最後の決戦の場へと向かう。 すべての危機を彼女と共に{{br}}乗り越えられると信じて。
'''ラブラモン：''' アオイさんに寄り添うケモノガミのひとり。 {{br}}見た目は犬によく似ている。 温厚で引っ込み思案で自己主張が苦手な{{br}}アオイさんを補うかのような活発な性格を{{br}}している。 ラブラモンはアオイさんが大好きで、{{br}}いつも彼女のそばから離れようとしない。{{br}}同じくアオイさんもラブラモンのことを{{br}}慕っていて、その様子はまるで{{br}}姉妹か親友のように見えるのだ。

{{S2 |featured={{FArticle2|371}} |drbentry={{DRBEntry|417|418|2a=Blastmon}} |name=Skull Knightmon: Big Axe Mode |kan=スカルナイトモン：ビッグアックスモード |dub=SkullKnightmon Mighty Axe Mode |rom=Sukarunaitomon: Biggu Akkusu Mōdo |da={{DigiAlph|Su|ka|ru|na|i|to|mon}} |da2={{DigiAlph2|s|k|u|l|l|k|n|i|g|h|t|mon}} |image=Skullknightmon_bigaxe.jpg |imagesize= |l1=No Level |l1ref={{ref|''[[Digimon Life]]''}} |l2=Adult |l2ref={{DD|sa|skullknightmonbigaxemode}} |a1=Virus |a1ref={{DD|sa|skullknightmonbigaxemode}} |a2=None |a2ref=''[https://web.archive.org/web/20200928042740/https://digimon.net/reference/detail.php?directory_name=skullknightmonbigaxemode Original Digimon Reference Book profile]'' |t1=Enhancement |t1ref={{DD|sa|skullknightmonbigaxemode}} |w= |wref= |pn=DRB |pe=[[Deadly Axemon]]'s "{{FM|Pulverization-armament Mode|Funseisenshū Keitai}}", which exploits its sturdy body to be transformed into a weapon.{{br}}That battleaxe, larger than an ordinary Digimon's body, exhibits more than enough weight and stiffness to pulverize everything. 必殺技は{{EQK|ビッグアックス}}を前方に構えた状態で突進する「{{ATK|アックスライダー}}」と、目にも留まらぬ速さで{{EQK|ビッグアックス}}を振り回す「{{ATK|ストームアックス}}」。 |pu=skullknightmonbigaxemode |pn2=DL |pe2={{SameProfile|DRB}} |pu2=zRSjIhNQDWk%3D |pn3=Misc |pun3=Digimon Web Official Twitter |pe3= |pj3=デッドリーアックスモンが大きい戦斧に変形し、その重量と堅さを発揮して、あらゆる物を粉砕する。生きた武器として自律して攻撃することもできる。 |pu3=https://twitter.com/digimonweb_net/status/1265470284187893760 |ety= |yd=2010 |ydr={{ref|''{{url2|http://web.archive.org/web/20160807160119/http://www.carddass.com/digimon/image/news/10/img_news10_01.jpg|Digimon Xros Wars Big Digimon Collection Poster}}''}} |drbed=February 18 |drbedy=2011 |ad={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars - {{S2 |featured={{FArticle2|356}} |drbentry={{DRBEntry|415|416|2a=Gaossmon}} |name=Skull Knightmon: Cavalier Mode |kan=スカルナイトモン：騎馬モード |rom=Sukarunaitomon: Kiba Mōdo |da={{DigiAlph|su|ka|ru|na|i|to|mon}} |da2={{DigiAlph2|s|k|u|l|l|k|n|i|g|h|t|mon}} |image=Skullknightmon_cavalier.jpg |imagesize=320 |l1=No Level |l1ref={{ref|''[[Digimon Life]]''}} |l2=Adult |l2ref={{DD|sa|skullknightmoncavaliermode}} |l3=Ultimate |l3ref={{ref|''[[Digimon Soul Chaser]]''}} |a1=Virus |a1ref={{DD|sa|skullknightmoncavaliermode}} |a2=None |a2ref=''[https://web.archive.org/web/20200928042802/https://digimon.net/reference/detail.php?directory_name=skullknightmoncavaliermode Original Digimon Reference Book profile]'' |t1=Enhancement |t1ref={{DD|sa|skullknightmoncavaliermode}} |w= |wref= |pn=Digimon Reference Book |pe=The "{{FM|Assault Mode|Tougeki Keitai}}" in which [[Skull Knightmon]] is mounted on [[Deadly Axemon]]. Its main purpose is for getting close to the opponent by speeding like a bullet, defying all obstacles.{{br}}With its "{{AT|Kiba Braver|Cavalier Braver}}" it cuts the opponent apart as it charges at them, and after that, Deadly Axemon transforms into "{{FMO|l=Skull Knightmon: Big Axe Mode|Pulverization-armament Mode|Funsaisenshū Keitai}}" to fight joined with Skull Knightmon. If the progress of a battle becomes unfavorable it changes back into "{{FM|Assault Mode|Tougeki Keitai}}" and hastily retreats from the battlefield.{{br}}Deadly Axemon, who possesses limitless stamina, does not tire, so it can repeat this process without accident, and for a short while Deadly Axemon's way of fighting is more feared on the battlefield than Skull Knightmon's. |pj=スカルナイトモンがデッドリーアックスモンに騎乗した『{{FMK|突撃形態}}』。障害を物ともせず弾丸のような走りで敵に接近するのが主目的である。「{{ATK|キバブレイバー}}」で敵を切り裂きつつ突撃し、その後、デッドリーアックスモンは『{{FMOK|粉砕戦集形態}}』に変形してスカルナイトモンと共闘することになる。戦況がかんばしく無くなると再び『{{FMK|突撃形態}}』となり、戦場から緊急離脱を計る。
無限の体力を持つデッドリーアクスモンは疲れることなく、これを繰り返すことができ、しばし戦場ではスカルナイトモンよりデッドリーアックスモンの方が恐れられている。 |pu=skullknightmoncavaliermode |pn2=Digimon Life |pe2={{LifeSame}} |pj2= |pu2=e5d3Qz3bglo%3D |pn3=Misc |pun3=Digimon Web Official Twitter |pe3= |pj3=スカルナイトモンがデッドリーアックスモンに騎乗した『{{FMK|突撃形態}}』。障害を物ともせず走り抜け敵に接近し、必殺技「{{ATK|キバブレイバー}}」で切り裂く。 |pu3=https://twitter.com/digimonweb_net/status/1265485762491043841 |ety={{ENG}} {{ETY|Skull}}

Spiral.wikitext:
{{S2 |kan=スパイラル |rom=Supairaru |ipa= |da= |da2= |image=Spiral.png |imagesize=320x320 |l1= |l1ref= |a1= |a1ref= |t1= |t1ref= |f1= |w= |wref= |s1= |p= |e=They look a lot like Digimon, but they are nothing alike. They appear together with Digital Points, and the purposes of their various actions, such as "expanding Digital Points" and "attracting Digimon to Digital Points," are unknown. |j=デジモンによく似た姿をしているが、似て非なる存在。 デジタルポイントと共に現れ、「デジタルポイントの拡大」「デジタルポイントにデジモンを呼び込む」など、その様々な行為の目的は不明。 |un=Digimon ReArise Official Twitter |u=https://twitter.com/digimon_rearise/status/1148868459222061056 |ety={{ENG}} {{ETY|Spiral}}. |yd=2018 |ydr= |ad= |gd={{hdr|Digimon ReArise}} |cd= |dlf=y }} =Attack Techniques= {{T |name=??? |ref={{--}} |kan={{--}} |rom={{--}} |desc={{--}} }} =Evolution= ==Evolves From== ==Evolves To== * '''[[Rasenmon: Fury Mode]]''' (with '''[[Stiffilmon]]'''){{ref|''[[Digimon ReArise]]''}} * '''[[Digitama]]'''{{ref|''[[Digimon ReArise]]''}} =Appearances= ==Manga== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon ReArise}}=== The Spirals are Digital Lifeforms that originated as fragments of data which were generated by the rifts between the Real and [[Digital World]], spontaneously materializing in both. The fragments developed their Hive Mind and quickly concluded that their purpose was to continue consolidating, but eventually grew lonely and resentful with feeling like outsiders to both Worlds. They initially attempted to achieve {{w|self-actualization}} by assembling into shapes resembling Digimon, but were unsatisfied with mere mimicry and ultimately began trying to create a Digimon-like vessel from their own data. On a fluke, one of their attempts resulted in [[Herissmon (ReArise)|Herissmon]]'s [[Digitama]]. When Herissmon turned out to have his own sense of self, preventing assimilation, the Hive Mind originally discarded him as a failure. However, they learned that Herissmon had managed to achieve self-actualization on his own, and decided to exploit his Spiral roots to mold him into their vessel. By attacking Herissmon continuously and en masse, they weakened him and forced him to "synchronize" with them, transforming him into [[Rasenmon: Fury Mode]] and successfully realizing their vessel. Unfortunately, the Hive Mind began panicking when they realized that by forcing Herissmon to assimilate with them, they had in turn been assimilated with Herissmon, and now felt his emotions and sense of self. In pain, they began lashing out wildly at the world around them. Eventually [[Rasenmon]] would destroy itself - reverting Fury Mode back into a Digitama - however the Spiral refused to give up and continued in their goal to control Herissmon and finally be accepted in the World. There are also {{nihongo|'''GSpiral'''|Gスパイラル}}, giant versions of Spira, and {{nihongo|'''XSpiral'''|Xスパイラル}}, a special type of Spiral that have access to an "X-Barrier". The "X-Barrier" reduces all damage for their team by 85%, though Digimon with the [[X-Antibody]] can bypass the Barrier entirely and deal regular damage, in addition to taking 25% less damage from the XSpiral's "X-Charge" attack though this is only applicable to Digimon like [[War Greymon (X-Antibody)]], and not natural carriers like [[Alphamon]]. [[DORUmon]] that evolve from [[Dorimon]] can also bypass the barrier. ==Virtual Pets== ==TCG== =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Spiral.png{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon ReArise]]

Spirit.wikitext:
[[Image: Agnimon digimon profile.png|thumb|right]] The '''Legendary Spirits''' (伝説のスピリット ''Densetsu no Supiritto''), or '''Spirits''' (スピリット/{{jap|魂|スピリット}} ''Supiritto'') for short, are a set of twenty ancient artifacts that are used to [[Evolution|evolve]] into [[Hybrid]] [[Digimon]]. Each contains the sealed power of a member of the [[Warrior Ten]], a group of ten [[Ultimate]] [[Digimon (species)|Digimon]] who saved the [[Digital World]] in its distant past. The Spirits grant their respective Warrior's power to their users.''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report014/ Digi-Basics: "Hybrids" and "Spirits"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 014|Translation on Wikimon]]) =Overview= ==Origin== The Legendary Spirits are remnants of the [[Warrior Ten]], created in the aftermath of their victory over [[Lucemon]]. Although all ten of them died in the battle, they left behind the Spirits—artifacts which embody both their souls and their power— in various places around the [[Digital World]], to someday be inherited and used in the event that the [[Digital World]] comes under threat from another menace.''[[Digital Monster Card Game]]'' [[Starter Ver. 7|Starter Ver. 7: "Hybrid Warriors"]] [[Starter Ver. 7#Back Story|(Back Story)]] ==Properties== When a human fuses with a Spirit, they form a "new species":''[[Digital Monster Card Game]]'' [[Booster 15|Booster 15: "Burning Beast Fighter"]] [[Booster 15#Back Story|(Back Story)]] [[Hybrid]] Digimon. Both humans and Digimon are capable of using the Spirits. Each Spirit is associated with one of ten elemental powers corresponding to the element of its member of the Warrior Ten. There are two Spirits for each element: * a '''Human Spirit''' {{ETYK|{{j2|ヒューマンスピリット|Hyūman supiritto}}}}, or '''Spirit H''' {{ETYK|{{j2|スピリットＨ|Supiritto eichi}}}}, which creates [[Hybrid#Human Form|Human Form Hybrid Digimon]]; and * a '''Beast Spirit''' {{ETYK|{{j2|ビーストスピリット|Bīsuto supiritto}}}}, or '''Spirit B''' {{ETYK|{{j2|スピリットＢ|Supiritto bī}}}}, which creates [[Hybrid#Beast Form|Beast Form Hybrid Digimon]]. In certain circumstances, multiple Spirits can be combined to create more powerful Hybrid Digimon: using both Spirits of the same element creates [[Hybrid#Fusion Form|Fusion Form Hybrid Digimon]] such as [[Raihimon]], and using ten Spirits in specific combinations creates [[Hybrid#Transcendent Form|Transcendent Form Hybrid Digimon]] such as [[Kaiser Greymon]]. The union of all twenty Spirits creates the [[Ultimate]] Digimon [[Susanoomon]]. It is possible for Spirits to be corrupted into alternate, sinister forms that produce different Hybrid Digimon. The only current corrupted Spirits are the [[Corrupted Human Spirit of Darkness]] and the [[Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness]]. ==Design & Analysis== [[Image:Legendary Spirits Symbols.png|thumb|right|200px|The ten elemental symbols of the Spirits.]] The Spirit items themselves resemble the Hybrid Digimon that they create in some way, such as being stored arrangements of their armor or body. Every Spirit sits on a hexagonal base, which has the word "SPIRIT" ({{DigiAlph2|s|p|i|r|i|t}}) inscribed on it in [[Digimoji]] repeating six times in a pattern around its edge. The elemental attributes of the Spirits are each represented by a symbol that is a stylized/simplified version of the respective {{w|kanji}} for that element. Both the Spirits and their respective Hybrid Digimon often have the respective element's symbol inscribed somewhere on them. In addition, two Digimon created using multiple Spirits have other symbols: * A symbol based on the kanji {{ETYK|炎|honō}} ("flame"), instead of the usual {{ETYK|火|hi}} ("fire" or "flame") symbol, is inscribed on [[Kaiser Greymon]]'s breastplate * A symbol based on the kanji {{ETYK|天|ten}} ("heaven") is inscribed on [[Susanoomon]]'s headgear The concept of the ten elements embodied by the Spirits and their related Digimon follows the common trope of elemental powers widely seen in modern fiction, as well as historical philosophical concepts of elements which comprise or underpin reality, such as the Japanese ''{{w|Godai (Japanese philosophy)|godai}}'', the European and Indian {{w|classical elements}}, and the Chinese ''{{w|Wuxing_(Chinese_philosophy)|Wuxing}}''. In ''[[Digimon Frontier]]'', the groups of Spirits that were originally held by members of the [[Three Archangels]], as noted in ''[[Digimon Frontier - Episode 38|"Endless Death Match! The Prelude of Lucemon's Revival"]]'', may be interpreted as references to particular elemental schemes or cultural perceptions: * Four of the five elements whose Spirits [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]] had—Water, Wood, Metal, Earth, and Darkness—match the phases of the ''Wuxing'', differing only in that Cherubimon had the Spirits of Darkness instead of the Spirits of Fire. * The three elements whose Spirits [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]] had—Fire, Ice and Thunder—match a common combination of elemental powers or magic in modern Japanese popular media, such as ''{{w|Final Fantasy}}''. ==List of Spirits== {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse; font-size:90%; text-align:center;" |- !style="background: #262626;" rowspan="2" width="150px" | Warrior Ten !style="background: #262626;" rowspan="2" width="150px" | Element !style="background: #262626;" colspan="2" | Human Form !style="background: #262626;" colspan="2" | Beast Form !style="background: #262626;" rowspan="2" width="150px" | Fusion Form !style="background: #262626;" rowspan="2" width="150px" | Transcendent Form |- !style="background: #262626;" width="150px" | Spirit !style="background: #262626;" width="150px" | Evolution !style="background: #262626;" width="150px" | Spirit !style="background: #262626;" width="150px" | Evolution |- !style="background: #ffffff;" | [[File:Ancientgreymon.jpg|120px]]

(Japanese LCD game) |- |} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}=== The Legendary Spirits, which were born from the same data as the [[Digital World (Frontier)|Digital World]] itself, were created in the distant past after the Warrior Ten's final battle with [[Lucemon (Frontier)|Lucemon]]. The Warrior Ten released the Spirits and entrusted them to the [[Three Archangels]]:{{rfe|DF|38|Endless Death Match! The Prelude of Lucemon's Revival}} * [[Seraphimon (Frontier)|Seraphimon]] possessed the Spirits of two elements: Light and Wind * [[Ofanimon (Frontier)|Ofanimon]] possessed the Spirits of three elements: Fire, Ice, and Thunder * [[Cherubimon (Frontier)|Cherubimon]] possessed the Spirits of five elements: Darkness, Earth, Wood, Steel, and Water After Lucemon corrupted him, Cherubimon bonded four of his five Spirits to Digimon to create the [[Evil Warriors]],{{rfe|DF|34|Decisive Battle!! Rose of Venus, Ofanimon's Rescue Plan}} and corrupted the Spirits of Darkness and bonded them to [[Kimura Kouichi]]'s to create [[Duskmon]].{{rfe|DF|33|The New Warriors of Darkness! Löwemon & Kaiser Leomon}} However, the Beast Spirit of Water does not appear to have been in his possession at this point, as [[Ranamon (Frontier)|Ranamon]] only later finds it in a shipwreck at the bottom of the ocean.{{rfe|DF|15|Cool Beast Evolution! Calmaramon}} Seraphimon and Ofanimon's Spirits were all hidden to protect them from Cherubimon. When she brings the [[Chosen Child#Digimon Frontier|Chosen Children]] into the Digital World, Ofanimon instructs them to search for and collect the remaining Spirits, so that they can use them to [[Evolution#Digimon Frontier|Spirit Evolve]] and resist Cherubimon.{{rfe|DF|02|Wolfmon of Light, The Underground Labyrinth Battle!}} When not in use, the Spirits that they have collected are stored in their [[D-Scanner]]s. A Hybrid Digimon who also possesses the other Spirit of their respective element can undergo Slide Evolution to transform into that Spirit's respective Hybrid Digimon at any time. Both the Chosen Children and the Evil Warriors do this. A recurring theme with the Beast Spirits is that, unlike the Human Spirits, they are very difficult to control. [[Kanbara Takuya]],{{rfe|DF|11|Defeat Me! Legendary Warrior Vritramon Runs Wild}} [[Minamoto Kouji]],{{rfe|DF|10|An Uncontrollable Beast Spirit!? Garmmon's Evolution}} Ranamon{{rfe|DF|15|Cool Beast Evolution! Calmaramon}} and [[Duskmon]]{{rfe|DF|30|Soaring! Warrior of Darkness Velgrmon}} are all overwhelmed by their Beast Spirits and run wild when they first obtain them. [[Himi Tomoki]]{{rfe|DF|17|Blizzarmon, Blow the Snow, Call the Glaciers!}} and [[Shibayama Junpei]]{{rfe|DF|14|Thunder! Even Rock Breaks! Bolgmon's Do-or-Die Challenge}} also have some difficulty, but [[Orimoto Izumi]]{{rfe|DF|16|Strength Does not Matter! The Beautiful Warrior, Shutumon}} and Kouichi{{rfe|DF|33|The New Warriors of Darkness! Löwemon & Kaiser Leomon}} do not. By the time that they reach the [[Rose Morning Star]], the Chosen Children's collection of the Spirits is as follows: {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;width:80%;font-size:90%" |- !style="background: #262626;width:15%"|Chosen Child !style="background: #262626;width:15%"|Spirit !style="background: #262626;width:50%"|Method of Obtaining !style="background: #262626;width:20%"|Episode |- |rowspan="5" | '''[[Kanbara Takuya]]''' |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:Human Spirit of Fire.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Human Spirit of Fire]]''''' |The first Spirit discovered by any of the Chosen Children. Takuya discovers it in [[Digital World (Frontier)#Geography|Fire Terminal]], when his [[D-Scanner]] responds to its presence and reveals it. He uses it to Spirit Evolve into [[Agnimon]] and defeat [[Cerberumon]]. |{{epn|DF|01|The Legendary Warrior! Agnimon of Flame}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF11 Beast Spirit of Fire.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Beast Spirit of Fire]]''''' |This Spirit was hidden in [[Shamamon]]'s temple, near the [[Digital World (Frontier)#Geography|fortune-teller village]]. Shamamon is involuntarily bonded to it when it reveals itself, and evolves into [[Vritramon]]. He runs wild until Takuya (as Agnimon) subdues and purifies him, and takes the Beast Spirit. |{{epn|DF|11|Defeat Me! Legendary Warrior Vritramon Runs Wild}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF12 Beast Spirit of Earth.JPG|120px]]
[[Beast Spirit of Earth]] |Takuya (as Vritramon) takes this Spirit when he defeats [[Grottemon (Frontier)|Grottemon]] (as [[Gigasmon]]) for the first time. |{{epn|DF|12|Roar, Vritramon! Defeat Gigasmon!}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF28 Human Spirit of Steel.JPG|120px]]
[[Human Spirit of Steel]] |Takuya (as [[Aldamon]]) takes this Spirit when he defeats [[Mercuremon (Frontier)|Mercuremon]] for the first time. Later, during the battle at the [[Rose Morning Star]], Takuya permanently gives this Spirit to [[Minamoto Kouji]] for use in [[Evolution/Anime#Digimon Frontier|Hyper Spirit Evolution]].{{rfe|DF|35|Spirits Become One! Takuya and Kouji's Ultimate Evolution!}} |{{epn|DF|28|Takuya's Fusion Evolution, Aldamon's Explosive Attack!}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF29 Beast Spirit of Steel.JPG|120px]]
[[Beast Spirit of Steel]] |Takuya (as Aldamon) takes this Spirit when he, Tomoki, Izumi, and Junpei defeat Mercuremon (as [[Sephirothmon]]) for the final time. Later, during the battle at the Rose Morning Star, Takuya permanently gives this Spirit to Kouji for use in Hyper Spirit Evolution.{{rfe|DF|35|Spirits Become One! Takuya and Kouji's Ultimate Evolution!}} |{{epn|DF|29|Escape! The Phantasmagoric Sephirothmon}} |- |rowspan="6" | '''[[Minamoto Kouji]]''' |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:Human Spirit of Light.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Human Spirit of Light]]''''' |Kouji discovers this Spirit in [[Digital World (Frontier)#Geography|the labyrinth]] underneath Fire Terminal, when he falls into a pit. He uses it to Spirit Evolve into [[Wolfmon]] and defeat [[Raremon]]. |{{epn|DF|02|Wolfmon of Light, The Underground Labyrinth Battle!}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF10 Beast Spirit of Light.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Beast Spirit of Light]]''''' |This Spirit is sealed underground by [[Digital World (Frontier)#Geography|the guardian deities]], and is unlocked when all three are fitted with ruby "eyes" that fire lasers to reveal it from the ground. Following [[Gottsumon]]'s instructions, Kouji fits the final "eye" to reveal the Spirit, and uses it to Spirit Evolve into [[Garummon]] and fight Gigasmon. |{{epn|DF|10|An Uncontrollable Beast Spirit!? Garmmon's Evolution}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF20 Beast Spirit of Wood.JPG|120px]]
[[Beast Spirit of Wood]] |Kouji (as Wolfmon) takes this Spirit when he, Takuya, Tomoki, Izumi, and Junpei defeat [[Arbormon (Frontier)|Arbormon]] (as [[Petaldramon]]) for the final time. Later, during the battle at the Rose Morning Star, Kouji permanently gives this Spirit to Takuya for use in Hyper Spirit Evolution.{{rfe|DF|35|Spirits Become One! Takuya and Kouji's Ultimate Evolution!}} |{{epn|DF|20|Mysterious Warrior Lurking in the Darkness, Duskmon!}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF32 Corrupted Human Spirit of Darkness.JPG|120px]]
[[Corrupted Human Spirit of Darkness]] |Kouji (as [[Beowolfmon]]) takes this Spirit when he defeats [[Kimura Kouichi]] (as [[Velgrmon]]). He later loses custody of this Spirit when Kouichi receives his D-Scanner and the Spirit is purified. |{{epn|DF|32|The Past is Revealed! Duskmon's Secret}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF32 Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness.JPG|120px]]
[[Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness]] |Kouji (as Beowolfmon) takes this Spirit when he defeats Kouichi (as Velgrmon). He later loses custody of this Spirit when Kouichi receives his D-Scanner and the Spirit is purified. |{{epn|DF|32|The Past is Revealed! Duskmon's Secret}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF20 Human Spirit of Wood.JPG|120px]]
[[Human Spirit of Wood]] |Kouji (as Beowolfmon) takes this Spirit when he defeats Kouichi (as Velgrmon).{{note|The [[Human Spirit of Wood]] is not seen on-screen in ''[[Digimon Frontier - Episode 32|"The Past is Revealed! Duskmon's Secret"]]'', so [[Minamoto Kouji|Kouji]] is not seen physically taking it. That Kouji received it here is inferred based on [[Duskmon]] previously taking the Spirit in ''[[Digimon Frontier - Episode 20|"Mysterious Warrior Lurking in the Darkness, Duskmon!"]]'', and the Spirit later being in Kouji's possession in ''[[Digimon Frontier - Episode 35|"Spirits Become One! Takuya and Kouji's Ultimate Evolution!"]]'' before he transfers it to [[Kanbara Takuya|Takuya]].|n="humanwood"}} He had previously attempted to take it after defeating Arbormon in the [[Digital World (Frontier)#Geography|Forest of the Dark Sun]], but Kouichi (as [[Duskmon]]) had intervened and killed Arbormon to take the Spirit for himself.{{epn|DF|20|Mysterious Warrior Lurking in the Darkness, Duskmon!}} Later, during the battle at the Rose Morning Star, Kouji permanently transfers this Spirit to Takuya for use in Hyper Spirit Evolution.{{rfe|DF|35|Spirits Become One! Takuya and Kouji's Ultimate Evolution!}} |{{epn|DF|32|The Past is Revealed! Duskmon's Secret}} |- |rowspan="2" | '''[[Himi Tomoki]]''' |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:Human Spirit of Ice.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Human Spirit of Ice]]''''' |This Spirit is hidden in a frozen cave near the [[Digital World (Frontier)#Geography|Candmon Village]]. When Tomoki tries to defend Agnimon against the Candmon, the Spirit responds to him and reveals itself. He uses it to Spirit Evolve into [[Chackmon]] and fight [[Wizarmon]] and the Candmon. Grottemon (as Gigasmon) later briefly steals this Spirit from Tomoki when he defeats him near the fortune-teller village,{{rfe|DF|11|Defeat Me! Legendary Warrior Vritramon Runs Wild}} but Takuya (as Vritramon) soon returns it to him when he defeats Gigasmon{{rfe|DF|12|Roar, Vritramon! Defeat Gigasmon!}} |{{epn|DF|03|Bullying is Unforgivable! Evolve, Chackmon of Ice}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF17 Beast Spirit of Ice.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Beast Spirit of Ice]]''''' |This Spirit is hidden in a ''Space Invaders''-like arcade game machine that [[Nanomon]] owns at the [[Akiba Market]]. When Tomoki beats the game, Nanomon receives the Spirit on a cartridge, and transfers its data onto Tomoki's stolen D-Scanner as an experiment. He returns the D-Scanner to Tomoki when he becomes convinced of his honesty, and Tomoki uses the new Spirit to Spirit Evolve into [[Blizzarmon]] and fight Petaldramon. |{{epn|DF|17|Blizzarmon, Blow the Snow, Call the Glaciers!}} |- |rowspan="4" | '''[[Orimoto Izumi]]''' |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:Human Spirit of Wind.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Human Spirit of Wind]]''''' |This Spirit is hidden in a giant tree in [[Digital World (Frontier)#Geography|Soft Breeze Village]]. It emerges from the tree in response to Izumi's concern for [[Shibayama Junpei]], and she uses it to Spirit Evolve into [[Fairimon]] and fight [[Woodmon]]. Later, Grottemon (as Gigasmon) steals this Spirit from Izumi when he defeats her near the Gottsumon Village.{{rfe|DF|10|An Uncontrollable Beast Spirit!? Garmmon's Evolution}} She gets it back after Junpei (as [[Bolgmon]]) defeats Grottemon for the last time.{{rfe|DF|14|Thunder! Even Rock Breaks! Bolgmon's Do-or-Die Challenge}} |{{epn|DF|04|My Painful Kick! Female Warrior Fairymon}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF17 Beast Spirit of Wind.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Beast Spirit of Wind]]''''' |This Spirit is hidden in a clamshell at the bottom of the ocean near [[Digital World (Frontier)#Geography|Goma Island]], due to the effects of Cherubimon's magic. It creates powerful whirlpools, and also generates breathable atmosphere around it even underwater. When [[Ranamon (Frontier)|Ranamon]]'s {{ATO|Rain Stream}} knocks Izumi (as Fairimon) into the ocean, she finds and claims the Spirit, and uses it to Spirit Evolve into [[Shutumon]] and defeat Ranamon. |{{epn|DF|16|Strength Does not Matter!
