Female Digimon Personal Combat}} |- |rowspan="3" | '''[[Shibayama Junpei]]''' |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:Human Spirit of Thunder.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Human Spirit of Thunder]]''''' |This Spirit is hidden in the core of the [[Digital World (Frontier)#Geography|Factory of Wind]]. When the wreck of a crane breaks the core open and reveals the Spirit, Junpei uses it to Spirit Evolve into [[Blitzmon]] and fight [[Snimon]]. |{{epn|DF|05|Thunder Power Shaking the Ground, Blitzmon!}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:Beast Spirit of Thunder.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Beast Spirit of Thunder]]''''' |This Spirit was hidden in a bed of seaweed, until [[Whamon]] ate it by accident when feeding on the seaweed. He inadvertently releases the Spirit when he uses his {{ATO|Jet Arrow}} during the Chosen Children's final encounter with Grottemon in the [[Digital World (Frontier)#Geography|Blue Cave]], and Junpei uses it to Spirit Evolve into [[Bolgmon]] and defeat Grottemon. |{{epn|DF|14|Thunder! Even Rock Breaks! Bolgmon's Do-or-Die Challenge}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF14 Human Spirit of Earth.JPG|120px]]
[[Human Spirit of Earth]] |Junpei (as Bolgmon) takes this Spirit when he defeats Grottemon for the final time. Later, during the battle at the Rose Morning Star, Junpei permanently transfers this Spirit to Takuya for use in Hyper Spirit Evolution.{{rfe|DF|35|Spirits Become One! Takuya and Kouji's Ultimate Evolution!}} |{{epn|DF|14|Thunder! Even Rock Breaks! Bolgmon's Do-or-Die Challenge}} |- |rowspan="2" | '''[[Kimura Kouichi]]''' |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF33 Spirits of Darkness.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Human Spirit of Darkness]]''''' |When Kouichi stands up to Cherubimon and his D-Scanner appears, the Corrupted Human Spirit of Darkness emerges from Kouji's D-Scanner, transforms back into its true form, and enters Kouichi's D-Scanner. Kouichi then uses the Human Spirit of Darkness to Spirit Evolve into [[Löwemon]] and fight Cherubimon. After [[Lucemon (Frontier)|Lucemon]] fatally wounds Kouichi in the battle in the [[Dark Area]], Kouichi passes this Spirit on to Kouji.{{rfe|DF|48|Light and Darkness are One! Kouichi's Last Wish}} |{{epn|DF|33|The New Warriors of Darkness! Löwemon & Kaiser Leomon}} |- |style="text-align:center;" | [[File:DF33 Spirits of Darkness.JPG|120px]]
'''''[[Beast Spirit of Darkness]]''''' |When Kouichi stands up to Cherubimon and his D-Scanner appears, the Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness emerges from Kouji's D-Scanner, transforms back into its true form, and enters Kouichi's D-Scanner. Kouichi then uses the Beast Spirit of Darkness to Slide Evolve from Löwemon into [[Kaiser Leomon]] and finish his fight with Cherubimon. After Lucemon fatally wounds Kouichi in the battle in the Dark Area, Kouichi passes this Spirit on to Kouji.{{rfe|DF|48|Light and Darkness are One! Kouichi's Last Wish}} |{{epn|DF|33|The New Warriors of Darkness! Löwemon & Kaiser Leomon}} |- | colspan="4" style="text-align:center;" | Spirits listed in '''''bold and italics''''' are used by the respective Chosen Child to Spirit Evolve. |} [[Image: Legendary Spirits DF50.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Legendary Spirits, reborn as Digimon.]] The ability to use more than one Spirit at a time requires the power of one of the Three Archangels. During their battles inside [[Sephirothmon]], Seraphimon's [[Digitama]] gives power to Takuya and Kouji's D-Scanners to allow them to Double Spirit Evolve using both the Spirits of their respective elements.{{rfe|DF|27|Double Spirit Miracle! Beowulfmon is Born}}{{rfe|DF|28|Takuya's Fusion Evolution, Aldamon's Explosive Attack!}} In their confrontation with Cherubimon at the Rose Morning Star, Ofanimon upgrades Takuya and Kouji's D-Scanners again to transform them into New D-Scanners, allowing them to Hyper Spirit Evolve using ten Spirits each. When they gain this ability, the group reorganizes their collected Spirits to permanently give the Spirits of Earth and Wood to Takuya, and the Spirits of Water and Steel to Kouji, so that they can be used for their respective Hyper Spirit Evolutions.{{rfe|DF|35|Spirits Become One! Takuya and Kouji's Ultimate Evolution!}} In the group's second battle with Lucemon in the [[Dark Area]], Kouichi gives the Spirits of Darkness back to Kouji when he is killed by Lucemon. The reaction of Kouji's Spirits of Light and the Spirits of Darkness triggers Kouji and Takuya's Ancient Spirit Evolution into [[Susanoomon]] using all twenty Spirits.{{rfe|DF|48|Light and Darkness are One! Kouichi's Last Wish}} In the final battle against Lucemon, the Warrior Ten are reborn as the ten Human Form Hybrids as entities in their own right, who protect and encourage the Chosen Children. As a side effect of the powerful bond established between the Spirits and Chosen Children from all of their time spent traveling together and Spirit Evolving, the Warrior Ten have inherited some of the traits of the Chosen Children (for instance, [[Fairimon]] now speaks Italian, like Izumi), and there is some sort of link between each Chosen Child-Warrior pair (e.g. [[Löwemon]]'s time spent with Kouichi allows him to sense that Kouichi is still alive in the Real World).{{rfe|DF|50|Go Beyond Time! The Beginning of a New Legend}} ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars: The Young Hunters Who Leapt Through Time}}=== [[Kanbara Takuya]] uses the Spirits of Fire to become [[Aldamon]] for the fight against [[Quartzmon]]'s forces. Near the end of the battle, [[Himi Tomoki]] sends the rest of the Spirits to Takuya so that he can become [[Susanoomon]] by himself.{{rfe|XW|78|The Gathering of the Legendary Heroes! The Digimon All-star Showdown!!}} {| |[[File:XW78 Spirit Delivery.jpg|thumb|Aldamon receives the rest of the Spirits in ''Digimon Xros Wars''.]] |} ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== In the ''[[V-Tamer Frontier Generation|"Frontier Generation"]]'' special, [[Kanbara Takuya]] and [[Minamoto Kouji]] use their Human Spirits to defeat a [[Megadramon]], following the same Spirit Evolution mechanics as in ''[[Digimon Frontier]]''. Takuya also uses his [[Beast Spirit of Fire]] to Slide Evolve into [[Vritramon]] when fighting [[Metamormon]] with [[Yagami Taichi (V-Tamer)|Yagami Taichi]] and [[Zeromaru]]. ==Video Games== [[Image:Legendary Spirits Symbols DMO.png|thumb|right|200px|Legendary Spirits Symbols as seen in ''Digimon Masters Online'', in the order:
闇 土 火 水 光
鋼 雷 氷 風 木]] ==={{hdr|Digital Monster: D-Project}}=== Five Spirits—the [[Human Spirit of Fire|Human Spirits of Fire]], [[Human Spirit of Light|Light]], [[Human Spirit of Ice|Ice]], [[Human Spirit of Wind|Wind]] and [[Human Spirit of Thunder|Thunder]]— are equippable items which can be obtained by inputting their respective codes into the meat machine. When equipped onto a compatible [[Child]] Digimon, the next time that the Digimon enters a battle, it will evolve into the matching [[Hybrid]] Digimon for its duration. Spirits are single-use only, and after the battle in which they are used, they disappear. The same Spirit can be freely re-acquired from the meat machine, and the player may stockpile multiples of the same Spirits. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Lost Evolution}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory}}=== The four Spirits of Fire and Light appear as items that, once obtained, allow compatible Digimon to evolve into the respective Digimon ([[Agnimon]], [[Wolfmon]], [[Vritramon]], [[Garummon]]) as long as they also meet the other evolution criteria. The item is not consumed in the evolution, so the Digimon can be evolved into any number of times. Kaiser Greymon, Magna Garurumon, and Susanoomon also appear in the game, but their evolution criteria do not involve the Spirits at all aside from the former two evolving only from Vritramon and Garummon. In compilations of both ''Cyber Sleuth'' games—the Japanese Nintendo Switch and PlayStation 4 versions of ''Hacker's Memory'', and the western ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Complete Edition]]''—any of the Digimon evolved using the Spirit items can be transferred back to ''[[Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth]]'' after both games have been cleared, but the Spirits are not made available in ''Cyber Sleuth'' so these Digimon cannot be evolved into natively there. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-Scanner Toy}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Tector Toy}}=== The Spirits are collectible items which the player's party of DigiDestined ([[Chosen Child#Digimon Frontier|Chosen Children]]) may use in battles to "[[D-Tector Toy#Spirit On|Spirit On]]" into their respective [[Hybrid]] Digimon. In order to use a Spirit, the matching DigiDestined must currently be present in the party, and the player must have enough Spirits Power points to afford to use the Hybrid Digimon's attacks. Each Spirit comes with a set of stat bonuses which are added to the respective DigiDestined's own base stats, meaning that unlike regular Digimon, Hybrid Digimon stats scale as the game progresses and the player levels up. The Spirits available depend on the device's firmware version: * Only the ten Human and Beast Spirits of Fire, Light, Ice, Wind and Thunder are available in firmware revision A. * Revision B adds the true Human and Beast Spirits of Darkness. * Revision C adds: ** the Human and Beast Spirits of Earth, Water, Wood, and Gold (Steel); unlike in ''[[Digimon Frontier]]'' or any other ''Digimon'' media, they may respectively be used by J.P. ([[Shibayama Junpei]]), Zoe ([[Orimoto Izumi]]), Tommy ([[Himi Tomoki]]) and Koichi ([[Kimura Kouichi]]) just like their usual Spirits ** the "Extra" (Z) Spirits for KaiserGreymon ([[KaiserGreymon]]) and MagnaGarurumon ([[Magna Garurumon]]) ** the ability to become "Combo" ([[Hybrid#Fusion Form|Fusion Form]]) Hybrids and Susanomon ([[Susanoomon]]), albeit not represented with Spirits unlike the Japanese D-Scanners ==={{hdr|D-Spirit}}=== In the Quest mode, the player's goal is to collect Spirits by defeating the [[Hybrid]] bosses of each Area. They begin with one of five Human Spirits, chosen based on which [[Trailmon]] they selected to board at the beginning of the mode. Any Spirits that the player has obtained in Quest Mode may also be used for the "V.S. Spirit" multiplayer battle mode. In the Database, a Human Form, Beast Form or Extra (Transcendent) Form Hybrid Digimon's corresponding Spirit may also be viewed by selecting the "Spirit" option in the Digimon's Digivolve ([[Evolution]]) menu. ==TCG== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster Card Game}}=== All of the Legendary Spirits, including the corrupted forms of the Spirits of Darkness, have one card each in the [[Digital Monster Card Game]]. The Human Spirits (excluding the true [[Human Spirit of Darkness]] but including [[Corrupted Human Spirit of Darkness|the corrupted version]]) were all introduced in [[Starter Ver. 7]], the Beast Spirits (again, excluding the true [[Beast Spirit of Darkness]] but including [[Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness|the corrupted version]]) were introduced in [[Expansion Kit S]], and the true forms of the Spirits of Darkness were introduced in [[Booster 17]]. The Spirits' cards are all Option Cards of the Item Category, and are used as part of the Appearance Requirements of many (but not all) cards featuring Human Form and Beast Form Hybrid Digimon. The Appearance process for these Hybrids is labeled "Spirit Evolution" {{ETYK|{{j2|スピリット進化|Supiritto shinka}}}}, and requires that the Spirit card be played in the player's Support Box to evolve a Digimon of an appropriate level ([[Adult|Level IV/Adult]] for Human Form Hybrids, and [[Perfect]] for Beast Form Hybrids). The Spirit remains in the Support Box until the resulting Digimon is removed from play. In addition to the Appearance Requirements use, each Spirit card also confers a number of additional effects onto the Digimon that used it to Spirit Evolve. Unrelated to Hybrid Digimon, some Human Spirits have an additional effect that allows them to substitute for certain [[Digimental]] cards. Spirit cards are not used to evolve into [[Hybrid#Fusion Form|Fusion Form]] or [[Hybrid#Transcendent Form|Transcendent Form]] Hybrid Digimon. These Digimon all have other Evolution/Fusion/Appearance Requirements instead. In the ''Digital Monster Card Game'', all Spirits are given formulaic, abbreviated names: Human Spirits are "Spirit of ''(x)'' H" {{ETYK|''(x)''のスピリットＨ}} and Beast Spirits are "Spirit of ''(x)'' B" {{ETYK|''(x)''のスピリットＢ}}. Because the Corrupted Spirits of Darkness were released first, they occupy the formulaic H and B initials for Darkness, so the two true Spirits are instead called "Spirit of Darkness L" {{ETYK|闇のスピリットＬ}} and "Spirit of Darkness K" {{ETYK|闇のスピリットＫ}}, taking their initials from the respective Digimon that they create, [[Löwemon]] and [[Kaiser Leomon]]. {| |valign="top"| *[[Human Spirit of Fire|Spirit of Fire H]]: [[St-676]] *[[Human Spirit of Light|Spirit of Light H]]: [[St-677]] *[[Human Spirit of Ice|Spirit of Ice H]]: [[St-678]] *[[Human Spirit of Wind|Spirit of Wind H]]: [[St-679]] *[[Human Spirit of Thunder|Spirit of Thunder H]]: [[St-680]] *[[Corrupted Human Spirit of Darkness|Spirit of Darkness H]]: [[St-681]] *[[Human Spirit of Steel|Spirit of Steel H]]: [[St-682]] *[[Human Spirit of Water|Spirit of Water H]]: [[St-683]] *[[Human Spirit of Wood|Spirit of Wood H]]: [[St-684]] *[[Human Spirit of Earth|Spirit of Earth H]]: [[St-685]] |valign="top"| *[[Beast Spirit of Fire|Spirit of Fire B]]: [[St-739]] *[[Beast Spirit of Wind|Spirit of Wind B]]: [[St-740]] *[[Beast Spirit of Earth|Spirit of Earth B]]: [[St-741]] *[[Beast Spirit of Steel|Spirit of Steel B]]: [[St-742]] *[[Beast Spirit of Ice|Spirit of Ice B]]: [[St-743]] *[[Beast Spirit of Thunder|Spirit of Thunder B]]: [[St-744]] *[[Beast Spirit of Water|Spirit of Water B]]: [[St-745]] *[[Beast Spirit of Light|Spirit of Light B]]: [[St-746]] *[[Corrupted Beast Spirit of Darkness|Spirit of Darkness B]]: [[St-747]] *[[Beast Spirit of Wood|Spirit of Wood B]]: [[St-748]] |valign="top"| *[[Human Spirit of Darkness|Spirit of Darkness L]]: [[Bo-852]] *[[Beast Spirit of Darkness|Spirit of Darkness K]]: [[Bo-853]] |} =Additional Information= {{R}} [[Category:Items]] [[Category:Legendary Spirits| ]] [[Category:Digital Lifeforms]]

'''System Omega''' (システムΩ, ''Shisutemu Ω'') is a program located in the [[Omega Area]] of [[Lost Space]] that is used by [[Barbamon]] in order to revive [[Armamon]]. It may potentially gather all the Digimon's [[Digicore|Digicores]] from the [[LS-tou Zone]] and, as revealed by [[Spadamon]], it can only be accessed by the [[Legend-Arms]].

{{S2 |drbentry={{DRBEntry|907|908|909th, 910th, 911th, 912th, 913th, 914th, 915th, 916th, 917th, 918th, 919th, 920|921|2a=Agumon (Black) (X-Antibody)|3a=Gomamon (X-Antibody), Metal Greymon (Virus) (X-Antibody), Seadramon (X-Antibody), Black War Greymon (X-Antibody), Mega Seadramon (X-Antibody), Allomon (X-Antibody), Mantaraymon (X-Antibody), Kokuwamon (X-Antibody), Kuwagamon (X-Antibody), Okuwamon (X-Antibody), Nefertimon (X-Antibody), Lady Devimon (X-Antibody)|4a=Orgemon (X-Antibody)}} |nam={{PAGENAME}} |name=Triceramon |kan=トリケラモン（X抗体） |rom=Torikeramon (X-Koutai) |add= |dub= |dref= |image=Triceramonx2.jpg |imagesize=320x320 |da={{DigiAlph|to|ri|ke|ra|mon}} |da2={{DigiAlph2|t|r|i|c|e|r|a|mon}} |l1=Perfect |l1ref={{DD|ta|triceramon_x}} |a1=Data |a1ref={{DD|ta|triceramon_x}} |t1=Ceratopsian |t1ref={{DD|ta|triceramon_x}} |f1=Nature Spirits |f1ref={{rfc|Bx|53}} |f2= |f2ref= |w=46 |wref= |s1= |pn=DRB |pe=A bipedal Ceratopsian Digimon that has the appearance of the {{w|Triceratops}}, which has offensive power competing for first or second among herbivorous dinosaur-types. The sturdiness of its skin is top class among biological Digimon. Just like its skin, the two horns growing from its brow are super-sturdy, and are far harder than that of [[Monochromon]]. It has a fundamentally gentle personality. However, its charging attacks have the offensive power to obliterate even the sturdy-bodied Mineral-species Digimon, though you wouldn't guess it from its ordinarily sluggish movements. Its Special Move is charging at the opponent with the two horns on its brow and the horn on its snout ({{AT|Tri Horn Attack}}).{{xab|Triceramon}}The three horns growing from its head spin rapidly in order to pierce anything that comes to attack it, and their penetrating power has increased even more. Due to its gentle personality, it is often attacked by the enemy, but tries to drive them away using the sturdiness of its skin and its {{EQ|Spike Armor}}. Its "{{AT|Golden Tri Horn Attack}}", which adds the high-speed rotation of its horns to its Special Move "{{AT|Tri Horn Attack}}", is a Special Move with enough power to pierce the enemy even without charging into them. |pj=草食恐竜型では一、二を争う攻撃力を持つトリケラトプスの姿を持つ二足歩行の角竜型デジモン。表皮の頑丈さは生物系デジモンの中ではトップクラス。表皮同様、額から生えた２本の角は超硬質で、モノクロモンよりもはるかに硬い。基本的に性格は温厚。しかし、通常時の緩慢な動作からは推測しかねる突進攻撃は、硬質の体を持つ鉱物系デジモンでさえ破壊してしまう攻撃力を持っている。必殺技は額の２本角と鼻先の角で敵に突進していく『{{ATK|トライホーンアタック}}』。
■Ｘ抗体によるトリケラモンのデジコアへの影響
襲い来るあらゆるものを突き刺すため、頭部から生えた３本の角は高速回転し、さらに貫通力が増している。温厚な性格のため、敵に襲われることが多いが、表皮の頑丈さと{{EQK|スパイクアーマー}}で敵を寄せ付けないようにしている。必殺技の『{{ATK|トライホーンアタック}}』に角の高速回転を付け加えた『{{ATK|ゴールデントライホーンアタック}}』は、突進せずとも敵を貫く程の威力を持つ必殺技である。 |pu=triceramon_x |pn2=Video Games |pun2=Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode |pe2={{SameProfile|DRB}} |pu2= |pn3=Virtual Pets |pun3=Digimon Pendulum X |pe3=Although it is a Ceratopsian Digimon with offensive power that competes for first or second among herbivorous dinosaur-types, it has a gentle personality, and the sturdiness of its skin is top class among biological Digimon. The three horns growing from its head now spin rapidly and their penetrating power has increased even more.{{br}}Special Move: "{{AT|Golden Tri Horn Attack}}", which adds the high-speed rotation of its horns to "{{AT|Tri Horn Attack}}". |pj3=草食恐竜タイプでは一、二を争う攻撃力を持つ角竜型デジモンだが性格は温厚、表皮の頑丈さは生物系デジモンの中ではトップクラス。頭部から生えた3本の角は高速回転するようになり更に貫通力が増している。

A bunch of them were a part of [[Silphymon]]'s rebel group. ==Manga== ==={{hdr|D-Cyber}}=== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon All-Star Rumble}}=== ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|Pendulum X 2.0|Pendulum X 2.0 & 2.5}}=== Triceramon X-Antibody evolves from [[Allomon (X-Antibody)]], [[Ginryumon]], [[Growmon (X-Antibody)]], [[Monochromon (X-Antibody)]], or [[Tobucatmon]], and can evolve to [[Dinorexmon]], [[Dinotigemon]] (2.0), [[Medieval Dukemon]], [[Ouryumon]], [[Ulforce V-dramon (X-Antibody)]], or [[Ultimate Brachiomon]]. V-dramon Zero.wikitext:
{{Char |name=Zeromaru |image=Vdramon zero.jpg |imagesize=x320 |kan=ゼロマル |dub=Zeromaru''[[Digimon Journal]]'' (English edition): [https://digimon.net/journal/topic004/ "Digimon Highlights: UlforceVeedramon, Galacticmon and Angoramon!​"] |hp=[[Yagami Taichi (V-Tamer)|Yagami Taichi]] }} '''Zeromaru''', or '''Zero''' {{ETYK|ゼロ}} for short, is the [[partner Digimon]] of [[Yagami Taichi (V-Tamer)|Yagami Taichi]] in ''[[Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01]]'', and a member of the [[Royal Knight]]s in [[Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|the ''Digimon Xros Wars'' manga]]. =Evolution= {{Box Evo |Baby1=[[Botamon]] |Baby2=[[Koromon]]{{Note|In a flashback in ''[[V-53|"The Super Ultimate Appears"]]'', Zeromaru is depicted as a [[Botamon]] on Taichi's [[Digital Monster]] virtual pet, which would likely make it a [[Digital Monster Ver. 1]].

Zeromaru is never depicted in his [[Baby II]] or [[Child]] forms, but in ''[[V-2|"Reversal Combination"]]'', Taichi comments on the many [[Agumon]]s that they encounter that Zeromaru was once ''"like that"'' {{ETYK|おまえもあんな頃あったんだぜ}}, suggesting that his Child form was Agumon (also from the Digital Monster Ver. 1). The logical conclusion from these details is that his Baby II form would have been [[Koromon]], the Baby II Digimon from the Ver. 1.|n=VER1}} |Child=[[Agumon]]{{Note|n=VER1}} |Adult='''[[V-dramon]]''' |Perfect=[[Aero V-dramon]] |Ultimate=[[Ulforce V-dramon]] |Super Ultimate=[[Ulforce V-dramon Future Mode]] (with or without the [[Digimental (V-Tamer)|Digimental]]) }} =Profile= ==Personality== Zeromaru, like Taichi, has a lighthearted and often goofy demeanor. The two often celebrate their wins with a silly victory dance, but are just as prone to bickering over the pettiest things, as Zeromaru is highly emotional and it takes very little to offend or anger him. Despite this, his bond with Taichi is very strong and there is no genuine bad blood between them, to the point that Zeromaru is able to resist [[Vamdemon]]'s attempts to brainwash him and drive a wedge between the two.[[V-13|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Wake Up!! Combination"]] Zeromaru and Taichi have absolute trust in each other and total understanding of each others' feelings.[[V-34|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Anger Overload!!"]] Both will gladly risk their lives for the other, and neither will hesitate to enact or follow plans that put themselves in jeopardy out of faith that the other will not let them down. Zeromaru is also strongly driven by the need to protect his friends and allies, and will push himself well past his own limits if it means protecting them. He is especially protective of Taichi and his honor, and is angered by any insults toward him. He secretly hopes that his relationship with Taichi will never change, to the point that, for a time, he even stops himself from evolving.[[V-18|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Future Potential"]] ==Properties== As a [[V-dramon]], Zeromaru is an [[Ancient Species]] Digimon, which makes his [[Overwrite]]s characteristically violent and causes him to express various unusual powers in response to his emotions, such as powering up when angered.[[V-34|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "VS Omegamon ①, DEAD OR ALIVE"]] Zeromaru is the first Ancient Species Digimon known to have been raised by a [[Tamer]], and after evolving, he becomes a rare living specimen of the supposedly extinct [[Aero V-dramon]] species[[V-34|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "VS Omegamon ①, DEAD OR ALIVE"]] and, ultimately, the first-ever Ancient Species Digimon known to have evolved to the [[Ultimate]] level.[[V-43|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "HOPE ON"]] In the original run of ''[[Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01]]'', Zeromaru's evolutions are one-way and permanent, aside from [[Ulforce V-dramon Future Mode]]. This is no longer the case in ''[[V-Tamer New Courage|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01 ~New Courage~]]'', in which Zeromaru stays in his V-dramon form by default and, at first, is only able to evolve into Aero V-dramon temporarily in order to fight. Zeromaru himself speculates that this could be due to damage he took in his original adventure with Taichi, or an effect of the polyhedron.[[V-Tamer New Courage|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01 ~New Courage~'']] ==Production== ''V-Tamer 01'' creators [[Izawa Hiroshi]] and [[Yabuno Tenya]] wanted the name of Taichi's partner Digimon to include the word "zero," to both make it sound fighter jet-esque, and to pair with the {{ETYK|一|ichi}} ("one") sound in Taichi's name for {{w|binary data|a digital theme}}. However, they moved away from just calling him "Zero" for the purposes of trademarking. Since the name "Zeromaru" is a compound of two Japanese words/symbols which can be taken to mean 0— * {{ETYK|ゼロ|zero}}, a loanword from English, and * {{ETYK|丸|maru}}, "circle," {{w|Japanese numerals#Basic numbering in Japanese|which is used as both a notation (as the symbol 〇) and reading of zeroes in certain contexts}} —the potential interpretation as "00," taken together with the {{ETYK|一|ichi}} in "Taichi," resulted in the "100%" motif with which the duo are associated (e.g. being called the "100% Combination").[[Habu Kazumasa|Habu, Kazumasa]]. [https://digimon-game.bn-ent.net/free/detail.php?id=7 "関係者に聞く　スペシャルインタビュー！　≪　やぶのてんや先生　＆　渡辺けんじ先生　≫"] [''"Listen to the Staff : Special Interview! <<[[Yabuno Tenya]]-san & [[Watanabe Kenji]]-san>>"'']. ''デジモンゲームコミュ'' [''Digimon GameCommu'']. November 12 2017.
([https://garmtranslations.wordpress.com/2018/12/23/digimon-production-secrets-4-tenya-yabuno-interview-part-1-of-2/ English translation by garmmy]) In ''[[V-3|"Folder Continent's Military History"]]'', the two characters even directly call attention to the "100%" interpretation, as does [[Lord Holy Angemon]] to the reference to binary data.[[V-3|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Folder Continent's Military History"]] (pp. 12-13) =Appearances= ==Anime== ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}=== {{UC}} Zeromaru originated from Taichi's [[Digital Monster]] virtual pet.{{note|n=VER1}} Taichi developed a very strong attachment to Zeromaru, and often spent time with him and taking care of him.[[V-53|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "The Super Ultimate Appears"]] One day, Taichi attempted to enter the [[D-1 Grand Prix]] with Zeromaru, but was disqualified from entering as his species, [[V-dramon]], was not one that had been programmed into the devices and was suspected to be the result of cheating. Shortly after, Taichi was brought into the [[Digital World]] and met Zeromaru in the flesh. Zeromaru joined Taichi on a journey across the Digital World to find his way home, and the two quickly develop a reputation as an unbeatable team, or "100% Combination," with a flawless win rate against other Digimon.[[V-1|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "100% Tamer!!"]] When they meet [[Gabou]], he brings them to [[Holy Angel Castle]] for an audience with [[Lord Holy Angemon]], who has called on them to help stop [[Demon (V-Tamer)|Demon]]'s scheme to create a [[Ultimate#Super Ultimate|Super Ultimate]] Digimon. They accept this mission.[[V-3|''Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01'': "Folder Continent's Military History"]] ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|Digimon Xros Wars}}=== Zeromaru (although he is not referred to by this name) is, in his [[Ulforce V-dramon]] form, a member of the [[Royal Knights]], having been chosen by [[Homeostasis]] to join their ranks some time after his adventures with Taichi. Several hundreds of years in the Digital World's past,[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 16|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Wars!! The Prologue to the End!!"]] he, along with the other Royal Knights, tried and failed to resist the [[Bagra Army]] when it launched its assault during their search for the "red-black two-headed dragon," during which he was blinded by the miasma from [[Tactimon (Xros Wars)|Tactimon]]'s sword, and he and his squires [[Spadamon]], [[Coronamon]] and [[Lunamon]] were caught up in the shattering of the [[Digital World (Xros Wars)|Digital World]] and were sent to the Real World with no way back and no way of contacting their comrades. They took shelter in a secret base in Tokyo.[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 13|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Memories!! The Boys' Old Days"]] On April 3, 20XX, Spadamon brings [[Kudou Taiki]] to Ulforce V-dramon for an audience. Ulforce V-dramon explains the events of the battle and [[Homeostasis]]'s prophecy about the "red-black two-headed dragon," and asks Taiki to bring the group back to the Digital World with him so that they can stop the Bagra Army in time to prevent the prophecy from being fulfilled. When a battle breaks out between [[Blastmon (Xros Wars)|Blastmon]], Taiki and [[Aonuma Kiriha]], Ulforce V-dramon and his squires are present, and Ulforce V-dramon senses that [[Shoutmon (Xros Wars)|Shoutmon]] has the presence/code of his missing-in-action comrade [[Omegamon]].[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 15|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Alliance! The Final Battle in Odaiba!!"]] When Taiki returns to the Digital World, he brings Ulforce V-dramon with him in his [[Xros Loader]], which heals his eyes enough to enable him to join his fellow remaining Royal Knights in battle when they arrive to reinforce [[Xros Heart]] and [[Blue Flare]] at the [[Bagra Pandemonium]]. He and the Royal Knights volunteer to take on [[Arkadimon Ultimate|Arkadimon]] while [[Shoutmon X7]] focuses on [[Lilithmon (Xros Wars)|Lilithmon]]. With the support of a vaccine program developed by [[Duftmon]], he destroys Arkadimon with his {{AT|Dragon Impulse X}}. After the armies force Lilithmon to retreat, he reflects on his own youth with Taichi, how once again the Digital World has turned to the power of human children to be saved, and how the [[General]]s likely will not be able to stay once the war is over. The group comes under attack from [[Amano Nene]]/[[Shademon]] and [[Dark Knightmon (Xros Wars)|Dark Knightmon]], and while the Generals enter the castle to confront Bagramon, Ulforce V-dramon and the Royal Knights hold off Amano and Dark Knightmon's army of [[:Category:Composite Type|Composite Type]] Digimon.[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 17|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Warrior!! The Released Demon!!"]] They witness the formation of the "red-black two-headed dragon," [[Zeed Millenniumon]], by Nene, and he laments that they had failed to notice the warning signs that it, as a likely candidate for the "dragon's" identity, would be revived. Ulforce V-dramon and the other Royal Knights stay outside while the Generals enact their plan to undo Zeed Millenniumon's [[DigiXros]] from within,[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 19|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Apocalypse!! The Beast of Revelation!!"]] and witness both the success of their plan and the appearance of the horde of evil Digimon released from Zeed Millenniumon's separation.[[Digimon Xros Wars - Chapter 21|''Digimon Xros Wars'': "Xros Songs!! To All The Teens Who Are Chasing After Their Dreams!!"]] After the battle, before the Generals return home, Ulforce V-dramon approaches Nene with a request for in case Digimon-related incidents occur in the Real World again. ==={{hdr|V-Tamer New Courage|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01 ~New Courage~}}=== When the polyhedron appears in the Digital World, Zeromaru and Taichi join forces once again to stop it, but before long, they are the only ones left to fight it as the endless wave of Virus Digimon it spawns takes all of their allies, and eventually [[Lord Holy Angemon]] himself, out of commission. Zeromaru takes Lord Holy Angemon to a healing capsule. The best that they could manage was to seal it away. After a battle with several of the polyhedron's Digimon, Taichi and Zeromaru are excited to encounter [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure:)|a different Yagami Taichi]] from [[Digimon Adventure:|another reality]] and his [[Agumon (Adventure:)|Agumon]]. While the excitement quickly gives way to the two Taichis bickering, Zeromaru politely introduces himself to Agumon. After explaining the situation, the other Taichi and Agumon volunteer to join them, and together they face another wave of Digimon from the polyhedron, led by a [[Belphemon: Rage Mode]] who quickly overwhelms the group. Despite this, Zeromaru is determined to keep fighting, and when Agumon insists on continuing the fight as well, the two Taichis' determination and courage synchronize, causing a massive [[Crest of Courage]] to manifest in the sky, fulfilling Lord Holy Angemon's foretelling of "new courage," and evolve the two Digimon into [[Ulforce V-dramon Future Mode]] and a specter of [[War Greymon]], who easily destroy the polyhedron. After the battle, Zeromaru (reverted into V-dramon) celebrates with Taichi and the inhabitants of Holy Angel Castle. ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure: Anode Tamer}} & {{hdr|Digimon Adventure: Cathode Tamer|Cathode Tamer}}=== Some event distribution [[V-dramon]]s, such as the one from {{w|Jump Festa|Jump Festa 2000}},ぎょっちゃん (@Gyotchan). [https://twitter.com/Gyotchan/status/1351737797405601792 "ブイドラモン（ゼロマル）のいるアノードテイマーかな🤔 理由は ・通常のプレイで出現しない、リアルイベントで配信されたこと ・20年前のデータが消えずにいること （ワンダースワンのゲームはデータが消えやすい） #フォロワーさんが絶対持ってないであろう物を晒せ #デジモン #digimon"] January 20, 2021, 3:46 AM UTC ±00:00. Tweet. bore the nickname "Zeromaru" {{ETYK|ゼロマル}} in reference to the ''V-Tamer 01'' character. It is currently unknown how many of the other events' distributed V-dramons had this nickname. ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Zeromaru |s1=HC |s2=DCG }} =Additional Information= {{R}}

Warrior Ten.wikitext:
[[Image:Warrior Ten.JPG|thumb|right|The Warrior Ten seen in the Tunnel of History.]]
The '''Warrior Ten''' (十闘士 ''Juttōshi'' or alternatively 伝説の十闘士 ''Densetsu no Juttōshi''; lit. "Legendary Ten Warriors" and 古代十闘士 ''Kodai Juttōshi''; lit. "Ancient Ten Warriors") are ten Digimon that protected the [[Digital World]] in the past. They were created from the same data that created the Digital World and are connected with their 10 [[Legendary Spirits]]. After their fight against [[Lucemon]], they separated into a [[Legendary Spirits|Human and Beast Spirit]], used by the [[Chosen Child|Legendary Chosen]] for [[Evolution#Spirit Evolution|Spirit Evolution]]. The abilities of each member of the Warrior Ten were later passed on to certain types of Digimon.

=Appearances=
==Anime==
[[File:Warrior Ten DF flashback.jpg|thumb|right|Warrior Ten appearing in flashback from ''Digimon Frontier''.]]
==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier}}===
After Lucemon began his tyrannical reign of the Digital World, they rose up and defeated him by sealing him into the Dark Area. After this, they were separated into two spirits each (likely because they were injured in battle). The Chosen Children later inherited these spirits to take their place. 

''See also: [[Evil Warriors]]''

==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Revival of the Ancient Digimon!!|Revival of the Ancient Digimon!!}}===
[[File:Digimon Frontier early sketch.jpg|thumb|right|Early sketch of the Warrior Ten sealing Lucemon.]]
Ancient Greymon and Ancient Garurumon brought down Ornismon. Their spirits and force were temporarily revived in order to defeat their enemy due to the legend of the island.

==Manga==
==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure V-Tamer 01}}===
==Video Games==
==={{hdr|Digimon Frontier: Battle Spirit}}===

==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}===
One of the game's storylines reveals that the Warrior Ten live in almost inaccessible locations near [[Byte Island]], while [[Ancient Wisemon]] lives in a mobile library in a desert area that is always traveling between worlds and never appears twice in the same place.

==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize}}===
The Warrior Ten appear as collectable cards.

==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}===
The Warrior Ten appear as collectable cards.

==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth}}===
After Aiba collects all of the Warrior Ten medals, the Medal Man rewards them with 2 Vigor Mushrooms and 2 Mental Melons. The Five Warriors of Justice medals reward them with HP Attach B and SP Attach B.

==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory}}===
After [[Amasawa Keisuke]] collects all of the Warrior Ten medals, the Medal Man rewards them with 2 Vigor Mushrooms and 2 Mental Melons. The Five Warriors of Justice medals reward them with HP Attach B and SP Attach B.

==={{hdr|Digimon ReArise}}===
In the distant past, the Warrior Ten fought against [[Lucemon]] in an attempt to stop its rebellion. During the fight, eight of them are killed and the remaining two seal Lucemon away.

X-Antibody.wikitext:
[[Image:Agumon hakase xantibody digimon profile.png|thumb|right]] The '''X-Antibody''' (X抗体 ''Ekkusu-kōtai'') is an {{w|antibody}} that, when present in a [[Digimon (species)|Digimon]]'s [[Digicore]], provides them with resistance against the effects of [[Program X]]. If it is present in high enough concentrations, it is also able to cause a Digimon to undergo [[Evolution#X-Antibody and Evolution|X-Evolution]] into an X-Antibody-enhanced form with new or improved traits. =Overview= ==Origins== The X-Antibody originated with [[Program X]], a virus created by [[Yggdrasill]]''[[Digimon Profile]]'': [https://digimon.net/profile/report027/ "Program X" and the "X-Antibody"] ([[Digimon Profile/Report 027|Translation on Wikimon]]) that it used in [[Project Ark]] to cull Digimon and end the overpopulation of the [[Digital World|Old Digital World]]. However, powerful Digimon with strong survival instincts were able to survive by altering their [[Digicore]]s to incorporate Program X itself. This created the X-Antibody, which made them resistant to being deleted by Program X. However, since the X-Antibody in a Digimon's Digicore would expire if the Digimon continued to be exposed to Program X, this resulted in two major societal shifts: first, competing with each other to acquire more X-Antibodies, and second, an exodus of Digimon from the Old Digital World to the [[New Digital World]], where Program X was not present.[http://web.archive.org/web/20090310002240/http://www.digimon.channel.or.jp/chronicle/ndw-03.html Digimon Chronicle: What is the X-Antibody?] ==Mechanics== ===Acquisition=== There are three main ways for a Digimon to acquire the X-Antibody. * When exposed to/infected with Program X, alter their Digicore to incorporate Program X into it by producing antibodies to it. Only strong Digimon are able to do this. * Kill and absorb the data of a Digimon who already possesses the X-Antibody. There is intense survival-of-the-fittest competition between Digimon for X-Antibodies, and weaker X-Antibody Digimon (such as [[Tokomon (X-Antibody)]], a rare case of a Baby X-Antibody Digimon) are often preyed upon as easy targets.{{refd|Tokomon (X-Antibody)|Tokomon_x}} * Receive a serum of the persistent strain of the X-Antibody. ===Temporary Nature=== In its original strain, the X-Antibody population within a Digimon who acquires it is not permanent. They must continue to intake more X-Antibodies in order to maintain the X-Antibody population within themselves.''[[Digimon Chronicle X]]'' In particular, continuous exposure to Program X will destroy a Digimon's X-Antibody population, again unless they intake more X-Antibodies. Failing to maintain the X-Antibody within oneself results in death. This restriction does not apply to Digimon who naturally possess the X-Antibody, such as [[Alphamon]], [[Tobucatmon]], and [[Metal Piranimon]]. The X-Antibody strain that they carry does not disappear from their bodies. The deaths of Digimon who carried the X-Antibody have resulted in the mutation of another, persistent strain. When an X-Antibody Digimon dies and is sent to the [[Dark Area]], they have been known to leave behind the data of duplicate X-Antibodies as residue. Originally, such duplicates would quickly cease to exist. However, over time, the strong survival instincts in the Digicores of the carrier Digimon caused these X-Antibody particles themselves to acquire self-preservation functionality, resulting in a persistent strain of X-Antibody that does not require its host to continue to intake more X-Antibodies. The persistent strain can be acquired by absorbing the data of a Digimon who innately carries it, or it can be administered as a serum to a Digimon. ===X-Antibody Concentration Levels=== The effects of this antibody differ depending on the concentration of antibodies present on the Digicore. There are three levels of concentration. The presence and concentration of the X-Antibody in a Digimon can be identified by the [[X-Antibody Indicator|X-Antibody Indicator (XAI)]] system invented by [[Yggdrasil]]. This system would later be implemented on the [[Digimon Pendulum X]] and [[Digital Monster X]] devices. {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;font-size:90%" |- !style="background: #262626;width:100px"|Image !style="background: #262626;width:100px"|XAI Reading !style="background: #262626;width:8%;"|Concentration !style="background: #262626;"|Effects !style="background: #262626;width:8%;"|Attack Power !style="background: #262626;width:8%;"|Evolution !style="background: #262626;width:8%;"|Behaviour |- |[[File:X antibody protoform.png|100px]] |[[File:XAI crop weak.png|100px]] | Weak | Provides the host with resistance to Program X.
Incomplete form, called the '''X-Antibody Protoform''' (Ｘ抗体プロトフォーム ''Ekkusu-kōtai purotofōmu''). | Weak[[:File:XAntibody2.png|Digimon Pendulum X: Principal Effects on Digimon of the X-Antibody]] | Wild | Aggressive |- |[[File:Xantibody logo2.png|100px]] ||[[File:XAI crop normal.png|100px]] | Normal | Provides the host with resistance to Program X. Extracts latent data from the host's Digicore to induce X-Evolution.
Complete form. | Normal | Natural | Ordinary |- |[[File:StrongXAntibody.png|100px]] ||[[File:XAI crop strong.png|100px]] | Strong | Provides the host with resistance to Program X. Extracts latent data from the host's Digicore to induce X-Evolution.
This level of concentration is characteristic of the strongest X-Antibody Digimon. | Strong | Artificial | Docile |- |} ==Effects== ===Resistance to Program X=== Any concentration level of the X-Antibody within a Digimon's Digicore is sufficient to protect them from the effects of [[Program X]], in the same way that a real {{w|antibody}} protects an individual from a particular foreign pathogen. ===X-Evolution=== [[File:XAntibody.png|thumb|right|200px]][[File:XAntibody3.png|thumb|right|200px]] The X-Antibody has the ability to, if concentrated within a Digicore at normal level or higher, actively extract latent data from the Digicore to a high degree. When this happens, it triggers '''X-Evolution''' (ゼヴォリューション ''Zevoryūshon''/X-進化 ''Ekkusu-shinka''), where the Digimon is transformed into a form that expresses that extracted data. As a result of this, the Digimon becomes able to express both power and its potential to a degree far superior to a common Digimon.[http://web.archive.org/web/20090126190950/http://www.digimon.channel.or.jp/chronicle/30/ch-01.html Digimon Chronicle: Digital World] Typically, X-Evolved variants of pre-existing Digimon are distinguished from the regular variants by the suffix "X-Antibody", usually in parentheses, at the end of their name. For example, a [[Gomamon]] who has undergone X-Evolution is called '''[[Gomamon (X-Antibody)]]''' 「ゴマモン（X抗体）」. This is sometimes shortened to just the letter "X" (for example, '''Gomamon X''' 「ゴマモンX」). The nature of what X-Evolution does to a Digimon is entirely dependent on what, and how much, latent data they have for the X-Antibody to extract. In addition to a common increase in the Digimon's power and potential, effects include: {| border=1 style="background: #363636;color:white; border:1px solid #; border-collapse:collapse;width:900px;font-size:90%" |- |style="width:350px" | Altering the Digimon's Type to reflect latent data of another species. |style="width:350px" | A change in the Digimon's temperament that often intensifies a pre-existing trait. |style="width:350px" | Fully embracing a pre-existing latent power or trait and turning it toward a new direction. |- | style="background: #ffffff; color:#262626;" align="center" | '''Example:'''
[[File:Agumon.jpg|125px]] '''→''' [[File:Agumonx2.jpg|125px]] | style="background: #ffffff; color:#262626" align="center" | '''Example:'''
[[File:Orgemon.jpg|125px]] '''→''' [[File:Orgemonx.jpg|125px]] | style="background: #ffffff; color:#262626" align="center" | '''Example:'''
[[File:Guilmon.jpg|125px]] '''→''' [[File:Guilmonx2.jpg|125px]] |- | An ordinary [[Agumon]] is a [[:Category:Reptile Type|Reptile Type]] Digimon, but the X-Antibody draws out its [[:Category:Dinosaur Type|Dinosaur Type]] data and qualities for evolving into an especially outstanding [[Greymon-species]] Digimon.{{refd|Agumon (X-Antibody)|agumon_x}} | X-Evolution intensifies [[Orgemon]]'s wild temperament and streak of cruelty, turning it into [[Orgemon (X-Antibody)]], a crazed warrior who attacks with glee.{{refd|Orgemon (X-Antibody)|orgemon_x}} | X-Evolution enables [[Guilmon (X-Antibody)]] and its evolutions to accept and contain the power of the [[Digital Hazard]] within itself, giving it the potential to evolve to use that power constructively.{{refd|Guilmon (X-Antibody)|guilmonx}} |- |style="width:350px" | Altering a Digimon's natural anatomy or body plan. |style="width:350px" | Restoring corrupted compositional data in the Digimon's Digicore. |style="width:350px" | Developing new adaptations based on the Digimon's environment. |- | style="background: #ffffff; color:#262626" align="center" | '''Example:'''
[[File:Metalgarurumon2.jpg|125px]] '''→''' [[File:Metalgarurumonx.jpg|125px]] | style="background: #ffffff; color:#262626;" align="center" | '''Example:'''
[[File:Mammon.jpg|125px]] '''→''' [[File:Mammonx.jpg|125px]] | style="background: #ffffff; color:#262626" align="center" | '''Example:'''
[[File:Anomalocarimon.jpg|125px]] '''→''' [[File:Anomalocarimonx.jpg|125px]] |- | [[Metal Garurumon]] is fully quadrupedal, but X-Evolving into [[Metal Garurumon (X-Antibody)]] changes it into a biped so that its body can be fully armed for both short- and long-range combat.{{refd|Metal Garurumon (X-Antibody)|metalgarurumon_x}} |The X-Antibody improves [[Mammon]]'s self-repairing ability, enabling it to near-totally restore the significant corruption of Mammon's data. As a result, [[Mammon (X-Antibody)]] is nearly identical to ancient Mammons.{{refd|Mammon (X-Antibody)|mammon_x}} | [[Anomalocarimon (X-Antibody)]] has evolved an active camouflage ability, {{ATO|Ghost Surprise}}, from all of the elements of its native ocean environment that it has taken into its body.{{refd|Anomalocarimon (X-Antibody)|anomalocarimon_x}} |- |style="width:350px" | Conferring new weapons, equipment or body parts. |style="width:350px" | Enhancing weapons or equipment that the Digimon already had. |style="width:350px" | Providing the Digimon with new attack techniques or powers. |- | style="background: #ffffff; color:#262626" align="center" | '''Example:'''
[[File:Leomon.jpg|125px]] '''→''' [[File:Leomonx.jpg|125px]] | style="background: #ffffff; color:#262626;" align="center" | '''Example:'''
[[File:Justimon.jpg|125px]] '''→''' [[File:Justimonx.jpg|125px]] | style="background: #ffffff; color:#262626" align="center" | '''Example:'''
[[File:Omegamon.jpg|125px]] '''→''' [[File:Omegamonx.jpg|125px]] |- | [[Leomon (X-Antibody)]] acquires a new longsword, "{{EQ|Jūga-maru|Beast Fang}}", which it dual-wields together with its [[Leomon|original]] "{{EQ|Shishiou-maru|Lion King}}". It also acquires armor.{{refd|Leomon (X-Antibody)|leomon_x}} |[[Justimon]] can only use one of its {{EQO|Arms}} at a time, but all three {{EQO|Arms}} are integrated into [[Justimon (X-Antibody)]]'s body, so it does not need to switch between them to use them.{{refd|Justimon (X-Antibody)|justimon_x}} | By X-Evolving, [[Omegamon (X-Antibody)]] has acquired the {{EQO|Omega inForce}}, the power to read ahead and respond accordingly, and the attack technique {{ATO|All Delete}}, which erases anything that the {{EQO|Grey Sword}} cuts.{{refd|Omegamon (X-Antibody)|omegamon_x}} |- |} If a Digimon simply does not have much latent data that its original form was not previously expressing, X-Evolution will not cause much change in them. For example, the relatively minor changes that [[Holydramon]] and [[Goddramon]] undergo when they X-Evolve into [[Holydramon (X-Antibody)]] and [[Goddramon (X-Antibody)]] indicate that the two Digimon had already been expressing their latent data and abilities in their original forms.{{refd|Holydramon (X-Antibody)|holydramon_x}}{{refd|Goddramon (X-Antibody)|goddramon_x}} A side effect of X-Evolution is that any wounds the Digimon had prior to the X-Evolution are healed, and their stamina is replenished.[http://web.archive.org/web/20090126205426/http://www.digimon.channel.or.jp/chronicle/20/ch-02.html Digimon Chronicle: Digital World Collapse Crisis Advent] ===Other Effects=== X-Antibody Digimon can bypass the [[Spiral|XSpiral]]'s "X-Barrier" entirely and deal regular damage, in addition to taking 25% less damage from the XSpiral's "X-Charge". However, this is only applicable to Digimon like [[War Greymon (X-Antibody)]], and not natural carriers like [[Alphamon]].''[[Digimon ReArise]]'' ===Death-X-Evolution=== :''Main article: '''[[Death-X]]''''' Related to the X-Antibody is the phenomenon of [[Death-X]], in which the X-Antibody forces a Digimon to keep evolving even after it has died, in a process called Death-X-Evolution.{{ref|''{{url|http://web.archive.org/web/20090126184052/http://www.digimon.channel.or.jp/chronicle/30/ch-04.html|Digimon Chronicle: Death-X Evolution}}''}} A Digimon who has undergone Death-X-Evolution remains active and animated despite being deceased and despite the decay of its own body, and it is governed by a powerful instinct to consume Digicores so that it may continue to evolve and remain active.{{refd|Death-X-DORUgamon|death-x-dorugamon}}{{refd|Death-X-DORUguremon|death-x-doruguremon}}{{refd|Death-X-DORUgoramon|death-x-dorugoramon}} Digimon normally have no access to Death-X-Evolution by themselves. It can only be triggered by a third party that knows how it works, like [[Yggdrasill]] or [[Grand Dracumon]]. ==Possible retcons to natural carriers== On August 10, 2021 the ''[[Digimon Reference Book]]'' added the X-Antibody to Digimon's profiles, re-confirming them as Digimon with the X-Antibody — however they did not include it for [[Clavis Angemon]], [[Death-X-mon]], [[Dracumon]], [[Grand Dracumon]], [[Matadrmon]], [[Sangloupmon]], [[Medieval Dukemon]], and [[Volcdramon]]. Whether this is a mistake, or is Bandai retconning them to no longer having the X-Antibody is unconfirmed. They are all stated to be X-Antibody carriers in ''[[Digimon Masters]]'' and/or the ''[[Digital Monster Card Game]]''. =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster X-evolution}}=== ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Chronicle}}=== Every Digimon that appears in ''Digimon Chronicle'', except [[Okuwamon]], is an X-Antibody carrier, either natural or not. [[DORUmon|DORUmon's]] evolutions are influenced by his X-Antibody and are referred to as [[Evolution#Digimon Chronicle|X-Evolutions]]. In ''[[Digimon Chronicle - Chapter 1|"X-Evolution"]]'', Okuwamon tried to steal DORUmon's X-Antibody by killing him. [[War Greymon (X-Antibody)]] protected him and [[Doumoto Kouta]], then, after Okuwamon demanded he left as he already possessed the X-Antibody, proclaimed that Digimon like Okuwamon should never obtain that new power and that Kouta needed it. Every time DORUmon evolved, his X-Antibody flashed in his eyes. During his Blast Evolution into [[Alphamon: Ouryuken]], Kouta's eyes also showed the X-Antibody. The same happened to [[Ryudamon (Chronicle)|Ryudamon]] when powered-up by the X-Cast. ==={{hdr|Digimon D-Cyber}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Chronicle X}}=== ==Novel== ==={{hdr|Digimon Chronicle X (Web Novel)|Digimon Chronicle X}}=== ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon World X}}=== ''[[Digimon World X]]'' is based on the history of the X-Antibody. In the game, the X-Antibody can be used to increase the powers of Digimon as well as objects. ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== The ''X-Antibody Factor'' is an item needed to [[Evolution/Video Games#Digimon Masters|evolve]] certain [[Ultimate]] Digimon into their X-Antibody forms, considered [[Burst Mode]]s in the game. Other X-Antibody Digimon are also playable as their own evolution lines. ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== The X-Antibody is a plot point during the '''Lament of the X-Antibody chapter''' (慟哭のＸ抗体編 ''Dōkoku no X-Kōtai-hen''). It protects Digimon from the [[X-Program]], which [[Yggdrasill]] launched once again, as well as greatly powering them up. [[DORUmon (X-evolution)|DORUmon]] possesses it, but doesn't know why, and is traumatized because it allowed him to survive when all of his friends were killed by the X-Program. Due to that, he's attracted to [[Kuga Yuuya]] and [[Black]], who himself obtained the X-Antibody in the past for reasons unknown even to them. DORUmon himself attracted [[Plotmon (Re:Digitize)|Plotmon]], who in its search for power, later obtains the X-Antibody itself and [[Evolution/Video Games#Digimon_World_Re:Digitize_.26_Decode|X-Evolves]] into [[Plotmon (X-Antibody)]], the sight of which terrifies DORUmon. To brave [[Infinity Mountain]], which is infected by the X-Program, [[Omegamon (X-Antibody)]] and [[Dukemon (X-Antibody)]], who themselves possess the X-Antibody, created the Infinity Mountain Exploration Team, which consists of other X-Antibody Digimon like [[Agumon (X-Antibody)]] and [[Greymon (X-Antibody)]]. [[Seadramon]] later joins it, reactivating its own X-Antibody and X-Evolving into [[Seadramon (X-Antibody)]]. Other X-Antibody Digimon also appear in Infinity Mountain to challenge [[Taiga]]/[[Shinomiya Rina]] and their allies, some clones, some real, including the [[Seven Great Demon Lords|Demon Lord]] [[Beelzebumon]] who X-Evolved into [[Beelzebumon (X-Antibody)]]. Clearing the ones that appear as fixed boss battles may award Taiga/Rina specific items which can be traded to Agumon (X-Antibody) for X-Antibody items. In the Colosseum Heavenly Hall Free Battle "We'll put you to the test", [[Yagami Taichi (Adventure)|Yagami Taichi's]] [[Agumon (Adventure)|Agumon]] and [[Matsuda Takato|Matsuda Takato's]] [[Guilmon (Tamers)|Guilmon]] implicitely obtain the X-Antibody, as they can be fought as [[War Greymon (X-Antibody)]]/Omegamon (X-Antibody) and Dukemon (X-Antibody), respectively, though no explanation is given as to when or how they did so. Clearing their fights rewards Taiga/Rina with the X-Antibody items for those specific Digimon. In terms of gameplay, Digimon with the X-Antibody are immune to the X-Program constantly sucking their health in certain Infinity Mountain battles as well as the first Omegamon (X-Antibody)/Dukemon (X-Antibody) fight, otherwise a special item is needed to stop the drain. As well, using certain X-Antibody items on specific [[Ultimate]] Digimon will allow them to evolve into their X-Antibody forms, powering them up and making them immune to the X-Program. Using these items is the only way to obtain these evolutions. Uniquely, the item for [[Black War Greymon (X-Antibody)]] can be used on any [[Perfect]] Digimon and can be obtained from an obligatory story fight against Yuuya and Black separate from the ones mentioned above, significantly earlier into the game, though it's unlikely to be obtained outside of a New Game + as the fight is very difficult for that point of the game and the player is allowed to lose in it and still proceed with the story. {| border=1 style="background: #363636; color:#fff; border: 1px solid #; border-collapse: collapse; font-size: 90%" width="850px" class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed" |- |colspan=4 align="center" bgcolor="#262626" | '''X-Antibody items in ''Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode''''' |- ! bgcolor="#262626" width="150px" | Item ! bgcolor="#262626" width="150px" | Digimon ! bgcolor="#262626" width="250px" | Requirements ! bgcolor="#262626" width="300px" | Source |- | '''Courage X-Antibody''' || '''[[War Greymon (X-Antibody)]]''' || Current Digimon: [[War Greymon]] || Trade Courage Piece to Agumon (X-Antibody) in Infinity Mountain |- | '''Crimson X-Antibody''' || '''[[Rosemon (X-Antibody)]]''' || Current Digimon: [[Rosemon]] || Trade Crimson Piece to Agumon (X-Antibody) in Infinity Mountain |- | '''Ultimate X-Antibody''' || '''[[Omegamon (X-Antibody)]]''' || Current Digimon: [[Omegamon]] || Win the Colosseum Heavenly Hall Free Battle: "We'll put you to the test". Trade Ultimate Piece to Agumon (X-Antibody) in Infinity Mountain |- | '''Supreme X-Antibody''' || '''[[Dukemon (X-Antibody)]]''' || Current Digimon: [[Dukemon]] || Win the Colosseum Heavenly Hall Free Battle: "We'll put you to the test". Trade Supreme Piece to Agumon (X-Antibody) in Infinity Mountain |- | '''Swiftness X-Antibody''' || '''[[Ulforce V-dramon (X-Antibody)]]''' || Current Digimon: [[Ulforce V-dramon]] || Trade Swiftness Piece to Agumon (X-Antibody) in Infinity Mountain |- | '''Gluttony X-Antibody''' || '''[[Beelzebumon (X-Antibody)]]''' || Current Digimon: [[Beelzebumon]] || Trade Gluttony Piece to Agumon (X-Antibody) in Infinity Mountain |- | '''Miracle X-Antibody''' || '''[[Magnamon (X-Antibody)]]''' || Current Digimon: [[Magnamon]] || Win the Colosseum Heavenly Hall Free Battle: "We'll put you to the test". Trade Miracle Piece to Agumon (X-Antibody) in Infinity Mountain |- | '''Jet-Black X-Antibody''' || '''[[Black War Greymon (X-Antibody)]]''' || Current Evolution Stage: [[Perfect]] || Dropped from boss [[Black]] in [[Signpost Woods]] |- |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Crusader}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Linkz}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon New Century}}=== Sometime before meeting [[Tokumori Katsuhiro]], [[Kotemon (New Century)|Duftmon]] and [[Omegamon]] fought [[Barbamon]] and [[Megidramon]]. During the fight, Omegamon gave Duftmon an X-Antibody causing Duftmon to X-evolve to [[Duftmon (X-Antibody)]]. The new evolution allowed the duo to defeat the two evil Digimon. [[Grand Dracumon]] creates an immortal army of [[Vamdemon-species]] Digimon using the data leftover from [[Yggdrasil]], in an attempt to replicate the effects of the [[Death-X]]. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|Digimon Pendulum X}}=== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster D-Cyber}}=== ==={{hdr|D-Spirit 2}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Accel Ultimate Genome}}=== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster X}}=== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster X Ver.2}}=== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster X Ver.3}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |d=Xantibody |s1=HC |s2=DCG }} ==={{hdr|Digital Monster Card Game}}=== In the [[Digital Monster Card Game]], cards depicting [[:Category:X-Antibody Digimon|Digimon who possess the X-Antibody]] are identified as such by their [[:Category:Red Frame Cards|red frame]] (as opposed to the [[:Category:Blue Frame Cards|blue]], [[:Category:Gold Frame Cards|gold]], or [[:Category:Silver Frame Cards|silver frames]] used by other cards). The red frame is regularly cited as a necessary criterion in cards' Evolution/Fusion/Appearance Requirements for evolution into or from X-Antibody Digimon, and in some cards' Special Abilities that target X-Antibody Digimon in some way. Option Cards relating to the X-Antibody and the related story also have the red frame. ==={{hdr|Digimon Card Game}}=== In the [[Digimon Card Game]], [[:Category:X-Antibody Digimon|Digimon who possess the X-Antibody]] are identified as such by the listing of an "X-Antibody" property in the Digimon's Type. The X-Antibody's own card also lists this property. The property is cited as a necessary criterion in the effects of Digimon and Option Cards that in some way relate to or target X-Antibody Digimon. =Image Gallery= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Dch-7-470 front.png{{!}}200x200px |c1=[[Digimon Crusader]]
(Card) |i2=Xantibody.png{{!}}200x200px |c2=[[Digimon Profile]] }} =Additional Information= {{R}} [[Category:Terminology]] [[Category:X-Antibody Digimon| ]]

Unnamed mother |vjp=[[Ōwada Hitomi]] (大和田 仁美) }} '''Yamada Haruko''' is a former minor antagonist in ''[[Digimon Beatbreak]]''. =Profile= =Shademon= {{Char |name=Shademon |image=Shademon DB09.jpg |imagesize=320x320 |kan=シェイドモン |hp=Yamada Haruko |vjp=[[Miyata Kouki]] (宮田 幸季) }} '''Shademon''' is Yamada Haruko's [[Partner Digimon]]. ==Evolution== {{Box Evo |Adult = '''[[Shademon]]''' }} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digimon Beatbreak}}=== =Image Gallery= ==Artwork== ==Miscellaneous== =Additional Information= {{R}} {{Template:NavboxBeatbreak}} [[Category:Digimon Beatbreak characters]]

Yggdrasill.wikitext:
{{FArticle|5|27}} [[Image:Yggdrasill Digimon Savers.png|thumb|right|One of Yggdrasill's avatars from ''Digimon Savers'']] '''Yggdrasill''' (イグドラシル, ''Igudorashiru''; Dub: ''King Drasil''), is the mysterious {{w|host computer}} that rules over the Digital World on one of the [[server]]s on the [[Network]] as well as being the name of the system that manages the Digital World. Originally, "Yggdrasill" was a generic name for the system that was operating on the supercomputer where the [[Net Ocean]] was discovered, and later, as a self-defense instinct to eliminate [[bug]]s, it began to function without making any judgment between good and evil.{{ref|''Digital Monster ART BOOK Ver. PENDULUM'' (page 216)}} According to one hypothesis, it sent [[N.E.O]] to be the [[Prototype Digimon|basis]] ({{w|Adam}}) for "Next-generation Digimon".{{refd|N.E.O|neo}} It has created an avatar in the form of "[[Yggdrasill_7D6]]".{{refd|Yggdrasill_7D6}} [[Shakamon]] is rumored to be the being closest to Yggdrasill in the Digital World.{{refd|Shakamon}} Yggdrasill gathered the [[Royal Knights]] in order to manage the world,{{ref|''{{url|p-bandai.jp/b-boys-shop/item-1000089867/|Premium Bandai: Digimon Adventure Digivice Ver.15th}}''}} and even modified the code of [[Craniummon]]'s armor, changing it into [[Black Digizoid]].{{refd|Craniummon}} It seems that its will will eventually lead the Royal Knights to cross servers to [[Digital World: Iliad]] and meet the [[Olympos XII]] face-to-face.{{ref|''{{url|p-bandai.jp/b-boys-shop/item-1000089867/|Premium Bandai: Digimon Adventure Digivice Ver.15th}}''}} =Background= Yggdrasill is commonly treated as the equivalent of the [[God]] of the Digital World in various adaptations throughout the franchise. =Depictions= Being a digital entity without a physical form, Yggdrasill usually employs many kinds of avatars in order to interact with the physical world. ==Ring of information== Yggdrasill is sometimes shown as a ring of information surrounding the Digital World itself.{{ref|''[[Digimon Chronicle]]''}} This depiction was used in [[Digimon Chronicle]] and [[Digital Monster X-evolution]]. {| |- |[[File:Yggdrasill ring TCG.png|thumb|Yggdrasill's "ring of information" floating above the Digital World.]] |[[File:Yggdrasill artbook ver.pendulum.png|thumb|Yggdrasill's "ring of information" surrounding the Digital World.]] |} ==Crystal Orb== Not much is known about this depiction of Yggdrasill. It appeared in the [[Digital Monster X-evolution]] movie and served as the core of the Digital World. {| |- |[[Image:Yggdrasil.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Yggdrasill depicted in Digital Monster X-evolution]] |} ==[[Yggdrasill_7D6]]== "7D6" is an avatar created by the mysterious host computer "Yggdrasill".{{refd|Yggdrasill_7D6}} It is its most commonly depicted avatar. {| |- |[[Image:Yggdrasill 7d6.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Yggdrasill_7D6]] |} ==[[Mikihara Norn]]== Norn is a mysterious girl born as a physical manifestation of Yggdrasill splitting its consciousness in order to go to Earth to discover what Digimon meant to humans, as it was unsure about the bonds between the two. This depiction of Yggdrasill has only been used in the [[Digimon Next]] manga. {| |- |[[Image:Mikihara norn.png|thumb|right|200px|Mikihara Norn from [[Digimon Next]]]] |} ==Yggdrasill's Card Terminals== In [[Digimon Crusader]], Yggdrasill had disappeared. All that remained were scraps of data, the five coloured "Cards" that served as Yggdrasill's Terminals. Although they were extremely powerful, at the same time, they were completely meaningless, because under the system of this world, no one was able to make use of them. However, [[Aegiomon]] was able to master all five "colors" that represented the species of the Digital World, and, when evolved into [[Jupitermon]], was able to use the Terminals in a fight against [[Plutomon]].{{ref|[[Gallery:Digimon_Crusader/Storyline#Chapter.11_Our_Digital_World|Digimon Crusader Chapter 11]]}} {| |[[Image:Aegiomon's Chronicle chap.11 1.png|thumb|right|200px|The five Terminals of Yggdrasill]] |[[Image:Aegiomon's Chronicle chap.11 17.png|thumb|right|200px|Jupitermon using the five Terminals]] |} =Appearances= ==Anime== ==={{hdr|Digital Monster X-evolution}}=== Very similar to the case of Chronicle, it maintained the Digital World and Digimon as part of an artificial life experiment run by an unknown party. Eventually, Digimon multiplied until its memory started to overload, causing the Digital Hazard. It activated Project Ark, using the X-Program to eliminate 99% of [[Digimon]], saving the remaining 1% for follow-up experiments in a New Digital World. However, another 1% of the Digimon population managed to survive by developing the [[X-Antibody]], giving them a resistance to the X-Program and mutating them into newer forms. Yggdrasill dispatched the [[Royal Knights]] to hunt down the X-Antibody Digimon, which they did in the belief they were preventing the destruction of the Digital World. However, Yggdrasill was programmed to intervene in and administrate problems in the Digital World, but decided that it had become too complicated and needed to be done away with entirely to keep order, so it unleashed a plan to delete all Digimon. Yggdrasill was eventually destroyed by the Royal Knights [[Alphamon]] and [[Omegamon]], when they realized that it intended to end the lives of all Digimon. ==={{hdr|Digimon Savers}}=== ''See [[Yggdrasill (Savers)]]'' In [[Digimon Savers]], [[Daimon Masaru]]'s father [[Daimon Suguru|Suguru]] goes on a quest to find Yggdrasill in order to bring peace between Digimon and Humans. 10 years later, Yggdrasill dispatches the [[Royal Knights (Savers)|Royal Knights]] to destroy the Human World after [[Kurata Akihiro|Kurata]]'s use of the [[space-time oscillation bomb]]s causes the [[Digital World]] to fall into the Human World. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure tri.}}=== ''See [[Yggdrasill (Adventure)]]'' [[Himekawa Maki]] works with Yggdrasill to force the reboot to bring [[Bakumon (Adventure)|Bakumon]] back to life. The [[Mysterious Man]] and [[Alphamon (Adventure)|Alphamon]] work for Yggdrasill, and claim the entity is their ally. The Mysterious Man states the actual plan for the [[reboot]] was to revive all the Digimon that had been killed in the real world, and then use them to take over and then destroy the Human World. After [[Hackmon (Adventure tri.)|JESmon]] cuts [[Tailmon (Adventure)|Tailmon]] out of [[Ordinemon]], Yggdrasill replaces Tailmon's place with new files, causing her to return to the fight. Ordinemon however is killed by [[Omegamon: Merciful Mode]], and shortly afterwards, [[Homeostasis]] shuts down Yggdrasill. ==Manga== ==={{hdr|Digimon Chronicle}}=== Yggdrasil was established as the Digital World System, responsible for managing the entire Digital World and whose function was to carry out experiments in favor of evolution of Digimon. It is theorized that Digimon where a direct creation of Yggdrasill, and it was responsible of creating the [[Prototype Digimon]] of the "DORU Series" and "Ryu Series". Alongside these experiments, Yggdrasill also created the [[Death-X]], an old relic of evolutionary research that artificially increases the survival instinct of Digimon to its limit. As Digimon multiplied, Yggdrasill became unable to cope with things, causing the Digital Hazard. It activated [[Project Ark]], using the X Program to eliminate unwanted Digimon, saving the remaining for follow-up experiments in a [[New Digital World]]. This New Digital World consisted of three layers for the past, present, and future - [[Ulud]], [[Versandi]], and [[Skuld]], respectively. Yggdrasill was said to be responsible for managing the entire Digital World and carrying out experiments in favor of the evolution of Digimon. It is theorized that Digimon were a direct creation of Yggdrasill, and it was responsible of creating the [[Prototype Digimon]] of the "DORU Series" and "Ryu Series".. Alongside these experiments, Yggdrasill also created the [[Death-X]], an old relic of evolutionary research that artificially increases the survival instinct of Digimon to its limit.{{ref|''[[Digimon Chronicle]]''}} ==={{hdr|Digimon Next}}=== ''See [[Yggdrasill (Next)]]'' Yggdrasill is the host computer of the [[Digital World]] and resides in the [[System World]]. {| |[[File:Barbamon Yggdrasil.png|thumb|[[Barbamon (Next)|Barbamon]] fused with Yggdrasill.]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)|Digimon Xros Wars}}=== When Yggdrasill's AI had gone berserk during the events of [[Digimon Savers]], leading to multiple attempts to destroy the human world and threatening the Digital World itself, the Yggdrasill System was deactivated for some time in the quest to better understand humans and their bond to Digimon. [[Bagramon]] remade the right side of his body with wood from Yggdrasill's server tree. Learning from Yggdrasill's mistakes, the [[Homeostasis|Security System of the Digital World]] took it's place as the God of the Digital World in an attempt to be a more calm and protective god. ==={{hdr|Digimon Chronicle X}}=== After the activation of the [[Project Ark]], the old Digital World had become a world of death due to the contamination by the [[X-Program]], and was thus under strict lockdown. However, after a decade or so had passed, the concentration of the X-Program there had thinned enough that it would not affect the bodies of Digimon directly; as such, Yggdrasill ordered the [[Royal Knights]] to conduct a survey on the current state of the old Digital World. And so [[Craniummon]] and [[Sleipmon]] were given the [[X-Antibody]] by Yggdrasill. Well-prepared, they began their descent into the old Digital World. ==={{hdr|Hiragana Digimon: A Tale of the Royal Knights}}=== ==Novel== ==={{hdr|Digimon Chronicle X (Web Novel)|Digimon Chronicle X}}=== ==={{hdr|Digimon Seekers}}=== Mentioned only. ==Video Games== ==={{hdr|Digimon World X}}=== Yggdrasill is the {{w|Host Computer}} of this Digital World. As hinted in the game, it is possible that Yggdrasill was behind the creation of the [[Mecha Rogue]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Sunburst}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Moonlight|Moonlight}}=== Yggdrasill is mentioned by [[Grimmon]] in the "Search the Digiarea" quest. It taunts Yggdrasill by claiming its project Ymir will rewrite the entire [[Digital World]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Lost Evolution}}=== [[Kernel (Lost Evolution)|Kernel]] believes that the existence of Tamers, Digimon and even evolution is nothing but useless data that needs to be wiped out. The plan of the Erasers was to send all the Data back to where they came from, the "Ancient Tree" (エンシェントツリー) which is the origin of the data and the evolution itself. The final plan of Kernel is to gather all the data of all the Tamers and Digimon of the Digital World so that all returned to Ancient Tree, then Kernel would take this data and to become one with all that already existed, it also affirms that the power which he was using when he turned the [[Giga Devast]] is the power that comes directly from Ancient Tree. So indeed the Ancient Tree is Yggdrasill, then all the data that exists comes from Yggdrasill, just as all the power of the Digimon really belongs to Yggdrasill itself. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Super Xros Wars Blue}} & {{hdr|Digimon Story: Super Xros Wars Red|Red}}=== It is revealed that [[Uno]], [[Dos]] & [[Tres]] are Digimon-like beings created by Yggdrasill itself as a result of a project of the old Tamer Union. ==={{hdr|Digimon Collectors}}=== Yggdrasill sends a Digitama to the Lower World which is then guarded by [[Hackmon]] and the [[Sistermon]] and eventually hatches releasing [[Arkadimon]], who will spread the data to form the "[[Seven Great Demon Lords|Tower of the Seven Deadly Sins]]", and there would be the fate of the [[Royal Knights]] sealed. The Towers were collecting the essence of all sins, and ultimately give birth to [[Ogudomon]], who is sent by the will of Yggdrasill as a trial to find [[JESmon|superior beings]]. [[Gankoomon]] and [[Bao Hackmon]] together manage to send Ogudomon into space, and then Ogudomon simply stops acting, whereas even the Royal Knights do not fully understand the will of Yggdrasill. ==={{hdr|Digimon Crusader}}=== The Digital World is undergoing a great chaos, a vortex of war never seen before. The cause: the main computer had the task of maintaining order; however "[[Yggdrasill 7D6|Yggdrasill]]" suddenly vanished. Due to the lack of rules, the Digimon tried to gain power to become the "[[God|Next Ruler of the Digital World]]". In the forest, far from the wars, [[Aegiomon]] was living in peace with his friends. He was born in [[Digital World: Iliad]] and originally was [[Jupitermon]], one of the [[Olympos XII]], but at some point he became Aegiomon and lost his memory, suddenly appearing in the Digital World ruled by a missing Yggdrasill while having to fight for his fate in the battlefield. ==={{hdr|Digimon Adventure (Game)|Digimon Adventure}}=== [[Gankoomon (Adventure)|Gankoomon]] is one of the [[Royal Knights]] and [[Hackmon (Adventure)|Hackmon's]] guardian. ==={{hdr|Digimon World Re:Digitize Decode}}=== Yggdrasill is the {{w|Host Computer}} of the [[Digital World]]. Some time before the beginning of the story of the game, Yggdrasill spread the [[Program X]] to eliminate unnecessary Digimon, thus optimizing the Digital World. Some time after that, it is deactivated. After being sealed and deactivated within the [[Infinity Mountain]] for a long time, Yggdrasill reactivates on its own when it feels the danger that the aritificial lifeform called [[Vitium]] represents, and reuses the Program X in order to prevent Vitium from destroying the Digital World. It should be noted that it's highly implied that this Yggdrasill is the same one from [[Digital Monster X-Evolution]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth}}=== Several Yggdrasill terminals are fought leading up to the final boss of the game, the main Yggdrasill unit fused with the [[Mother Eater]]. Once Yggdrasill is freed from the [[Eater]] influence, its normal functionality is restored and it begins repairing the [[Digital World]]. Yggdrasill also appears on a Digimon Medal. {| |[[File:Mother Eater.jpg|thumb|The [[Mother Eater]] fused with Yggdrasill.]] |} ==={{hdr|Digimon World -next 0rder-}}=== The various members of the [[Royal Knights]] mention they work for Yggdrasill. ==={{hdr|Digimon World -next 0rder- International Edition}}=== The various members of the [[Royal Knights]] mention they work for Yggdrasill. ==={{hdr|Digimon Story: Cyber Sleuth Hacker's Memory}}=== The various members of the [[Royal Knights]] mention they work for Yggdrasill. Craniummon begins to doubt its leadership, which leads Yggdrasill to mind control it and force it to do as it is told. Yggdrasill also appears on a Digimon Medal. ==={{hdr|Digimon Masters}}=== [[Gankoomon]] states it works for [[Yggdrasill (Savers) |Yggdrasill]]. ==={{hdr|Digimon New Century}}=== The captain and the [[Player (New Century)|Player]], under the guidance of Yggdrasill, were able to defeat [[Hyuuga Hiroyuki]] and [[Fantomon]]. ==Virtual Pets== ==={{hdr|D-Spirit 2}}=== ==TCG== {{NeoTCG |s1=HC |s2=AE |s3=DCG }} =Image Gallery= {{IGTabs2 |1=bandai |t1= {{IG |bg=white |w=200 |h=200 |i1=Yggdrasill 7d6.jpg{{!}}200px |c1=[[Digimon Reference Book]]

([[Yggdrasill_7D6]]) |i2=Yggdrasill vpet spirit2 3.gif |c2=[[D-Spirit 2]] }} =Trivia= * Yggdrasill is named after the {{w|Yggdrasil|giant ash tree}} that connected the {{w|Norse_cosmology#Nine Homeworlds|nine worlds}} in {{w|Norse mythology}}, tended by the {{w|Norns|three Norns}}, after which the Terminals in the [[NEW Digital World]] that Yggdrasill created are named. * In ''[[Digimon Fortune]]'', there's a huge "[[World Tree]]" in the Packet Island that has a connection with the Orbital Elevator called "[[Noah]]" that connects it to the [[Dark Area]]. Although the name is the same as the avatar created by Yggdrasill that supports the Digital World, and has a similar appearance to the Tree used in ''[[Digimon Savers]]'' and ''[[Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)]]'', no relation between it and Yggdrasil is made in the game itself. * In ''[[Digimon Adventure:]]'', a "Big Tree of Information" appears, similar to the "Information Tree" in ''[[Digimon Xros Wars (Manga)]]'' (which [[Bagramon (Xros Wars)|Bagramon]] states is Yggdrasill). Whether they're the same thing in ''Adventure:'' and ''Xros Wars'' is unconfirmed. * By forcibly combining the powers of the [[Seven Great Demon Lords]] and the [[X-Antibody]], Ogudomon X can no longer be identified as a Digimon, but a part of the system itself, an existence that can almost be said to be God; a ‘God’ with the power to destroy and recreate the Digital World. Ogudomon X is composed of data that’s much like Yggdrasill’s, thus making Yggdrasill and Ogudomon X-Antibody into opposing kinds of gods, like the two sides of the same coin.{{ref|''{{url|https://digi-lab.blog/digital-monster-art-book-ver-x-novel-of-x/|Digital Monster - Artbook Ver.X, Novel of X}}''}} {| |- |[[File:Ogudomonx.jpg|thumb|200px]] |} =References= {{R}} [[Category:Host computers]] [[Category:Digital Monster X-Evolution characters]] [[Category:Digimon Chronicle characters]] [[Category:Digimon Next characters]] [[Category:Digital Lifeforms]]
